act49side.tagline: The story of a fool who's played the same game of Go for a thousand years, trying to bring his dead sister back to life. Using the whole land as his board, searching for the single move that saves everyone except himself.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: A former Lungmen Guard Department superintendent who infiltrates the capital Baizhao chasing her missing uncle, Wei Yanwu · Operator Ch'en the Dawnstreak

chars.name: Wang
chars.desc: The second-born of the Twelve, born from Sui's shattered consciousness. A grandmaster who's played the same game of Go for a thousand years · Operator Wang

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: A Fourth-Order agent of the fourth generation of Yan. An earnest young man tangled up in a token brought back from the Vigil Garden · Operator Zuo Le

chars.name: Chongyue
chars.desc: The eldest of the Twelve. A martial arts instructor at Yumen who remembers and holds onto everything · Operator Chongyue

chars.name: Jun
chars.desc: The judge among the Twelve, a musician. The fairest and least self-interested of them all (no Operator release yet)

chars.name: Ling
chars.desc: The eldest sister of the Twelve. A poet devoted to wine and verse · Operator Ling

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: The ninth of the Twelve. A high-spirited blacksmith who loves fireworks and film · Operator Nian

chars.name: Shu
chars.desc: The sixth of the Twelve. Quietly sees what's coming · Operator Shu

chars.name: Dusk
chars.desc: The sleepyhead of the Twelve, in charge of painting · Operator Dusk

chars.name: Yu
chars.desc: The youngest of the Twelve. A cook who keeps 'Yu's Kitchenette' running · Operator Yu

terms.name: Yan
terms.desc: A great nation in Terra's Far East. Ruled for a thousand years by a single line of monarchs known as the True Dragon.

terms.name: the True Dragon
terms.desc: Yan's monarch. Bound by bloodline to the very first Originium, which has kept power concentrated in a single person for a thousand years.

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: An ancient Beast sleeping in the 'Mausoleum of Sui' beneath Baizhao. Endlessly evolving in its dream, seeking to swallow the whole world.

terms.name: the Twelve
terms.desc: Twelve agents born from Sui's shattered consciousness. Brothers and sisters who each search the human world for their own answer to 'who am I,' and live as themselves.

terms.name: the Scribe's Blade
terms.desc: An ordinary knife once used for carving characters into bamboo slips. Infused with the fifth sibling Jie's power, it can now erase or rewrite characters, names, and even history itself.

terms.name: Wraith-Craft
terms.desc: The ability some Beasts have to breathe life into lifeless objects. The 'Word Wraiths' — born from characters given life this way — appear across Baizhao.

terms.name: the Vigil Garden
terms.desc: The garden kept by the eighth sibling, Yi. Where the Rite of Vigil takes place — part of the power that keeps Sui in check.

terms.name: the Token
terms.desc: The mark given to a Fourth-Order agent who has completed the Rite of Vigil. This time, it takes the shape not of a jade token, but of a small doll.

terms.name: the Fourth Order
terms.desc: An organization founded on the old battlefield of the Great Hunt. For a thousand years, its sole mission has been 'making the best choice for Yan regarding the Beast problem.'

terms.name: the Unreturning
terms.desc: "It never comes back." The first Originium, created when an ancestral True Dragon sacrificed himself. Each successive True Dragon can only wield its power at the cost of their own life.

blocks.x: Arknights's ultimate 'match' event. The stage is the great nation of the Far East that the True Dragon has ruled for a thousand years — **Yan**.
The story opens as **Ch'en**, an Infected former Lungmen Guard Department superintendent, infiltrates the capital Baizhao in pursuit of her uncle **Wei Yanwu**, who vanished without a word.

But really, this is the story of a fool who's been playing the same game of Go for a thousand years — to bring his dead sister back, and in the end, to remain himself — using the whole land as his board.

When Ch'en was a child, her sister Tallu once told her, "Hurry and grow up. Once you're an adult, you'll be able to change everything." That one line comes back around a thousand years later, right at the very end.
1. A ghost army appears in Baizhao (and it's made of 'characters,' no less)
From the very first scene, Ch'en and her uncle clash sharply at an inn. Wei Yanwu tells her to go back; Ch'en insists on finding out for herself.

That night, Yan's Grand Tutor visits Wei Yanwu and leaves him with a loaded remark: "Your brother is the True Dragon, Yan's True Dragon" — meaning the current monarch is Wei Yanwu's own younger brother. And a warning besides: "Keep a trump card in reserve."
No sooner is it said than Wei Yanwu vanishes without a trace.
Meanwhile, as evacuations begin in Baizhao, ghostly soldiers appear out of nowhere — no sound, not even a drumbeat.

blocks.cap: Blood-stained old armor, soldiers of the Great Hunt from a thousand years ago — 'Word Wraiths' march silently through the streets.

blocks.x: The Imperial Guard calls these things **Wraith-Craft** — a Beast breathing life into the lifeless. It comes out that the source, of all things, is a single sword sealed atop the Tianjing Pavilion.

For Ch'en, this chaos feels far too personal. Historian **Jin Xiaotian** at the Tianjing Pavilion reads her ID card the Yan way — "Jin Huijie." Only here does Ch'en learn that she is the daughter of the vanished Princess of Yan, and blood of the True Dragon. "I thought I was alone," she murmurs.
2. Five hundred years ago, a fool bets his life on a board of Go
Here's where the real protagonist appears. The second-born of the **Twelve**, born from Sui's shattered consciousness — the Go master, **Wang**.
The Twelve are fragments of a monster, yet each of them searches the human world for their own answer to "who am I," and lives as themselves. That's exactly what sets them apart from Sui.

blocks.cap: The fifth sibling Jie points at a sheet of paper — "No matter what you use to record it, only what's remembered lasts forever."

blocks.x: Wang is clumsy in a human body — clumsy enough that, playing Go with the eldest sibling **Chongyue** (Shuo), he once slammed the board hard enough to crack it. His brother always scolds him for it, but never once leaves his side.

The fifth sibling **Jie** is the one who guards history. With her knife, the **Scribe's Blade**, infused with her own power, she can even erase the very character for "Sui" wherever it has seeped into the world.

Wang takes the Scribe's Blade and dives into Sui's dream, carving away the character "Sui" one stroke at a time. Amid pain like a natural disaster, he erases 1,393 of them... and in the end, his body shatters, leaving behind only three black stones.
That was Wang's first defeat, 120 years ago. And without bending an inch, he declares, "I will keep playing this match."

But as he pulls free of the dream that time, Wang unknowingly drags one Go stone out along with him. That stone would later become 'the One Who Took White' — a puppet wearing Wang's face, that spends centuries turning the siblings against the people, and eventually drives him back to the mausoleum.
3. So Jie decides to disappear, in her brother's place
Jie was someone who would never distort history under anyone's pressure. Even when officials begged her to undercount the casualties, she refuses flatly.
History isn't written by any one person's word — it's recorded in the unspoken words of countless ordinary people.
The problem was Jie herself. The deeper history grew, the more her power kept expanding without end — until someday, she was fated to become something more terrifying than Sui itself.
Jie's Fourth-Order agent, **Chun**, pushed by Yan's will, lies to her. When Jie asks, "How did it feel when Mother died?" Chun answers that it was nothing much, that she got over it quickly.
In truth, it felt like my heart was being torn apart.
That lie pushes Jie toward believing that "the death of family is something bearable." Jie, knowing full well what's really in Chun's heart, quietly says, "You truly are a responsible agent."
Jie entrusts the Scribe's Blade to **Jun**, the fairest of her siblings, and walks alone into the darkness in the dying Wang's place.

blocks.cap: "Wait. Until I come back. Until you... come back." A golden light swallows Jie.

blocks.x: 4. That little doll's true identity — it was Jie
Back to the present. The **Token** Zuo Le brought back from the Vigil Garden wasn't the old jade token — it was some small doll instead.

Zuo Le, the thief girl **Mo Yi**, and Ch'en chase this doll and each other in circles, until finally its identity is revealed. Wang had scooped out part of Sui's arrogant imagination inside the Vigil Garden, left only the shell, and filled it with his siblings' memories to make a Wraith — **Jie's last breath.**
Chun breaks down in front of it, unable to bring herself to destroy it.
And Jun hands over the Scribe's Blade, kept for a hundred years, to Ch'en.

blocks.cap: "Tie off this knot for us." Jun hands the Scribe's Blade to Ch'en.

blocks.x: Because Yan's history has to be written by Yan's own people.
5. The Grand Marshal's plan: rewrite history to kill a man
Beneath Baizhao lies the first Originium, keeping Sui in check — **the Unreturning.** Each successive True Dragon can only wield that power at the cost of their own life.
The Unreturning. It never comes back.

But the hardline war hawk, **the Grand Marshal**, makes a chilling move. If he uses the Scribe's Blade to rewrite history so that Wei Yanwu becomes 'the reigning True Dragon,' he can pass the sacrificial burden onto him — exploiting the loophole that the sacrifice need not be the current True Dragon.
Learning of this, Wei Yanwu tries to offer himself as the sacrifice, and Ch'en blocks him with her sword.

blocks.cap: Jeokso in her left hand, the Scribe's Blade in her right — Ch'en stands against her uncle's 'severing sword.'

blocks.x: Ch'en's answer was this:
An apology from the dead means nothing. If there's someone you want to apologize to, you have to meet them alive.
Ch'en wins, proving there's an option that saves everyone without sacrifice, and Wei Yanwu names this new swordsmanship after her — **Cheonwi.**

Meanwhile at Yumen, Zuo Le's father, General Zuo Xianliao, flatly refuses the Grand Marshal's order for an unauthorized war. "I may be just a soldier, but I am also the Marquis of Pingsong of Yan — I cannot obey an order this absurd." Zuo Le, beside his father, throws his whole body in front of a cannon aimed wrongly.
6. At last, the whole family is together
For Wang's final match, the twelve siblings, scattered and living apart, gather in one place for the very first time.

blocks.cap: The ninth sibling Nian arrives hauling a firework weapon, the 'String Cannon' — "Let me spice things up a bit, no need to thank me!"

blocks.x: Nian sweeps away Wraiths with fireworks, the youngest sibling Yu cooks rice, the shy merchant sibling Zhi quietly lends a hand, and the healer sibling Fang talks the rampaging Beast down and sends it home.
In the river, the eldest sister Ling recites poetry while the second sibling Jun plays music, weaving together a dream that lulls Sui into a long sleep.

blocks.cap: Poetry and music intertwine, locking the raging Beast back into its dream.

blocks.x: Ch'en points the Scribe's Blade straight at the sky, reviving the memory of the Great Hunt in the approaching fortress of Yumen. "The sword of Cheonwi — when it's time to shine a light, shine it."

blocks.cap: As golden sandstorm settles like snowflakes, Yumen Fortress slows to a stop, like an old man lost in memory.

blocks.x: 7. Wang kills Sui, and becomes Sui
Meanwhile, in the deepest part of Sui's dream, Wang plays his final match. First, he takes on that old bane, 'the One Who Took White.'
Wang splits himself into 181 pieces, scattering his puppet in every direction — proving that the more Sui, badly wounded, pours its mind into endless nightmares, the weaker it makes itself.

Sui is a god that sees through all time from creation to the end, yet has never once had a single kindred spirit to speak with. Wang digs into exactly that, with the full weight of his own desolation.
Do you really think a man who's already lost everything he can lose has anything left to fear?

blocks.cap: The eldest sibling Chongyue, using a thousand years of martial skill, tears through the boundary between dream and reality to show Wang Jie's last breath.

blocks.x: Chongyue tells him, "Come back alive," and Wang answers, "I won't promise anything."
And Wang scrubs clean Sui's empty consciousness, and takes over its body in its place. Sui dies, and a new 'Sui' is born.
Whatever fills you now will be far more terrifying than you ever were.
8. The final shape of vengeance
The problem now is another Beast, **Ya** — a being awakened solely by a thousand years of obsession with taking revenge on Sui.
But by the time it arrives, Sui is already dead. The target of its revenge has vanished entirely.

blocks.cap: Ya, having lost the object of its revenge, suddenly bursts into laughter.

blocks.x: So Ya leaves behind the ultimate revenge instead. It cuts out the Originium Yan used to keep Sui suppressed, and drives it deep into Wang's own body. "Pay for Sui's sins yourself, across an eternity of time," it tells him.

blocks.cap: A colossal shadow appears before the True Dragon, about to end his own life in a sealed chamber — "Sui is dead."

blocks.x: Wang avoids both taking the True Dragon's life and his own annihilation — and in the end, calculates out the single move that has him carry an eternity of suffering all on his own.
Go master, you've won this match.
The True Dragon Yan Li, about to take his own life, is rescued from the collapsing chamber by his brother Wei Yanwu's own hands.
Epilogue — A Record Without Gaps
Once the great upheaval ends, the twelve siblings gather at Yu's Kitchenette for a meal. But at that table, Wang and Jie's seats are still empty.
The note the sleepyhead Dusk left behind reads: "Don't wake me until brother Wang comes back."
Twelve siblings who've lived a thousand years, and yet never once, in all that time, sat down together to share a meal. A quiet grief, one that lingers a long while.

blocks.cap: A reconciliation between brothers across a curtain — the True Dragon Yan Li, and his older brother, the deposed crown prince Yan Wu (Wei Yanwu).

blocks.x: You're the only one with nothing to be ashamed of before Yan. ...Now, you can finally set it down.

Ch'en decides, in the end, never to use the Scribe's Blade she inherited to "correct" history. Together with her relative, historian Jin Xiaotian, she chooses instead to leave an unsolved case exactly as it is — to break free from a cradle that always hands you the right answer.
And at Tianjing Pavilion, Ch'en meets a young girl, the next True Dragon. She tells the child not to learn only "responsibility" — that she has the right to love everyone.

blocks.cap: "Loading so much power onto just one person is unfair to that person too."

blocks.x: In truth, the True Dragon Yan Li's real dream was to complete Yan's gazetteer at Tianjing Pavilion — the one earlier scholars never finished — and spend his whole life among forty million volumes of books. A thousand years of power loaded onto a single person had stolen away even that modest dream.
And now, over the matter of Originium, an envoy from Rhodes Island comes knocking at Yan's gate. Just as a thousand years of grudges finally end, another story is about to begin.
The last words Ch'en offers in parting are the very same ones Tallu once said to her, a thousand years ago.
Once you grow up, you might be able to change something. So... hurry and grow up.
The ancient Beasts Jishou and Housou carry Ya's remaining breath out of Yan — quietly gathering up a thousand years of grudge and taking it from this land at last.

Chongyue still wanders the whole land searching for Wang, believing, "If Wang left any trace outside the board, it must be somewhere in this world." "The next time Wang needs help," he says, "I won't ever leave him alone again."
In some rural village, a boy losing a game of Go for stakes hears a voice from nowhere, out of no one.
4-7, shoulder hit.
With that single move, the boy turns the whole game around. At his collar, a black jade talisman glows quietly.

A Go master who's lived a thousand years is still out there somewhere, playing his moves, chasing the one thread of hope that might bring his dead sister back. Just as she once told him to hurry and grow up — hoping that someday, he really will be able to change everything.

act48side.tagline: A seasick Sargon ambassador mediates an international dispute with arm-wrestling, and ends up becoming an 'honorary Minosian' in the very nation her country stole from. And one lyre breaks a hundred years of silence.

terms.name: the Lyre
terms.desc: The instrument of founding hero Kereseira. "It rings only for a hero's tale." Passed to Sargon during the war a hundred years ago.

terms.name: Minos
terms.desc: A land of coexisting city-states. Terra's Greece. Occupied by Sargon for over a century, before a ceasefire five years ago.

terms.name: Athenus
terms.desc: Minos's first city-state, 'a garden ringed by mountains.' Converted into a nomadic city during the occupation.

terms.name: Sargon
terms.desc: The neighboring great power that occupied Minos. Its ruler bears the title Padishah. This time, it has sent a 'cultural exchange' delegation.

terms.name: Kereseira
terms.desc: Minos's founding hero and the epic's own bard. Owner of the First Temple and the Lyre. Her statue stands in the plaza.

terms.name: the First Temple
terms.desc: The first temple of Minos, built by Kereseira and refugees. Destroyed in the war and now being rebuilt by volunteering citizens.

terms.name: the Oracle
terms.desc: A hero's revelation delivered through dreams and visions. Interpreted by the high priestess of the Endymion temple.

terms.name: Itakos
terms.desc: Xanthos's old friend. Received an oracle of 'a river run red' nine years ago and went off to war.

terms.name: Potsherd Ballot
terms.desc: A pottery shard inscribed with a yes or no and dropped into a jar — exactly like ancient Greek voting.

terms.name: Burdenbeast
terms.desc: A large draft animal of Minos. A hooded, docile bovine... though it isn't very docile in this arc.

chars.name: Titi
chars.desc: Sargon delegation ambassador · museum director · seasick

chars.name: Mio
chars.desc: Titi's companion · tsundere beastkin noble · comfort duty

chars.name: Xanthos
chars.desc: Boatman of the Delphi Canal · former guerrilla · Operator Varkáris

chars.name: Pallas
chars.desc: Proposer of the temple reconstruction · fond of drink

chars.name: Cassandra
chars.desc: Dream-reading high priestess of the Endymion temple

chars.name: Lydia
chars.desc: Athenus city supervisor · shrewd operator

chars.name: Rena
chars.desc: Rhodes Island doctor · Lydia's older sister · Operator Perfumer the Distilled

chars.name: Hekademos
chars.desc: City supervisor's clerk · embodiment of clocking out on time

chars.name: Lykeion
chars.desc: Xanthos's disciple · can't sail a boat to save his life

chars.name: Venui
chars.desc: Deputy ambassador of the delegation · quote machine

chars.name: Periandros
chars.desc: Merchant high priest of Corinia

chars.name: Siniska
chars.desc: High priestess of Lacedaemon · fingers like weapons

blocks.x: The stage is Minos — Arknights's answer to Greece. City-states, temples, theater, heroic epics... and disputes settled by arm-wrestling. Literally.

But this romantic nation has a history of being occupied by its neighbor Sargon for over a hundred years. And barely five years after the ceasefire, that same Sargon comes calling for a 'cultural exchange.'
The repatriated artifact the delegation brings is the founding hero Kereseira's lyre. **"My lyre rings only for a hero's tale!"** — that legendary instrument, exactly as the epic tells it.
1. Madam Ambassador, you're late

blocks.cap: A garden ringed by mountains, the nomadic city Athenus. The moment Titi and Mio both went 'wow...' at once.

blocks.x: Our lead, **Titi** (whose real name, Mesetiketi Nasutu Masayer, is why everyone just calls her Titi), is the Sargon delegation's ambassador. A fallen noble, a museum director, and a Minos-history nerd down to her bones.

True to her nerd nature, she wanders off from the main party crying, "I want to walk the old road Kereseira once walked!" and gets lost while reciting from a tour guidebook — leaving the 50-kilogram crate of cultural artifacts to be hauled by her sole companion, the beastkin noble **Mio**. Naturally, they arrive late.
I picked you thinking I'd find some rich enough kid to coast through the rest of my life with — never imagined you'd turn out to be this restless a headache...

blocks.cap: Crossing the Delphi Canal. One foot on the boat, and — "Blegh."

blocks.x: The boatman **Xanthos**, who helps them across, is a kind old man handing an apple to a half-dead-with-seasickness Titi.

At the same time, he's also a former guerrilla who lost his most precious friend, Itakos, to the war and hates Sargon to his bones. He doesn't yet know that this Columbia-dressed young woman is the Sargon ambassador.
2. The First Annual First Temple Arm-Wrestling Tournament
Athenus is currently rebuilding the founding hero's First Temple. The slogan: 'pure spontaneity.' Sounds nice, doesn't it? It means no plan and no one in charge.

Naturally, progress is a mess, and orchard farmers stuck waiting on quarrying, and the temple craftsmen, are on the verge of throwing punches. This is where Titi's first act of diplomacy comes in. **"Only the winner of an arm-wrestling match gets to speak"** — and an impromptu tournament is born.

blocks.cap: A Firyline girl darts out from under the stone table and forces the two feuding men to clasp hands.

blocks.x: After a few exhausting rounds, both sides finally listen to each other's grievances, and Titi ends the dispute with a bold compromise: "The mountain blocking the sun? Just cut it down. There's marble underneath anyway, so it doubles as a quarry, right?"

blocks.cap: Mr. Pallas, proposer of the temple reconstruction and reported missing person, gets shipped out of an apple crate.

blocks.x: Meanwhile, the reconstruction proposer **Pallas**, said to have 'gone missing,' is found sound asleep inside an apple crate. Even his sleep-talk manages to worry about both the construction and his apple wine — the man loves his drink.

Sharing a cup with Titi, he abruptly declares them friends... except this man was once known as the 'Goddess of Victory' at the Minos border, a legendary former high priest. Nowadays he's recuperating at Rhodes Island, running little more than a drinking club.
3. The ostracon that gets thrown

blocks.cap: A mysterious statue

blocks.x: At the suggestion of the shrewd city supervisor **Lydia** (read: political calculation), whether Titi should become the one overseeing the temple reconstruction's final stretch gets put to Athenus's traditional potsherd ballot.
The craftsmen who worked alongside her happily inscribe their yes votes. But then—

blocks.cap: "Is this year really 1100? Feels like we've gone back a hundred years."

blocks.x: Megas, a former guerrilla living with war trauma, cuts across the plaza. And instead of dropping his ostracon into the jar, he flings it to the ground — meaning it isn't even worth the effort of casting a vote against her.

Titi feels as if she's been slapped. To the craftsmen she'd grown close to, she's 'Titi' — but to a stranger from Minos, she's just a Sargon national from the invader's nation. That day, for the first time, she truly feels the weight of it.

blocks.cap: That night. On comfort duty: the tsundere beastkin.

blocks.x: I'll make a special exception. You can pet my tail, mess up my back like you're working off stress, or wipe your tears on my fur. ...Actually, scratch the tears part. I hate getting damp.
4. Operation Lyre Heist (culprit: the kind boatman)
Xanthos, having caught on to both Titi's true identity and the artifact's, makes his move. Here's the plan.

His disciple **Lykeion** rams the hotel kitchen head-on with a supply boat, sparking a fire scare — and in that chaos, Xanthos steals the lyre from a fourth-floor room and leaps out the window. For the record, Lykeion cannot pilot a boat. He failed every single chariot-racing course back at priest school. So that collision wasn't acting — it was raw ability.
A chase erupts along the docks, and the craftsmen, oblivious to what's actually happening, cheer them on: "Is it a footrace? You've got this, miss!" How peaceful...

blocks.cap: After a tug-of-war on the little boat, a thousand-year-old artifact traces an arc through the air. Both their faces go white in the same instant.

blocks.x: Luckily, the lyre survives unscathed. Thanks to the excessive padding Titi had wrapped around it before departure — "Even if the building collapses, this lyre stays safe!" — only the stand takes a crack.

Xanthos and Lykeion are arrested. Titi tries to smooth things over — "It's my personal property, a small misunderstanding" — but deputy ambassador **Venui** politically blackmails Lydia, and the two of them get thrown into death-row cells on charges of treason.
5. But that lyre is a fake

blocks.cap: A mysterious statue

blocks.x: While repairing the crack, Titi notices something. A telltale Sargon drying-prevention treatment left on the cross-section. And Sargon resin from a hundred years ago.

**The artifact Sargon brought to return was a forgery.** A fake so precise even the Padishah might not have known. When Titi insists the exchange ceremony must be stopped, Venui drops a line for the ages.
What matters at a cultural artifact exchange ceremony isn't the 'artifact' — it's the 'ceremony.' So what if the artifact's fake? It still makes a sound, doesn't it?

Titi's conclusion: exposing this as ambassador would spark a diplomatic incident. So, instead, **as a private individual, on the day of the ceremony, she'll steal the fake lyre and become a fugitive for the rest of her life.** That would also let Xanthos and Lykeion's crimes quietly fade away.
Yes — she has a real gift for cooking up plans where only she loses. As Mio puts it: "That's not the best option, that's just a very 'Mesetiketi' way of solving things!"
6. A silent night

blocks.cap: A disappointed statue

blocks.x: That's when **Cassandra**, high priestess of the Endymion temple, appears before Titi and yanks a fence post out of the burdenbeast pasture. What had been disguised as a fence post was — **the real Lyre of Kereseira.**

An instrument that hasn't rung with any string strung on it since the day old Athenus fell — a lyre plunged into silence exactly as the legend says. Cassandra tells her: if she can make it ring, she may take it and complete the ceremony.

blocks.cap: Titi falls asleep after trying everything all night. Her father appears in her dream.

blocks.x: A quick word on her father, from the dream. A fallen house, a feeble man who never accomplished a single thing. Someone the nobles openly mocked, even at his own funeral.

And yet, for some reason, that same father's voice always grew vigorous whenever he told stories of heroes. Sioppi, the hero of silence who kept a secret for thirty years. Kereseira, who refused to play for a stranger. And — "We are all given the chance to become a hero, Titi."

The lyre never rings, in the end. Instead, beside the sleeping Titi, Mio mutters, "Did someone say you need a 'miracle'? That's easy enough!" ...and by morning, dozens of burdenbeasts from the pasture have all escaped. She's the one who caused it.
7. The past of the man with the oar
The scene shifts to death row. It turns out the homeless old man in the next cell is **Itakos's father**. And here, Xanthos's past finally unfolds.

The friend who'd promised to come home once the war ended never returned, so Xanthos left the village to search for him. For a year, drifting through every guerrilla unit, he tracked down his dead friend's armor, boots, shield, and Arts unit, one by one. And every time he recovered a keepsake, he'd hand it to a fellow soldier — "You need this, take it" — and leave them with the same line.
Countryman — just point me in the right direction.

Then the old man drops the real bombshell. His grandfather had been a Minosian engineer **conscripted for the construction of the nomadic city**, and the engineer's notes back then recorded the names and origins of every laborer conscripted by force — a notebook that Sargon confiscated in full.

Which means the 'technical documents' Sargon is trying to reclaim at the ceremony are actually — **the original evidence of its own war crimes.** The final piece needed to complete the official history that says Padishah Mala never forced Minosians into labor.
8. On the day of the ceremony, the whole city goes wild

blocks.cap: A burdenbeast stampede tears through the streets. On the horns of the very last one hangs the real lyre — Mio-style delivery.

blocks.x: On the day, everything happens at once. The clerk **Hekademos**, embodiment of clocking out on time, Rhodes Island doctor **Rena**, and Cassandra conspire to pull off a jailbreak.

But inside the prison, Itakos's father has already provided full service — from the guard shift schedule to unshackling the prisoners — so Hekademos, sneaking in to save them, ends up mistaken for an intruder by Xanthos and gets gagged first. The stampeding burdenbeasts get subdued with a single finger by the taciturn Lacedaemon priestess **Siniska** (the floor tiles crack beneath her), and the lyre gets safely 'delivered' to the First Temple.

blocks.cap: In the middle of that chaos, a public garden. Two sisters share one last conversation.

blocks.x: Amid all that commotion, one quiet story shines through. Rena and Lydia, the sisters.

Rena, the older sister who taught the adopted Lydia about gardens. Lydia, who became heir in her sister's place after Rena contracted Oripathy, then gave up both family and inheritance to become city supervisor. "Why do you always have to be so 'great,' sister?" is a complaint, but also decades' worth of longing.
Lydia could have held her sister back, but chose not to. "These ten-odd minutes are the last gift I'll give you. I don't owe you anything anymore."

And later, Rena finds it in the public garden the burdenbeasts trampled through: the pale blue boronia her sister had promised her as a child — "If you ever get lost, come find this flower" — planted throughout the whole garden. — "Thank you, Lydia. I'm home."

The jailbreak crew has one more unforgettable scene. Hekademos and Lykeion. Alongside the truth of the day Lykeion got infected (the culprit who drew the bowstring too hard was Hekademos), their tied three-to-three debate record finally gets settled. The topic of the final debate: "Can the Infected become heroes?"
I'll make you into a hero everyone recognizes as real. And the only 'profit' I want in return is... to beat you in our final debate.
Lykeion's answer: "...You lunatic." Hekademos's answer: "Thanks for the compliment. Guess that means you agree."
9. A play, a real dagger, and the lyre rings

By Titi's own choice, the opening play of the exchange ceremony is the forbidden classical tragedy no one has staged in a hundred years, **Kereseira and the Stranger**. Titi, a Sargon national herself, takes on the role of the Stranger. "This is a responsibility I ought to bear as a Sargon national," she says.

But on stage, Titi notices something. The rubber prop dagger meant for the execution scene **has been swapped for a real one.** The actor playing the soldier is also an unfamiliar face. A murder disguised as an accident.
Titi still... closes her eyes. She chooses to trust her friend.

blocks.cap: Pallas, playing Kereseira, raises the executioner's dagger. Its edge glints with a metallic sheen.

blocks.x: It's the fugitive Xanthos who bursts in at that exact moment. He kicks away the fake soldier to stop the assassination, and roars out to the entire city from the stage: "What they're trying to take is the record of the blood and sweat we shed building this nomadic city! And all of you who encouraged them are accomplices too!"

Titi's answer to that fury is neither an apology nor an excuse. — "This is a battle I owe you, as a Sargon national." In the middle of the exchange ceremony, a formal duel breaks out between ambassador and boatman.

blocks.cap: An oar that had forgotten the river for a year, heard again.

blocks.x: At the end of the duel, Xanthos's blood splashes onto the strings of the real lyre beside the statue. And in that instant — **the lyre, silent for a hundred years, rings.**
The three of them on stage share a vision of the oracle. What Titi sees is her father's past.

A frail Sargon guard, conscripted to Minos. A man who begged a Minosian prisoner for hero stories every day and copied down over a hundred of them (some of which the prisoner had simply made up on the spot — Sioppi's story was one of those). A man who 'forgot' to lock the cell door, and who 'couldn't help' failing a chase because his armor was too heavy.

An incompetent man repatriated by military tribunal blunder. He was the one who planted the seed of a hero in his daughter's heart. Xanthos tells her, right beside her: "He planted the seed of a hero in your heart."

blocks.cap: "What in the world is an oracle? How can it drive a person to their death?" — Xanthos and Cassandra.

blocks.x: Xanthos, too, finds his answer within the vision — the reply he could never quite recall, the one he'd given the day he saw Itakos off.
Yeah, but it's fine. Spring will come, and it'll be warmer than before.
Epilogue: The one who will become Padishah

blocks.cap: A disappointed statue

blocks.x: Titi takes the engineer's notebook back to Sargon in its entirety. Instead of burying it as the Padishah wished, she — **puts it on public display as a special museum exhibit, for the entire world to see.** She lets the people of Sargon see with their own eyes that their history book is a lie.

Lydia has already prepared full copies of every record, along with papers and even a special issue of a Columbian academic journal. For the record, this city supervisor — despite knowing all about the jailbreak plan — was the type to 'accidentally' leave her keys sitting right on her desk.

Xanthos chooses to submit himself to a citizens' vote rather than face detention. "Whatever the reason, my actions deserve judgment," he says. Titi receives an 'honorary Minosian' medal, along with one suspicious oracle reading from Cassandra: "I believe it was a sign that you are destined to become Padishah."

Three months later, an envoy from the King of Kings arrives at the museum. He wraps up Venui's frantic self-promotion in a single line ("You there, noisy sir, I didn't come here for you") and delivers an invitation to the Golden Citadel — to Titi.

Meanwhile in Minos, the merchant high priest Periandros of Corinia proposes reviving the city-state alliance and a traditional games festival... while Cassandra remains worried about the 'unsettling oracle' Pallas received. The seeds for the next Minos story have already been planted.

To sum it up: the seasick, tardy ambassador became a hero of the very nation her people stole from; the thief who stole the lyre became the one who made it sing; and only the side attempting the real theft — laundering history — walked away empty-handed. Exactly as the epic of Kereseira says. The lyre rings only for a hero's tale.

act47side.tagline: A tour guide who only takes on the 'impossible' tries to grant a dying banshee client's last wish, and ends up shouldering the conspiracy of a whole nation gone sky-mad. And in the end, one mother tree stays behind — not in the canyon, but in the sky, not as grief, but as laughter.

chars.name: Nasti
chars.desc: Section chief of Rhine Lab's engineering department. A banshee from Kazdel — few words, quick hands.

chars.name: Fen
chars.desc: A one-woman tour guide who only takes on the 'impossible.' Has crossed the Wasteland of Death three times · Operator Cairn

chars.name: Vayla
chars.desc: An old banshee client, losing her memory, whom Fen met in the Wasteland of Death.

chars.name: Gordon
chars.desc: Director of 'Propeller Paradise.' Despises rules and smuggles contraband · Operator Skybox

chars.name: Mercia
chars.desc: Gordon's secretary. Recites 'secretarial protocol' no matter what crisis hits.

chars.name: Sky
chars.desc: A junior researcher at Rhine Lab who rose up from the slums. Idolizes Christine.

chars.name: Tin Man
chars.desc: A tin-armored survivor of the Kazdel furnaces. A veteran of Maylander · Operator Tin Man

chars.name: Christine
chars.desc: The Rhine Lab director who split the sky over Trimounts two years ago. Now deceased.

chars.name: Aspen
chars.desc: Owner of 'Lucky Kismet,' who's staked everything he owns on the sky.

terms.name: Propeller Paradise
terms.desc: A floating science park suspended 600 meters over Columbia. The crystallization of the 'sky craze.'

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: The foremost science corporation in Columbia, once led by Christine. The park's original proposer.

terms.name: the Canyon
terms.desc: A refuge built by banshees beyond the Fog Lake. A place with no war, no death — only songs and desserts.

terms.name: the Roost Tree
terms.desc: The great tree believed to be a banshee's final resting place. 'Bones to bones, souls to souls.'

terms.name: Banshee
terms.desc: The Sarkaz subgroup entrusted with funerals and guidance of the dead. For them, 'forgetting' is itself the sign of death.

terms.name: Bonequill
terms.desc: The tool a banshee inscribes spells with. For Nasti, it also happens to be the key that runs the whole ecodome.

terms.name: Maylander
terms.desc: The foundation that manages — and manufactures — Columbia's 'accidents.' Oversees the Emergency Management Board.

blocks.x: Two years ago, Rhine Lab's Christine Wright split the sky over Trimounts. From that night on, all of Columbia went utterly sky-mad.

From baby strollers to nomadic cities, everything got a propeller bolted on, and any stock or liquor with 'sky' in the name shot up in price. The crystallization of that craze is a science park floating 600 meters up: 'Propeller Paradise.'

And into that dazzling paradise step two people from opposite ends of the world. One is **Fen**, a tour guide who only takes on the 'impossible.' The other is an elderly banshee who's crossed the Wasteland of Death.

blocks.cap: A nation that calls the sky its next frontier. Floating at its peak: 'Propeller Paradise.'

blocks.x: 1. The company that opened the sky is, of all things, stuck underground
The park's original proposer is Rhine Lab. And yet Rhine Lab's own ecodome has been pushed to the ground level — right next to an unremarkable dirt pit, no less.
The one keeping that ecodome running is **Nasti** Runoray. A banshee from Kazdel and the section chief of engineering — few words, quick hands, and someone who eats brick-hard hardtack for lunch.
Her assistant **Sky** is the exact opposite. A young man who clawed his way up from the slums and idolizes Christine, he grumbles daily about why nobody remembers her.
Nasti answers flatly: recording and looking back is a historian's job. We just have to keep moving.

blocks.cap: Amid a sky crowded with other people's inventions, only Rhine Lab's ecodome sits quietly on the ground.

blocks.x: 2. Mr. Smuggler-Director, and a fake docent (the real one's in prison)

The park's director, **Gordon** Tannen, is a very special businessman. Mocking the rule that only Columbian-made parts may be used, he smuggles in banned Originium devices from other nations, mounts them as 'hearts' on corporate exhibits, and pockets the profit.

Into that park sneaks the tour guide **Fen**. She got hold of the résumé of Oakley, the real docent who'd just gone through a breakup, and infiltrated under that identity. Her motive is pure — she's searching for a way to restore her client's, an old banshee's, fading memory.
But of all times, that very banshee client vanishes from her room without a trace.

To make matters worse, the real Oakley shows up at the director's office shouting, "I'm Oakley," and Gordon spits out the champagne he was drinking. The fake docent had already made contact with half the park by then.

blocks.cap: "Was that... that brick... supposed to be lunch?" "It's hardtack." Secretary Mercia gets her first taste of Nasti-style hospitality.

blocks.x: 3. A tree that must die to live (the ecodome was built to die from the start)
Soon, disaster strikes the ecodome. Seventy-two mature trees and three hundred fifty saplings wither and die in five minutes flat.
On the surface, it looks like Fen's fault for pulling out a 'twig' stuck in one of the trees. But that twig was actually Nasti's **Bonequill** — the key that runs the entire ecodome.
What's more, Gordon had guards posted at the door even before the disaster happened. This catastrophe wasn't an accident — it was a script written from the very start.

And quietly, one truth surfaces here. What Rhine Lab actually wanted wasn't for the plants to 'live' — it was for them to 'die,' the way a banshee understands dying. All so that one very special tree could live.
4. The secretary's true identity, and a tree that rises into the night sky

Gordon's secretary **Mercia** is someone who recites 'secretarial protocol' no matter what happens. But there's a different reason behind her composure — she's a special agent of **Maylander**, the foundation that 'manages' Columbia's accidents.
Meanwhile, as Muelsyse uses a water clone to scare off intruders by impersonating 'Nasti' — a saluting, cheerful Nasti even makes Gordon flinch — the real incident breaks out.

blocks.cap: A fake 'Nasti' shaped from water. She scares off the intruders with an expression that doesn't suit the name at all.

blocks.x: The sealed basement collapses, and a colossal 'tree,' woven from rotted wood and metal, rises silently into the night sky wrapped in a faint glow. No propellers, no engine sound.
The secret Rhine Lab has staked its life on hiding is revealed to all of Columbia, at the absolute worst possible moment.
5. A lie two hundred years old (why the Canyon has been quietly rotting)
The old banshee's name is **Vayla**. And Fen's client, it turns out, had actually come all this way looking for Nasti.
Two hundred years ago, Kazdel was swept up in the Allied Forces' steel and gunfire. Fewer than a thousand banshees survived, and half of those lost their voices.
A war in which the very people who conduct funerals couldn't even finish singing an elegy for their own dead.

blocks.cap: Green Firyline and endless steel. On the day the banshees burned and fell, the battlefield was filled with nothing but wailing.

blocks.x: The survivors, Vayla and Lakeramalin, built **the Canyon** beyond the Fog Lake. A life with no war, no death — only songs and desserts. Proof that banshees could set aside their nature and live differently.
In exchange, Vayla secretly intercepted the 'tokens' of death arriving from outside the Canyon for two hundred years — at the cost of leaving the ungiuded souls with nowhere to return.
And the banshees who defied their nature slowly turned into hollow shells during a long, agonizing bout of forgetting, right before they died.
6. The day Nasti didn't look back
The child Vayla brought to the Canyon was young Nasti herself — a girl raised in Kazdel, and so the very first one who would see through the lie.

blocks.cap: No matter how hard you blew it, the bone flute made no sound. Vayla played a mournful melody with a single leaf instead.

blocks.x: If it's just an accompaniment for a smile, you don't need a bone flute.
An accompaniment for laughter needs no grand instrument — that's what she meant. A single quiet phrase that lingers a long while.
But Nasti already knew that the Canyon stood on a fragile lie. So she decided to leave and find an answer — one that had to exist somewhere outside the Canyon, outside Kazdel.

blocks.cap: A windless Fog Lake. The little boat, stirred by her mother's spell, cut across the water like scissors through paper.

blocks.x: On the boat of farewell, all Vayla could manage was, "Eat well, my daughter."
Nasti never once looked back, taking in only her mother's face reflected on the water.

blocks.cap: She felt she couldn't speak calmly if she looked back. So she took in only the face reflected in the water.

blocks.x: And she said a small goodbye. "Farewell... Mother."
7. But this park was doomed to fall from the very start
Maylander's veteran agent **Tin Man** tells Mercia the truth. The hearings kept getting postponed, and it was no accident.

Columbia's sky craze is red-hot but completely useless. In an age where Kazimierz arms its knights with powered exoskeletons and Leithanien teaches children Arts warfare, Columbia has been left building nothing but floating carousels.

So Maylander's plan is this: bring down 'Propeller Paradise' disguised as 'a foreign spy's terrorist attack.' Then the passion for the sky will turn, for the first time in eighty years, back into rage and armament.
The contraband Gordon smuggled in was, in truth, foreign military components hand-picked by his mentor Gustave. Even Gordon had been someone else's chess piece.
But there was one crack in this perfect script. Mercia.

She grew up in the estate of Selene, the foundation that took in homeless orphans as 'children of Columbia.' That's why she could never simply accept that people with nowhere else to go should become sacrifices for someone else's future.

The job Maylander gave her was erasing the memories of agents whose missions were done. But this time, for the first time, she makes the opposite request — please, senior, whatever else you forget, don't forget how many lives and dreams lie behind that price.
8. Let's steal the whole park and run
Here, two rebellions erupt at once.

One is Gordon's. Instead of delivering his mentor's scripted 'apology speech,' he subdues Gustave with his modified robot Unit 054 and puts that face live on broadcast — kicking away the uncomfortable defendant's chair for good measure.

blocks.cap: "Ah, this is so much more comfortable." The director sprawled on a couch instead of apologizing, his mentor subdued on the floor.

blocks.x: The other is Fen's. She leads the park's test pilots and company representatives in a charge toward the blockade line — calling out the names of their dreams, one by one. Rice Bush, Owen, Aspen...

blocks.cap: "Run! Sprint! Fly!" Past everything that stands in our way without ever trying to understand us.

blocks.x: Fen has a reason for going this far. Her father, Martin Van, never accomplished a thing and spent his days spinning nonsense tales for the neighborhood kids. For a long time, Fen was ashamed of him.

Only after he died did she understand what he meant by "a story is always more fun than an explanation." Never slapping the label 'impossible' onto a dream that hasn't come true yet — that was the one thing he left her.
Aspen too speaks his own name into the megaphone: swindled, chased by debt, left by his wife, and now a shopkeeper who's staked everything he has left in the name 'Lucky Kismet.' That was enough.
The plan is simple and reckless: gather up smuggled parts to build new engines, and U-turn the entire park right in front of the special district, then run it across the border.
9. The mother who became a tree
Meanwhile, Nasti finds Vayla inside the tree's roots — pale roots standing in for her legs, bark covering her face.

Watching the path back to Nasti collapse, Vayla had fused herself with the tree to keep that path alive — the tree sustaining her life, her keeping the tree moving. Like a program limping along, each propped up by the other's flaw.

blocks.cap: Countless hands, pleading, writhing. A mother who has become one with the tree.

blocks.x: Two hundred years ago, she'd said her burning, falling kin looked like kites with their strings cut. Now she herself thrashes, caught in countless roots and branches — like a kite tied to too many strings, unable to ever break free.

In truth, the Canyon's Roost Tree was nothing special — just ordinary lumber. It only became sacred because Vayla mourned and made it a symbol. There's only one reason Nasti planted a tree just like it in the sky: she missed her mother.

Nasti erases all the temporary data she'd loaded into the tree beforehand — so that Vayla can finally face, head-on, that the roars and curses tormenting her were really her own lingering attachment and grief.

And then she asks: what do you actually want to leave behind? This tree could become a resting place for departed souls to return to. What gets left behind at the end — this time, let's decide that ourselves.

blocks.cap: If something other than a roar, a curse, a wail could be left behind. A Sarkaz resting place, moved into the sky.

blocks.x: Vayla agrees to become the tree's very first resident. "Just as I found you a home over twenty years ago, now you find one for me."
Her final memory, once every sorrow of the Canyon had faded away, was a single scene: a night, long ago, tucking in the child she'd just brought to the Canyon, telling her how adorable she was.

blocks.cap: The gentlest spell of her whole life, woven from a single leaf. "May you rest in peace."

blocks.x: Nasti catches the falling leaf in her palm and blows across it. Because if it's just an accompaniment for a smile, you don't need a bone flute.
One tree left in an empty sky

blocks.cap: Hundreds of floating capsules fell in flames, like kites with their strings cut.

blocks.x: In front of millions of citizens of the special district, the park falls in a blaze of glory — exactly as Maylander scripted, under the name of 'terrorism.'
But once the smoke clears from the empty sky, only one tree still floats above the clouds. Fowlbeasts are already preening their feathers on top of it.

blocks.cap: After everything else has fallen. Only the tree remains in Columbia's sky.

blocks.x: Vice President Jackson could have defined that tree either as 'a victim of terrorism' or as 'Columbia's new symbol.' But a single word from Yara changes his mind — a memory of the mobile mining platform he'd chased after, skipping class as a young man, forever pursuing the sunset.
If that sky up there is the soil that will nurture Columbia's new age... then there's no reason we can't make room for one tree.

And so Rhine Lab stands at the door of a new era, confirming that all the ghost stories Christine left behind — the 'Star Gate' at the Infinite Ice Field, the Kjerag records, the disappearance at Black Flow — actually lead to a single answer.

blocks.cap: The long-postponed year-end party. The scattered members of Rhine Lab gather together once more.

blocks.x: The sky was always empty. What always filled it was people — merchants ruined by the craze, a guide who only chased the 'impossible,' and—
One mother tree, who chose to stay behind not in the Canyon but in the sky, not as grief but as laughter.

act46side.tagline: While the Saintess climbs a blizzard-swept mountain in search of a god's trace, her brother turns back a Grand Duke's fleet with a single warship. And the god, it turns out, only came to say one last goodbye.

chars.name: Enya
chars.desc: The Saintess of Kjerag. After the Catastrophe, she questions the god's silence and sets out on a pilgrimage into the snow · Operator Pramanix

chars.name: Ensiodes
chars.desc: Head of the SilverAsh family, founder of Karlan Trade Co. An ambitious man who wants to remake Kjerag through trade and industry · Operator SilverAsh

chars.name: Joanna
chars.desc: A young hunter living alone on the snow mountain. A daughter of a branch family split off from the Paleosh line

chars.name: Elena
chars.desc: A young researcher with Rhine Lab, determined to explain the Catastrophe through science

chars.name: Aktoz
chars.desc: Head of the Paleosh family. A hot-blooded, blunt-spoken Ursus warrior

chars.name: Latatos
chars.desc: Head of the Browntail family. Known for a calm, calculating way of handling people

chars.name: Degenbrecher
chars.desc: The dark knight Ensiodes keeps at his side. A bodyguard famed for sheer skill

chars.name: Gnosis
chars.desc: An executive of Karlan Trade Co. and Ensiodes's longtime associate

chars.name: Caroline
chars.desc: A young researcher at the Rhine Lab observatory. A colleague who used to share night shifts and idle chatter

terms.name: Kjeragand
terms.desc: The god the people of Kjerag have served for a thousand years. Said to rule the blizzards and the mountains, and to have kept this nation safe from Catastrophes

terms.name: Manjuwon
terms.desc: The religious institution devoted to Kjeragand, and the spiritual center of Kjerag. Its Grand Elder and monks oversee matters of faith

terms.name: Karlan Trade Co.
terms.desc: The trading company Ensiodes built. It holds a near-total monopoly on Kjerag's foreign trade, diplomacy, and even its military strength

terms.name: The Three Clans
terms.desc: The three families that make up Kjerag — SilverAsh, Paleosh, and Browntail. The political power they once shared has since split along industry, warfare, and diplomacy

terms.name: Heart of Karlan
terms.desc: The lake that never freezes, from Kjerag's founding myth. Said to be where Kjeragand first appeared

terms.name: Silver Bell
terms.desc: A bell only the Saintess carries. Said to ring with a sound that calls Kjeragand when shaken

blocks.x: Kjerag is a nation that hasn't seen a Catastrophe in a thousand years. Legend says the god Kjeragand left one last precept on the mountain peak: "If you keep this precept, no Catastrophe shall come to this land."

But one winter, the dying Grand Elder makes an unexpected confession to the Saintess **Enya**: "For a long time now, I haven't heard Kjeragand's voice." That very night, something falls from the sky, the mountain collapses, and the first Catastrophe in a thousand years begins.

The Saintess sets out on a pilgrimage to answer the god's silence, while her brother **Ensiodes**, president of the trading company, sees this chaos as the opportunity he's waited twenty years for. Two utterly different people begin walking toward the exact same question — what makes Kjerag, Kjerag?
1. Catastrophe strikes the snow mountain, of all possible times
The avalanche swallowed the train station in an instant. As the SilverAsh family rushed in to dig out survivors, the terrified villagers cried out as one: "Kjeragand, save us."

blocks.cap: The first Catastrophe to strike a nation that had gone a thousand years without one. The station lay buried whole under the snow.

blocks.x: **Aktoz**, head of the Paleosh family, personally leads his men in digging through the snow. But when Browntail's head, **Latatos**, is nowhere to be seen, whispers start behind her back.

At a mine near Mount Karlan, an unidentified black crystal is found — evidence that something fell from the sky the night before the avalanche. Neighboring nations, sensing the scent of Originium, begin to gather.
Ensiodes seals off the site and locks down information first. He knows full well that the moment the word "Catastrophe" leaks out, neither the Three Clans nor Manjuwon will be able to hold.
2. The friendly neighbors' oddly-timed offensive of kindness

As news of the Catastrophe spreads, Victoria is the first to send a convoy of trucks. The Victorian noble **Harold** arrives with a mountain of relief supplies and grins shamelessly. "We're a good deal warmer than those aggressive types, wouldn't you say?"

The Victorian journalist **Alberta** takes the chance to cover every corner of Kjerag. Meanwhile, a newlywed couple empties their entire storehouse for the disaster victims, saying, "Kjeragand is watching, so we ought to help each other now."

Columbia threatens to send troops under the pretext of protecting its observatory researchers, while the Victorian ambassador presents an aid package with 40% of the mining development rights tacked on. Behind them, Duke Caster secretly plants spies to dig up the secrets of Kjerag's mines. Everyone knew all too well that a Catastrophe is also an "opportunity."

Meanwhile, faith begins to waver inside Manjuwon. A young monk cries out, "Kjeragand grew angry and sent the Catastrophe upon us," and Enya calmly counters, "Surely you're not claiming to make promises to the people in Kjeragand's stead?"

blocks.cap: Before a blazing hearth, the Saintess and Grand Elder **Adso** sit facing each other for a long while. Every time, their questions miss each other.

blocks.x: 3. The Saintess sets off on pilgrimage, her brother starts scheming

Enya makes up her mind. The "pilgrimage" — normally held only in spring and autumn, in which the Saintess walks the whole of Kjerag on foot in atonement — she'll undertake it in the dead of winter. Her brother's objections mean nothing.
The night before she departs, Enya's head handmaiden **Kiyar** vanishes without a trace. In the empty room lit only by the hearth's fire, Enya calls out the name, out of old habit.

blocks.cap: All that Kiyar left behind: a hand mirror, a comb, and one unfinished letter.

blocks.x: Her grace has always rested beneath a traveler's feet.

The one who saved Enya after an unknown attacker's arrow found her on the snow mountain was the young hunter **Joanna** — a girl who resented SilverAsh for the home she lost when the railway was built, but not someone who could stand by and watch an injured person suffer.

As it happens, Joanna's father was also at that train station when the Catastrophe struck — he'd gone down the mountain to find medicine for his daughter's illness and never came back. Not knowing this, Joanna's first thought had simply been to report the Saintess's party.
4. How a fake corpse catches a spy

The Saintess wasn't the only target. Aktoz and SilverAsh's youngest are attacked one after another, and a mysterious figure secretly reaches out to Latatos. The three families start suspecting each other.

The attackers wore Paleosh armor, but Joanna caught it at a glance from the arrow fletching. Paleosh combat arrows use three feathers; these all had two. In the end, it was the work of Victorian spies imitating Ursus's traditional craft.
Behind it all was **Caroline**, a Victorian spy posing as a Rhine Lab researcher. She was simply the colleague who used to pull all-nighters at the observatory — no one had ever suspected her.

Latatos goes so far as to fake her own death in an explosion — a plan to use her own death as bait to lure out the Victorian spies. It works, but she hides even this from her own sister, earning nothing but resentment.

At the sham funeral, her sister Siurus, long overshadowed by her, steps up as head of Browntail for the first time. "Only today have I come to understand all of my sister's anguish — and now there's no way left for me to tell her so." Ironically, it's this very funeral that finally prepares Siurus to stand on her own.

Meanwhile Ensiodes leads his troops to surround Manjuwon entirely, brandishing "evidence" that Manjuwon has colluded with Victorian spies. It turns out to be true — a few young monks really had been in contact with Victoria.
When attackers storm even the front courtyard of Manjuwon, Ensiodes's dark knight **Degenbrecher** stands alone against them.

blocks.cap: In Manjuwon's front courtyard, the dark knight Degenbrecher single-handedly faces seventeen attackers. Drenched in blood, he never once steps back.

blocks.x: 5. "Kjeragand does not exist" — the Grand Elder's confession

Rhine Lab researcher **Elena**, tracking the "celestial matter" that fell on Mount Karlan the night before the Catastrophe, runs into Enya and Joanna. The three end up an unlikely team — people chasing the same answer through science and faith, by wholly different paths.

Camping on the mountain trail, the two talk long into the night. A scientist who admits that a custom of divining by snowflakes is "a placebo that still works," and a Saintess who answers, "Even if fate exists, this snow still fell on my cloak all the same." Different words, saying the same thing.

A Victorian journalist comes to Manjuwon to interview Adso. When asked what use faith is if it can't even protect a miracle, Adso fires back: "Your camera always seems to frame only the chaotic and the pitiable — is that the truth, or a picture book?"

But when Enya meets Adso again on the road, he makes an unexpected confession of his own: "Kjeragand does not exist." In his youth, he'd seen the original scripture with his own eyes, and had learned it was nothing more than a patchwork of stories stitched together by countless people over a long span of time.
Worse, Adso reveals something even more horrifying to Ensiodes. The train that killed his parents twenty years ago, in what was called an "accident" — the man behind it was Adso himself.
The man who killed your parents stands before you.

Faith in Kjeragand, order within Manjuwon — all of it, Adso admits, was a lie he built up to preserve. He sets a condition for Enya: he'll show her, inside the cave, that there is no god — but in exchange, she must banish Ensiodes from Kjerag.
6. What the frozen Saintesses point toward

In a vision, Enya sees Kjerag's origin. Long ago, three tribes that drifted into these mountains fought over a lake called the "Heart of Karlan," until a nameless being appeared, stopped the conflict, and was given the name "Kjeragand."

And deep within the mountain, in a cave blown open by explosives, frozen girls stand in a row. All of them past Saintesses — women who died of hypothermia while seeking an answer from a god who never replied. Alongside them, a voice pleads: "Stop. That's enough now. Go any further and you'll die."

blocks.cap: At the cave's end, a colossal figure of ice appears — the very form of the god that people saw a thousand years ago.

blocks.x: The moment Enya touches the figure, a vision floods in. Inside it, she meets the missing handmaiden Kiyar — who quietly reveals that she herself is Kjeragand.
Kjeragand was never an all-knowing, all-powerful god. Just one of the nameless "life forms" that fell to this land — one who had never once handed down anything resembling a precept.
What your kind has always had is the strength to help one another. You call that virtue.
And then Kiyar says her goodbye. "My time is up."
Go, and clear away the last dark cloud, and open a new tomorrow.
7. One warship turns back a Grand Duke
The Catastrophe alarm sounds again. A 72% chance of a second Catastrophe within three hours. As Enya rushes down the mountain, Victoria's Duke Caster leads a fleet to personally surround Manjuwon.

Aktoz stands alone in the fleet's path. "If you mean to set foot on Kjerag soil, you'll have to walk over my corpse first!" Before Manjuwon, Alberta — until now cornered as a hostage — steps forward herself and exposes Victoria's false pretext.
Ensiodes takes out a single high-speed warship, alone. In truth, that ship carries a card he's kept hidden for twenty years — a massive cannon concealed within the mountain.

blocks.cap: On the deck of the *Gloriana*, one modest-looking warship comes to a halt before the great fleet.

blocks.x: At the negotiation, he fires a single warning shot at the Duke's very feet. At the controls is none other than the new head of Browntail, Siurus.
"To think Ensiodes had a toy this good hidden away — it's been a while since I got to have real fun!" Siurus shouts, delighted.
I will make sure Kjerag gets to have its 'tomorrow.' Whether I live to see that tomorrow or not makes no difference to me.
At the same moment, atop the mountain, Enya offers one last prayer. Not a plea to a god — a farewell to the time they'd walked together.

blocks.cap: The Saintess of Kjerag stands alone atop the mountain, the silver bell in her hands.

blocks.x: The dark clouds part and light pours down. Duke Caster, his soldiers, even the hands gripping their weapons — all of them stop without meaning to. Before that light, no one could act recklessly.

blocks.cap: Light splitting the clouds. Whether it was wrath or blessing, even those who don't believe could tell.

blocks.x: The Duke finally orders a retreat, saying, "The scales of victory have tipped the other way." What became of the spy Caroline, and the evidence Alberta had gathered — all of it gets settled within that single day.
Kjerag, afterward

Enya punishes the young monks who took part in the betrayal not with excommunication but with a winter of farm labor, "because they were brave in the face of an invader." Adso, after his penance, tries to throw himself off a cliff — until a single word from a passing girl stops him.

blocks.cap: The Saintess's fingertips brush the old traitor's brow. "You never denied what he meant."

blocks.x: **Gnosis** becomes the new head of Karlan Trade Co., while Degenbrecher, to the very end, refuses to attend the promotion ceremony. Ensiodes steps down from his post as president — but as a meeting with Columbia's vice president makes clear, the wheel of Kjerag's fate still rests firmly in his hands.

blocks.cap: Before the hearth at Karlan Trade Co., Ensiodes welcomes a new guest. He stepped down from his seat as president — nothing else.

blocks.x: A young monk at Manjuwon named Matthew tentatively asks whether the light that drove off the Victorian fleet looked less like a miracle and more like a farewell. The youngest of five brothers, he'd been sent to the temple just to lighten the family's burden.

Instead of answering, Enya recites the old precept once more. But this time the final line is subtly changed. Not "No Catastrophe shall come to this land," but "No Catastrophe shall defeat you in this land."
The god may no longer hold Catastrophes at bay — but the people, she was saying, will not be beaten by them either.

Months later, Elena dons a new-model powered exosuit and reaches out to touch the object herself. Her superior Muelsyse cheerfully encourages her over the radio: "It's the most brilliant moment of everything you've ever studied — you should enjoy it."
No explosion, no flash of light — just a quiet contact, like a handshake.

Analysis shows 70% of the object is an unidentified substance; the remaining 30%, astonishingly, shares the same cellular structure as elves. The signal the object keeps repeating, Enya translates from the old Kjerag script. The translated sentence is a single line: "Ruin... has come."
The dying Grand Elder had once asked Enya whether she could still hear Kjeragand's voice.
Now Enya can answer. The voice is gone — but something else has taken its place.

act45side.tagline: A band leader working undercover as a rookie operator takes one wrong nap — and falls into a perfect dream where all her bandmates are trapped. The happier a dream looked, the more loneliness no one could fill was hiding underneath.

chars.name: Sakiko Togawa
chars.desc: A brand-new operator who just joined Rhodes Island. In reality, she's the leader and keyboardist of the five-member band Ave Mujica.

chars.name: Uika Misumi
chars.desc: Ave Mujica's vocalist and guitarist — currently one of the most popular idols on Terra.

chars.name: Mutsumi Wakaba
chars.desc: Ave Mujica's guitarist — a quiet girl of few words whose expression rarely changes.

chars.name: Umiri Yahata
chars.desc: Ave Mujica's bassist — calm and thoughtful, formerly a bodyguard who drifted between several mafia families.

chars.name: Nyamu Yūtenji
chars.desc: Ave Mujica's drummer, who's also a rising actress and influencer active in Lungmen.

chars.name: Vigna
chars.desc: A Vanguard operator at Rhodes Island — Sakiko's senior coworker and a caring nag.

chars.name: Iris
chars.desc: The mistress of the 'Dream Castle' — a fairy who gives children good dreams.

chars.name: Bena
chars.desc: Iris's partner — an all-around fairy who can fix and handle just about anything.

terms.name: Ave Mujica
terms.desc: The five-member band Sakiko formed. It's won popularity with a concept of masked 'dolls' putting on flawless stage performances.

terms.name: Masquerade
terms.desc: The name of Ave Mujica's live shows — performances designed to invite guests into a dreamlike world.

terms.name: Dream Castle
terms.desc: A fairy-tale place said to lie deep in the forests of Victoria, where fairies are said to show children beautiful dreams.

terms.name: Dream Weaver
terms.desc: A term for those among the Dream Castle's fairies who have the special power to create dreams themselves.

terms.name: Dreamless Border
terms.desc: A collapsed gap between dream and reality, where someone who can't even escape a dream ends up wandering forever.

terms.name: Siracusa
terms.desc: A city notorious as a crime-ridden district run by a tangle of mafia families and their own order — the place Umiri once drifted through as a bodyguard.

blocks.x: Ave Mujica is a five-member band led by **Sakiko Togawa**, Rhodes Island's newest keyboardist. On stage, they're flawless 'dolls' without a hair out of place — but off stage, they barely knew anything about each other.

Even on the way home after a Masquerade show, the gap between the five members remains. There's the drummer who wants to soak up every bit of fame, the vocalist absorbed only in polishing new lyrics, and amid it all, a member simply happy that they're together again.

One night after a live show, Sakiko has a strange dream in the middle of a crosswalk, flooded with unfamiliar voices. Dreams scatter the moment you wake, so Sakiko brushes it off without much thought.
But after that night, the place Sakiko actually wakes up isn't Rhodes Island. It's an unfamiliar castle beneath two moons — and on the castle's stage, her four bandmates lie asleep side by side.
1. The Rookie Employee Has a Premonition at the Crosswalk
On the way home from the live show, Nyamu brings up Sakiko out of nowhere, stirring up Uika and Mutsumi. Amid the laughter and chatter, nobody notices that Sakiko alone has gone quiet.

While waiting for the light to change, a strange afterimage flickers past — visible only to Sakiko. A voice pressing her hard, and pouring rain. When she comes to, she's lying in bed aboard the Rhodes Island ship.

blocks.cap: Just before the light changes, unfamiliar voices only Sakiko can hear.

blocks.x: Her coworker Vigna worries that Sakiko, still a rookie, is already taking on work across both the Medical and Engineering departments. Having lost her mother to Oripathy, Sakiko sees Rhodes Island as a debt she needs to repay, so she barely thinks about resting.
The supply truck carrying Sakiko out on a delivery run has two unexpected passengers — Iris and Bena, who introduce themselves as 'fairies,' along with an urgent story about needing to find someone.
2. 'This Is My Last Letter' — A Girl Named An

The two of them are searching for 'An,' an aspiring fairy-tale writer they've only known through letters. The line she left in her final letter — "this is my last letter" — felt ominous. Iris also mentions that tomorrow is the very birthday when An comes of age.

blocks.cap: A back alley washed by rain, as Sakiko sets out alone to find the girl.

blocks.x: An old flower-seller Sakiko meets on the night road, her eyes dim, still points out the way and tells her to be careful. Somehow, that brief kindness leaves Sakiko with an inexplicable sense of longing.

In a rundown apartment, Sakiko finds a drunken father sobbing and calling out his daughter's name — and beside him, An, holding her breath. The 'happy fairy tale' An had been writing turns out to be a lie, the exact opposite of her real life.
Watching the clock creep toward midnight, Sakiko tells An she'll stay with her for this last moment. Right before she comes of age, An falls into a peaceful sleep to the sound of Sakiko's piano.

Iris, arriving late, says she went into An's dream and called forth every character from the girl's own stories to hold a tea party for her. She explains that the Dream Castle's rule is that "entering a child's dream without their consent is an unforgivable act."
Iris gives Sakiko a necklace as a gift, said to ward off nightmares. That night, wearing the necklace, the place Sakiko opens her eyes to is... not Rhodes Island, but an unfamiliar castle.
3. Morpheus's Challenge

Morpheus, a girl who calls herself the castle's owner, is the one who trapped Sakiko here. Among the children she'd caught for amusement, Sakiko is the only one who ever managed to escape a dream on her own — and Morpheus finds that fascinating.

blocks.cap: On stage, her bandmates lie asleep like dolls put on display.

blocks.x: On stage, Nyamu, Umiri, Uika, and Mutsumi lie asleep with peaceful expressions. No matter how many times Sakiko calls their names, none of the four wakes up.

By Morpheus's side there's only 'Wolf,' a beast that can't even speak properly, and 'Carlton,' a pack that only makes crying noises — no one to actually talk to. Maybe that's why Morpheus readily admits she's secretly peeked into Sakiko's unforgettable memories and unfamiliar scenery over and over.
Morpheus proposes a wager: go into each bandmate's dream one by one and make them wake up on their own. If Sakiko fails, all four will remain in this dream forever.
Until every last member of Ave Mujica is together again, I'm not leaving this place.
4. Nyamu — Behind the Glittering Stage
In Nyamu's dream, Sakiko arrives in Lungmen, a city of skyscrapers piercing the sky. Rising actress Nyamu is buried under a leading film role, explosive popularity, and an endless schedule.

blocks.cap: "Even if the doll is sad, everyone smiles." On the interview stage, Nyamu wears a perfect smile.

blocks.x: On set, the director and screenwriter get into such a huge fight over the script that they both end up hospitalized. Thanks to the unexpected break this gives her, Nyamu calls her parents back home for the first time in a long while.

Her parents ask after her in a thick accent, and her little brother excitedly tells her he's started learning the drums. But the moment the call ends, Nyamu walks straight back under the dazzling lights, into her broadcast schedule with Sora.
Behind the glamorous surface, all that sits in the corner of Nyamu's room is a pair of dust-covered drumsticks. No one treats Nyamu as her real self anymore.
Sakiko asks Nyamu, smiling under the dazzling lights, whether the love she's won by losing her drums, her bandmates, and even herself is really the dream she wanted.
You've lost your drums, your bandmates, even yourself. No matter how many people love you now, can you still call this your dream?
5. Umiri — A Wolf That Belongs Nowhere
The stage in Umiri's dream is Siracusa, city of mafiosi. Umiri, who drifted between families on skill alone, keeps getting framed as a 'traitor' and driven out every time she tries to settle down.

blocks.cap: In a narrow alley, Sakiko and Umiri face off, just the two of them, for a duel.

blocks.x: In truth, Umiri once genuinely opened her heart to that family. She was invited to a party and even asked to play music together — but the very day after she decided to settle down, she was framed and thrown in prison for half a year.
Francis, her former comrade who now treats her like an enemy, and the new boss trying to claim her for himself — both only want to use Umiri. Neither of them sees the real Umiri.

Sakiko uncovers that all this conflict was a trap planned by someone else, and reaches out her hand to Umiri — asking whether a life of being abandoned and used over and over was really the 'trust' Umiri had wanted.
Ms. Yahata, you don't need to suffer in a place like this. Come back with me.
6. Uika's Island, and the Sinking Ship

The setting of Uika's dream is a small, secluded island. Uika lives there alongside Eyjafjalla, a volcano researcher slowly losing her hearing — while Uika herself can't even remember when 'that girl,' the one she loved most, left.

Their bond began the night Eyjafjalla got lost on the mountain and happened to meet Uika, who was gazing up at the night sky. Uika can't help but worry about her friend, who won't give up volcano research even as her hearing keeps getting worse.

blocks.cap: Sakiko rides an old bicycle toward a cottage at the foot of the mountain.

blocks.x: The cottage Sakiko reaches by old bicycle is a recreation of the very house where Uika played and lived with Sakiko as a child. All Uika ever wanted wasn't some grand dream — just one thing: being with you.

Thanks to Eyjafjalla's encouragement, Uika rushes out belatedly to find Sakiko, but the two of them miss each other at the harbor at the last moment. Left alone on the ship, Sakiko is hit by a massive wave this time.

blocks.cap: Underwater, breath nearly gone, a familiar voice calls out to Sakiko.

blocks.x: Just as she's about to suffocate, a completely different voice wakes Sakiko up. When she opens her eyes, she's not in the sea anymore — she's in the middle of a desert swept by sandy winds.
7. Mutsumi's Last Party

In Mutsumi's dream is the poor but warm family she'd always longed for — a mother who hugs her tight without caring that they live in a mud-walled hut, Mutsumi proudly showing off the money she made selling cucumber juice, and even a dinner spread laid out by neighbors.
A dinner made with care, a neighborhood party held for the first time in ages, and Mutsumi picking up her guitar once more.

blocks.cap: By candlelight, Mutsumi plays that old song once again.

blocks.x: I'll play it now... 'Spring Sunshine.'
Mutsumi actually knows all of this is a lie. Even so, she asks Sakiko to let her sing freely, just like anyone else, just this once.
Faced with Mutsumi's playing, which looks so happy, Sakiko can't bring herself to reach out — she turns her back on the stage and flees, believing that's the choice that's best for Mutsumi.
8. "You're Not Mutsumi"

What waits for Sakiko after she flees isn't Mutsumi or Morpheus, but an unfamiliar voice. Countless 'Mutsumis' who'd been hiding inside Mutsumi step forward in front of Sakiko, standing in for the real Mutsumi.
The 'Mutsumis' try to persuade Sakiko that if she stays by their side, they can keep this perfect dream forever. As Sakiko refuses, they slowly reach out toward the edge of the cliff, toward her.

blocks.cap: At the cliff's edge, the hands of the 'Mutsumis' push Sakiko off.

blocks.x: Mutsumi doesn't need Sakiko anymore. Goodbye, Sakiko.
9. The Mother Who Returned
This time, as she falls, Sakiko opens her eyes inside her own dream — her beloved hometown of Victoria. And inside that house, sitting at the piano... is the mother who had already passed away.

blocks.cap: A silhouette wrapped in light — 'Mother' sits before Sakiko.

blocks.x: 'Mother' and 'Father' are exactly the loving parents Sakiko always longed for. 'Father,' who never used to cook at all, proudly says he practiced over and over just to make his daughter's favorite dish, and Sakiko accepts that love so wholeheartedly she eats until her stomach hurts.
As Sakiko excitedly rambles on about her bandmates, she forgets everything for a moment and lets herself sink into that warmth.
But at the thought that she has to go find her bandmates, Sakiko suddenly feels afraid — because if she leaves to find everyone, she feels like she'll never be able to see this mother again.
10. Mother's Confession
