And the master of that house is none other than the family's current head, **Aktos**—the man who'd been drowning himself in drink since noon under the pretense of 'choosing wine for the unveiling ceremony.'

blocks.cap: 'You gave me honey, so I've got to bring something out too!' Aktos hauls out his entire wine cellar.

blocks.x: Getting tipsy on the honey drink Leto brought, Aktos hauls out his entire cellar as a thank-you gift. He doesn't hesitate to crack open a twenty-year-old wedding celebration wine, gone sour with age, right in front of his own daughter.
Drunk as he is, Aktos doesn't recognize Leto's face. But the old monk knows it at a glance—the face of Tatiana, the woman his son fell in love with on this mountaintop twenty years ago.
The next morning, still not quite sober, Aktos hears the truth from the old monk: 'Rosalind is your daughter.' And by then, his daughter had already climbed up the mountain.
4. The Secret of Her Birth, Heard at the Cliff's Edge

At the mountain summit, Leto tries and fails to hide her mother's keepsake in the crook of a tree. Just then, she rescues a 'John Smith' caught in a hunting trap—only to realize he's none other than Gray Hat.
Just as she's about to corner him, Aktos comes bursting in, and all three go tumbling into the snow. And there, Aktos finally confesses the truth to his daughter.
Twenty years ago, one winter, he met an Ursus woman named Tatiana on this very mountain, fell in love, and married her against everyone's objections.
Kjerag back then was a closed, exclusionary country. Marrying an outsider alone was considered enough to shame an entire family.

But after the reform-minded SilverAsh couple died in an 'accident,' hostility toward his foreign wife grew beyond anything he could manage. The Paillosh family, too, had been on the side opposing those reforms.
Overcome by fear, Aktos sent his wife and daughter away from Kjerag—terrified that staying by his side would mean they, too, might one day suffer an 'accident.'
'Could you call me father again, just once more?' Aktos asks, laying his axe down before his daughter.

blocks.cap: After a long, rambling apology, this was the answer he got.

blocks.x: As if! Dream on!

Leto points out that Tatiana is nowhere to be found in the excuses Aktos strings together. A passionate woman who loved unfamiliar places and delighted in meeting people—an excuse that captures not one bit of who she actually was.
And with that, she kicks him square in the back, sending him tumbling into the snow. A carefully calculated fall, of course, just short of actually hurting him.
5. The Child-Support Bill, and a Reconciliation That Isn't One
'Send over a decade's worth of back child support, straight to my account. Miss a payment and I'll add interest.' Leto heads down the mountain looking thoroughly satisfied.
But mid-call, Aktos mistakes her mother's story for a 'keepsake' and assumes she's passed away, tangling the situation up all over again.
Tatiana is alive. She simply contracted Oripathy a few years back in a Catastrophe zone accident, and she's still by Leto's side to this day.
And the finishing blow: Leto's mother, she says, has never once resented Kjerag, or Aktos. She even taught her daughter how to pray.
Leto doesn't call that 'forgiveness.' She just states, flatly, that holding a grudge simply wasn't worth the trouble.
6. Rematch on the Ice
Gray Hat flees down the mountain, paying extra to rent the fastest burden beast around and making a run for it. But Degenbrecher is right on his tail, mounted on another beast from the same ranch.

Having borrowed someone else's ice skates on the spur of the moment, Gray Hat wobbles along on skates for the very first time in his life. And yet, oddly enough, he picks up the knack almost immediately.
Their duel eventually carries them all the way up to the top of the statue. The Holy Maiden's head attendant watches, lost in her own thoughts: 'That sculpture really could use a slimmer jawline.'

blocks.cap: Gray Hat sent flying by a kick from above. Even amid all this, the head attendant is busy planning the statue's makeover.

blocks.x: Beaten black and blue and dunked in the lake, Gray Hat somehow survives. Fleeing into Silverheart Lake's back alleys, he stumbles onto a decisive piece of evidence.
What was supposedly a barrel of 'wine being carried' turned out to hold metal, not wine. So where exactly was the leftover material from the statue's construction actually being hauled off to?
7. The Karlan Trio's After-Party

Meanwhile, on the eve of the unveiling ceremony, another kind of family is gathered in the lobby of the emptied-out banquet hall—**Gnosis**, passed out drunk; Degenbrecher, kicking him; and Ensiodes, laughing beside them.

blocks.cap: A decade-long ritual of after-hours drinking. What Degenbrecher's handing out isn't a hangover cure—it's poison she made herself.

blocks.x: None of the three will ever actually say what they want to say to each other. And yet this lobby, heating shut off and all, seems to be the place where the three of them feel most at ease.

If the family Leto met at the cliff was bound by blood and resentment in equal measure, this one has held together for ten years without a drop of shared blood between them. Kjerag, it turns out, had another family just like that.
8. They Said They Came to Celebrate—So Why the Ballistae?
On the day of the unveiling ceremony, two thousand Victorian soldiers suddenly form ranks and march on Silverheart Lake, under the pretext that 'prohibited goods have been hidden beneath the statue.'
As it happens, it was true—Karlan Trade CO., LTD had used the statue's construction as cover to secretly ship something out. And Gray Hat had already gotten his hands on the proof.
Ensiodes's subordinates Weiss and Matterhorn had already been captured and bound the night before. There was no way left to get word out.
Rather than a head-on collision, Ensiodes decides to buy time. And without a moment's hesitation, Degenbrecher volunteers to be the one to buy it.

blocks.cap: 'No one passes beyond this point. Not a single one of you.' One against two thousand—the blockade begins.

blocks.x: When Harold asks if she's serious, Degenbrecher answers plainly. Living nameless in the back alleys of Leithanien, she'd made herself a name with nothing but a bladeless weapon.
I am Degenbrecher.
There's no conviction, no noble cause behind that name. Just violence—and, somehow or other, this cold country she's grown attached to over the past ten years.
Even so, Harold refuses to give up on negotiating. Right up to the end, he insists that even accepting just 30 percent of their demands would be enough to convince the Duke.
Degenbrecher cuts him off flatly. In this moment, she says, she is nothing more than 'an enemy of Victoria.'
9. The Real Trump Card Was Already on the Train
In the very last train car, Gray Hat prepares his decisive blow, convinced he's finally uncovered SilverAsh's true secret.

blocks.cap: Opening the door reveals a Kazimierz war-knight, a spokesperson for the Commercial Confederation, and people from Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: And what's more, Leto and her group are riding in that very car. Her earlier request to 'go just a little faster' turns out to have been for exactly this moment.
Only then does Gray Hat realize: the real card Ensiodes had hidden away wasn't a weapon at all—it was guests Victoria could never afford to touch.

The moment the Kazimierz knight and the Commercial Confederation's spokesperson arrive at the ceremony, the Victorian soldiers stop their advance cold. They can't afford to turn Kazimierz into an enemy too.
A war that ended without a single drop of blood spilled—leaving behind nothing but the smile of a man who'd stacked the deck like a card game from the very start.
Epilogue: When Spring Comes
The banquet resumes—Harold's soldiers carve flowers out of ice to propose with, and Degenbrecher signs autographs for her fans. It's hard to believe this was a battlefield only moments ago.

In her remarks at the unveiling ceremony, the Holy Maiden Enya says that someday outsiders will remember this country not just for Karlan Trade CO., LTD, but for something beyond its snow and mountains.

Rhine Lab proposes building a launch facility on the sacred mountain, while the Holy Maiden and her head attendant bicker over 'exactly how many cannons to fit on the statue.' Fairly laid-back, for a negotiation that decides the nation's future.
And the three-year-old truth comes to light. Hidden beneath the Kjeragandr statue at Silverheart Lake was, in fact, a single high-speed warship. Its name: 'Nuts'—the same silly name Siurus gave it.

blocks.cap: From beneath the feet of the Kjeragandr statue, the first warship, hidden for three years, reveals itself.

blocks.x: Ensiodes believes this has earned Kjerag the right to sit across the table from greater nations like Kazimierz and Rhine Lab. Degenbrecher retorts that all it's really earned them is more enemies.
Leto tosses her father the account number for child support and calls her mother to check in. Tatiana, even with her injured leg, is already picking out her next destination.
You can go mountain climbing in Kjerag, and rock climbing too. Rosalind, have you ever tried it?
Whether Kjeragandr truly watches over this land, no one knows. But this winter, no one died, a daughter kicked her father off a cliff, and spring comes again.

act29side.tagline: The story of the 'Candlelight Knight,' who returns home chasing her father's death and only finds freedom after seeing off, with her own hands, the person who raised her. And before the dead tyrant's final question, everyone rises with an answer of their own.

chars.name: Viviana
chars.desc: A 'Candlelight Knight' retired from Kazimierz. The hidden daughter of the Prince-Elector of Sturmland, she becomes a candidate for Voice of the Empress while chasing the truth of her father's death.

chars.name: Ebenholz
chars.desc: The Count of Urtica and the Witch King's only living bloodline. A caster with Rhodes Island, he sets foot again in the homeland he has fled for so long.

chars.name: Arturia
chars.desc: A Sankta cellist who wanders the borderlands. She wields Originium Arts that resonate with the deepest emotions in a person's heart · Operator Virtuosa

chars.name: Federico
chars.desc: An Executor with Laterano's Notary Office. He has followed the wanted fugitive Arturia all the way to Leithanien · Operator Executor the Ex Foedere

chars.name: Lessing
chars.desc: A young swordsman raised under the lich scholar Fremont. He hunts Ebenholz on the orders of those seeking to reclaim the 'Horn of Origin.'

chars.name: Loris
chars.desc: Captain of the Zwillingstürme gendarmerie. Originally from Laterano, he lives steeped in drink and bribes—a self-proclaimed 'failure.'

chars.name: Brandt
chars.desc: A Gesetzschwerter, a sword of Leithanien. Born of the Hochberg family, he grew up like a brother to Werner in childhood.

chars.name: Michael
chars.desc: A young courier who serves Empress Grimacht alone. He carries a tuba on his back as he keeps watch over the city.

terms.name: Leithanien
terms.desc: A nation that united ten culturally distinct tribes through the 'Güldenes Gesetz,' a movement completed a thousand years ago. Its capital is Zwillingstürme.

terms.name: Güldenes Gesetz
terms.desc: The golden movement that defines Leithanien. A vast Originium formula that shapes the very cognition, morality, and order of the Leithanien people.

terms.name: Zwillingstürme
terms.desc: The capital of Leithanien. Named the 'Twin Towers' for the black tower Grimacht and the white tower Ewigegnade, home to the two empresses, standing side by side.

terms.name: Gesetzschwerter
terms.desc: Knights who have dedicated sword and Arts to the Güldenes Gesetz and Leithanien. They are dispatched across the land to act on the empresses' will.

terms.name: the Witch King
terms.desc: A tyrant of the previous age, felled by the twin empresses 23 years ago. Even in death, followers remain who still chant his name.

terms.name: Voice of the Empress
terms.desc: A courier who acts on behalf of the twin empresses, chosen from among the children of the regional nobility. Formal appointment takes place at the High Tower after the Empress Festival concludes.

terms.name: Horn of Origin
terms.desc: A term referring both to the 'Echo of Herkunftshorn,' the remnant faction that invokes the Witch King's name, and to the tyrant's final legacy that they seek to reclaim.

blocks.x: Leithanien is a nation built on song. What united its ten disparate tribes wasn't an army, but the 'Güldenes Gesetz'—a golden movement completed a thousand years ago. In Zwillingstürme, the city of twin towers that guards that movement, the Empress Festival is held every year.

But this year, three days before the festival, the aging Prince-Elector of Sturmland is murdered in his own tower. And his hidden daughter, Viviana, returns to the unfamiliar capital as a candidate for 'Voice of the Empress,' honoring her father's final wish.

Twenty-three years since the Witch King died, everyone thought his name as a tyrant had been forgotten. But masked figures have begun chanting that name again, and beneath this autumn's sunset wait reunions and farewells no one could have foreseen.
1. Her Father's Death, and a Mission That Begins at Once

On a stormy night in Sturmland, Prince-Elector Werner is murdered in his own tower. That same night, **Viviana**, the 'Candlelight Knight' retired from Kazimierz, is led by a stranger named Cora out through a general store window as they flee.
Only then does Cora tell her the truth: that Viviana is Werner's hidden daughter, that her father has already passed away, and that she must now go to the capital to become 'Voice of the Empress.'
In Kazimierz, Viviana was always just called 'the knight from Leithanien.' But now that she's actually back home, she still feels like a stranger.

That same night, down a different alley, **Ebenholz** returns to his homeland in secret, his identity hidden. The spy tailing him mutters bitterly, "Former Count of Urtica, are you now… waiting for a true 'death'?"

Arriving in Zwillingstürme, Viviana has an audience with Empress Ewigegnade and is ordered to hunt down the 'Echo of Herkunftshorn' that killed her father, alongside the Gesetzschwerter **Brandt**. Her formal appointment won't come until after the festival, so until then she must move under a hidden identity.

blocks.cap: The colors of classical paintings, the timbre of an accordion, the scent of marigolds. A city that had only ever existed in her imagination now stood before her eyes.

blocks.x: 2. A Dead Painter's Final Work, Not Meant to Be Seen

On the opening day of a gallery exhibition, the painter Madam Siman collapses and dies before her own final work. The painting is titled 'The Death of the Witch King.' Guided there by gendarmerie captain **Loris**, this painting carves a black spiral into the mind of anyone who looks at it.

At that moment, the Sankta cellist **Arturia** appears and calms the chaos. She explains that Madam Siman had admired the Witch King in her youth, and that her heart became entirely trapped the moment she watched him fall.
If it's sincere enough, even a painting or a melody can move a person's heart. Don't be afraid… just feel it.
Meanwhile, **Federico**, an Executor from Laterano, also arrives in the city. The fugitive he's chasing turns out, remarkably, to be none other than his own older sister.

Loris finds Federico exasperating, yet quietly looks out for him. The drunken gendarmerie captain and the Sankta unfamiliar with emotion—these two mismatched people end up traveling together almost by accident.

blocks.cap: A black spiral spills out of the canvas, consuming the entire room. A power that can drive people mad with a single name: the 'Witch King.'

blocks.x: 3. An Atonement That Brought Down an Entire School

The Witch King's remnants believe that the 'Note of the Mortal World' remaining within the Urtica bloodline—within Ebenholz himself—is the key to reviving His Majesty. **Lessing**, the remnants' young swordsman, is ordered to capture him.

It's Gerhard, a gentle scholar at Ludwigs University, who ends up saving Ebenholz. A survivor of the assault on the Witch King's tower 23 years ago, he had long been searching for a way to atone. He leads Ebenholz beneath the university, to the hidden chamber where the Witch King once rewrote the old Güldenes Gesetz.

**Fremont**, the lich scholar guarding that chamber, was in fact an old friend of the Witch King, yet he feigns indifference, insisting he has no interest in national affairs. Even he can't help but turn serious in the face of his student's betrayal.
Gerhard raises a brass instrument and brings it down as hard as he can on the head of the very young man he'd just saved. He, too, turns out to be one of the remnants.

blocks.cap: The person who tried to protect me is the same one who has, for a second time, bound me before the Witch King's throne.

blocks.x: His guilt was, in truth, self-consolation. He wanted proof that there had been a 'better choice,' even if it meant reviving His Majesty. Fremont and Lessing join forces to rescue the students from the collapsing university.

Ermengarde, Fremont's younger lich colleague, grumbles placidly even amid the chaos: "My cube's worn down to nothing, and now this whole mess." But when things actually turn dangerous, she's the first to send out her threads and pull people to safety.
4. A Case Unsolved for 15 Years, and Yesterday's Hero
Loris, chasing the remnants, ultimately loses his life in the sewers. His last request before dying was a single one: "someone, please take up Julia's case."

Julia was Loris's lover, who vanished fifteen years ago. The two parted on the morning of a café's reopening—the very day Loris had planned to propose—and Loris had gone on gathering evidence about the case alone for fifteen years since.
Federico accepts the dying wish according to an Executor's rules, and for the first time begins to understand that 'emotion,' too, can be a form of information.
I'm too tired now, old man. In the end, I… can't die like you. Like a hero.

But that night, Loris finally sits face to face with the fear he had always run from. And in his final moment, he stands against the remnants not as the coward he'd always been, but as a hero who chose, willingly, to return.
5. The Empress Festival, a Blade Hidden Within the Song

On the day of the Empress Festival, tens of thousands gather beneath the twin towers to perform the Güldenes Gesetz together. Once this piece begins, the entire city is dyed with a single emotion—but somewhere within that movement, the remnants had infiltrated, secretly layering another song on top.

Viviana and Cora try to stop the performance, but there isn't enough time. While Federico holds off the empress's casters, Viviana and Cora spend the remaining twenty-three seconds severing the connections of some of the musicians.
As the sunset falls, the earth splits open and a dark crimson tower rises up.

blocks.cap: The storm clouds part, and the tower that fell 23 years ago reveals itself once more. The 'Horn of Origin.'

blocks.x: 6. How to See Off the Person You Love Most

Thirteen years ago, it was Cora who took young Viviana, freshly orphaned of her mother, all the way to Kazimierz. From then until now, Cora had always been the one family who stayed by Viviana's side.
But Cora had long been connected to the remnants' 'Chief.' What she wanted wasn't betrayal—only a future where no one would ever be hurt again the way Lucinda and Werner had been.
But that longing had slowly consumed Cora herself, and only one path remained: to meet her end at the hands of someone she loved.
Viviana raised her candlelight. She did not hesitate.

blocks.cap: Cough, cough… Viviana, are you crying? It seems I don't even have the strength left to wipe your tears away.

blocks.x: You deserve to live a better life, Viviana… if such a life truly exists.

"Thanks to you, Viviana and I will go somewhere far away." And with that, Cora vanished into black flame. Brandt left only a single line behind: "No need to explain. You made your own judgment, and you carried it through."
7. The Throne Was Empty

As the tower rises, people all across the city are pulled into the 'Horn of Origin,' where reality and chaos intermingle. At the end of an endless spiral staircase, Ebenholz finally comes face to face with the Echo of the Witch King seated upon the throne.

Before the voice that has tormented him his whole life, the name that bound him to an examination table since childhood, Ebenholz has come prepared with every curse and insult he knows. But the tyrant, unexpectedly calm, simply points out that Ebenholz's true enemy has always been himself.

blocks.cap: "You have defeated your enemy. You have escaped the high tower that once caged you." And so the tyrant acknowledged the descendant he had once despised.

blocks.x: Ebenholz can't help but burst into laughter at those words. When the laughter fades, the throne is empty. The 'Witch King' he had feared for so long had, in the end, existed only within his own imagination.
8. For Hearts Going Hollow, the Sankta's Final Melody

Within that same chaos, Federico once again encounters traces of the dead Julia. As she disappears, forever unaware of the proposal Loris never got to make, even the ever-composed Federico finds himself, for the first time, unable to speak.
Arturia, too, completely loses the sound of her own cello. She, who has always read the hearts of others, comes face to face for the first time with just how fragile she herself is.

blocks.cap: Silence, too, is a performance, and every piece must have an ending. For the first time, the Sankta acknowledged her own tears.

blocks.x: In the square beneath the tower, people who had lost every last emotion were turning into hollow shells. This time, Arturia chooses to play not someone else, but herself.
I will share my feelings with everyone. With this small, unremarkable pride of mine, I will connect every sorrow and every joy.

blocks.cap: At the sound of the Sankta's cello, expression returns once more to the faces of the hollowed-out people.

blocks.x: 9. The Knight in the Sunset, and the Two Empresses' Final Duet
Viviana, too, opens hundreds of doors within that same chaos. Behind them lie lives that could never have been: 'parents who made better choices,' 'a self who came home sooner.'

She even comes face to face with her own childhood self. That child asks her back: isn't wandering in search of a better answer really just an escape—a way of comforting herself with the thought that 'there was never any other way'?
With collapse bearing down on her, Viviana chooses not to run. In her hand, the same light that shone from the knight in the novels she read as a child now takes hold.

blocks.cap: Viviana Droste, don't forget: you are a knight!

blocks.x: Meanwhile, Grimacht and Ewigegnade confront the Echo of the Witch King before the throne. The two empresses bring him down, but through the cracks of the collapsing tower, the gaze of things far more terrifying begins to seep in.

blocks.cap: Black and red, and dazzling gold and violet. Two generations of Leithanien's sovereigns clash before the throne.

blocks.x: Today, or in some distant future, I will lose my life here and become part of the chaos. This is my fate. But it is never… never the fate of Leithanien or its people.

blocks.cap: The sword vanishes together with its shadow. In the end, she never looked back.

blocks.x: The sunset returns. With the instruments in their hands, people begin to play, each their own, an entirely new song—one that is not the Güldenes Gesetz.

blocks.cap: Golden light pours down through the storm clouds. A new sunset for Leithanien.

blocks.x: Having lost Grimacht, Ewigegnade chooses to hold onto that sunset forever, alone. "Because I am more perfect than Herkunftshorn." She said it with a smile, but tears ran down her cheeks.
Michael, recalling Grimacht's final order, plays his tuba alone. Now the mountain of paperwork left to handle, and the one remaining tower to guard, have both fallen to him.
Epilogue: The Sunset, Once More

Two weeks later, after hearing Lessing's long story, Ebenholz decides to return to the Urtica estate. If he was the one to inherit the Witch King's name, he reasoned, then it was also his to remake that name into something entirely different.

Hibiscus, his colleague from Rhodes Island, tracks him down belatedly and scolds him for disappearing without so much as a leave request. But seeing Ebenholz's composed expression, she realizes this is a decision he's already thought through more than enough, and quietly sorts out the paperwork for him.

Viviana politely declines the position of Voice of the Empress. When a reply arrives from the Nearl family of Kazimierz, she leaves the tree in the garden of the small house Cora once bought her to grow freely, and sets out once more, following a new lead.

Even as he takes Arturia back to Laterano, Federico lingers a while at her side, watching the sunset together. Just as she once promised, this was a moment when even the most rational of men could not, in the end, escape the beauty of this city.
Light is eternal, and it is darkness that is fleeting. Our feelings never truly disappear… just as the sunset has never once left the sky.

blocks.cap: Even the answer that the most rational of men arrived at was, without fail, beautiful.

blocks.x: Leithanien remains a nation that stands on song. Only this autumn, that song carries within it a silence someone chose for themselves, and a sorrow someone else chose to bear in another's place. And once the marigolds fall, another sunset will come.

act28side.tagline: Mercenaries who came to fix a single power plant realize that saving the town means robbing a bank first. And this season's gentlest confession: he chose her not for her gun, but for her tears.

chars.name: Jessica
chars.desc: A young sniper with Blacksteel. A soft-hearted soul who can never hold back tears in a crisis · Operator Jessica the Liberated

chars.name: Woodrow Bianchi
chars.desc: A gruff old man who lives and hunts alone on the outskirts of Davis Town. The townsfolk just call him 'Woody'

chars.name: Helena
chars.desc: The proprietress of Davis Town's only restaurant. She's appointed herself guardian of the whole neighborhood · Operator Coldshot

chars.name: Leon Theremin
chars.desc: A demolitions engineer at the mining plant. A father raising two sons alone

chars.name: Benny
chars.desc: Leon's youngest son. A precocious, warm-hearted boy

chars.name: Sylvia
chars.desc: A young, quiet employee at Davis Town's bank

chars.name: 'Clip' Cliff
chars.desc: The head of the Blacksteel mercenary company. A man who keeps business and feeling strictly separate

chars.name: Franka
chars.desc: A commander-ranked mercenary with Blacksteel. Breezy and quick with words

chars.name: Liskarm
chars.desc: Franka's partner. A cautious, principled team leader

chars.name: Laura
chars.desc: Blacksteel's chief engineer. An optimist who talks to herself constantly · Operator Almond

terms.name: Davis Town
terms.desc: A small mining sector stranded in Columbia's eastern forestlands. Its finances collapsed after a power plant accident

terms.name: Blacksteel Worldwide
terms.desc: A mercenary company rooted in Columbia. It looks moderate on the surface, but handles the dirty work behind the scenes

terms.name: Baron Base
terms.desc: Blacksteel's mobile mothership. It ferries mercenary units between sectors

terms.name: the Frontier
terms.desc: A harsh settlement zone where bankrupt debtors or criminals are forcibly sent

terms.name: Sankta
terms.desc: A race bearing rings of light. Said to be able to faintly sense one another's pain

terms.name: Laterano
terms.desc: Homeland of the Sankta, a nation famed for its idealism

terms.name: Colus Camp
terms.desc: A POW camp from the Columbian War of Independence. Infamous for the saying, "of ten who go in, only one comes out"

blocks.x: There's a familiar combo in the world of Arknights: a sector with no money, and the bank that comes to collect it anyway. Davis Town, a mining sector stranded in Columbia's eastern forestlands, was no exception.

A year after its power plant blew, with the government doing nothing but watching the snow pile up, a Blacksteel mercenary unit is dispatched to this town. Their stated mission: repair the power plant.
But rookie mercenary **Jessica** is carrying a suspicious bullet secretly slipped to her by a superior — along with a vague errand: "give it to Woodrow Bianchi of Davis Town," and nothing more.
A few months later, the mercenaries who came to save this town are robbing its bank. A bit out of order, isn't it? Let's unpack what happened in between.
1. Picked up a hunter in the snow — turns out he had a name after all

Before the power plant mission even starts, Jessica gets separated in a blizzard. What appears with a gunshot is a gruff old hunter. Mid-dressing a deer he'd just shot, he casually offers to walk her to Davis Town.

On the way, they find a fawn caught in a trap. The hunter says to just wring its neck and let it go easy; Jessica insists on holding it in her arms until it dies naturally. "First time I've seen a mercenary do hospice care," the hunter remarks.

blocks.cap: Follow the broken branches through the fog, and a sector appears, gasping for breath like a patient in critical condition.

blocks.x: That evening, at Helena's restaurant, Jessica hears the hunter called 'Woody' and asks, "Do you happen to know Woodrow Bianchi?" Helena and Leon both blink. "Who......?" Nobody knew the real name of the man they saw every day.
2. A single bank has the whole town mortgaged

Davis Town's bank is the kind of place that answers a request for a power of attorney with "the seal suddenly went missing yesterday." The government office squats in the bank's garage, and residents scoop up snow outside their windows to melt and drink, just to save water.
Leon volunteers for the dangerous power plant repair, his eyes lighting up at the mention of a 200,000-check bonus if it's done within a week.
Pressed for the reason, Leon opens up. "Basically the whole sector's in debt. Most of the people who walked into the snow and disappeared did it because of money."

In fact, Jessica had just witnessed one of those missing people herself — a man who didn't turn even when called, walking into the blizzard and vanishing, leaving only footprints. She'd thought this was a cheerful little town; the chill sets in from the very start.
3. The power plant, and the people, were already wrecked

Inside the power plant, things are bad enough that even Laura says, "I must be hallucinating." There's even evidence of 'legal looting' from well before the accident — caravans quietly swapping forklifts and canned goods on the sly.

Laura normally talks to herself nonstop while working. So when she suddenly falls silent, a coworker explains, "When Laura stops talking, that means......" Before he can finish, the evacuation alarm blares.

Thankfully everyone makes it back without major incident. Laura's first words — "Move it, Jessica...... I'm starving to death" — finally let everyone breathe easy. Meanwhile, in the alley behind the bank, Jessica rescues Leon's son **Benny**, who'd nearly been kidnapped by thugs.
4. A friendly town makes for a scarier plan

The bank hands Liskarm a 'public order maintenance list' — essentially treating people in debt as would-be robbers to be dealt with. Liskarm refuses to take the list at face value and insists on re-investigating.

A Blacksteel colleague Jessica meets on patrol lets something slip. "A sector on the verge of bankruptcy like this — debt, tax audits, security loopholes...... plenty of excuses to go around." Once reclaimed by the government, whether it's industry, facilities, or residents, everything would get replaced.
Jessica bristles. "That's illegal!" The reply comes back flat. "Don't care."

That night, working late alone at the bank, **Sylvia** finds it strange that the main power switch sits exactly one hundred steps from the front door. Ever since her mother died, she's had a habit of walking the same route home every day and counting her steps. "No, that can't be...... it doesn't make sense." A suspicion even she doesn't want to believe.
5. Benny decides to leave Davis Town

Leon, Helena, Woodrow, Miles, and Jessica all sit down to dinner together for the first time in a while. Everyone humming an old song about the town — the warmest scene in this whole story. Then Benny quietly raises his hand.
"I'm leaving." A scholarship, he announces, to a school in Ironforge City. Leon just repeats "It's fine...... it's a good thing......" like a machine, unable to string together a proper sentence.

blocks.cap: The son wanted to see anger in his father's eyes. But all that was there was pure devastation.

blocks.x: Right before leaving, Benny drops a bomb on Jessica: his late brother **Karl** was a Blacksteel mercenary, a 'Blackplate.' Jessica brushes it off, saying they didn't know each other long — but she's clearly hiding something.

That night, in the bank vault, Sylvia clutches a necklace and mutters to herself. "Karl...... what am I supposed to do?" Between the dead mercenary and the bank employee, there was a story nobody knew about.
6. Turns out the boss was an old comrade-in-arms

Jessica finally hands that bullet over to Woodrow. The moment 'the boss' in an old photo hidden in his drawer overlaps with Cliff's face, he murmurs low. "So he changed his name...... your boss...... it was him."

blocks.cap: Autumn, year 1019, Colus Camp, Columbia. The man who survived and came back sits face to face again with the man who never came to rescue him.

blocks.x: The fellow Sankta imprisoned alongside them died in Woodrow's arms in the end. Cliff was the one who gave up on the rescue operation. Woodrow goes to Cliff's office and demands he "call back his men and stop driving out the residents," but Cliff won't budge, insisting, "if Blacksteel leaves, the government will just send another mercenary company."
That night, thugs from the sector's east side riot. Miles fends off the intruders with a steam-valve trap, and Woodrow strings one up under the eaves with a noose.
And Leon's house burns down. "Not so bad, being burned down...... one less thing to worry about now," Leon says bitterly, and that's all.

blocks.cap: After a fifteen-day voyage, Baron Base breaks through the thick snow fog and arrives at Davis Town.

blocks.x: 7. A threat by the name of 'public order maintenance'
The bank manager hands Liskarm a document — a three-way contract between the bank, the government, and Blacksteel, making it a legitimate order, she claims.
Liskarm refuses. "I won't be firing my gun. And I won't let anyone on my team fire theirs, either."

blocks.cap: With a single sheet titled "Authority Delegation Agreement," the bank tries to borrow the mercenary company's gun barrels.

blocks.x: Residents gathered outside the bank sing a protest song. "We're the ones starving, we're the ones who gave everything for a worthless promise...... farewell, my hometown ♪" With that song behind them, Helena quietly dances with Woodrow — a brief moment of peace in a town that could vanish any day.
But while sorting documents, Sylvia discovers a stack of blank contracts prepared for people who never signed anything, who never even set foot in the bank.
We don't even care about the paltry interest. What we're after is everything here. That's how it's always been.
8. In the end, everyone decides to become thieves

Jessica confronts Cliff. "It seems like you've lost almost all your humanity just to get those things." Cliff calmly asks back, "Humanity, huh...... what good is a pretty word like that to the head of a mercenary company?"
In the end, Jessica walks away on her own, effectively splitting from Blacksteel.

Sylvia plays her final card: a large sum sitting in the bank vault, set aside for the new settlers. "Do you want us to return that money to its rightful owners, or do you want it taken from you?" Woodrow cuts straight to the point, and Sylvia nods quietly.

Helena is the first to set an old weapon down on the table — the resolve of someone who's survived twenty years wandering the Frontier. "If none of you want in, I'll do it alone." Leon pulls out a wrench too.

Woodrow points his gun at Jessica one last time, telling her to "leave," but Jessica lowers her own weapon and answers instead. "If I don't do something, I'll get used to a life where I never accomplish anything. I don't want that, even if it kills me...... let me in too."
9. How to rob a bank: one hundred steps and some dynamite

Following the bank's layout and passcode Sylvia memorized through years of footsteps, the four of them move. Woodrow and Jessica control the hostages, while Helena and Leon breach their way to the vault — the roles were split that way.

blocks.cap: The black-and-white snowfield regains its color. Asked what they'll do once this is over, neither of them can easily answer.

blocks.x: With their lives on the line, both the bank president and the manager give up the passcode without much fight. The president crumbles under Woodrow's countdown, and the manager, trying to provoke Leon, gets restrained by Jessica instead.
At the final door, Sylvia's instructions are simple. "Easy bonus question — blow it up."

blocks.cap: With the blast, shredded bundles of cash pour down heavier than the snow that ever fell on Davis Town.

blocks.x: The four of them stand speechless in front of the pile of money for a moment. "I've never seen this much money in my life," Helena says, and Leon answers, "But it's still a lot less than what the bank ever squeezed out of us."
Jessica just finds it strange — she's never wanted money this much in her life.
10. Leon stays behind with the power plant
The escape doesn't go as planned. While Woodrow stays behind alone to hold off Blacksteel, Leon and Helena rig the hidden passage with explosives.
With each charge he sets, Leon sees hallucinations — Benny, already gone; Karl, killed in action; and finally, the mother who once left him behind, all passing before his eyes one after another.

In the end, Leon decides to stay and seal off the passage himself. Over the radio, to a desperate Jessica, the last thing he leaves her is a song. "I won't die...... I'll be here forever, living forever in her embrace......"

blocks.cap: From a massive crack in the tower, smoke keeps pouring out without end.

blocks.x: At the same moment, Woodrow stands facing Cliff, guns drawn. For the first time, he calls him by his real name. "Rupert."
Cliff practically begs to be killed. "You can end me." But instead of pulling the trigger, Woodrow shoots and destroys his radio.
I can't kill you. This sin is yours to carry.

blocks.cap: The old man's hat flies off in the blizzard. Gun barrels aimed at one another, neither fires first.

blocks.x: Through her scope, Jessica sees strangers' faces flash past — a praying girl, a woman running through snow, a man who walked off into flames, one after another.

blocks.cap: Faces flashing past through the scope. The last was the man who walked off into the flames.

blocks.x: Jessica fires every bullet she has straight into the sky.
My tears can't stand in for their grief. But my gun will ring out for their grief instead.
In five years, did you ever once realize why I chose you back then? I chose you not for the gun in your hand, but for your tears. Tears are never a weakness, Jessica.

Jessica lets herself be arrested without resistance, as Franka fastens a pair of handcuffs she'd warmed up beforehand. Realizing the night has passed and the snow has stopped, Jessica quietly hums along to the song Leon used to sing.
Epilogue. People starting over
Cliff quietly looks the other way. Jessica admits her guilt and volunteers for the Frontier instead of a reduced sentence; Helena also escapes safely.
Woodrow is offered a new identity and a mansion down south, but he refuses. "I'm going to look for family, not friends."

As they part, Cliff asks one thing of him. "Don't die somewhere I don't know about." Woodrow answers, "Even if there's no undoing it, I suppose I can still call you again sometime." Not a complete break, in other words.

blocks.cap: To a young Jessica crying in front of a collapsed sandcastle, her grandfather says, "Don't be afraid to start over."
The memory fades, and the Jessica of today answers. "...... Yes, let's start over."

blocks.x: Woodrow climbs onto the transport heading to the Frontier alongside Jessica. Fitting a wide-brimmed hat over her head, he says,
Cry if you need to...... every new life is born crying, after all. That's the first step to breathing.

blocks.cap: Under the hat, Jessica finally breaks into tears. The old man looks out at the horizon, blaming it on the morning glow.

blocks.x: Davis Town is still right there, gasping for breath like a patient in critical condition. The only thing different is that it wasn't the bank, and it wasn't the government, that saved this town — it was four people who chose to become thieves. And what brought one of them back to life wasn't a gun, but tears.

act27side.tagline: A mysterious creature promised to grant a wish if three little lost things were found for it — but what it actually gave back to a grieving daughter was her parents' last, true feelings. On the night the volcano burns pure white, everyone learns, little by little, how to smile again.

chars.name: Adel
chars.desc: Daughter of a volcanologist couple. A rookie researcher who carries on her late parents' work despite suffering from Oripathy · Operator Eyjafjalla

chars.name: Ennis
chars.desc: A Siesta boy shouldering his whole family's livelihood alone, juggling four part-time jobs while putting his own health dead last

chars.name: Keller
chars.desc: An old colleague of the Naumann couple and the new volcano museum's curator, whose evasive eyes keep nagging at everyone's mind

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: Commissioner of the Lungmen Guard Department, insisting he's just on vacation — a claim nobody believes from this conglomerate heir · Operator Swire

chars.name: Bison
chars.desc: Heir to Mountain Dash Logistics, a diligent young master trying to step out of his father's shadow · Operator Bison

chars.name: Ceylon
chars.desc: The mayor's daughter and a doctor with Rhodes Island, distant from her own father · Operator Ceylon

chars.name: Dolly
chars.desc: A fluffy, unidentified creature spotted all over Siesta, who out of nowhere proposes a treasure hunt to Adel

chars.name: Kahn
chars.desc: A volcanologist who once studied under the Naumanns, unable to let go of his suspicions toward his mentor Keller

chars.name: Costa
chars.desc: A city hall clerk going door to door collecting relocation forms, who is secretly the son of a shuttered café

terms.name: Siesta
terms.desc: A resort city-state that once lived off its volcano, obsidian, and rock music. When volcanic activity resumed, the entire city relocated as a nomadic city.

terms.name: Chokomi Sheep
terms.desc: Fluffy avatars Dolly claims to have created. Mischievous little things that carry off anything eye-catching, be it a street sign or a wallet.

terms.name: Volcano Warning Flower
terms.desc: A legendary plant said to change color according to volcanic activity. The very research subject the Naumann couple chased in their lifetime.

terms.name: Obsidian
terms.desc: Ore once mined from the Siesta volcano. Once the city's main source of income, mining was later banned over environmental damage concerns.

terms.name: Obsidian Music Festival
terms.desc: A rock music festival once held in Siesta. It naturally ended when the city relocated.

blocks.x: Siesta was a resort city that lived off its volcano, its obsidian, and a rock festival once said to be the continent's finest. But when that volcano showed real signs of waking up, the entire city packed up and moved 100 kilometers away as a nomadic city.

Adel, daughter of the late volcanologist couple, comes to Siesta to sort through her parents' research materials at the newly built volcano museum. As it happens, that very volcano is on the verge of a real eruption.

But on her very first night there, a talking flock of sheep appears and makes her a proposal. "Just find the three things I lost here." A north wind, a seed, and some fur — that's all it takes, apparently.
1. Is it normal for sheep to talk like this — first time I've seen one talk back

Siesta was originally a beach town at the foot of a volcano. Famous for its hot springs, obsidian, and the **Obsidian Music Festival**, the entire city packed up and relocated 100 kilometers away as the nomadic city 'New Siesta' when the volcano stirred again.

Four years ago, volcanologist couple Katia and Magna Naumann died in an accident while observing Mount Una. Their daughter **Adel** is a rookie researcher carrying on her parents' final work despite suffering from Oripathy. Her hearing has worsened to the point she struggles to converse without a hearing aid, but her eyes light up the moment volcanoes come up.

**Keller**, an old colleague of her parents, called Adel over — asking her to organize the Naumanns' materials at the new volcano museum. With the Siesta volcano also due to erupt soon, she says, it's a perfect opportunity for observation.

blocks.cap: A downtown where buildings rise instead of mountain peaks. New Siesta was built to mimic the shape of the old volcano across the whole city.

blocks.x: A display case in the museum holds an old volcano exploration suit — one all too familiar to Adel, her mother Katia's own gear. She reaches out across the glass, but can't quite touch it.

blocks.cap: A daughter and her mother's suit, separated by a single pane of glass. The hand that reached to touch it pulled back, as if burned.

blocks.x: Strange things start happening from the very first night. A cloud-fluffy creature perches on a mailbox and speaks to her. "Hello there, lovely night."

This creature introduces itself as **Dolly**. It claims to have known Adel's parents long ago, but is vague about what it actually is. All over Siesta, there are fluffy sheep invisible to human eyes — and apparently, they're all Dolly's 'avatars.'
Simply put, I want you to find a few things I lost around Siesta. Think of it as a sort of treasure hunt.

Dolly is after exactly three things: a **north wind**, a **seed**, and **fur**. In return, it promises to give Adel "the thing she's been searching for." Asked what that might be, it never gives an answer.

Meanwhile, several suspicious people had already arrived in town. One of them is **Swire**, Commissioner of the Lungmen Guard Department and heir to one of the biggest merchant houses, insisting this time it's "just a vacation." Naturally, nobody believed her.
2. A boy juggling four part-time jobs, hiding only his own body

**Ennis** is a Siesta boy juggling several part-time jobs at once — freight driver, ticket seller, swim coach. Feeding his two adopted siblings Root and Liv, plus his mother Hailey who runs the bar 'Pure White Volcano,' is his job.
That same Ennis collides head-on with Adel while running down the street. Guilt-ridden, he even covers her treatment costs — but says nothing at all about his own condition.

In truth, Ennis is an Oripathy patient too. Three months ago, he scraped himself on Originium while secretly mining obsidian. He tried to hide it from his family to the end, but it shows plainly on his face.
In exchange for a research sample of **obsidian**, Adel earnestly warns Ennis never to go near the volcano again. That promise, of all things, is one Ennis can't keep.
Later, when his mother Hailey learns the truth, what she says cuts straight to the heart. "You're my son whether you do anything or not — this isn't a relationship that needs a transaction."
Family is family, blood or not.
3. A student suspects his mentor — but the more he suspects, the more suspicious his own heart looks
A letter arrives for Adel, its envelope half torn open, containing a photo of her father taken at Mount Una — as if sent by the father who'd been out of contact for years.

That's not the only strange thing. Among the museum's materials, several pages that should have been in her mother Katia's research notebook are missing entirely. In their place sits an unfamiliar notebook that only Keller ever had.

**Kahn** was a student of the Naumann couple, and he's long harbored suspicions about his mentor Keller. A year before the accident, he says, the Leithanien military kept coming and going from the couple's lab.
By Kahn's account, only one person could freely view the couple's research back then — Keller, and Keller alone. What's more, she suddenly left the expedition the day before the climb.
That's why I absolutely cannot accept their sacrifice being buried under some unknown shadow.

Adel still doesn't know whether Kahn's suspicions are right, or whether Keller has some other circumstance she can't voice. Keller only ever repeats, "I'll tell you everything once the observation is done."
4. Two rich kids in a mudslinging fight who somehow end up business partners

**Bison** is heir to Mountain Dash Logistics. Trying to step out of his father Euril's shadow, he throws himself alone into the bid for Siesta's new district development rights — against, of all people, Swire.
The two clash the moment they meet at a bar, locked in a tense standoff — Swire pushing for a water park, Bison for a logistics center. Yet somehow, each secretly rather likes the other.

Swire recruits shop owner Ennis as an informant, strikes a deal with hot spring hotel owner Felipe using his obsidian collection as bait, and secretly throws together a water park practically overnight. In the process, Dolly's Chokomi Sheep flood the waterway in a disaster involving surfboards flying through the sky.

blocks.cap: On surfboards soaring above the hot spring water park, little lambs invisible to anyone else were having the time of their lives.

blocks.x: In the end, Swire's plan wins the public poll. But Swire had been calculating something other than victory all along — offering to drop out of the bid in exchange for shares in Bison's company.

**"A smart merchant always finds a way for both sides to win."** The two go from rivals to partners. Swire uses this to strengthen her standing within the Lungmen clan, while Bison declares his independence from his father's company. Quite a scale for a couple of rich kids' summer vacation.
5. A café shut for over a year gets a customer — turns out to be the owner's old friend

**Costa** is a city hall clerk going around collecting residents' relocation forms. In truth, he's the son of 'Café Mockingbird,' shuttered for a year now, and once played in a rock band in his younger days.
One night, Costa reopens the empty café and brews some coffee. Then he hears a knock at the door. "Are you still open, by any chance?"

blocks.cap: Under the café's light, lit again after a year, the two of them recognize each other.

blocks.x: Keller was standing at the door. The two reminisce together about the summer twenty years ago, when they first met Katia and Magna at this very café — even Costa's secretly brewed 'volcano coffee' from back then.
That summer Keller suddenly left Siesta, Costa had to see her off without ever knowing why. And the two of them walked separate paths all the way to now.
Costa's grandfather quietly passes away that same night. All he left his grandson was an old kalimba, and a nagging reminder to do what he wants rather than settle for stability.
6. A night walk with two little sheep turns out to be a road leading skyward
One night, wearing her mother's old exploration suit, Adel falls into a strange dream. Two Chokomi Sheep — one gentle, one solemn — lead her walking through a shopping street.
These shops don't sell compasses or tents. They sell 'knowledge' and 'courage,' and a very expensive 'luck.' Oddly enough, the currency here is bottle caps.
The two creatures tell Adel an old story. They once planted a stone and lost it, and that stone eventually became magma deep underground. Asked if they regret it, both shake their heads and smile.
Nothing from the past truly passes away — it just stays by your side in a different form.
Adel vaguely senses this story is really about them. But then, a strange heartbeat echoes from the direction of the volcano, and the two creatures' expressions suddenly change.

blocks.cap: Farewell, Adel. She couldn't hold back the two creatures as they turned toward the volcano.

blocks.x: "We have to reach the volcano before the rain falls." With those words, the two vanish into the night view. The next morning, Adel can't find a single Chokomi Sheep anywhere in Siesta.
7. A Chokomi Sheep suddenly buries its head in someone's chest — the owner of that heartbeat hasn't even been born yet
**Ceylon** is Mayor Herman's daughter and a doctor with Rhodes Island. She returned to Siesta to help the Infected, but stays distant from her own father.
While investigating illegal obsidian mining laborers, Ceylon hears an unexpected name — Barbara, a pregnant scholar who once frequented the mines. The workers say she looked just like Ceylon.
Among the collection of hot spring hotel owner Felipe was a geode of obsidian the workers once tried, and failed, to give to that scholar — a gift meant to celebrate the birth of her baby.
That evening, one of the Chokomi Sheep Adel had been chasing suddenly leaps into Ceylon's arms. It rests its head against her chest and stays still, as if listening for a heartbeat lost long ago.

In that moment, Ceylon feels an inexplicable longing — thoughts of a mother she's never met surface. She begins to vaguely realize whose will Siesta's generous policy toward the Infected really was, all along.
8. An interrogation right at the volcano's foot — but both interrogator and suspect are trembling
Signal cuts off from Observation Post No. 1. The last data it sent shows an unexplainable spike in heat. Keller tries to brush it off as an equipment glitch, but Adel doesn't back down.
"Turning away now is as good as admitting their sacrifice meant nothing, isn't it?" In the end, the two of them risk it and head to the volcano themselves.

But Kahn had already arrived at the observation post ahead of them. In his hand, an old document envelope — letters exchanged between Keller and the Leithanien military, evidence even Adel had never seen.

Even as the volcano trembles, Kahn demands answers from Keller — why she left the expedition right before the accident, and why Katia's research was mixed in with her correspondence with the military.
Mom, Dad...... was your sacrifice really just an accident......?

The truth comes out later. Keller had only tried to buy time by meeting with the military, so the couple could finish their expedition undisturbed. That an accident would happen in the meantime was something even Keller never saw coming.
9. The night the volcano erupts, everyone throws a party instead of running away

A broadcast announces the eruption is imminent. Instead of luggage, the shop owners bring out grills and guitars, throwing one last party for the old Siesta. After all, this is a sight they'll never see again.

In the commotion, little sister Liv goes missing. Ennis chases her all the way to the old shore where seawater is already rising. There, for the first time, he feels the Originium crystal inside his body burn hot.
See...... I told you...... I can do magic......

Ennis, who'd always played at magic with clay dolls for his siblings, actually raises earth and stone to hold back the waves. And countless Chokomi Sheep swarm in, forming a living dam with their own bodies against the tide.

blocks.cap: Fluffy bodies piled layer upon layer, swallowing the wave whole. That's how the siblings survived.

blocks.x: In the distance, the volcano finally erupts. People clasp each other's hands tight and hold their breath. Dark red smoke covers half the sky, then astonishingly turns a brilliant white.

blocks.cap: Farewell, Siesta. Pure white volcanic ash split the sky and settled over the old city.

blocks.x: **The 'pure white volcano,' known only as a legend, finally revealed its true self the moment it erupted.** Everyone, each in their own way, bids farewell to the vanishing hometown.
Epilogue. Summer, again

The day after the eruption, Adel writes a letter to her parents — stories of the old friends she met in Siesta, a confession that she's not as strong as she thought, and a vow that she still loves volcanoes.

blocks.cap: To my beloved mom and dad. Adel wrote out a letter she'd never be able to send.

blocks.x: Keller shows Adel one last remaining box. Inside are documents stamped with a discard order for the 'Shower Project' — evidence that her parents' research had once been caught up in weaponization talks. And bit by bit, Keller begins to let go of the guilt she'd carried, believing the real fault was hers alone.

Ennis finally tells his family he's leaving on a journey. Just as his father once did, he wants to see the world with his own eyes. And instead of being torn down, the bar 'Pure White Volcano' survives as part of the water park.
Ceylon builds an Infected treatment center in New Siesta. What her mother Barbara had tried to do, the daughter now carries on in her own way.

Lastly, Dolly hands Adel her real reward — none other than the **Volcano Warning Flower**. Secretly planted on a slope beyond the volcano, it was the very flower her parents had spent their whole lives searching for.
"I look forward to seeing a truly happy Adel next time we meet." With that farewell, Dolly hurries off to greet the opening of the volcano amusement park.

At the very start of this story, an unknown voice complained it couldn't bear the boredom without something new and fun. Now we can guess that voice belonged to none other than Dolly. This summer, when the volcano burned pure white, both Dolly and Adel finally found their own interesting story to tell.

act26side.tagline: A story about an Executor who judged every incident by pure reason alone, encountering a despair the law cannot save in a wasteland monastery called paradise. And for the first time in his life, he asks himself a question.

chars.name: Federico
chars.desc: A newly appointed Executor of the Laterano notary office who operates by strictly separating emotion from reason. He recently received the title of 'Saint' · Operator Executor the Ex Foedere

chars.name: Lemuen
chars.desc: A cardinal's aide dispatched from Laterano who lost contact. She relies on a wheelchair after injuring her leg.

chars.name: Serfilia
chars.desc: A cheerful Sankta technician skilled at firearm maintenance, and Lemuen's longtime junior · Operator Spuria

chars.name: Arturia
chars.desc: A mysterious traveler who appears out of nowhere playing the cello · Operator Virtuosa

chars.name: Stefano Torregrossa
chars.desc: An old bishop who has led the mobile monastery Ambrosius for sixty years

chars.name: Gerald
chars.desc: A seasoned hunter who leads a band of Sarkaz settled at the monastery

chars.name: Clement
chars.desc: A gentle gardener at the monastery who tends its flowers with care

chars.name: Delfina
chars.desc: A Sankta girl. Fortuna's childhood friend, and one who takes the side of the Sarkaz she grew up alongside

chars.name: Raymund
chars.desc: A hot-blooded young Sarkaz born and raised at the monastery

chars.name: Fortuna
chars.desc: A warm, sociable Sankta girl. She's been close with Delfina and Raymund since childhood

terms.name: Laterano
terms.desc: The sacred city of the Sankta, called the world's only recognized paradise.

terms.name: Sankta
terms.desc: A race bearing halos above their heads, possessing the power of 'empathy' to read one another's hearts. The dominant people of Laterano.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: A race with horns on their foreheads. Often wandering in search of somewhere to settle, amid war and exclusion.

terms.name: Saint
terms.desc: A title once granted only to the saints who founded Laterano. Federico is the first to receive this title without having been Pope.

terms.name: Fallen Angel
terms.desc: A Sankta whose halo has twisted and whose forehead has grown horns after breaking a taboo. They can no longer empathize with their own kind.

terms.name: Deep Sea Church
terms.desc: A suspicious faith group said to operate near Iberia. It's said to transform people into something unknowable.

blocks.x: Laterano is called the paradise of the Sankta. Halos crowning their heads, the power of 'empathy' to read one another's hearts, and a faith that hasn't wavered in a thousand years — this city believes itself the world's one and only utopia.

But if that paradise really is the only one, where are those pushed outside it supposed to go? Federico, a newly named Saint, has never once entertained such a question. He is an Executor of the Laterano notary office, someone who moves purely by reason and regulation, with emotion set aside.

The Pope assigns him an unfamiliar mission. In a mobile monastery that lost its course sixty years ago and has drifted through the wasteland ever since, find two envoys who were dispatched there and lost contact. When they arrive, the monastery turns out to house Sankta refugees living alongside Sarkaz mercenaries — and the single condition Laterano has set for their return is: 'Paradise does not accept the Sarkaz.'
1. The Sainthood inauguration, and the balloon man ascending to the heavens

For the first time since his coronation, Pope Evangelista XI grants the title of 'Saint' to someone who isn't Pope. The candidate is none other than Executor Federico — who himself cares less about the weight of the title than about 'exactly what disaster is coming.'
The Pope holds a ledger he's told no one about. The name written at the top is today's honoree, and beneath it are several more familiar names — names even Federico doesn't recognize.

blocks.cap: "Evaluating threat level... simulation failed. Activating emergency response system." The night before the coronation, an unidentified warning swept through the Vatican.

blocks.x: On the way to the ceremony, a Laterano citizen who tried to fly by strapping balloons to himself floats straight up into the sky. Federico, passing by, pops every balloon with a single shot to bring him back down.
I simply made the most rational judgment given the situation at hand.

Instead of thanks, the man only asks where he got his uniform tailored. To this man, the Pope leaves a strange remark: once the ceremony ends, he says, they should go meet the 'friends' on the ledger.
2. Paradise stranded in the wasteland

Federico's team sets out to find the mobile monastery Ambrosius, missing for sixty years. Joining him are his chatty fellow Executor Rikelle, and Serfilia, a Sankta technician who volunteered for the mission.

blocks.cap: In the middle of the wasteland, a paradise untouched by anyone for sixty years reveals itself.

blocks.x: Raymund, the young Sarkaz guarding the front gate, is openly hostile from the start. Bishop Stefano and hunter Gerald, whom they finally meet, say that Sankta and Sarkaz have lived together in this barren land for over ten years.
We simply took in people who had nowhere else to go.

The monastery is also home to Sankta girls Fortuna and Delfina, and Clement, the gentle gardener who tends the flowers with care. Each of them drifted in with their own circumstances, and somewhere along the way became like a family.
But the single condition Laterano has set for taking this monastery back is that paradise must have no Sarkaz.
3. The Executor's interrogation, and hunger in the darkness
Federico senses something off about Gerald's movements — too refined for a mere hunter — and the weapon hidden behind his back. But for now, finding the missing envoys Lemuen and Oren takes priority.

Meanwhile, somewhere beneath the monastery, something unidentified groans low in the dark. Aulus, an Iberian missionary who came calling it 'kin,' offers strange comfort to the Sarkaz collapsing under hunger and Oripathy.
Still can't hear my voice, can you?
The other envoy, Oren, has vanished entirely without a trace. Serfilia is secretly in contact with Oren, plotting something behind Federico's back.
And in a corner of the chapel is the missing envoy Lemuen — relying on a wheelchair after injuring her leg, having chosen on her own to stay behind.
4. A single shot, and black horns
Childhood friends Delfina and Fortuna bickered daily, yet cherished each other. But once they learn Laterano intends to abandon the Sarkaz, an unbridgeable rift opens between them.

Fortuna wanted to believe everyone could someday go to Laterano together, while Delfina saw that belief as naive betrayal. In the argument, Delfina tries to take Fortuna's guardian gun away, and Fortuna clings to it, refusing to let go.

blocks.cap: A single gunshot. The gun falls to the chapel floor, and black horns sprout from her forehead.

blocks.x: Am I unworthy as a Sankta? But I'm more worried about you and Raymund. This house is all I have, and these people here are my only family.
The trigger is pulled unintentionally. Delfina dies on the spot, and Fortuna becomes a being who can no longer empathize with her own kind — a Fallen Angel.
Fina, Fina died because of me! Whether it was an accident or not, what does that change?!
5. The burning cathedral, and a suspected child

That night, an unexplained fire breaks out in the cathedral. As the statues crumble and the flower beds turn to ash, everyone's eyes naturally turn to Raymund the Sarkaz — for no reason other than that he happened to be nearby at the time.

blocks.cap: The chapel ablaze. Even years spent living together are powerless before the flames.

blocks.x: Federico suspects deliberate arson, saying the fire spread at an unnatural speed — yet no trace of the culprit can be found anywhere.
Race cannot serve as evidence to escape guilt. If it were necessary to destroy this cathedral, I wouldn't hesitate to do so myself.
This single remark unexpectedly clears the suspicion hanging over Raymund. But the true arsonist has been quietly watching them all along, from somewhere no one has noticed.
6. The hunter hiding his name, and the tender monster
Federico finally uncovers Gerald's true identity: Horst Tippindahl, the notorious deserter mercenary from the Kazdel civil war. Gerald admits it without resistance, unshaken in the least.
Does it matter if it's not my real name, after ten years of being called it?

Meanwhile, the fate of the missing Sarkaz hunter Hyman also comes to light. The being howling beneath the ground was Hyman himself. Secretly raising two orphaned siblings, Estara and Erendel, he's been sharing food with them every night even as he loses his human shape.

That night too, Hyman, now a monster, visits the children's side without fail. Pulling the blanket over them with a tail that carries no warmth, he holds on to just their names with a voice nearly forgotten.
E...ren... tara...
When Federico levels his gun, Gerald steps in front of him.
Look behind Hyman. Two children are sleeping past that door. They trust you.
7. Brother, I can't run anymore

Gerald once had a comrade, Eileen, from whom he parted long ago. Since the day they both turned their backs on Kazdel's two queens, they'd held on to the belief that even a Sarkaz could live an ordinary life.
If you really put down your weapon and became a country hunter, have you ever imagined what you'd look like?
Eileen closed her eyes at this very monastery long ago. Ever since that day, Gerald has feared even the sound of bells, yet every year, when the flowers bloomed, he went to see her.
Word arrives that Laterano's special forces have surrounded the monastery. Gerald leaves a final word for his old friend Clement and picks up a dagger.
"If my throat can be traded for other people's lives, then it's worth something." He takes his own life, entrusting Raymund, Fortuna, and the remaining Sarkaz to Clement's care.

blocks.cap: Clement stands alone in the moonlight. In his hands are a dagger, and something wrapped in cloth.

blocks.x: This... is the head that 'sinner' Horst Tippindahl offers up of his own will.
Clement delivers Gerald's final request to Federico — to spare Fortuna, Raymund, and the remaining Sarkaz. And the next morning, he goes out to tend the flowers again as if nothing had happened.
8. What the gardener hid

Bishop Stefano finally reaches his last resolve. He decides to secretly mix the flesh left by the Deep Sea Church into the bread, and at the final Mass, turn everyone into something that can never be undone. But at the last moment, he pulls back, and locks the bread away in the underground storeroom.
The problem is that the storeroom was already empty. Someone had gotten there first.
Lemuen and Serfilia belatedly turn up a clue: the day Fortuna first entered the chapel, there was clearly one more person present.

blocks.cap: Arturia asks with a smile. "Given your rational, logical judgment, I thought you'd have handled the higher-priority matter first."

blocks.x: The place Federico tracks it to is the burned cathedral. There stands the gentle gardener, Clement.

blocks.cap: A single flower survived alone beneath the fallen statue. But this wasn't what Clement had come looking for.

blocks.x: Clement, who had long wanted to believe in people's kindness, finally broke down watching Raymund be suspected and Gerald's death. It was he who set fire to the cathedral and stole the sacrament.
I simply want an answer. Whether there truly exists a being in this world who can save us.
Clement swallows the bread in front of Federico. Nothing happens. Only the blood loss from an old gunshot wound slowly tightens around his breath.

blocks.cap: The gardener collapses before the fallen statue. Murmuring that neither god nor paradise ever truly existed, he closes his eyes.

blocks.x: People of Laterano... our paradise is a lie, and so is your faith.
9. A war stopped by a single gunshot
Meanwhile, universal messenger Oren mobilizes fifty of Laterano's special forces to seize the monastery, under the pretext of rooting out the Sarkaz entirely.
Oren has a logic of his own. If word spreads that Laterano colluded with the Sarkaz, the goodwill it has painstakingly built with other nations could collapse entirely — or so his reasoning goes.
Stefano holds a final Mass with those who remain. This time, truly, with bread and wine free of anything wrong.

blocks.cap: "For these past thousands of days, we have lived together." The old bishop's voice quietly fills the chapel.

blocks.x: Outside, Lemuen levels her gun at Oren, and Oren refuses to back down. Just as the standoff between them is about to explode, a single gunshot cutting through the sky brings everyone to a stop.

blocks.cap: Federico's warning shot. For the first time, he acts on his own judgment, with no one's order behind him.

blocks.x: I have no need to understand your positions, and even less need to mediate this conflict. But this monastery needs order right now. I will be the one to maintain it.
Before spring comes
Raymund declines to inherit Gerald's dagger, saying he isn't ready yet. Instead, he leaves it with Federico for the time being, promising to come back for it someday.
Fortuna decides in the end to leave with the Sarkaz band, following Raymund. Serfilia lets her go, but extracts a promise in return — to keep in touch, wherever she ends up.
Estara and Erendel never find their mother. They never learn that the 'sleeping man' who tucked a blanket over them each night was in fact their mother, no longer in human shape.
Before leaving, Lemuen hands Stefano an old monk's notebook — a record filled with the worries of someone who once lived here long ago.
Stefano steps down from his post as bishop and sets off alone toward Laterano, saying he wants to see with his own eyes whether he can reclaim his lost faith.
Let's meet again in that paradise.
Seeing him off at the door, Lemuen has a sudden premonition — that this stooped back might be the last she ever sees of him.
Reporting the mission to the Pope, Federico asks himself a question for the first time in his life. He made no mistakes — so why didn't things reach the best possible outcome?
Can our law apply to every situation? And if it can't, on what basis am I supposed to act from now on?
If paradise really is the only one, where are those pushed outside it supposed to go? Federico still hasn't found the answer. But now, at least, he's learned how to hold on to the question.

act25side.tagline: The secret weapon the military mistook for a superweapon was actually Rhine Lab's rocket punching through the sky toward the stars. A story about a genius who inherited her parents' dream and burned her own life to become the 'eye' gazing at the universe on everyone's behalf.

chars.name: Christine
chars.desc: Director-General of Rhine Lab. Daughter of the Wright couple, inventors who lost their lives challenging the sky.

chars.name: Saria
chars.desc: Former Head of the Defense Department at Rhine Lab. Christine's longtime friend, and a wyvern who upholds reason and order.

chars.name: Muelsyse
chars.desc: Head of the Ecology Department at Rhine Lab. One of the few remaining elves who can control water.

chars.name: Silence
chars.desc: A researcher and doctor at Rhine Lab. A serious, diligent scientist. · Operator Silence the Paradigmatic

chars.name: Ho'olheyak
chars.desc: A special agent of the Mayländer Foundation. A Revenant chasing the bloodline and power of the ancient Kukulkan.

chars.name: Ifrit
chars.desc: A Sabra girl of Rhodes Island. She looks up to Silence and Saria like family.

chars.name: Rosmontis
chars.desc: An Elite Feline Operator of Rhodes Island. She lets go of what she wants to forget, and records what she wants to keep.

chars.name: Ferdinand
chars.desc: Former Head of the Energy Department at Rhine Lab. Jealous of Christine, and a self-proclaimed foremost high-energy physicist.

chars.name: Yara
chars.desc: Head of the Human Resources Investigation Department at Rhine Lab. Once a celebrated actress of the silver screen.

chars.name: Nasti
chars.desc: Head of the Engineering Department at Rhine Lab. A Sarkaz banshee who works with rebar.

chars.name: Justin
chars.desc: Head of the Business Department at Rhine Lab. A man who uses money and connections to help the 'success of madmen.'

chars.name: Loken
chars.desc: Prisoner No. 30 of the Special Zone's mobile prison. A fallen scientist buried by academia.

chars.name: Blake
chars.desc: A colonel of Columbia's Department of Defense. He tries to make up for his failure in Bolívar with 'Project Ark I.'

terms.name: Starshell
terms.desc: An invisible barrier blocking the sky of Terra at roughly 6,000 meters. Humanity has never once gone beyond it.

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: Columbia's foremost science and technology company, based in the science city of Trimounts. Founded by Christine and Saria.

terms.name: Project Ark I
terms.desc: Christine's project, believed by the military to be a superweapon that could change the continent's balance of power. Its official name is 'Project Horizon Ark.'

terms.name: Trimounts
terms.desc: A science city of Columbia perpetually shrouded in dark clouds. Home to Rhine Lab's headquarters.

blocks.x: There is an invisible ceiling over the sky of Terra. At roughly 6,000 meters altitude, a barrier called the **Starshell** confines humanity to this narrow strip of land.
Everyone who ever tried to go beyond it failed — like the Wright couple, who lost their lives during a flight experiment. The world mocked them as madmen who'd burned tax money for nothing.
But one night, a massive ring rose into the sky over Trimounts. The military called it a superweapon. Some called it a catastrophe.
But it was, in truth, the answer a daughter had spent twenty years preparing — inheriting her parents' dream.

blocks.cap: "A thousand, ten thousand years from now, when humanity walks among the stars, people will surely sing her name."

blocks.x: 1. A star fell from the sky
The story begins when a chemical plant in Trimounts' District 13 explodes entirely.
The cause wasn't an accident — it was an object that fell from the sky. A massive ring-shaped machine: the test flight vehicle of '**Project Ark I**.'

Rhine Lab's Director-General **Christine** had vanished two weeks earlier with the machine in tow. Colonel **Blake**'s military believes it to be a superweapon that could shake the continent, and pursues Christine even at the risk of assassinating the Vice President.

Into the middle of this chaos step Rhodes Island's Doctor, Kal'tsit, Ifrit, and Rosmontis. Christine's longtime friend **Saria** and Rhine Lab researcher **Silence** also begin to move, each for their own reasons.

The military's assassination attempt on the Vice President is blocked by Ifrit, who throws her own body in the way. She recalls the old tale that if a star falls from the sky, everyone's wish comes true. That star was truly about to fall.
2. A company of madmen
**Rhine Lab** looks, on the surface, like Columbia's foremost science and technology company. But look inside, and it's a place festering with everyone's private ambitions.
**Ferdinand**, the jealousy-eaten former Head of Energy, becomes the military's dog to bring Christine down. Head of Rescue Parvis clings to forbidden experiments to transcend his own aging decline.

Banshee **Nasti** searches the sky for 'permitted land' for the Sarkaz, while Business Head **Justin** tries to grasp other people's fates with money and connections. Each of them believes the direction they're looking is 'progress' itself.

And then there's Human Resources Investigation Head **Yara**. Once an actress who lit up the silver screen, she alone sees Christine not as a Director-General, but as a stubborn child — out of guilt over that accident twenty years ago.
I've never once been a mother. But I do have one child.

What's more frightening is the state itself. Vice President Jackson, and the President watching over him, already know everything and are simply stalling for time. While **Blake**'s military struggles just to catch Christine, the politicians see Rhine Lab's downfall as nothing more than an acceptable loss.
3. Silence, the earnest scientist
Within this company of madness, the most ordinary researcher, **Silence**, quietly begins to waver.

Chasing the scene of the accident, she runs into her mentor Parvis. He had been using 'transmitters' to create artificial consciousness, trying to forge a 'superhuman' that would transcend his own ordinariness.
Silence stands directly against her mentor's ambition. She knows all too well how many innocent people have been used up as expendable tools in the name of progress.
Science isn't a god. Science is just a road that people walk on. ...Science looks at everyone in earnest.
But Parvis dissolves his own consciousness into the transmitter, turning himself into raw material for the experiment. Unable to stop him, Silence collapses.

blocks.cap: "You're the strongest person I've ever seen on this earth!" Ifrit grabs the collapsed Silence by the collar.

blocks.x: That's when Ifrit grabs her by the collar. Why are you trying to carry it all alone, she asks — we're right here with you. With that one line, Silence gets back on her feet.
4. Narcissa's judgment
