Meanwhile, a letter arrives for **Rosmontis**. Prisoner No. 30, who should be locked away in prison, **Loken**, has summoned her 'home.'
Loken was Rosmontis's lost past — the very man who called her 'Narcissa' and put her on the operating table, and also the gentle old man who visited every week with flowers.

blocks.cap: As he lies dying, Loken opens a 'trial' for himself and asks Narcissa to become the judge.

blocks.x: Facing death, Loken opens a trial for himself, hoping Narcissa will resent him and kill him out of that fury. He doesn't want to die alone.
Rosmontis, on the verge of running out of control, is held back by Ifrit — only we ourselves can control who we are. Rosmontis neither forgives Loken, nor becomes his weapon.
I was simply waiting for you to die. ...Now, I've forgotten you.
5. A drop's dream
**Muelsyse**, the Ecology Head who has been helping the Doctor, carries her own sorrow.
She is an elf uniquely vulnerable to Originium — a race that can't survive a month once Oripathy takes hold. Her parents, her village, had already become a graveyard.

So Muelsyse grew her own private garden in Rhine Lab's ecology dome. And Christine made her a promise: if this land isn't your place, maybe there's somewhere beyond the stars where your people can live. So let's leave together.
Dancing with the Doctor and pouring out her past, Muelsyse ultimately sides with Christine — pushing the Doctor out the door, for the sake of her own dream.
I walked such a long road and finally found a small forest... but I'm not a leaf. I'm just a drop of water.
6. The woman from yesterday
**Ho'olheyak**, who betrayed the Mayländer Foundation to help Christine, is Christine's exact mirror opposite.

Where Christine risked her life to see the future, Ho'olheyak inherited centuries of memory chasing only the past — the revival of the ancient Kukulkan bloodline. If she could touch that old glory within her short lifespan, her life meant nothing to her.
Tin Man, leader of the Mayländer, stands in her way. To Ho'olheyak, who inherited 300, 500 years of memory, this Revenant shows what the weight of ten thousand years truly means.

blocks.cap: With bare fists and not a single Originium Art, Saria cuts through the warped air and brings Ho'olheyak down.

blocks.x: In the end, what Ho'olheyak encounters is the final revelation left by Christine's 'god' — a vast moon, and a tiny Terra floating in the universe. Before that, even her own bloodline and obsession were mere dust.

Having had everything she believed in denied, she realizes for the first time that she wants to fight for 'herself,' not her bloodline. Carrying that obsessive hunger for the future, she sets foot in Rhodes Island.
7. What sleeps beneath the sky
The Doctor and Kal'tsit chase Christine's energy source deep beneath the wasteland, and there they encounter the 'Preserver.'

It was a consciousness that, tens of thousands of years ago, froze hundreds of thousands of its kin to escape some ruin, remaining alone and awake ever since. The Doctor's lost kin were sleeping inside those coffins.

The Preserver challenges the Doctor to a 'debate.' Within that technique of mixing thoughts, the Doctor confronts himself. Who were you before you lost your memory? Do you have the resolve to reclaim the truth? The voices of Amiya and Theresa shake him.

The being Christine called 'god' was this very Preserver. It decides to hand the last blood of its civilization over to Christine — better to offer it to a new heart than to keep guarding a dying lamp.

The Preserver also lets slip a clue tying the Doctor's past to Originium — his memory loss was never mere chance. Still, it says, that answer must be faced by the Doctor himself. And in its final dying moment, it asks to be erased simply as a single human being named 'Trevor Preston.'

Kal'tsit says this land isn't ready to look at the stars yet — that she fears Christine's spark could become the next horn of conquest. Even so, she answers, she is now a member of Rhodes Island, and a person of Terra.
8. Stellaria
'Project Ark I' never actually existed. It was a bundle of scrap paper to fool the military. This flight vehicle's true name is **Stellaria** — an ark bound for the stars.

blocks.cap: The Focus Generator, once hidden in a junkyard, tears through Trimounts' sky and rises toward the stars.

blocks.x: Ferdinand clings to his instrument panel, mocking even the military colonel, determined only to record the truth of this moment. Amid people each lost in their own madness, Stellaria keeps rising.

blocks.cap: Toward Blake, clinging to power, Ferdinand only sneers, his eyes fixed solely on science.

blocks.x: At its heart, **Saria** finally catches up to Christine. In a planetarium where artificial stars pour down like a galaxy, the two old friends stand face to face.

blocks.cap: The heart of Stellaria, where artificial stars orbit. Two friends face each other again after twenty years.

blocks.x: 9. A star that goes alone

Muelsyse first tries to stop Saria to protect Christine, but Christine pushes even Muelsyse — along with her own water — outside. It's a very Christine kind of tenderness, cherishing her fragile friend's true body.
Saria hasn't come to stop Christine. She's come to bring her down. Instead of forcing her way through, she walks step by step through the storm and takes Christine's hand.
A true pioneer should open the way for all of humanity — not be the first to leap into an abyss with no return.
But in the final moment, Christine drops Saria alone through an escape route prepared just for her. Stellaria had no return program at all. From the very start, Christine never intended to come back.
Christine says she was never the pioneer leading everyone forward. She says she's simply an eye — a pair of eyes gazing at a sky no one has ever seen, on behalf of everyone on this land.
A scientist never believes a dream is a lie. Because I'm someone who proves to all of humanity that every dream can come true.
At last, the false sky tears apart completely, and two ancient moons reveal their true form. That night, humanity glimpses the truth for the very first time.
Epilogue — Those left behind
Saria, having fallen from thousands of meters up, barely survives thanks to the water wall Muelsyse pours out with everything she has, and Silence's own hands.

blocks.cap: Beneath a sky finally clear of dark clouds, Silence props up a battered Saria. "The sky really is beautiful."

blocks.x: Silence decides to become a proposer of the 'Trimounts Scientific Ethics Joint Declaration.' Not a genius like Christine, she says — she'll be a buffer and a switch keeping science from running out of control. A bridge connecting dream and reality.

Muelsyse joins hands with Nasti and dreams of a flying city, while Yara, reaching retirement, sets off on a long journey in search of wine. Rosmontis passes judgment on Loken, leaves a single line in her notebook, and lets him go.

Shadows linger deep, too. Ho'olheyak rescues the Preserver's last consciousness from the abyss and flees, while Columbia's President Mark Max, the figure lurking behind the shadows all along, finally reveals himself — his ambition as tyrant, to unite every will into one, seize every truth, and forge Columbia into the sword and shield of Terra.

What he's after is the 'Black Crown' the Doctor's group carried out with them. Kal'tsit answers him like a nail driven home — Preston never once expected a tyrant. Meanwhile, in prison, Ferdinand writes down his own theories until his dying day, believing scholars a hundred years from now will honor his name.

Beyond the Barrier, alone in her life-support capsule, Christine leaves her final record. Only once we understand where we stand in the universe, she writes, do we earn the right to decide where to go.

blocks.cap: Beyond the stars, surrounded by surviving plants — Christine has become a pair of eyes gazing at the universe on behalf of everyone.

blocks.x: Goodnight, everyone on Terra. Goodnight, universe.

The old tale said that if a star fell from the sky, humanity's conflicts would vanish and everyone's wishes would come true. But Christine didn't become a falling star — she became a star that climbed into the sky on her own.

On a clear night, set up a telescope somewhere with a wide view. A single star will twinkle just for you. It's not a star that grants wishes — it's a pair of eyes, gazing alone at the sky on behalf of everyone on this land.
A thousand, ten thousand years from now, if the day comes when humanity walks among the stars — people will surely sing her name.

act24side.tagline: Two Operators chase a fire-breathing 'King of the Skies' into the mountain villages of Higashi, and end up tangled with an old man who's spent seven years hunting nothing but his dead son. A story about burning away a past piled up like fallen leaves, and learning how to survive.

chars.name: Yato
chars.desc: Squad leader of Rhodes Island's A4 Action Team. A former assassin — cold, and always puts the mission first · this event's 6★ Operator Kirin R Yato

chars.name: Noir Corne
chars.desc: Yato's longtime partner and shield-bearer. Warm-hearted and looks out for everyone · this event's 5★ Operator Rathalos S Noir Corne

chars.name: Terra Research Commission
chars.desc: Bipedal cats called Palicoes who crossed over from the Monster Hunter world. This event's reward Operator, made up of the Scholar, Artisan, and Veteran Palicoes

chars.name: Yoshitaka Kashiwau
chars.desc: An old hunter of Roka Village. The only one in the village who still climbs the mountain

chars.name: Akira Kashiwau
chars.desc: Yoshitaka's son, and a young hunter of Roka Village

chars.name: Ataru Takii
chars.desc: The head of Roka Village. The man who lifted the village out of poverty

chars.name: Mirai Takii
chars.desc: The village head's niece. An ecologist who's returned from the city

chars.name: Kazuya
chars.desc: A village boy who idolizes hunters. He calls Yoshitaka 'Grandpa' and follows him everywhere

terms.name: Roka Village
terms.desc: A village at the foot of the mountains in Higashi. Once a hunting community, it's grown prosperous through its specialty tea, ironwork, and woodcraft

terms.name: Rathalos
terms.desc: The 'King of the Skies' from the Monster Hunter world — a massive fire-breathing wyvern the villagers simply call 'the Fire Dragon'

terms.name: Higashi
terms.desc: Terra's Far East. A region resembling Japan in our world, and the homeland of both Yato and Noir Corne

blocks.x: Terra's Far East, Higashi — a mountain hunting village somewhere, in the season of falling leaves. What happens here is **Arknights' first crossover with Monster Hunter**.
**Yato** and **Noir Corne** of Rhodes Island's A4 Action Team came up this mountain for an Oripathy survey. Twenty-three samples in, and nothing had turned up — just an ordinary job.
Then, beneath a pile of fallen leaves, they find a claw mark the size of a house. And from the sky, a shadow descends like a fireball scorching the earth.
1. The King of the Skies, and the Dancing Cats
The first thing to leap out beside the claw mark, unexpectedly, was a pair of talking cats. "The Fire Dragon's coming, nya!" they wave and dance. These creatures are **Palicoes**.

A bipedal cat race that fell into this world alongside the Fire Dragon, straight out of the Monster Hunter world. A self-proclaimed scholar of the Original Royal Paleontology Society, and a quiet, taciturn artisan.

Before the warning even finishes, the King of the Skies swoops down. Yato's blade bounces off its scales, and Noir Corne's shield gets scorched by the flames. Even accounting for how many times these two have fought back to back, this opponent was bad news.

blocks.cap: A forest of drifting embers, two backs pressed together against a threat from the sky. And the mission had only just begun.

blocks.x: Worse still, the Fire Dragon's claws carried poison. Yato collapses, and the Palicoes scramble to give first aid.
The two cats dash off toward the village for an antidote. "You dumb, horned hunter! Sit tight and wait for us, nya!"
2. The Hunting Village With No Hunters

The two who came down to the village were greeted by a boy named **Kazuya**. Seeing the injured Yato, he leads the way, saying, "Let's go to Grandpa's house" — to the hunter grandfather who lives at the village's south end.

That's how they meet the old hunter, **Yoshitaka Kashiwau** — a gruff old man who silently treats the wound of a total stranger. But the moment he hears the two are chasing the Fire Dragon, his expression changes completely.
"That's my quarry."
The old man throws the two out, warning them not to touch his prey. Why is he so furious? Noir Corne can't shake the feeling something's wrong, but Yato brushes it off as none of their concern.

blocks.cap: A village where red maple leaves fall. In the middle of the square, a hunter's statue stands alone, gazing off into the distance.

blocks.x: What's strange is that a hunting village has no hunters left. The hunter's statue in the square sits weathered and neglected, and every villager treats these strangers with wary eyes.
3. The Butterfly You Saw in Autumn Was Only a Fallen Leaf
Village head **Ataru Takii** treats the pair lavishly, yet flatly refuses their advice to evacuate — all while going on about the specialty chilled-grass tea, and about Originium.

Just a few years back, they say, Roka Village barely scraped by on hunting alone. Now, roofs have been replaced with proper wood paneling, streetlamps line the roads, and children can bathe in warm water every day. No one, he says, needs to go into the mountains anymore.

"If you saw a butterfly flying in autumn, it was really just a fallen leaf." The village head gently brushes off Yato's sighting as nothing but that kind of illusion — as if to say what he's been chasing might just be a leaf too.

Meanwhile, a child being treated is rushed in coughing blood. Ever since his father went into 'the cave,' stones had begun growing all over his body — and that father was hauled off by the village head. It's clear the village is desperately hiding something.

Digging for that something, the two are lured into a cave in the back mountain — a trap using a hunk of meat as bait, meant to draw in and finish off the Fire Dragon. They barely escape with explosives, but the cave collapses and seals shut behind them.
4. The Forest Itself Is a Monster

Back into the forest, chasing the Fire Dragon's trail. But this forest isn't normal. Herds of horned beasts rampage for no reason (someone had scattered luring powder), and a strange force that steals your sense of direction traps them wandering the same spot over and over.

A stinky little incident along the way gives the heavy mood some breathing room. The artisan-nya pokes at a rain-bird's eggs, and Noir Corne ends up buried under a bombardment of bird droppings. After that, no one wants to go near him.

blocks.cap: The carpet bombing of the rain-bird flock. From that day on, 'never go near a rain-bird nest marker in the forest' becomes Noir Corne's personal motto.

blocks.x: At an abandoned campsite, the pair find an old group photo of hunters, and a broken hunting spear. The name carved into the spear is **Akira Kashiwau** — a young hunter from the nine-year-old photo, sharing Yoshitaka's family name.

Noir Corne can't stop worrying about the old man. Someone shouldering everything alone, stubbornly running from something — it reminds him of someone. "Don't waste your energy on nonsense," Yato snaps at his partner, but there's something guilty in that scolding too.
5. Is This Outfit Really Armor?
The artisan-nya whips up new gear from Fire Dragon materials — the iconic **Kirin Armor** of Monster Hunter fame. When Yato changes into it, Noir Corne is left utterly speechless.

blocks.cap: "Light, agile, and powerful." Recalling this gear afterward, Yato would always marvel at it this way. "That's not what we were amazed by!" his comrades would always answer.

blocks.x: Right on cue, a seasoned **Veteran Palico** joins them. An old warhorse of a cat with polished manners, except for a 'nya' habit he just can't shake. This old cat teaches Yato how to wield dual blades and the great sword, and the hunt slowly starts to take shape.
6. That's My Quarry

Night at the abandoned lakeside camp. Yoshitaka is talking with the vision of his dead son, **Akira**. Seven years ago, his son left only the words "I'm this village's hunter" before charging into a herd of beasts — and when he came back, he was unrecognizable if not for his clothes and gear.

blocks.cap: By a dying campfire, the old man is still trapped in that night, listening to his dead son's voice.

blocks.x: To Yoshitaka, the Fire Dragon is the chief of every beast that took his son. It's an obsession — to hunt it down alone, to the death, out of revenge.
Yato walks step by step toward the old man aiming his spear, and says: "You can't kill me. You don't have the nerve."

And then Yato brings up his own story. Before coming to Rhodes, he was an assassin who killed his own kind on command. One day, he let a mother and daughter go — but simply not killing them wasn't enough to protect anyone, and another assassin who followed after him killed them instead.
The one who pulled him back up then was the comrade at his side, Noir Corne.
The past is the past. Every regret you have, every sin — pay it all back with whatever future you have left.

What Yato is trying to tell Yoshitaka comes down to the same thing. An obsession trapped in the past can't undo anything — it can only keep you frozen where you stand. The man he's talking to now isn't a hunter. Not anymore.
7. The Mine, and the Massacre Seven Years Ago
Meanwhile, Noir Corne and the scholar-nya, chasing the Fire Dragon deep into the mountain, uncover the truth in the depths of a cave. Beneath it all lay a massive **Originium mine**.

Here, the scholar-nya and Noir Corne share a conversation: Originium is dangerous, yet it's the very power holding up this world's civilization. Just like the monsters of the Monster Hunter world, Originium is both a threat to survival and an opportunity. If it's the only branch drifting your way before a Catastrophe, you have to grab it, thorns and all.

They also meet the village head's niece, **Mirai Takii**, who'd infiltrated the mine. An ecologist returned from the city, she knew excessive mining would invite disaster and had come to blow the mine up. She's also the one who tipped the two off, drawing them to this village in the first place.

And the truth of seven years ago comes to light. This vein was originally home to a burrowing beast called the **Melynx** — Village Head Ataru wiped out their nest to mine Originium, and the beasts, driven mad after losing their home, caused that year's stampede — the very incident that took Akira's life.
But then an accident sets off the Fire Dragon, and every last bit of Originium in the mine activates at once. What that means, the sky answers first.

blocks.cap: A blast of fire swallows the sky. Vegetation turns to ash, the mountain crumbles — this land's punishment, a Catastrophe, has arrived.

blocks.x: 8. We're Hunters Too

The Catastrophe and the beast stampede hit the village at the same time. **Hiroshi Rito**, who'd been embezzling from the village treasury, breaks the defense net and unleashes the beasts to sow chaos — planning to pin all the blame on the village head in the confusion.

Before the villagers, sunk in despair, Mirai steps forward. Holding up a single sprig of chilled grass, she says there's hope for tomorrow even without Originium, even without relying on hunting. First, let's just survive.

As it turns out, Village Head Ataru wasn't entirely a bad man either. Knowing the mine would eventually bring disaster, he'd secretly prepared a shelter, and even now his first concern is the villagers' lives. He reveals that he was once the leader of the old hunters, picks up a hunting spear, and stands before the beast horde.
"Weren't the hunters supposed to be long gone?" a frightened child asks. An adult answers: "No. The hunters were always here. When danger comes, they're the ones who step forward."
One by one, the village men take up spears and fall in behind the village head.
9. Give the Past Back to the Past

Around that time, Yoshitaka is alone, setting his own house on fire. Let the dead stay dead, he thinks — let old regrets burn clean down to a handful of ash. The past, piled up like fallen leaves, burned away by fire.

blocks.cap: With his burning house at his back, the old man grips his son's spear once more, resolved to give the past back to the past.

blocks.x: Then he lures the Fire Dragon out alone to face it. He wounds its head with his son's spear, but his aging body soon gives out.
Young Kazuya steps in front of him, bow drawn. "I'm this village's hunter too! Stay away from Grandpa!"
After saving the boy, it's Yato who takes up the fallen old man's spear — planting it in the ground, standing tall, back straight.

blocks.cap: "Thank you for the time you bought us. Now — leave the rest to us, and rest easy."

blocks.x: Only then does Yoshitaka fully understand his son Akira's final moments, trapped by that beast stampede.

And Village Head Ataru confesses the truth of that day: Akira could have run, but instead smeared himself with luring powder on purpose, drawing the beasts to himself to protect the village and the mine — along with one last message meant for his father.
From now on — lead everyone, and survive.
10. Showdown! The Burning Hunter's Spirit!

Now for the final hunt. Noir Corne puts everything into one blow with his great sword, severing the Fire Dragon's tail and bringing it down, while a flash bomb the artisan-nya whipped up on the spot (made, apparently, from Terran Originium bugs) drags the King down to the ground.

blocks.cap: Demon Mode. The dual-blade secret art of abandoning defense entirely and closing in like a beast — Yato dances through the air, driving the Fire Dragon back.

blocks.x: Yato's dual blades, in Demon Mode, dance through the air for the finishing blow, and the Veteran Palico's rope binds the King fast. The moment they finally subdue the King of the Skies —
It turns out the Fire Dragon was never infected with Oripathy. It was just an old, starving creature, covered head to toe in Originium dust.
The Originium crystals grown on its body were, if anything, traces of its resistance against Originium — and it's revealed to no longer be any kind of threat.

Yato lets the King go. Before a Catastrophe, he says, both people and beasts are equally small — this sky was never ours alone to begin with. Every life on this land is only ever struggling to survive its one thread of life, sometimes gloriously, sometimes not.
Epilogue — Morning Light
Half a month later. The village was rising again, past the Catastrophe.

The 'chilled grass that grows anywhere,' which Mirai had used to sway the villagers, turns out to have been a lie — but now she proposes a real tomorrow to the village head. Not going back to barbarism, turning away from Originium, but respecting it more, handling it properly, and living safely alongside it.
The Palicoes stay behind at Rhodes under the official designation 'Terra Research Commission.' Their captain is the Veteran Palico, though the scholar-nya still insists that he's the one in charge.
And once the mission is done, Yato and Noir Corne secretly return to those ruins on their way back. Yato had come here for one reason alone — to watch the Fire Dragon's fall.

blocks.cap: Ruins at sunset, the Fire Dragon rising into the sky. A flight hanging by a single thread, destined one day to snap and fall to the earth.

blocks.x: The Fire Dragon grows distant against a sky full of drifting ash. Noir Corne quietly reaches out a hand, using the ash on his shoulder as an excuse, and Yato smiles faintly at the sight. The two leave the place without a word.
By the end of the story, the hunter never truly beat the monster. But every villager walked away alive, and the wounded monster, having lost its purpose, returned alone to the forest.

Before it left, the monster seemed to leave behind these words: "I'll come back again, someday." Long years from now, where the old hunter once stood, a young hunter will stand, and a newborn monster will come face it, eye to eye.
Because fallen leaves scatter, and grow anew. **Survive.** From beginning to end, that was the one thing this land had been repeating, all along.

act23side.tagline: The legendary 'Grandmaster' who guarded Yumen for a hundred years turns out to be a fragment of a Catastrophe-class beast. A disciple seeking revenge, a father who abandoned his daughter, and an old man who once threw the city to a Catastrophe out of an old grudge — around a single sword, they all learn to let their grudges go.

chars.name: Chongyue
chars.desc: The legendary martial arts grandmaster who has guarded Yumen for nearly a hundred years. He's about to retire and leave the city.

chars.name: Wei Yanwu
chars.desc: Governor of Lungmen and an old friend of the Grandmaster. He's come all the way to Yumen just to see his old friend off.

chars.name: Lin Yusha
chars.desc: An envoy dispatched from Lungmen, and the Rat King's daughter. She's secretly investigating the dangerous forces hiding in Yumen · Operator Lin

chars.name: Waai Fu
chars.desc: An employee of Lee's Detective Agency and a recent university graduate. She's skilled enough to hold her own on Yumen's training grounds.

chars.name: Jieyun
chars.desc: A girl of the nomadic Anasa tribe. At her late master's last request, she smuggled herself into Yumen carrying a single sword.

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: Son of Yumen's general and a young agent of the Sishi Bureau. He's tasked with tracking down the stolen sword.

chars.name: Qiubai
chars.desc: The Grandmaster's only disciple. She's stayed at his side learning the sword for five years.

chars.name: Lee
chars.desc: A private detective from Lungmen. He's come to Yumen searching for a sworn brother who's been out of contact for years.

chars.name: Du Yaoya
chars.desc: A rookie boss from Shangshu who's just opened a courier company, 'Xingyou Logistics,' in Yumen. Fearless and always on the move.

chars.name: Waai Tinfu
chars.desc: A hulking, mysterious man working out of a clinic in Yumen. He trusts his own fists more than any weapon.

terms.name: Yumen
terms.desc: A nomadic fortress-city of Yan that sails the desert, guarding the northern frontier. Defenses like the Screenwall hold off both Catastrophes and outside enemies.

terms.name: the Screenwall
terms.desc: Four mobile outer ramparts that encircle Yumen. They stay folded away in normal times, rising only when a Catastrophe-class sandstorm bears down.

terms.name: Sanhaizhong
terms.desc: A fanatical cult that worships the 'Behemoth,' a Catastrophe-class beast that vanished a thousand years ago. It struck Yumen once before, twenty years ago, inflicting heavy damage.

terms.name: Anasa
terms.desc: A nomadic tribe with no fixed settlement, wandering the wasteland. Jieyun comes from this tribe.

terms.name: the Sishi Bureau
terms.desc: A special bureau the court established to handle Behemoth-related cases exclusively. Zuo Le is one of its agents.

terms.name: the Beacon-Watch Festival
terms.desc: A traditional three-day rite held every spring in Yumen. Drums are struck once for every disaster the city weathered that year, so none are forgotten.

terms.name: the Sand Mill
terms.desc: A massive Originium turbine mounted on Yumen's outer wall. It clears the sand from the city's path — and when it slows, there's just enough of a gap for someone to sneak in or out.

blocks.x: Yumen is a mobile fortress-city that sails across the desert. For nearly a hundred years, it's been guarded by a legendary martial arts grandmaster — everyone just calls him 'the Grandmaster.' As this story begins, he happens to be on the verge of retirement.

As luck would have it, right at this moment Yumen docks with its old sister city, Lungmen, and all sorts of people come pouring in. The Lungmen governor come to see off an old friend, a private detective searching for his missing sworn brother, and a desert girl clutching a single sword as she runs for her life.

Then that night, with a coincidence too perfect to be chance, three incidents erupt at once: an assassination attempt on the governor, the theft of the Grandmaster's beloved sword, and the massacre of a patrol outside the walls. With a Catastrophe bearing down, Yumen has no idea yet that all three are actually one and the same thread.
1. The First Night of Docking, Three Disasters at Once

On the inn's TV plays 《Storms Over Yumen》, a drama based on a real incident from fifty years ago — a tale of wandering swordsmen and soldiers joining forces to repel outside invaders. Except half the people in that story are sitting right here in this very inn, watching themselves on screen.

On the training grounds, the Filaine girl **Waai Fu** is sparring with a stranger from another people. Her opponent is **Jieyun**, who's just scaled Yumen's walls for the first time today — a girl of the nomadic Anasa tribe. Waai Fu wins in the end, but Jieyun only asks about a sword before disappearing.

Meanwhile, General Zuo's son **Zuo Le** has come home after a long absence, yet keeps putting off seeing his father. Up on the watchtower, the Grandmaster **Chongyue** is reminiscing with his little sister **Ling**.

A young man dressed in Sargonian clothes, claiming to be sightseeing in Yumen, adds to the chaos. In halting Yan, he boasts, "That Grandmaster taught me martial arts back in Sargon!" — only to get scolded by the innkeeper for mixing up the words for 'inn' and 'tavern.'

blocks.cap: Yumen's night skyline, on the eve of deceleration. Above the siblings' old stories, three fuses are quietly burning down.

blocks.x: Night falls, and all three go off at once. An assassin trailing the scent of peach blossoms targets Lungmen governor **Wei Yanwu**; Jieyun steals Chongyue's sword and flees; and outside the walls, a Catastrophe-relay patrol is wiped out.

blocks.cap: "I said I'm going — who dares stop me?" In the moonlight, the assassin raises her blade.

blocks.x: 2. Zuo Le Gets Knocked Flat Before He Can Even Ask a Question

Having wounded the assassin badly, Zuo Le searches every clinic in Yumen for the culprit. At the last, run-down clinic he visits, a hulking man who won't even give his name turns him away at the door.

Zuo Le tries to force his way in with his martial arts, and gets knocked straight back out. The man, named **Waai Tinfu**, loudly declares he trusts nothing but his own fists — and Jieyun is already hiding inside the room.

But something about this man looks familiar. In fact, there's someone in this city who's been searching for him for over a decade. The story of a father who vanished, leaving his daughter behind, is something no one here knows yet.

It was this very giant of a man who'd secretly taken in and treated the wounded Jieyun. "Don't move. Just be still and let yourself heal." Gruff on the outside, he kept a straight face in front of Zuo Le right to the end.
3. The Detective and the Envoy, Chasing Different Shadows Through Yumen
The Lungmen private detective **Lee** has come to Yumen looking for a sworn brother from long ago. For days, all he's done is grab passing swordsmen and ask, "Have you seen a big, hulking man?"

At the same time, Lungmen envoy **Lin Yusha** is squeezing smugglers for information, chasing down the name 'Sanhaizhong.' It's supposedly a cult of bandits who worship the Behemoth — and they've targeted Yumen once before, twenty years ago.

In the marketplace, Zuo Le is ambushed by Sanhaizhong. Lee, passing by, steps in to help — and in the process, Zuo Le lets slip a crucial clue for the first time: "That big guy, he was a Filaine." Lee's expression goes still.

With that clue in hand, Lee finally tracks Waai Tinfu down, hiding in a clinic across from the inn. And by coincidence, an old friend also shows up there — **Yang Xian**, newly posted to Yumen as a magistrate.
It turns out Lee, Yang Xian, and Waai Tinfu swore brotherhood together over a decade ago. But their reunion over drinks is nothing but awkward.
"It's bad for my health." Waai Tinfu turns down even a drink, and Lee can only sigh. "Waai Tinfu, you idiot — same as any father, and yet somehow..."
4. The Rookie Courier Boss Picks Up an Armband

**Du Yaoya**, who came from Shangshu, has just opened 'Xingyou Logistics' in Yumen. Her very first job was escorting Catastrophe-relay couriers — and two of her own people were in that massacred patrol.
Du Yaoya and Waai Fu make the rounds of the victims' homes together and notice something strange: every single one looks untouched, as if nothing had happened.
Then an armband that Lin Yusha picked up from a blacksmith's storehouse points every suspicion in one direction — toward the elderly owner of the southside smithy, **Meng Zheyi**.
To Du Yaoya, Meng Zheyi is the only adult in Yumen she can truly rely on. But that night, as Du Yaoya and Zuo Le knock on the smithy's door, black-hooded figures burst out in front of them.

After the fighting, the person they find is Jieyun, wounded and hiding. The Grandmaster's disciple **Qiubai** arrives late and makes short work of the Sanhaizhong men — but Meng Zheyi, who should be at the center of all this, is nowhere to be found.
5. A Twenty-Year-Old Reckoning, Uncovered in the Storehouse

Lin Yusha comes face to face with Meng Zheyi in the storehouse. He hands over the real Catastrophe data without resistance and confesses everything: colluding with Sanhaizhong to fake the patrol's deaths, letting the Grandmaster's sword be stolen — it was all his own plan.

The reason goes back twenty years. After Sanhaizhong once infiltrated Yumen disguised as wandering swordsmen, General Zuo Xuanliao pushed every swordsman entirely out of Yumen's affairs. Meng Zheyi never forgot how those who'd bled for Yumen back then were cast aside.
"This whole affair started because of me, and me alone," Meng Zheyi says. But the one who comes for him isn't Sanhaizhong's boss — it's the true master who commands Sanhaizhong himself.
Xingyou Logistics was a fine idea... but even so, I couldn't just stand by and watch a fine young sprout get cut down right in front of me...

In front of Du Yaoya, who arrives too late, Meng Zheyi takes a blade meant for her back. Du Yaoya runs, clutching the data box to her chest, hoping that neither grudges nor apologies have come too late — not yet.
6. Father and Daughter, One Punch in the Middle of the Street

Sanhaizhong appears again, targeting Du Yaoya as she tries to protect the data. This time Waai Fu blocks the way — but her opponent's blade is strange, fast with no form at all, some bizarre kind of Originium Arts.
At the critical moment, a back like a wall steps in front of Waai Fu. It's her father, whom she hasn't seen in over a decade — **Waai Tinfu**.

blocks.cap: An autumn night...? In front of an opponent who commands unnatural weather, this reunion arrives without any warning at all.

blocks.x: Only after shielding his daughter does Waai Tinfu glance back. "Thanks for the save." "Nothing else to say?" "No need." After that short exchange, father and daughter trade a punch.

blocks.cap: A reunion over a decade in the making was sealed not with an embrace, but with a single punch.

blocks.x: "I lost," Waai Fu admits first. But both of them know that the kick she landed on her father's chin wasn't something her father ever taught her.
7. Forty Years of Martial Arts, Settled with a Single Punch

Meanwhile, the Grandmaster's disciple Qiubai keeps a promise she made to herself. As a child, she lost her father when her river village was raided — and she's stayed at the side of Chongyue, who led that band of raiders, learning the sword from him for five years.
On the day the Catastrophe arrives, Qiubai points her sword at Chongyue, knowing full well she can't win.

blocks.cap: Before the sword-aura even fades, she draws back her blade herself.

blocks.x: I struck at you, and this strike failed to kill you. That I couldn't kill you doesn't mean I've given up on revenge — it's only that 'grudge' is no longer the reason I draw my sword.

And then, another duel three years in the making. Waai Tinfu finally throws down his challenge to the Grandmaster, on the eve of the Catastrophe, under the pretext of a friendly bout to boost the soldiers' morale.

blocks.cap: Forty years of martial arts collide with a hundred years of swordsmanship. Victory and defeat are decided by a single exchange.

blocks.x: The Grandmaster wins. But Waai Tinfu, coughing up blood, still laughs. "Took me forty years just to beat one move of yours — give me three hundred sixty more, and I bet I'll win outright!" This man finds optimism in the strangest places.
8. The Catastrophe Descends, the Old Rat's Last Strength

The foretold Catastrophe finally bears down on Yumen. Instead of steering the city around it, General Zuo Xuanliao chooses to meet the Catastrophe head-on, right now, instead of waiting the usual two months.

blocks.cap: After the seventeenth drumbeat, the sky splits open.

blocks.x: One side of the gate is held single-handedly by the Grandmaster's sister, **Ling**. Cracking open a jar of liquor before a storm even hits is just her style. "Glad you came!" She takes on the Catastrophe swigging straight from the jar, no cup needed.

When the Astronomical Bureau casters guarding the core give ground, Lungmen's shadow, **the Rat King** (Lin Yusha's father), steps in himself. Everyone had taken him for just some old man who liked to take walks — but he shows the strength to push back an entire Originium storm.

blocks.cap: Like a hundred-year-old tree, the old rat stands his ground in the middle of the storm.

blocks.x: But the cost of such an effort is steep. As her father collapses, coughing blood, Lin Yusha throws herself in front of him.
Enough talk. Everyone's hurt, so catch your breath. Not much further now. Eyes forward!
9. What the Drawn Sword Revealed

In the middle of the Catastrophe, the unconscious Jieyun unknowingly draws the Grandmaster's sword. The moment she does, memories sealed within it come pouring out. Chongyue, it turns out, is one of twelve fragments of 'Sui,' a Catastrophe-class beast hunted down a thousand years ago, who split and sealed itself into pieces.

blocks.cap: The old sword reveals itself. This was the secret behind how he had separated himself from it.

blocks.x: Chongyue has siblings beyond just **Ling** — Nian and Dusk among them. All of them are fragments of Sui, scattered across the human world in different forms.

And behind all this commotion was yet another fragment, 'Ya.' Badly wounded in that hunt a thousand years ago, he'd vanished without a trace — and had briefly used Sanhaizhong to pry into Sui's whereabouts.
Chongyue meets Ya in a vision, the two of them standing on a small boat, each on their own end, atop a frozen river.

blocks.cap: Eight thousand springs, eight thousand autumns. A conversation between the same kind, who have hated each other the longest.

blocks.x: You sealed yourself in a sword and took on a human body, just to think of yourself as one of them — and in the end, what did you get out of it?
The Catastrophe isn't over yet. People don't even know you exist. Someone has to answer for Yumen's sake — and no matter how I look at it, there's no one left to do that but me.
The conversation ends without resolution. Ya is sent flying with a single punch, and vanishes, leaving only the words: leave Yumen.
Chongyue says nothing at all. He knows well enough that every living thing facing death wears more or less the same face.
Epilogue: Everyone Lays Down Their Own Grudge

After the Catastrophe passes, everyone finds their own path forward. The sword leaves the Grandmaster's hands and passes to Waai Tinfu. "You may as well keep it as a token for our next match." The man who took forty years to win a single exchange accepts the sword, already looking forward to another forty.
Jieyun leaves the sword at a grave, only to come back for it and return it to its true owner — dimly realizing that the sword wasn't the only thing her master had missed.

Lin Yusha decides to stay in Lungmen instead of studying abroad in Victoria. "Whatever burden someone else sets down, someone else has to pick up." Waai Fu keeps training, promising her father a rematch, while Qiubai sets off with her sword in hand, in search of a new goal.

Waai Fu offers to pay off the rest of her father's medical bills for Lee, but the doctor just waves her off. "I figure saving and caring for people beats fighting all day, doesn't it." In the end, Waai Fu stays on and helps with the cleaning.
Last of all, Wei Yanwu shows the Grandmaster one final sword technique — the true final form of the Crimson Night Sword Style, 'Heaven.'
The sword moves with the heart, and there's no regret in a single stroke. The moment you hesitate, even slightly, the blade loses its edge and turns against you instead.
Before the gate, the Grandmaster runs into a stranger playing Go alone. He was, in truth, another 'sibling,' come to say a final goodbye.

blocks.cap: The traveler playing Go alone turns out to have come as a guest, to bid farewell.

blocks.x: Thanks to your sword, I learned something. We'll meet again. Farewell, sibling.

Only then does General Zuo Xuanliao receive, from Lu Wuguan, the Grandmaster's final gift — a manual called 《The Manual of Martial Arts》, complete with breathing techniques for tempering the body through swordplay. By then, the Grandmaster had already left Yumen without a word of farewell, half a watch earlier.
For the first time in a hundred years, the Grandmaster leaves Yumen. As the eighteenth drumbeat sounds, General Zuo Xuanliao himself beats the drum of farewell.

blocks.cap: Everyone else has returned to the frontier fortress — but where is he, alone, headed?

blocks.x: The drama 《Storms Over Yumen》 told of swordsmen who laid down their grudges for the sake of the nation. Yumen's real story turned out no different. Around a single sword, everyone learned how to lay down a grudge of their own.

act22side.tagline: A kind Infected traveler who tends to burns turns out to be the mysterious 'Leader' of the rebel group Dublinn. On the night a woman who's lived her whole life in her sister's shadow finally acknowledges the fire that's hers alone.

chars.name: Reed
chars.desc: An Infected woman who wandered the wilds alone, keeping her identity hidden. Takes in and leads a band of Tarans on the run

chars.name: Finn
chars.desc: A talkative, warm-hearted Taran merchant. Appoints herself the caretaker of the band of fugitives

chars.name: Celmoon
chars.desc: A rough-edged Taran woman who follows and mimics her brother, who joined Dublinn

chars.name: Bagpipe
chars.desc: A retired Victorian soldier turned operator, stubbornly determined to protect commoners

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: A cool-headed inspector skilled with the sword. Bagpipe's colleague

chars.name: Moraine
chars.desc: An Infected woman with night blindness. Quiet, but resolute

chars.name: Earl Warwick
chars.desc: An old earl who hosts a salon for the revival of Taran culture

chars.name: Harmonie
chars.desc: A Felinic spy playing both sides for Victoria and Taran

chars.name: Fisher
chars.desc: A mysterious city council investigator, chasing the covert struggle between Dublinn and the dukes alone — Operator Puzzle

terms.name: Dublinn
terms.desc: An armed rebel group active across southern Victoria, championing the liberation of the Taran people. Led by a mysterious 'Leader'

terms.name: The Wraithguard
terms.desc: A secret group that poses as Dublinn's elite while brutally eliminating witnesses

terms.name: The Iron Rust Guard
terms.desc: An elite unit under Duke Wellington. Moves covertly, posing as Dublinn when necessary

terms.name: The Curfew Bell
terms.desc: The curfew bell that rings at night. Once it tolls, not even a Taran can light a lamp

terms.name: Draco
terms.desc: The fiery bloodline of King Gael, ruler of the old Taran kingdom. Since Victoria seized the throne, passed down only as legend

terms.name: The Tarans
terms.desc: Natives annexed by Victoria, stripped of their names and language. Drift along with the nomadic city, plagued by discrimination and Oripathy

blocks.x: When night falls, a bell rings. The **Curfew Bell**. Once it rings, a Taran can't so much as light a lamp, for fear of looking like a poacher sneaking onto the hunting grounds.

Somewhere in the Scatana Plains, two Taran drifters had been caught and beaten by a patrol. Then, suddenly, a fire broke out near a warehouse, and the patrol rushed off in that direction. The one who quietly cut the rope binding the two, without ever giving her name, was an unfamiliar wyvern.

Finn and Celmoon simply called this woman the 'kind doctor.' But this woman had never even properly said her own name. And by the end of this journey, her true identity turns out to be tied to the roots of the largest rebel organization in this story.
1. The Night the Curfew Bell Rang, a Nameless Arsonist
Merchant Finn and hot-tempered Celmoon were being caught and beaten by a patrol. Then, out of nowhere, a warehouse caught fire.
While the patrol rushed off to put out the flames, an unfamiliar woman came over and cut Finn's rope. Finn asked her who she was.
……Who are you?
……Go, quickly.
Even after they fled into the marshes, the woman stayed by their side. Finn called her 'Doctor,' but the woman flatly insisted she wasn't one.

Once nine o'clock passes, even the radio stops broadcasting. Nothing makes a sound after the curfew bell. Yet this woman, despite saying she'd been to the Scatana Plains before, says "there was no curfew bell rule back then."
The woman quietly asks if they'd killed anyone. Celmoon readily admits to killing two patrolmen.
Everyone starts to sense this woman isn't just some ordinary wandering doctor.
2. Playing Doctor Without Being One, and a Suspicious Old Poetry Book
The next morning, Finn tries to hand out bread, only to find the woman reading an old Taran poetry anthology. Finn, embarrassed, admits he can't even read Taran script.
When Finn thanks her, the woman mutters, **"To keep the promise made with Rhodes Island, one must cherish the lives of others."** Finn just lets it slide, saying he didn't quite catch it.
Meanwhile, over in Trent County, prosecutor **Ch'en** and former Victorian soldier **Bagpipe** appear. The two are tracking a mysterious group called the 'Wraithguard.'
Then Ch'en suddenly freezes up, saying something reminds her of an incident back in Lungmen.
The Wraithguard is a group in strange garb spotted in Hillock County. Ch'en and Bagpipe cautiously begin to follow the trail.
3. The Day They Robbed a Wagon, Reuniting with an Old Comrade

When Finn's group runs out of food, they decide to rob a wagon. But Celmoon insists on imitating Dublinn while doing it. Finn gets angry — is he putting everyone in danger just to satisfy his own selfishness?
And your selfishness…… I don't like it either. That's the biggest lie of all.

Reed quietly scolds Celmoon. As luck would have it, that very wagon happened to have Ch'en and Bagpipe traveling with it. The moment Celmoon draws his sword, posing as real Wraithguard, Bagpipe's Castbreaker comes flying in.
In the middle of the chaos, Bagpipe runs into a wyvern wielding a spear she's seen somewhere before. The instant they recognize each other, Bagpipe's eyes go wide.
……Reed?
Surprisingly, the two know each other. The fact that they're both Rhodes Island operators comes out naturally in this very moment.
4. Night at the Safe House, Hay Bales, and a Beloved Old Home

With Bagpipe and Ch'en's help, Reed brings the drifters all the way to a safe house. She even gets tents and emergency supplies from Rhodes Island. Celmoon, though, still turns her back, saying, "I'm going back to Dublinn."

blocks.cap: Lying side by side in the hay, Reed brings up her old home for the first time in a long while, pretending she hasn't fallen apart.

blocks.x: At night, Bagpipe pulls Reed down into the hay. "The hay smells nice, doesn't it?" Idle chatter, but here, for the first time, Reed opens up about the home she lost.
Y'know, ye only say 'no' a lot when talkin' about yerself. Like……like yer tryin' to get away from something.
Reed says she has an older sister. But her words trail off beyond that.
Instead she answers quietly — someone once said something similar to her. That person saved her, she says, and she still wants to ask why.
5. Moraine Joins, and They Meet Soldiers Who Walk Even After Death
I don't want to be a burden…… as long as my legs can still carry me forward, I want to walk together with all of you.

Moraine, an Infected woman with night blindness, joins Reed. While on the move, the band of drifters runs into unidentified soldiers. Reed inspects the scene and finds it strange — signs of battle, but not a single body.
Bagpipe and Ch'en say they saw something similar in Hillock County: soldiers with fire blazing from their eyes.
Reed answers quietly. "Those…… are wraiths. People who are surely dead, but couldn't find rest."
Just then, Celmoon, who had been hiding, appears. She's spotted a familiar silhouette among the soldiers.
……Brother.
6. The Shadow Sisters' Old Promise

Celmoon secretly follows her brother. Two years ago, he'd left with the soles of his shoes worn through, heading off to find Dublinn. Celmoon recalls how she'd once tried to follow him, then ran instead.
And overlapping with it, a memory surfaces from long ago — a snowy holiday night. There were two sisters. The elder always excelled at everything; the younger only ever imitated her.
The younger tried mimicking her sister's voice to ask their father for something, but he saw through it right away. "Of course. What kind of father can't tell his own daughters apart?"
That night, the sisters lost their home. The elder took her sister's hand and made her knock on an unfamiliar door.
"Go on, don't be afraid. I'll be watching over you." And so the sisters hid away in their benefactor's study.

blocks.cap: The elder made a weapon of the flame in her hand, while the younger trembled in fear of that very fire.

blocks.x: Their benefactor handed the two of them spears. The elder used hers to strike down silence without hesitation, but the younger's hands trembled every time.
It's all right, little sister. If you don't know where your desire lies, just try being me for now. In the future, you and I will both become 'the Leader.'
Back to the present. Before Celmoon, cudgel in hand, stands the dead man who was once her brother.
No matter how many times she strikes him down, the soldier doesn't fall. In the end, it's Reed's flame that puts him to rest instead of Celmoon, and Celmoon collapses to the ground.
7. Songs by the Campfire, and an Inspector Posing as a Guest
A few days after settling at the safe house, the drifters share a warm dinner in their tent for the first time in a while. Finn goes around excitedly praising Bagpipe's cooking.

blocks.cap: Around the campfire, the fugitives sing for the first time in a long while. Raising empty cups for those not there.

blocks.x: The wanderer, setting off on a long road, wonders where this path might lead♪
At night, they all gather around the campfire and sing. Reed, pushed into it despite herself, hums along to a Taran song.
But amid the singing, an unfamiliar man cuts in. His name is 'Fisher.' He claims to be a city council investigator, and says he's had this camp under watch for quite a while now.

Fisher pulls Reed aside to sit alone with him. "Lovely evening," he says, adding this is a much more pleasant setting than an interrogation room. The song goes on, but underneath it, the interrogation has already begun.
8. The Interrogator's True Identity, and a Fire Erupts
Fisher is a slippery one. He points out that Reed has no identity records whatsoever. "Wouldn't that make you rather like a shadow?"
On top of that, Fisher already knows Reed's Dublinn comrades are coming to rescue her. "Come under our protection, and we won't ask any further about the crimes you've committed in the past."
……I refuse!
Reed refuses firmly. Bagpipe and Ch'en tear down the interrogation tent in a surprise strike, and at that very moment, flames erupt without any warning at all.
Shouldn't there be fire blazing on your spear right now?

Bagpipe sees the flame at the tip of the spear. And it suddenly overlaps with something she'd seen once before, in Hillock County. That Caster was the very same person who once stood watching, indifferent, as the streets burned.
9. That Night Eight Years Ago, and the Burning Forest
We return to eight years ago, the night of Earl Warwick's final gathering. Sensing something was off, the earl had set out to walk through the snowstorm with the poet.

blocks.cap: When the door opened, their benefactor lay dying before a cold flame. The younger sister simply stood there.

blocks.x: That night, the earl accepts a cup of poisoned wine. His counterpart was none other than the elder of the two sisters. "A greedy Draco, a Draco of endless ambition…… so what I've spent half my life investigating wasn't wrong after all." The earl closed his eyes with a smile.
The younger sister stood at the doorway and watched it all. Her reflection in the windowpane was a tangle of fear and pity.
Back to the present. In the fog and smoke, the drifters wander the forest, calling Reed's name.
Celmoon and Moraine also carve a path through, holding off the soldiers. People simply run toward the one light they can see ahead.

blocks.cap: The smoke thickens, the heat draws nearer. No one had ever called out their names before.

blocks.x: Somewhere beyond the fog, someone keeps calling Reed's name. Reed suddenly finds herself wondering. "……Reed. Are they calling for me?"
10. Reed Is, In Fact, Dublinn's 'Leader'

blocks.cap: The two meet through the fog. For the first time, Bagpipe sees Reed's true face.

blocks.x: As the fog clears, Bagpipe sees clearly the flame Reed has made. "Was that you, the Caster in Hillock County? Are ye Dublinn's Leader?"
Everyone waits for her answer. Reed always wanted to shake her head at this kind of question, but this time, she doesn't want to.
……I am Dublinn's 'Leader.'

Reed doesn't hide the wrongs she's committed. That among the countless lives thrown into the fire that was Dublinn, some of the sacrifices weren't even necessary. Even so, she says, starting from this very moment, she wants to live differently.
Bagpipe never really knew how to be disappointed. But right now, she doesn't even know who she's disappointed in anymore. Still, Bagpipe decides not to leave Reed — she just goes her own way instead.
11. Celmoon, Sleeping in the Mud
While escaping, Reed is nearly caught and dragged off again. This time, a woman named Harmonie appears and calls Reed 'Lochsinni' — claiming they're old friends.
Reed doesn't deny the name. Instead, she quietly asks back — did the Leader know? That innocent people would die in Hillock County?
Amid the chaos, Celmoon and Moraine set out to save those who'd fallen behind. Celmoon holds off the soldiers with a broken sword, sending Moraine ahead to safety first.
"They're trying to take our Leader — how could I just stand here?" Celmoon said with a smile. Those were her last words.
At dawn, Moraine returns alone. In her hands, a broken sword and Celmoon's blood-soaked headscarf.
Reed quietly asks what happened to Celmoon. Moraine only shakes her head.
……For everyone rising up from this mire.
Reed raises her hand, and a small flower of flame blooms over the mud, then quietly withers away.
12. In the Ruined Palace, Facing Her Own Shadow
The place they take shelter in the fog turns out to be, of all places, the ruined palace of King Gael. With almost no Originium crystals nearby, it was a safe spot.
Inside the ruins, Reed finds a familiar light. Traces that her sister had passed through.
And something with her sister's voice appears before Reed. "You want to snuff out that flame, don't you."
The shadow presses in on her. "You're nothing but your sister's shadow. She has the ability to reach the peak in every field, but you've always hesitated and shown nothing but weakness."
I'm not my sister's shadow. I'm the shadow of the 'Leader' that lives in people's ideals.
But Reed doesn't back down. Everyone needed a way out, she says, and it just so happened that she was there.
The shadow asks one last question. "Lochsinni, what is it you want?" Instead of answering, Reed reaches out to a wounded drifter. From her hand flows not flame, but a gentle, healing light.
I've simply found my own flame.
13. A Speech Aboard the Warship, and Reed's Answer

Meanwhile, out on Victoria's open sea, her sister Evelina returns to Oak Grove County aboard Duke Wellington's swift warship, after a seven-year voyage. This city, of all places, was where the sisters were born.

blocks.cap: On the deck of the swift warship, Dublinn's 'Leader' stands before the troops.

blocks.x: We do not fight to steal the crown Victoria holds. We fight to reclaim the destiny that was stolen from us, and to hold that destiny in our own hands.
Evelina begins her speech before the soldiers. She vows to reclaim the name and destiny stolen from them for so long.

blocks.cap: At the very moment her sister gives her speech aboard the warship, the younger sister delivers her own answer to the drifters among the reeds.

blocks.x: At the same time, somewhere among the reeds, Reed also stands before the drifters.
Moraine asks — saying it's 'for the sake of the Taran people's lives' feels vague. What exactly are they even supposed to be fighting?
Even if we can't defeat death…… we can still grant dignity to our lives.
Reed answers. She says the Taran deserve a fire to warm themselves on cold nights, and drink to share when they sing.
"Let's go — we're one of them too." Reed sets off, walking together with the drifters.
Asked what she'll do once she moves together with Dublinn, Reed answers calmly. "When that day comes, I'll go meet her too."
Epilogue: A House Built in the Wrong Place
'The Instructor' and Harmonie are still locked in a battle of wits over Lochsinni. Harmonie says she intends to visit, in person, the medical company where Lochsinni once hid herself.

Ch'en and Bagpipe hitch a ride on a cargo truck toward their next mission. Bagpipe fiddles with the old radio Finn gave her. It carries news that the clashes between Victoria and Kazdel are escalating.
Fisher accepts the Caster duke's offer of promotion. His old identity as an investigator is set to be discarded now.
On his way back, Fisher happens to run into Finn. Finn, selling wooden toys, proudly points to the new house he built just yesterday.
Run, Taran. Looks like you picked the wrong spot for your house.

At that moment, a massive roar sounds, and Duke Wellington's fleet rises over the horizon. Fisher leaves one last line and gets into his car. Behind him, the sound of the brick house collapsing is soon swallowed by the warship's din.

When the curfew bell rang, there was once a woman preparing to run. Now, she's become a woman who lights her own fire to warm others. Knowing now that she doesn't have to defeat her sister to be whole, she takes, once again today, one more step out of the shadow.

act21side.tagline: A runaway wolf comes home to settle an old debt, and ends up relearning how not to run. Instead of the fantasy of being a 'Siracusan,' Texas decides to just stay Texas.

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: Penguin Logistics' reticent dual-blade wielder. Actually the last blood heir of a great Siracusa mafia house — Operator Texas

chars.name: Exusiai
chars.desc: Texas's longtime partner. A Sankta who causes trouble by meddling, then cleans it up by meddling some more — Operator Exusiai

chars.name: Croissant
chars.desc: A warm-hearted, nagging bodyguard. A dependable companion who worries about everyone in her thick accent — Operator Croissant

chars.name: Sora
chars.desc: A Lungmen idol who followed Texas all the way to Siracusa. The grit to go so far as infiltrating a job as a musical actress — Operator Sora

chars.name: Lappland
chars.desc: The daughter expelled from the Salucho family. A wolf whose obsessive fixation on Texas borders on madness — Operator Lappland

chars.name: Lavinia
chars.desc: A young judge in Volsini. An idealist who won't give up on fairness even while under the Belloné family's protection

chars.name: Leontuccio
chars.desc: The young heir of the Belloné family. A young master ahead of his time, who trusts negotiation and profit over violence

chars.name: Bernardo
chars.desc: An elder of the Belloné family, disguised as the art director of Del Alba theater troupe. Leontuccio's father

terms.name: Siracusa
terms.desc: A nation where twelve 'Families' (mafia houses) effectively divide up and rule the cities. There's a court and judges on the surface, but the real order always lies with the Families.

terms.name: Family
terms.desc: The mafia houses rooted in each of Siracusa's cities. They pay lip service to Madame Sicilia, but each moves by its own rules behind the scenes.

terms.name: Volsini
terms.desc: The city where the twelve Families' turf war is hottest, over the construction of a new nomadic city. The stage for this entire story.

terms.name: Grayhall
terms.desc: The council where the heads of the twelve Families gather. A symbol of the rules Madame Sicilia established to prevent all-out war between Families.

terms.name: Madame Sicilia
terms.desc: The absolute ruler who has governed Siracusa for sixty years. She's managed the Families' conflicts with one bare-minimum line: 'no total annihilation.'

terms.name: Wolf Lord
terms.desc: Immortal beings who live in the wilds. Each picks out a human called a 'Fang' to fight in their stead, as part of a game they play.

blocks.x: Siracusa is a city-state that's effectively owned, in its entirety, by twelve 'Families' — mafia houses. There are courts, there are judges, but the shadow of the Families always looms over them both.

**Texas**, quietly making deliveries for Penguin Logistics in Lungmen, spots a wolf standing on a rooftop outside her window one night. That wolf was a visitor come to collect on a debt she'd owed for seven years.
Texas leaves without a word of goodbye — just "I'll be back soon." But what awaits her isn't a nostalgic homecoming; it's the surname she'd abandoned back then.
1. A Wolf Comes to Collect a Debt, In Person No Less

blocks.cap: Above Lungmen's night skyline stood a wolf that had no business being there.

blocks.x: The wolf outside the window was **Zalak** — an immortal being who calls himself a 'Wolf Lord,' and the creditor of the deal Texas struck when she left Columbia.
The demand was simple: return to Siracusa and act as his 'Fang' — his proxy — just once. Texas said yes.

Her boss **Emperor** hurled every ounce of half-baked Konglish curses at her but let her go in the end. "I'll give you vacation, so go take care of that not-at-all important business." And so, one human and one wolf walked off into the dark.
2. A Murderer, the Moment She Comes Back
The one who greets Texas in Siracusa is **Leontuccio** Belloné. The young heir of the Belloné family, he asks Texas to attend tonight's party and show off the name 'Texas' publicly.
But mid-party, Construction Minister Karachi is killed instantly by a car bomb, and Leontuccio himself is attacked. Texas, present at the scene, instantly becomes the prime suspect.

blocks.cap: There's no one left to ask if you're hurt, even after the fighting ends. Texas sinks into an emptiness she's never felt before in her life.

blocks.x: The case falls to **Lavinia** — a young judge who insists on unshakable fairness even under the Belloné family's protection. In the end, she arrests Texas.

The truth is, this attack was orchestrated behind the scenes by Leontuccio's right hand and Belloné's strategist, **Dmitri**. Believing the Family had gone too soft, he had, for the first time, bared his fangs against his own master.
3. A Lungmen Idol Infiltrates a Siracusa Theater Troupe

Meanwhile, back in Lungmen, **Sora** couldn't sit still. Even in the middle of her idol activities, she managed to pass an audition for a Siracusa theater troupe, and chose to head to Siracusa to find Texas.

Coming along were Texas's longtime partner **Exusiai** and **Croissant**, who appointed herself the two of theirs' bodyguard. The three of them get swept up in a Family street brawl the moment they arrive — their first taste of hospitality.

"They're way harder to deal with than the thugs back in Lungmen." Exusiai grumbles, yet still ends up saving a random citizen, while Croissant is too busy chasing after her big sis to do much else. That's about all Siracusa's first impression amounted to.

The Del Alba troupe the three set foot into is led by none other than **Bernardo** as its art director. The title of the new musical he's preparing? None other than *The Death of Texas* — the first work to put the real name Texas Family on stage.

The critic **Ben**, who took it upon himself to coach Sora's acting, was also a puzzling figure. Every offhand remark he tossed out while munching on pizza somehow hit the mark. "Norms build people false pedestals, and true reverence quietly slips away in the cracks between them."
4. Why a Truck Crashed Straight Into the Courtroom
To calm the situation quickly, Texas confesses to being the true culprit — a calculated move to stabilize things and lure out whoever's really behind it, using herself as bait.

But right before the sentencing, a truck comes bursting straight through the courtroom wall. The one who steps out of the driver's seat is **Lappland** — the daughter expelled from the Salucho family. She calmly turns herself in, saying, "I'm the real culprit."

blocks.cap: A truck bursts through the courthouse wall. A direct challenge to the order Madame Sicilia had upheld for so long.

blocks.x: Everyone is puzzled. Why would Salucho's problem child suddenly take the fall for Texas? Lappland herself gives away not a shred of her true intentions.
The trial is suspended and Texas is cleared of suspicion. But the true mastermind is still crouched somewhere in the shadows.
5. The Judge's Trusted Promise — Turns Out the Man Who Made It Was the Mastermind
Lavinia had a secret: on the day the Belloné family achieves final victory, she'd been promised a city where the fairness she believed in would actually be realized.
Meanwhile, Salucho boss **Alberto** also quietly begins to move — a covert alliance with Belloné to take down Rosati and swallow the new city whole.
But the man who made that promise to her was sitting right there at the table plotting with Salucho to strike Rosati. Lavinia storms in and demands to know why — the answer she gets is just one line.
The game is over, Lavinia.

blocks.cap: In a single line, Lavinia's past decade and more came crashing down.

blocks.x: Lavinia leaves the room without looking back, terrified she'll burst into tears. It was the moment the very reason she'd endured until now collapsed entirely.
6. Playwright Caterina — Turns Out She's Texas's Childhood Friend and Greatest Enemy

On the day of Sora's musical debut, the boss of the Rosati family arrives as an honored guest. It turns out that boss is none other than the playwright 'Caterina' herself — the person Sora had been friends with for weeks was the very top of Rosati.
On top of that, Belloné and Salucho conspire to assassinate Rosati right then and there. Texas draws the weapon hidden inside her bass guitar and sprints across the stage.

blocks.cap: Who knew there was a weapon hidden inside that bass. In a theater emptied of fleeing audience, the real show begins.

blocks.x: After a hard-won path to the room, the one who greets Texas has already set down her blade — a childhood friend from long ago. "It's been a while, Celinia." Rosati's boss, **Giovanna**.
7. The Quietest, Heaviest Reunion
Giovanna extends a hand to Texas. "Join me, and we can do something big here in Siracusa." But Texas's answer had already been decided.

"I don't want to be Siracusan or Columbian — **I'm just Celinia Texas.**" The two part ways in the end. Giovanna lets her old friend, now an enemy of Rosati, walk away alive, but leaves behind a warning: this is the last time.

blocks.cap: "Big sis, we're here!" Croissant and Exusiai, having chased her all the way to Siracusa, reunite at last.

blocks.x: The three friends who greet the fleeing Texas ask her nothing at all. "The mark of a good partner is not asking what the other doesn't want to say." The four of them come together once more.
8. The Weakest One Picks Up the Gun

The Construction Minister's seat, left empty since Karachi's death, is taken over by **Rubio**, the Food Safety Minister long stuck in an irrelevant post. On the day of his inaugural speech, he goes on the radio and begins to pour his heart out to the entire city.
His friendship with Karachi, and even the conspiracy Belloné and Salucho had cooked up. A confession delivered calmly, without flashy rhetoric or excitement.
That we are living people. That our lives are real. That we are not animals penned inside a fence.
The moment the speech ends, a gunshot rings out. Rubio protected the final broadcast with his own life.

blocks.cap: Lavinia gently covered her daughter's ears beside her. The gunshot cracked open all of Volsini, and Siracusa's long silence.

blocks.x: 9. Her Father's Will Was, In Fact, the Entire Nation
Rubio's exposé sends the city into uncontrollable upheaval. Belloné and Salucho's secret pact is laid bare for all to see, and all twelve Families begin tearing at each other.

Enraged, Alberto gives Leontuccio just ten seconds: submit to Salucho, or die right here. As the pocket watch's second hand ticks over that short span, Leontuccio has already realized what his father had been aiming for all along.
Leontuccio finally understands his father's true purpose. What Bernardo wanted wasn't the new city, wasn't victory — it was **a Siracusa without Families altogether**.
If the Families disappear, will Siracusa be better off than it is now?
Leontuccio's answer differs from his father's. "That's not enough — you have to let people know what's happening right now, and let them take part in it." The son resolves to go beyond his father.

blocks.cap: A face that took up its father's question, but meant to go past his answer.

blocks.x: Bernardo had already made his peace. Right before Wolf Lord Zalak comes to demand the price of betrayal, he swallows poison of his own accord, ending the game himself.

blocks.cap: He closed his eyes quietly, recalling some summer long ago when he worked, drenched in sweat, at a shoe store.

blocks.x: 10. Trial of the Wolf Lords, and a Farewell
The furious Zalak appears before Leontuccio to demand payment. At that very moment, boss Emperor, who flew in from Lungmen the instant he heard the news, cuts in.

blocks.cap: "Ladies and gentlemen." Lungmen's boss barges into the Wolf Lords' game — the flashiest meddling Siracusa has ever seen.

blocks.x: What Emperor brings with him are other Wolf Lords who've already lost this very game. Zalak, having broken the rules, has no choice but to bow his head before them.
Meanwhile, Lappland bids her father Alberto a final farewell and stands before Texas. "Today, I'm going to kill you." The two swords clash.

blocks.cap: No flourish, only blades aiming purely for each other's lives. A reenactment of that night seven years ago.

blocks.x: "Texas, so you really were just Texas." — "Of course I'm just Texas. What about you?" — "I guess I'm just Lappland too."
Neither of them wins. Lappland holds out her hand, and Texas takes it. As friend and enemy both, the two walk side by side toward Grayhall.
There, Texas, Leontuccio, Lavinia, and Lappland finally come face to face with this nation's true ruler, **Madame Sicilia**.
On the surface, a gentle old woman, but her mere presence was enough to freeze the room solid. Even Wolf Lord Zalak didn't dare act carelessly before her.

blocks.cap: Four people, each wanting to tear Siracusa apart for their own reasons, standing side by side.

blocks.x: Madame Sicilia demands no promises. Instead, she agrees to lend Leontuccio one new city — a fragment of the answer Bernardo had longed for his whole life: 'a city without Families.'
Epilogue: Sutures
On the day of his father's funeral, Leontuccio digs the grave alone. No one else attends, but the one who helps him push the coffin is Dmitri — his old right hand, the one who betrayed him.

blocks.cap: The pocket watch Dmitri tossed onto the grave. A wordless reconciliation, and a promise for what comes next.

blocks.x: In the now-empty Belloné mansion, Lavinia and Leontuccio talk about a new order. "We're going to build new things there. An order that belongs to everyone."

Car washer **Dambron** is still there at the car wash. Lavinia stops by often on her way through, checking in on him. In Siracusa, even the most ordinary conversation can sometimes bring the greatest comfort.
Emperor is still waiting for a party. "I don't know how I survived three thousand years without parties," he grumbles, and ends up settling in Siracusa a few more days anyway.
Sora returns to Giovanna's room with Texas, but she's already gone. What's left is a bundle of scripts — the never-finished Act 3 of *The Death of Texas*.
The first page reads: "I dedicate this script to my dear friend Celinia, and to my newfound friends at Penguin Logistics."

New rumors are already circulating in the theater district. Giovanna — or rather, Caterina — is said to be preparing her next work, *The Siracusan*. A name that points at every single person born in this city, yet one no one can fully define.

The wolf that had stood on that rooftop outside her window that night had, in the end, come only to collect an old debt. But Texas, who went to pay it off, only finished making ready to return to Lungmen once she'd built herself a reason not to need to flee Siracusa. The wolf that used to run now chooses, for herself, where to go home to.

act19side.tagline: A story about going to find missing coworkers, only to discover a paradise every one of them walked into willingly. And the kindest boss in the world tears down her own dream with her own hands, just to set the people she loves most free.

chars.name: Dorothy
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Applied Originium Arts Division. In charge of Base 359, she's unusually warm toward the pioneers.

chars.name: Elena
chars.desc: Dorothy's assistant and a researcher in Rhine Lab's Energy Division. An ambitious woman who idolizes her boss, Ferdinand — Operator Astgenne

chars.name: Silence
chars.desc: Joyce's attending physician and a Medic cooperating with Rhodes Island. A stickler for principle who trusts only what she sees with her own eyes.

chars.name: Greyy
chars.desc: An electrical engineering intern at Rhodes Island. Once Infected herself, she knows how to take a step forward even in the dark.

chars.name: Ptilopsis
chars.desc: An Infected doctor under contract with Rhine Lab. She has a special chip called the 'No. 9 Device' wired into her nervous system — Operator Ptilopsis

chars.name: Saria
chars.desc: A founding member of Rhine Lab and its former Defense Division head. She left the company, but cleaning up its messes still falls to her.

chars.name: Muelsyse
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Ecology Division. She disappears while trying to warn the director of danger.

chars.name: Sani
chars.desc: 'Captain' of the pioneer band. A former law student who lost everything after contracting Oripathy.

chars.name: Mary
chars.desc: A sheriff at the Trimounts police station. Sani's childhood friend.

chars.name: Ferdinand
chars.desc: Head of Rhine Lab's Energy Division. An ambitious man with his eye on the director's chair.

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: A major Originium Arts research corporation headquartered in Trimounts, Columbia. Under the director, nine division heads each oversee their own research department.

terms.name: Trimounts
terms.desc: A nomadic city that serves as Columbia's center of science and technology. Hundreds of corporations commission and operate experimental facilities here.

terms.name: The Pioneers
terms.desc: A band of laborers who work the wastes outside the nomadic city. Mostly made up of people the city has pushed out — the Infected, debtors, ex-convicts.

terms.name: Base 359
terms.desc: A new experimental facility Rhine Lab built on the outskirts of Trimounts. Home to Dorothy's lab, and the stage for this whole affair.

terms.name: Transmission Substance
terms.desc: A new substance developed by Rhine Lab, said to turn even people with low Originium Arts aptitude into skilled Casters. A silvery liquid that mediates neural signals.

terms.name: the 'Hub'
terms.desc: A massive machine at Base 359 that weaves together the consciousness of the missing pioneers into one.

blocks.x: Rhine Lab, based in Trimounts, Columbia, is a major Originium Arts corporation where nine division heads split five research departments among them. This story begins when three of them go missing at once.

Ecology Division head **Muelsyse** vanished after leaving only a frantic message on the answering machine, while Applied Originium Arts Division head **Dorothy** and her assistant **Elena** disappeared from the new experimental facility. Former Defense Division head **Saria** turned to Rhodes Island for help.
But what the operators found upon reaching the base was neither a hostage situation nor an experiment gone wrong. It was a paradise every one of the missing had walked into on their own two feet.
1. The Last Words Left on the Answering Machine
The incident begins with the answering machine in Rhine Lab's director's private office. "This is bad, Christine. I think I've really done it this time……" It was Muelsyse's voice.
……Christine, this thought just hit me out of nowhere…… if that person is still alive, then we……
The message cuts off there. In truth, Muelsyse had been seized that very night by an unidentified woman and was being interrogated about Rhine Lab's secrets.
When Muelsyse refused to talk, the woman left only a single, short line: "Deal with her." A massive suit of powered armor then appeared and closed in on her.
The next day, Saria's meeting with Muelsyse falls through — she's not at the meeting spot. What Saria gets instead is a missing-person notice, and the place she turns to for help is Rhodes Island.

The Doctor, arriving on scene with the **Mechanist**, is met first with an unenthusiastic briefing. "I'm a mechanic, not a detective's sidekick……" she grumbles, but tags along anyway. "I just hope I can get back to the ship tomorrow and see my beloved workbench again."

It's here they learn that three people vanished from Base 359 at once — pioneer captain **Sani**, assistant Elena, and lab head Dorothy. While searching the back alleys, the two are soon ambushed by energy weapons.
2. Base 359, and a Kind Boss

Inside the base, an entirely different kind of day was unfolding. It was an ordinary morning — Dorothy's assistant Elena handing coffee to her Infected coworker Joyce, listening to a new researcher's grumbling.
Dorothy was the type to secretly make heating modules for the pioneers. Elena wasn't happy about it. "They're just the pioneer band. They don't even think of us as friends."
