terms.desc: The Imperial Guard that shadows the Ursus Emperor. Said to seal the power of a 'Demon' within their bodies to carry out their duties — a force that goes beyond human.

blocks.x: Sargon's Ebert region, a nameless village. Waiting for Rhodes Island's Heavyrain and Sesa, who'd come to make contact, wasn't their expected counterpart — it was an unfamiliar information broker said to run the Reefstip black market.

Codename 'Sandsoldier.' The operator is safe, he says, and nothing was touched — the man opens negotiations at his leisure, wanting to know just one thing: a single name written on the two agents' contract. Kal'tsit.

The moment that name leaves his lips, the story rewinds twenty-two years. A burning village in Sargon, a snow-covered Ursus sanatorium, a snowy Victorian ballroom — everywhere Kal'tsit passed through, people ended up carrying their own private vendettas and promises.
1. The shameless negotiating style of an unfamiliar broker
Heavyrain draws her blade first, thinking it's an ambush. But the man calmly raises both hands and greets them in Sargonese. 'Good morning to you both.'

The mastermind behind every armed incident in Ebert, the finest information broker of the Reefstip black market — everyone calls him 'Sandsoldier.' He claims, with easy confidence, that he hasn't touched Rhodes Island's experimental materials or its operators. All he wants, he says, is to talk.
The man reveals he's Infected himself, dangling treatment as a bargaining chip. When Sesa presses him, he reluctantly gets to his real business — he's chasing the name 'Kal'tsit,' too.
Revenge... more than half the people living on this land are probably dreaming of it. Whatever the target may be.
The man even readily admits it was he who burned the Ebert Amir to death three years ago. But when he tosses out one question of his own, Sesa's expression hardens.
'Did she happen to hand either of you a single gold coin? An ancient Sargon coin.' With those words, the story leaps back twenty-two years.
2. The burning village of Redhorn, a boy carrying a corpse

Twenty-two years ago, Redhorn village in Sargon's Ebert region. A secret Columbian research team codenamed 'Sandsoldier' is wiped out in an ambush. The only survivor is a teenage boy, **Elliot Glover**, a dead professor's body on his back — his late mentor, Professor Thorne.

The Amir's mercenaries, Sarkaz mercenaries, even Sargon's own military are all after the boy — every one of them after the silver case he's clutching. Just as one mercenary snatches the case and is about to kill the boy, a monster appears out of nowhere and impales him.
The monster's name is Mon3tr. Its owner is a woman of unknown origin who introduces herself as **Kal'tsit**.
'Elliot, it's all right now.' When the boy asks how she knows his name, Kal'tsit answers plainly, as if there's no time to spare even for mourning.

'You've already done well enough, keeping your mentor's legacy out of the hands of scheming men.' The boy doesn't simply go along with it. In truth, the body he'd been carrying wasn't only his mentor's — it was the only trace recovered from the roughly hundred researchers who'd come to Sargon together.
The boy already knew he had no other choice. And so Elliot, half against his will, begins walking the desert together with Kal'tsit and Mon3tr.
3. Doctor and advisor — seventeen years ago, a revenge plot in Ursus

Time leaps forward to Ursus, seventeen years ago. **Lilya**, once Kal'tsit's student, comes to find her, now living as a village doctor. She brings a plan to assassinate **Grand Duke Vanya**, the man behind the 'Chernobog Incident' that took her husband's life.
Lilya has already infiltrated the sanatorium. She's bribed the head of security to pose as a trainee, and worked out exactly when the Duke takes his daily afternoon sunbathing.
All she needs now is Kal'tsit's hand, and a poison that leaves no trace even after death. Kal'tsit tries to talk her out of it, but Lilya's answer is unshakeable.
Louisa hasn't even learned to say 'Dad' yet.

Having left her daughter Louisa in someone else's care to throw herself into revenge, Lilya finally gets Kal'tsit to accept the request, in exchange for one promise — that no matter what happens, Kal'tsit will protect Louisa, and Lilya's old comrade Lyudmila too. With that, Lilya smuggles Kal'tsit, disguised as a servant, deep into the sanatorium.
4. One severed finger, and a single flower

A welcome guest happens to be visiting the sanatorium. Ursus's newly appointed Minister of Finance, **Wyth** — come to see an old, blinded veteran friend. Wyth all but wrestles the knife away from his friend, insisting on peeling the apple himself.

But the old soldier suddenly cuts off the second finger of his left hand and hands it to Wyth. His right hand, ruined in the war, left only this self-severed finger from his left as his sole remaining 'flesh and blood,' he says — and asks that it be buried in his homeland's soil.

Amid the commotion that loosens up security, Kal'tsit slips into the Duke's room disguised as a servant. Blind **Grand Duke Vanya** senses that his would-be killer has arrived, yet remains composed. He even asks her to describe the view outside his window.

blocks.cap: Sunlight pours down on new shoots breaking through the earth — the last scene the sightless Duke tries to picture.

blocks.x: As he dies, the Duke tells her the story of the 'pine lily,' the flower that symbolizes Ursus, believing it grows from soil that has taken on all the sins he's committed. Kal'tsit cuts off that excuse without mercy.
It seems you don't even have the right to forgive yourself in your final moments. I hope Ursus forgets you, Your Grace.
The Duke breathes his last, quietly, as if falling asleep. Once the task is done, Lilya thanks Kal'tsit.
Kal'tsit only answers briefly. 'It's not over yet. There may come a day when we have to face this empire again.'
Leaving those words behind, Lilya hurries to prepare to return to her daughter's side. The two of them go their separate ways.
5. Thirteen years ago, a snowy night ball — not exactly a first love

Time flows forward again to Victoria, thirteen years ago. The Laterano monk 'Kal'tsit' is invited to Count Vincent's New Year's party in the remote county of Torun. Also there, having snuck in wearing her mother's stolen dress, is a girl named **Heidi**.

Heidi, the only daughter of House Thomson, freezes the moment she sees Kal'tsit. Her face flushes red after one glass of ginger beer, and she practically demands the seat beside her — her obvious crush impossible to hide. Things take an odd turn when Kal'tsit suddenly starts telling the story of Ursus's 'Great Rebellion' with practiced ease.

blocks.cap: 'Kal'tsit, look outside! It's been so long since snow's come down like this.' Outside the party, the two of them walk alone.

blocks.x: In truth, Kal'tsit and Heidi's father Thomson were already in the same boat — exchanging letters full of intelligence from across the region, trying to head off a civil war among Victoria's Grand Dukes. Two letters, one from Victoria and one supposedly from 'Kazdel' — at that, Kal'tsit's expression stiffens just slightly.
'Is something wrong?' To that question, Heidi answers not with words, but by holding out the two letters. On the shoulders of a still-young girl, the schemes of adults quietly come to rest.
6. The night black snow fell — the truth was 'Emperor's Blade'
Just as the manor grows lively again, Kal'tsit's face hardens. 'Black snow.'
Invisible to Heidi, this snow falling is a sign — Kal'tsit warns her to let no one into the garden. The enemy has arrived.

Waiting in the garden is a guard of the Ursus Emperor, the so-called **Emperor's Blade**. This entity, said to seal the power of a 'Demon' within a human body, draws his sword, declaring he's come to execute the traitor who killed Grand Duke Vanya.

blocks.cap: Killing intent swells, and the 'Emperor's Blade' draws his sword.

blocks.x: Kal'tsit doesn't back down. Instead, she pushes back with words, laying bare the guard's true identity and the inner workings of the Ursus military down to the last detail. After a fight that draws in Mon3tr as well, the wounded guard finally sheathes his blade.
I trust a guard is capable of a reasonable judgment. I merely gave him a peaceful death.

As he withdraws, Emperor's Blade leaves one line behind. 'We'll meet again.' Kal'tsit erases her tracks in the snow and leaves the rest to Heidi — asking her, together with her father, to bury this truth, and someday, in her place, to protect Victoria.
7. The old fortune-teller forgot even his own name

Back to Sargon, twenty-two years ago. Kal'tsit and Elliot, hunted day and night, head for the Reefstip black market. Along the way they keep running into Sarkaz mercenaries, and Kal'tsit surprisingly tries to win them over speaking in a particular Sarkaz tribal dialect.

Negotiations break down, and after a fight, one mercenary loses his life. To him as he dies, Kal'tsit offers neither comfort nor threat — just the plain news that Kazdel, the Sarkaz homeland, is being built even now.

Watching the whole scene, Reefstip's guide, an old Sabra who calls himself 'Old Ishaan,' approaches. He's a strange old man who's dreamed the same dream for over a hundred years, yet remembers absolutely nothing of his own past.

A nomadic city standing tall in the middle of the desert, and a young Padishah standing before it — every single night he dreams the same dream, Ishaan laments, yet he can never recall what that Padishah actually said.

blocks.cap: 'Would you like a glimpse of your fate?' In the middle of the market, a first meeting with the old fortune-teller Ishaan.

blocks.x: With just a few hints from Kal'tsit, Ishaan recalls that he was once a retainer serving the old Padishah, Naitzmora. Forgotten memories burst forth along with his tears, but in the end, he collapses without ever managing to ask what he really wanted to know. Kal'tsit tells him she'll come back for him someday, and leaves him a single gold coin as her promise.
8. It was the boy himself who broke the case
While preparing for the journey at Reefstip, Elliot makes Kal'tsit an unexpected offer. He'll hand over the Originium sample in the case, if she'll take revenge on his behalf.

Kal'tsit answers with a question instead. 'Is that what you want me to do?' She adds that she has no intention of forcing anything on him — and that with today's Originium technology, one sample alone couldn't pose much of a threat anyway.

In that moment, Elliot ends up smashing the case open himself. With his own hands, he shatters the dangerous Originium crystal inside. A startled Kal'tsit throws herself forward to stop him, but it's already too late.
You hate the Infected, don't you? Perfect, then — this way I won't need to owe you anything anymore!
Kal'tsit's answer is only one line. 'I'm Infected too.'
At those words, Elliot erupts with anger instead. It feels, he says, as though everything — his infection, his survival, all of it — happened exactly the way Kal'tsit decided it would.

'No one can decide another person's fate.' Kal'tsit finally hands the choice back to Elliot — go together to Ursus, or stay behind in Sargon and continue his revenge. Elliot's answer was already decided.

blocks.cap: 'Then I suppose, Kal'tsit... it's time for us to part ways.' In the middle of the desert, each to their own path.

blocks.x: Elliot stays behind in Sargon. He vows to find, one by one, everyone tangled up in this whole affair, from Sargon all the way to Columbia, with Ishaan's help.
Old Ishaan watches the boy and murmurs quietly. 'It seems this child has made his decision, Miss Kal'tsit.' Kal'tsit says nothing more than that she respects that decision.
Epilogue. A new name, and the same promise

Twenty-two years pass like that. Reefstip's information broker 'Sandsoldier' erases his enemies from the Sargon affair one by one, and at last walks into Rhodes Island of his own accord. He opens the clinic door, and inside, Kal'tsit is exactly as unchanged as ever.
A package arrives at the Victoria office. Sender: Heidi Thomson — a package containing a data storage device.
Kal'tsit simply tucks it away in a drawer as if it's nothing much. Same as twenty-two years ago — telling only as much as needs to be told, and staying silent about the rest.
His new name is **Passenger**. The very same **Elliot Glover** left behind in Sargon twenty-two years ago.
'Still got a good memory, Elliot.' At Kal'tsit's greeting, Passenger's face tightens slightly.
'Please don't call me by that name. There's a reason we have secret codenames, you know.'
To the man who asks not to be called by his old name, Kal'tsit answers just as indifferently as ever. 'You can deceive yourself, but you can't deceive me.'
Would you answer me one thing — will the 'Kal'tsit' who called me here suddenly vanish again, in a few days, a few months, a few years?
Kal'tsit doesn't answer easily. Instead, she simply hurries her steps toward the Doctor.
'Doctor {@nickname}, here you are.' Toward the site where Amiya is waiting, Rhodes Island sets sail once again today.

Reefstip's information broker lived twenty-two years as a hunter to complete his revenge. And in the end, what he found wasn't a new enemy, but a single old contract. The boy who once wandered the desert boards Rhodes Island once more, under yet another name.

act17d0.tagline: Four mercenaries dive into a lab chasing radioactive waste, and end up dropped whole onto a strange desert planet instead. Guns raised against a plague that appears on no map, a young girl doctor leaves behind, in the end, one last handful of glowing dust.

chars.name: Ash
chars.desc: Leader of a four-person mercenary team from Earth. Real name Cohen — cool-headed and quick to decide.

chars.name: Blitz
chars.desc: The team's shield-bearing wisecracker. Real name Koetz — smug, but someone you can count on in a crisis.

chars.name: Frost
chars.desc: The team's scout and sniper. Real name Tina — calm and practical.

chars.name: Tachanka
chars.desc: The oldest of the team, carrying the heavy weapons. Real name Alexandr Senaviev — a bit of a blowhard, but his convictions run deep.

chars.name: Rangers
chars.desc: An elderly field operator of Rhodes Island. Rumor has it he was once a scout for some order or another, though nothing is confirmed.

chars.name: Schwarz
chars.desc: The taciturn sniper who travels with Rangers. Speaks only when he has to.

chars.name: Liskarm
chars.desc: A Blacksteel electric Arts user. Lightning dances at her fingertips.

chars.name: Franka
chars.desc: Liskarm's partner. Loosens up the mood with her light, teasing way of talking.

chars.name: Miarow
chars.desc: A young doctor who watches over the Longspring Infected district alone. She herself is Infected with this planet's incurable disease.

chars.name: Piquel
chars.desc: Daughter of Amir Tulla, ruler of Longspring, and captain of the guard. Headstrong and utterly inflexible.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: This planet's incurable disease, caused by a mineral called Originium. Skin gradually crystallizes; painkillers only ease the suffering — there is no cure.

terms.name: Originium dust
terms.desc: The phenomenon in which an Infected who dies of Oripathy dissolves into glowing Originium dust, returning to the earth.

terms.name: Originewtant
terms.desc: An artificial life-form synthesized from corpses and Originium. Named by Dr. Levy, it obeys the commands of the Caster controlling it.

terms.name: Sargon, Amir
terms.desc: Sargon is the wasteland nation the four of them landed in; Amir is the title of the local lord who rules each region.

terms.name: Team Rainbow
terms.desc: The name Levy gives the four of them. It seems to have something to do with Rhodes Island, though even they don't fully know who they are.

terms.name: Volvo Kochinski
terms.desc: A Columbian organization that funded Drudge's rebellion and supplied him with experimental weapons, eyeing the profits from mining rights.

blocks.x: Codename: Operation Lamarck. The target was Levy Klichko, a mad scientist hiding in Magnethill Lab No. 2, and the radioactive waste he was said to have hidden away.

The moment the four-person mercenary team, codenamed **Ash, Blitz, Frost, and Tachanka**, broke through the checkpoint and made it inside, the lab's reactor spiraled out of control and exploded completely.
When they came to, they were in a desert. Not on Earth. And on this planet, a plague unlike anything on Earth was eating away at the people here.
1. Operation Lamarck, and the lab that vanished
Rather than surrender quietly, Levy triggers a device. Everything disappears in a flash of white light.
When they open their eyes, there's a huge crack in the wall, and outside the window is nothing but desert. Their compass and radio are both dead.
Something massive comes rushing at them fast through the rubble. Tachanka mutters, not quite believing his own eyes. "You ever see a spider this huge? ...Battle stations!"
To make matters worse, a monster the size of a house — something like a sandbeast — attacks their hideout. Its shell is so hard that their bullets don't even leave a scratch.
Only armor-piercing rounds do any good. Against a creature that won't even stop when you shoot its legs, Ash bets everything on the one grenade they have left. Fortunately, the gamble pays off.

blocks.cap: The day the lab vanished entirely, the four of them began walking across a strange planet.

blocks.x: 2. Day 184 — Originium worms and two suns

By their count, it's already been 184 days. The four have survived by adopting aliases that fit this planet's way of speaking. Their real names hidden, here they simply live as Ash, Blitz, Frost, and Tachanka.

Tachanka keeps a strange snail called an Originium worm as a pet, and reads through a book written in Ursine, haltingly. "At a glance it looks like Cyrillic, but look closer and it's subtly different."
The village doctor, **Miarow**, hands out medicine to them every time. She herself turns out to be Infected with this planet's incurable disease, Oripathy.
Once infected, the skin gradually crystallizes, and eventually the person dies. There's no cure — only painkillers.

They manage to get their hands on a beat-up car, but the engine is such an unfamiliar design that they can't even fix it. "If there's any good news at all, I guess it's that the driver's seat is on the left," Ash notes in the log.
The Infected are driven out to live in an 'Infected district' outside the village. Somewhere between civilization and savagery, the four of them just try to make it through each day.
3. The great stoning incident, and an empty guardhouse
Armed villagers swarm in front of the Infected district, demanding someone be handed over.
Koetz, who'd gone into the village looking for a mechanic, gets caught red-handed. He tries to bluff his way out with "I'm just a junk collector," but nobody's buying it.
Miarow steps in to stop the mob, but among the crowd are unfamiliar faces mixed in — Sarkaz mercenaries, purpose unknown for now.
When Cohen's group draws their guns, the villagers scatter. But this was only the start of the raid. Fires break out all over the village, and monsters begin bursting up out of the ground.
4. Meanwhile, deep inside a cave

While the village is in chaos, another deal is going down somewhere no one knows about. **Drudge**, the Amir's son, is pressing the old scientist **Levy Klichko**. "Where's that brutal, powerful army you promised me?"
Levy demands corpses be brought to him. Infected ones, preferably.
Uneasy but compliant, Drudge goes along with it. He needs this madman if he's going to push his father out and take the Amir's seat for himself.
Backing Drudge is a Columbian organization called 'Volvo Kochinski.' For the sake of profitable mining rights, they don't care whether it's Sarkaz mercenaries or a mad scientist.
Inside the cave, a grotesque synthetic body wrapped in Originium writhes inside an incubator. Every piece of equipment around it is something that has absolutely no business being on this planet.
"This world has been richly blessed by the great Originium," Levy mutters to himself. No one knows where he really came from, or why he ended up all the way out here.
5. Unboxing the finest weapons of another world
Cohen's group brings the wounded to a Rhodes Island safehouse. The guard **Orpfen**, his leg broken, opens the door for them without protest.
The storeroom inside is a genuine treasure trove. Grenade launchers, mines, explosives of designs they've never seen before.
"Gun users who don't know how to use Originium Arts — how curious," Orpfen says, impressed. The four of them just exchange awkward glances.
On this planet there's something like magic called 'Arts' instead of bullets, and plain bandages instead of healing potions. Both are equally foreign to the four of them.

"A country with no Oripathy, no Infected at all — what on earth is a place like that like?" Orpfen asks, eyes shining. Seeing the four of them stiffen up, he quietly retracts the question. "...Forget I asked that."
"I wonder what kind of sci-fi novels this world has. There must be bookstores, right? History books, too?" Koetz asks eagerly, and Orpfen just looks puzzled. "You all really are strange people."

blocks.cap: Cohen, Koetz, and Tina rummage through crates of explosives they've never seen before. Their first shopping trip in another world.

blocks.x: 6. Polar Bear Logistics, nice to meet you
On the day the safehouse comes under siege, an unfamiliar reinforcement shows up and drives the mercenaries off — the elderly wanderer **Rangers** and his companions, a Rhodes Island field team.
Liskarm's lightning burns everything white before their eyes, and Schwarz's arrows find their marks precisely in the dark. After a few exchanges, the Sarkaz mercenaries withdraw to cut their losses.
"Who do you work for?" Liskarm asks, and Koetz mumbles something vague. Alexandr's improvised mission name in Russian, 'Polar Bear Logistics,' ends up sticking.
Their identity may stay murky, but their purpose is the same. Mercenaries protecting the Infected — both sides find each other strange and yet somehow familiar.
Liskarm attaches an Arts circuit to Koetz's half-broken shield. A flash — and the shield comes alive. "Whoa, whoa! Is this what Arts is?"
The villagers call Rangers the 'Halberd of Bloody Valley' and spin every kind of heroic tale about him. He himself just waves it off. "Back then, I was nothing more or less than an ordinary scout."

blocks.cap: The first technological aid ever given to another world. Liskarm's lightning brought Koetz's shield back to life.

blocks.x: 7. A visit from the guard captain, and a night raid
The next guest was not a welcome one. The Amir's daughter and guard captain, **Piquel**, surrounds the safehouse with her guards. "All of you inside, listen up!"

blocks.cap: Longspring's guard captain Piquel leads her guards to surround the safehouse.

blocks.x: "Kidnapping an Infected — I will not forgive this!" No matter how carefully Cohen tries to refute her point by point, it makes no difference. Piquel orders them to void their contract with Rhodes Island and send the Infected back where they came from.

Piquel's father built his fortune on mining, yet closed the quarry himself once Oripathy began to spread. Having inherited that legacy, Piquel finds it unbearable that these strange outsiders are stirring up trouble in the Infected district.
I'll crush every last one of you and grind you into dust.

"Seems like she's got more muscle than brain," Liskarm and Franka grumble from the back. But that night, on Piquel's group's way back to the manor, a swarm of Originium organisms lay in ambush along their path.
Schwarz, tailing them in secret, spots something behind the ambush — a face he recognizes.
8. Miarow's final prescription
Behind the ambush was Piquel's own older brother, **Drudge**. Having risen up to push his father aside and seize the Amir's seat, he declares he'll turn even his own sister into one of the Infected.
Schwarz's arrow just barely manages to save Piquel. But Miarow, captured alongside her, was dying, her whole body already covered in crystal.
With her last strength, Miarow grips an Originium spike-bomb. An explosive Arts — the one talent she had left.
The explosion shatters Drudge's ultrasonic weapon and scatters the Infected monsters. And Miarow never got up again.
"Tina! Bandages, quick!" Cohen shouts, but the Oripathy has already spread through her entire body. Alexandr grips her hand, repeating the same words.
Don't give up... think of the Columbia you always dreamed about.
Her ragged coughing slowly fades away.

blocks.cap: From where she took her last breath, glowing Originium dust rose up, tearing the darkness apart completely.

blocks.x: 9. A funeral, and what was left in a box
Commoners on this planet aren't even given graves, but the four of them raise a headstone for Miarow beneath a dead tree. 'She was Infected, and she was a doctor. She was a good person.'

"Did we really do the right thing?" Cohen blames himself, and Alexandr brings up an old memory. One night while sleeping on a rooftop, he'd seen a villager load a burlap sack onto a handcart and head toward the edge of the village.

Curious, Alexandr followed and found glowing dust scattering in the wind from between the rocks. The old woman who always used to sit in front of that house — the one the sack had come from — was never seen again after that day.
The local habit of covering one's nose and mouth wasn't only about the plague. It was this planet's own quiet way of mourning the dead, and forgetting them.
Don't blame yourself for something that wasn't your fault. Don't turn your back on the good we've done.
The Infected hand over a box of Miarow's belongings. A Columbia travel guide, an old map, a stack of checks, and a single note.
I don't know who you are, but I hope you live a happy life.
Miarow had held onto a 'dream,' a luxury for someone Infected — the dream of someday going to Columbia and becoming a real doctor. Even in her short time, she'd been quietly planning a future.

blocks.cap: In the shade of the dead tree, Cohen and Alexandr can't bring themselves to leave for a long while.
Inside an old box, the dreams Miarow carried her whole life but never got to live.

blocks.x: 10. The traitor's end, and something newly born
One thing they learned from Miarow: Originium organisms chase after scattered Originium fragments before they follow a Caster's commands. Alexandr places a bet on that fact.
While an Originium engine lures the swarm of monsters away, Cohen's group heads deep into the lab buried in the mine. Locked in a cage there was none other than Drudge himself.
Abandoned by Levy, he now begs for their cooperation. Cohen's answer is his fist.
This one's for Miarow.

Deeper inside the lab, Levy has already finished his preparations. "Behold this world. This is the evolution of life itself — this is the future." Behind the glass wall, inside the incubator, something enormous writhes.
"Praise it! Praise it!!" With Levy's cry, the glass wall shatters, and a dark red spine pierces through his skull. Two bodies fuse and become one.

blocks.cap: Abandoned by Levy, Drudge stands before Cohen — whose fist never stops.
The incubator shatters, and Levy himself becomes his own final creation.

blocks.x: On the surface, Alexandr and old Rangers were pouring out their very last rounds. Even after their magazines ran dry, neither of them backed down.
What Rangers draws is the previous Amir's ceremonial sword. Old and slowed as it may be, its edge is still sharp.
I'm not trying to play the hero, Tina. I'm just trying to do the right thing.

Just as the ammunition ran out, a rain of black arrows came pouring down. Reinforcements from Rhodes Island, called in by Schwarz — the sniper Stormeye. "It's an honor to fight beside you, Halberd of Bloody Valley."
Epilogue. A new name

Two weeks later, Piquel expresses her thanks to the four of them. When she tells them to take whatever they like from what's left in the manor instead of a reward, Alexandr picks out a helmet, and Koetz takes a shimmering claw ornament.
"You could've taken a weapon, or a family heirloom..." Piquel hesitates, and Cohen answers. "This is enough. You'll need it more than we will."

Rhodes Island grants the four of them an identity that lets them use its mission network, though not as formal contracted staff. They get supplies from the safehouse and get paid only for the missions they complete. This time, there's no plan to go back to Earth.
"Let's learn about this world. First stop's supposed to be a city called Siesta," Cohen says. Alexandr answers, "Until the day we go home, let's keep moving."

blocks.cap: No lab, no map. The four of them walk once more into the strange desert.

blocks.x: This isn't Earth. But it's a place with Oripathy too, and hatred too, and people who were lost too. The handful of dust Miarow left behind still drifts on the wind somewhere on this planet, even today.

act16d5.tagline: A story where an errand to fetch Nian's little sister turns into a whole mess trapped inside a single painting. And whether the people inside that painting were real or not, the time spent laughing and crying together was never a lie.

chars.name: Lava
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator dispatched deep into Yan's backcountry at Nian's request to find her sister. Diligent and quick on the uptake.

chars.name: Kroos
chars.desc: A sniper who comes along with Lava. Looks laid-back, but reads situations fastest of anyone.

chars.name: Mr. Nothing
chars.desc: A suspicious messenger who's rescued from a pack of Gloompincers and tags along claiming to be a guide. Turns out even his name was made up on the spot.

chars.name: Saga
chars.desc: A wandering monk from Higashi who rings the bell every day in Basan Village. Unusually well-versed in the affairs of this picture-perfect village.

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: Lava's Rhodes Island colleague. The one who dumped this mission on her, asking her to go find her little sister.

chars.name: Dusk
chars.desc: The little sister Nian hasn't seen in ages. Almost nothing is known about her except that she's a painter.

chars.name: Lai
chars.desc: The owner of a pawnshop in Basan Village. Minds his nearly empty shop alone, speaking only in riddles.

chars.name: Lan
chars.desc: A village child who used to follow the storyteller around. After the New Year's Eve incident, she's desperately looking for her mother.

chars.name: Beagle
chars.desc: Kroos's old Rhodes Island colleague. All anyone's heard lately is that she's settled down somewhere.

terms.name: Painting-Dweller
terms.desc: Someone who lives inside a painting. The title and central question running through this whole story.

terms.name: Basan Village
terms.desc: The village Lava's group stumbles into after losing their way. The sun and moon hang in the same sky, and the farther east to west you go, the closer it gets to night.

terms.name: Inkwraith
terms.desc: An ink-shaped monster that raids Basan Village every New Year's Eve. Saga's name for it means 'the ink-born wangliang.'

terms.name: True View of Jolsan
terms.desc: A painting said to have been left by an unnamed painter roughly a hundred years ago. Saga went looking for the true scenery inside it and vanished as though he'd disappeared.

terms.name: The Legend of Seok-a's Flight to the Moon
terms.desc: An old tale Saga heard as a child. The legend of someone who flew alone to the moon to win back heaven's love.

terms.name: Zhenlong
terms.desc: Yan's legendary young emperor. Said to have subdued the arrogant gods of heaven and earth to found the Yan of today.

blocks.x: The favor that landed on Rhodes Island operator **Lava** seemed simple enough. Her colleague **Nian** just wanted her to go find 'Dusk,' the little sister she hadn't seen in ages.

Deep in the mountains of Yan's backcountry, this errand seemed like it would be over the moment she opened a single thatched-hut door. Instead, the moment that door opened, it turned into a completely different story.
Beyond the door waited a village with the sun and moon sharing the same sky, and people living their lives with no idea they were beings inside a painting.
1. The Messenger Who Swore to Repay a Debt, Whose Very Name Turned Out to Be Made Up
It started with saving a strange man being chased by a pack of Gloompincers. He introduced himself as a 'messenger,' and later, made up the name '**Mr. Nothing**' on the spot.
Lava caught on immediately. "You just made that up, didn't you?!" Still, since Kroos seems to find him amusing, they decide to bring him along for now.
A month earlier, aboard Rhodes Island's flagship, Nian had said, "I know it's a hassle, but I'd like you to bring my little sister back." That was the entirety of the explanation.
Nian had already secured Closure's approval and arranged for Kroos to come along. For Lava, it was less a request than a notification.
Before they left, Nian pressed a talisman into her hand and said, "She's my little sister, after all..." Lava didn't catch the unease hidden in those words at the time.
On top of that, Nian let slip a teasing remark before they parted. "That stage costume from Ancient Forge, you actually liked it, didn't you?" Lava denied it firmly, but it was already too late.
2. They Opened the Door, and the Sun and Moon Were Hanging There Together
Following the address Nian gave them, the three climb Mount Huiji and open the door of a thatched hut in the mountains. And what happened after that, none of the three can remember at all.

When they come to, they're in an unfamiliar garden, listening to an old tale from a storyteller called 'Master Jasan.' When they mention they're looking for someone, the storyteller only tilts his head.

Just as the three, sensing something strange, are leaving the garden, Lava looks up at the sky and freezes. It's broad daylight, yet lanterns are hanging across the bridge, and up in the sky, the sun and moon are hanging side by side.

blocks.cap: The moment noon and dusk share the same sky, the three realize they're standing somewhere far from Niong Village.

blocks.x: To make matters worse, the moment a bell rings, the villagers start screaming and running. "A monster's coming from the western mountain! Get somewhere the sunlight reaches!"
Unidentified black things come swarming in, and Lava and Kroos decide to fight first and ask questions later. Mr. Nothing, caught off guard, just tags along after them.
Whether cut or shot, the things simply turn into black stains and vanish without a drop of blood. "I'm not seeing any sign of infection..." Even Kroos can't get a read on them.
3. This Is Basan Village, and Even the Era Name Was One They'd Never Heard

Only after driving off the monsters do they finally get a proper explanation from Master Jasan. This place is 'Basan Village,' and right now, apparently, is New Year's Eve of the seventh year of Jingzuo.
Mr. Nothing asks under his breath. "Jingzuo? I've never even heard of that era..." A chill runs down everyone's spine.
Even stranger was the geography. From east to west, the village runs the entire span from day to night, so the sun and moon just hang fixed in place and never rise or set.

And every year on New Year's Eve, monsters come down from Mount Hongdong and terrorize the village for thirty-five days straight. Master Jasan says the only answer he's ever found is simply 'hiding wherever the sun still reaches.'
Lava proposes a deal: they'll help the village get through this night safely, in exchange for a way out of here. Master Jasan agrees, somehow, without much resistance.
4. The Bell-Ringing Monk, and a Pawnshop Owner Hiding Something

A wandering monk named **Saga**, who keeps the bell tower, shows up in the village. He was also the first to notice that when he swings his sword, the monsters vanish, leaving nothing but an ink stain behind.
Saga gave these monsters the name '**Inkwraith**,' meaning a wangliang spirit that vanishes like ink. He claims he's just being modest, but everyone's already impressed.

Facing off against an Inkwraith, Saga mutters, "The length of a painting shifts from time to time, and the scenery here and there turns strange." Before Lava can even ask what he means, Saga's already calmly cutting down the next monster.

In the middle of the commotion, the pawnshop owner **Lai** shows his face for the first time. And yet, standing in front of an Inkwraith and saying, "Alright, alright, calm down already," as if soothing it, feels far from ordinary.
When Lava asks about it, Lai denies it flat out, calm as ever. "No, never seen it before." Only Saga, completely oblivious to the awkward air, is simply happy to see the two of them reunited.

Meanwhile, the village child **Lan** tags along after Kroos, asking, "They say you're the hero who saved the village. So you must be super strong, right?!" Kroos answers, unbothered, "A hero's just someone who wants to help other people, that's all."
5. Sleep, Wake Up, and the Whole Village Is Reborn
The next morning, Lan's mother, whom they'd thought was dead, is walking around perfectly fine. The eaves that had been smashed, the alley that had been trampled, all back to how they were.

Saga explains it plainly. When the four of them, people from outside the painting, close their eyes, this world has no one left watching it, and quietly slips back to how it was. Which means this really was inside a painting all along.

The pawnshop owner Lai poses a cryptic question to Lava. "Do you intend to go to all that trouble fishing the moon out of the water?" After all, once the waves settle, the moon goes back to being round regardless.
The moon in the water may shatter into any shape amid the ripples, but once the wind and waves die down and stillness returns, it becomes the round moon once more.
But at the next attack, the rules break down. Inkwraiths swarm in even in broad daylight, and this time, someone is really bitten. A villager who's lost her husband screams in anguish.
"Why! Why did my husband have to die!"
The crying sounds far too real to just stand by and tell themselves none of it's real. Lava and Kroos end up taking up sword and crossbow once again.
6. Leaving Behind Only a Fan, He Decides Not to Settle the Score

That night, Mr. Nothing, who's been playing it cool this whole time, finally opens up with the real story. At thirteen, he was practically sold into the Lian family's martial arts dojo, and grew up under his master for over ten years.

But last year, he ended up killing someone in a match. It turned out to be a trap set by people trying to tear down the fading dojo's reputation. In the name of 'the rules,' his master bled out in front of everyone and passed away.
All that was left to Mr. Nothing was his master's fan, the Yinqing Fan. Ever since, he's lived on the run, hiding who he is, posing as a fortune-teller, posing as a messenger.
Someday... I'll go back to Jiuwu Fortress. I'll go back and settle this.
So this was the reason Mr. Nothing has been hiding his skills, and the reason Kroos had noticed long ago but never asked.
Lava isn't particularly surprised. "You might fool other people, but you can't fool me." That one line alone sets Mr. Nothing off crying again.
7. One Bamboo Firecracker, and Lai's Cryptic Hint
Lai drops a subtle hint. This village has never had a thing called 'firecrackers,' but if they make some, the Inkwraiths should be afraid of them. The material: bamboo from Master Jasan's garden.
"It'll grow back by tomorrow anyway." Without a moment's hesitation, Lai chops down an entire full-grown bamboo for them.

Lava takes charge of managing the fire, and as the bamboo crackles and burns, the Inkwraiths really do get scared and flee. And so the three of them start a new nightly routine, lighting a bonfire at the village entrance every evening.
Then one day, Master Jasan lets slip a question. "...Nian?" And yet no one had ever said that name.
8. The Monk Who Napped for Ten Years, and a Painting Within a Painting
Saga, who'd shut himself in his room alone, finally opens his eyes after seven days. Master Jasan asks, "So you've finally woken from that long dream... How much time has passed?"
Saga's answer is perfectly calm. "I watched the plum blossoms bloom and fall ten times over." Counting it out, that comes to a full ten years.
Years earlier, Saga had stepped into yet another painting, one of Mount Cheonak, and quietly lived out ten years there. What he just stepped out of was only that one painting.
**Dusk**, the owner of the Mount Cheonak painting, finally appears before Saga. Saga asks the question he's carried for ten years. "Is the person inside a painting real, or false?"
Dusk asks back, "You know full well they're a person inside a painting, so why ask whether they're real?" Then she gives her answer, calmly.
You and I, all of us, are painting-dwellers. People inside a painting.
Dusk shows Saga an old memory. The sight of a young head monk walking for three days and nights among the starved dead, praying the whole way.

blocks.cap: The old memory Dusk opens up. Saga's teacher learned the mercy he'd carry for the rest of his life by walking through this ravaged field.

blocks.x: Only then does it become clear why Saga's teacher had secretly added one more character, 'still,' to the painting. It meant, 'Jolsan is not yet finished.' Saga sets off again, his face unburdened at last.
9. The Big Sister Finally Finds the Little Sister Trapped in the Painting

As it turns out, Mr. Nothing and Kroos had each been trapped in their own dream. Mr. Nothing's, dying all over again without ever avenging his master. Kroos's, meeting her old comrade **Beagle** again only to watch her fade away, hollow and empty.

It was Nian who woke them up. "If I hadn't come looking for you, you'd have had to live through everything again, starting from the moment you left Jiuwu Fortress." Nian says it as though it were nothing at all.
Nian tells Lava an old story. Long, long ago, Yan's young emperor **Zhenlong** subdued the arrogant gods of heaven and earth, or so the story goes.

blocks.cap: Zhenlong's crest, from the old story Nian tells. A name that isn't unrelated to the sisters' current dispute.

blocks.x: "Those beings are still out there across Terra today, just wearing different shapes." Nian says it with a smile, but there's something urgent hiding underneath those words.
Before long, Dusk appears. She bristles at being called Nian's "good little sister," but in the end, gives in and joins the conversation anyway.
"You can't afford to be more confused than me. Think about this long history, how many times have their shadows shown up already?" The more Nian talks, the stiffer Dusk's expression gets.
"We have to be ready, for our own sake, and for the sake of what we love." At Nian's words, Dusk finally raises the white flag. "Fine, I admit I lost the bet."
Right on cue, Saga wakes up and greets them, a grain of rice still stuck to his face. "Your elder sister, Teacher? What an honor to meet you!" The sisters' tense standoff deflates in an instant.

Once the mission wraps up, Mr. Nothing grabs Lava, eyes shining. "Does Rhodes Island happen to be hiring, by any chance? I can do just about anything!" Caught off guard, Lava ends up giving something close to a yes.
Epilogue. Basan Village Existed Both in a Painting and in Memory
Years pass, and an old, ailing **Lai** meets Dusk one last time. "You saved my life, and gave me the chance to survive... and even now, at the very end, you've come to see me like this."
Lai has a request. All he remembers is the village's name, the pawnshop next door, and a single mountain, but he asks her to paint Basan Village for him, in his place.
Was my life... a happy one, in the end?
To Lai's final question, Dusk gives no answer at all.
The scene shifts to Rhodes Island. Dusk, brought along by Nian, is still as prickly as ever, but she stays up all night watching movies, and quietly schemes to sneak into Nian's mahjong game.

Then Dusk suddenly asks, "You still haven't told me what kind of people the Doctor and Kal'tsit are. Do the two of them have something to do with why you're here?" Nian just smiles and puts off the answer. "Once you hear the whole thing... you'll understand why I like it here."

The errand to go find Nian's little sister ended up becoming a journey across an entire painting. Master Jasan, Saga's ten years, Lai's whole lifetime, all of it was a story inside a painting Dusk had made. But the time spent laughing and crying within it was never a lie to anyone.

act15d0.tagline: The Ecology Department chief who came to interrogate gets four months' worth of prison-break secrets out of them for the price of a drink — but still walks away without the one card that mattered most. All because of one line: 'A person is not a clue.'

chars.name: Silence
chars.desc: A Rescue Department researcher at Rhine Lab who has started to doubt the company. The architect of this prison break.

chars.name: Mayer
chars.desc: An Engineering Department colleague who tagged along with Silence. Also a decent cook.

chars.name: Muelsyse
chars.desc: Chief of Rhine Lab's Ecology Department. The one who rented out an entire bar just to start this interrogation.

chars.name: Kafka
chars.desc: Silence's longtime friend. True to her con-artist roots, she brazenly infiltrates the prison.

chars.name: Robin
chars.desc: A woman who took on a contract killing to pay for her father's treatment. She enters the prison disguised as a cleaner.

chars.name: Anthony
chars.desc: The only son of the fallen Simon family. He has been locked up in Mansfield Prison for six years.

chars.name: Domma
chars.desc: An undertaker born and raised in this prison. Anthony's only friend.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: An Operator of Rhodes Island. She offers advice on Silence's personal investigation.

chars.name: Jesselton
chars.desc: A suspicious man posing as a prison guard. He secretly keeps watch on Robin.

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: The massive science conglomerate that Silence, Mayer, and Muelsyse all belong to. Its influence reaches across Columbia.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: Where Silence went after leaving Rhine Lab. It plays an unexpected role in this incident.

terms.name: Mansfield Prison
terms.desc: A mobile prison that roams the wasteland. It docks in a city only once every few months.

terms.name: Hydrobro
terms.desc: A building-materials firm in Bunker Hill City. The one responsible for bringing down Simon Company.

terms.name: Simon Company
terms.desc: The company Anthony's family used to run. It collapsed after being charged with smuggling Originium goods.

terms.name: the "Diabolic" Incident
terms.desc: An experiment accident within Rhine Lab. It's what first made Silence start doubting the company.

terms.name: Columbia
terms.desc: The country where this whole story takes place. Commercial rivalry here is as fierce as war.

blocks.x: Rhine Lab researcher Silence and Engineering Department's Mayer were waiting for someone at a quiet bar on Columbia's border. The person they were expecting never showed up — instead, an unidentified internal-line call came through. 'Anthony is in your hands, and I know you helped him break out.'

The person who showed up instead was Muelsyse, chief of Rhine Lab's Ecology Department. She laughed, saying she'd rented out the whole bar — all to grill them thoroughly about the prison break that had happened at state-run Mansfield Prison four months earlier.

Silence was the mastermind behind that prison break. But even before the story began, Muelsyse already knew one thing: that the escape involved one more name — one that even Silence herself hadn't known about.
1. An interrogation with the whole bar rented out — but drinks are on the house
Muelsyse introduced herself first as chief of the Ecology Department. The rule was simple: one question, one answer, taking turns.

blocks.cap: Muelsyse: Life always needs a bit of formality, doesn't it?

blocks.x: Silence sent Mayer off to the kitchen first. She still hadn't told Mayer that Rhine Lab's Energy Department was behind this whole affair.

Anthony, the only son of the fallen **Simon Company**. The one who brought that family down was Hydrobro, a building-materials firm in Bunker Hill City — and behind Hydrobro stood Ferdinand, chief of the Energy Department.

blocks.cap: Two companies that once split the market down the middle. In the end, only one survived.

blocks.x: 2. The rookie con artist takes over the prison — one winning hand at a time
Kafka was a con artist. The moment she got into prison, she started fleecing the inmates' pocket change at card games.

blocks.cap: Muelsyse: I want to know how your accomplice operated inside the prison, step by step.

blocks.x: This prison was mobile. Uninfected inmates were held in Zone A, the Infected in Zone B, and Zone C was the tower reserved for the most violent offenders.
The guards actually enjoyed betting on brawls between inmates — the winning side supposedly got better meals. Kafka used the chaos as cover to dig up information here and there.
And in the Zone C tower, Kafka finally heard the name. Anthony — the strongest yet most courteous inmate in the prison, someone even the guards couldn't push around.
3. The undercover killer gets knocked out by someone else's fight on her first day
**Robin** had also entered the prison around that time. On the surface a cleaner, but in reality a contract killer sent after Anthony.
After her father's business failed and he fell ill, Robin took the job to pay for his treatment. She landed a cleaning assignment and managed to make it all the way into Anthony's cell.
But before she could even make her move, an entirely different team of assassins burst in first — other killers also after Anthony.

blocks.cap: Guards and several inmates were set loose all at once. Robin was simply caught in the crossfire.

blocks.x: Caught up in the fight, Robin was knocked out in an instant. The last thing she saw before losing consciousness was an unfamiliar inmate's back, shielding her. It was Anthony.
4. The man who bluntly asks his lifesaver to help him break out
In truth, Kafka had only been playing unconscious. While everyone else was out cold, she quietly spoke to Anthony. 'Call me Kafka. I'm here to help you.'
Kafka told him the truth — Simon Company had already been seized over Originium smuggling charges, and his whole family was serving time in a Bunker Hill City prison.
Anthony slammed his fist on the table beside him, sending things clattering to the floor. But he soon spoke calmly. 'I'm just glad my family is still alive.'
Then he bowed his head. 'Please help me escape.' For the first time in six years, the prison's most free inmate had spoken his true wish.
5. The guilt Silence carries, and two thieves gossiping behind the scenes
But why did Silence take on such a reckless venture in the first place? With a bit of liquid courage in her, she finally opened up.

The **'Diabolic' Incident** that occurred within Rhine Lab. Test subject Ifrit went through something no one should have to endure, and Saria took full responsibility and left the company. Watching the aftermath, Silence began doubting Rhine Lab for the very first time.

After transferring to Rhodes Island, she went back through some old records her mentor had once left behind. That's how she stumbled onto traces connected to the fall of the Simon family — something that had started as nothing more than idle curiosity.
When she asked Rhodes Island's Kal'tsit for advice, there was just one thing she said. 'I hope you can hold to the belief that what you're doing is good, all the way to the end.'

Drunk, Silence finally broke down in front of Mayer. 'I always thought that if I just tried hard enough, I could solve any problem. But this time, I'm scared I might not have the courage to solve it.'

Meanwhile, down in the prison basement, Kafka was secretly meeting with Mina, who was disguised as a cleaner. Mina bitterly remarked that Anthony had once saved her family, but the Anthony she saw now felt like a different person.

Kafka's reason for helping Silence was simple. 'Because Silence is a good person.' Mina pointed out the irony — that helping such a good person meant, of all things, breaking someone else out of prison.

No matter how good your intentions are, if what you're doing is wrong, you can't call yourself a good person, can you? If that's the case, it's better to just admit from the start that you're a bad person.
6. Jesselton reveals to Robin the true name of her enemy

But Robin had another shadow following her. A man posing as a prison guard, **Jesselton**. He too was actually a killer after Anthony, and he had secretly overheard the moment Anthony asked Robin for help escaping.
Jesselton dropped a truth on Robin: it was none other than the Simon family's last desperate act that had brought down her father's company, Blackcloud.
Pain is equal for everyone. Can you say your pain matters less than Anthony's?
Robin answered with silence. Her father's surgery costs, the things Anthony couldn't give her, the things Jesselton could. In the end, she gave a short answer.
'...I'll do it.'
7. Why the undertaker hasn't left the prison in six years
The escape plan was starting to come together. The prison was an artificial structure built in the shape of a mountain, and the Zone C tower at its center was designed to sink underground.

blocks.cap: Silence: I'll skip the prison's exterior for now. Okay, the structure looks roughly like this.

blocks.x: Trigger the trap from the control room at the top of the tower to sink it, cut the power to cause chaos, then escape through the tunnel Domma had dug. That was the plan.
But **Domma**, a member of the team herself, had no intention of leaving with them at all. Born and raised in this prison, she had never once been outside.
'Going outside... scares me,' Domma said. Even so, she was ready to risk her life to help with Anthony's escape. Robin tried to comfort her.
'You're the one who deserves a better life.'
8. On the day of the plan, only Robin didn't show up

On the day they were to go up the tower disguised as cleaning staff, only Robin failed to show. Kafka muttered anxiously, 'If you're going to blame someone, blame yourself for not being here at a moment this urgent.'
Kafka and Anthony headed to the control room as planned. Fumbling with the wrong buttons a few times, they finally managed to trigger the trap.

blocks.cap: The dust-covered control room. The trap was pressed for the first time in twenty-five years.

blocks.x: That's exactly when Robin appeared. She had secretly followed along using a spare card. With an explosion, the power went out, and the tower began sinking underground with a deafening roar.
Just as Robin was about to fall into the elevator shaft, Anthony caught her. 'Clench your back teeth, don't bite your tongue!' In the pitch darkness, the tower sank down underground like that.
9. The traitor's true choice
In the powerless corridor, Robin finally told him everything — who sent her into the prison, the real reason her father's company collapsed, and the choice she was making right now.
'I'm so tired. I don't want to judge what's right or wrong, good or bad anymore. Why should I have to!' Robin shouted.
You mustn't hand your right to choose over to someone else. We have no choice but to rely on ourselves.
Anthony added that he too was afraid of making mistakes, but that didn't mean he could avoid choosing. Robin turned those words over in her mind for a long while.
But then, from Domma's room at the end of the corridor, a familiar voice rang out. 'Oh, don't worry about not being able to find me. I'll be right here, waiting for you.'
10. The countdown, and the man covered in steel plating
In Domma's room, Jesselton was waiting, having had an inmate take her hostage. 'I'll give you five seconds, Ms. Robin.' He began the countdown.
Three, two... Robin finally took a step. She slowly moved toward Anthony.
But where her blade struck... wasn't Anthony. It was the throat of the inmate holding Domma.

Jesselton's hand turned black and transformed into a blade. Not only could he manipulate iron with Originium Arts, but he'd even had metal plating made with Rhine Lab technology implanted under his skin.

blocks.cap: Against the restraining device, Anthony's fist was no different from pounding on a steel plate.

blocks.x: 'You were someone whose strength showed through simply by existing. I was looking forward to the moment you'd cast off that pretense.' Jesselton pressed on.

Instead of answering, Anthony wrung out his last strength. 'You have no right to judge my true nature!!!' But no matter how hard he struck, that hand wouldn't budge. 'More guards will be swarming in soon. It seems your escape plan ends here.'

Just then, a familiar voice cut in. 'Unfortunately, the god of death won't be coming for a while yet.' One of the patrolling guards revealed her true identity, as if pulling off a mask. It was **Saria**, chief of Rhine Lab's Defense Department.

blocks.cap: Saria's hand turned white. Robin murmured, 'It's white... and pretty.'

blocks.x: Jesselton's black blade clashed against Saria's white blade. One step, two steps, three steps. It was always Jesselton who was pushed back.

blocks.cap: 'You're far too complacent and stubborn. I have no need for a subordinate like that.' Saria's blade split Jesselton's.

blocks.x: Leaving the fallen Jesselton behind, Saria asked Anthony about an unfamiliar name. 'Have you ever heard the name Ferdinand?' Anthony nodded. It was a name his father used to mention often.
But that savior was originally Muelsyse's card
Having heard the whole story, Muelsyse sighed. 'Why—was Saria—at Mansfield—of all places!'

Silence traced back through it. Saria had originally been Muelsyse's collaborator, but once she got her hands on the card named Anthony, she broke their agreement and handed him over to Rhodes Island instead.
Muelsyse admitted it without a fight. 'Correct. I got played by Saria.' Then she turned around and offered Silence a deal — hand over Anthony, and she'd give her a chance to return to Rhine Lab.
Silence flatly refused. 'To me, Anthony isn't just a clue — he's a living person. I don't think I should keep him around as a card for the future. I think helping him is the right thing to do.'

As the negotiation fell through, Muelsyse laughed. 'You don't actually think I'd just let him go that easily, do you?' At that moment, Silence called out Anthony, whom she'd left back at their lodging. The claim that he'd already left Columbia had been a lie from the start.
If you want to do the right thing, you first need to know what the right thing actually is. And also — doing the right thing doesn't mean there's no price to pay.
Leaving that last piece of advice, Muelsyse dissolved away like water. The whole time they'd been talking face to face, it had only been her avatar.
All that remained in the bar were Silence, Mayer, and Anthony — just the three of them. In the end, that one call four months ago had brought one person not to prison, but to Rhodes Island.
'What kind of place is Rhodes Island?' Anthony asked. 'It's a long way from here to there, so let me explain slowly.' Not as a clue, but as a person — and so a new journey began.

act13d5.tagline: The youngest daughter of the Nearl family picks up a sword with hands that used to fix motorcycles, dives into the corporations' gambling ring, and six years later even calls her sister down from the sky. Light, it turns out, isn't inherited — you light it yourself.

chars.name: Maria Nearl
chars.desc: The youngest daughter of the Nearl family. She'd rather be tinkering with motorcycles than mastering Originium Arts, but she debuts in Knight Sports to defend what's left of her family's pride.

chars.name: Sophia
chars.desc: The Nearl family's longtime butler and a former Knight Sports fighter. She explodes with anger at Maria's reckless gamble, only to end up volunteering as her coach.

chars.name: Margaret Nearl
chars.desc: Maria's older sister, who left Kazimierz six years ago. All that remains of her is a legend of three consecutive tournament sweeps · Operator Nearl

chars.name: Młynar
chars.desc: Maria's uncle. A meek company man in front of the corporations, yet he flatly opposes Maria's foray into Knight Sports.

chars.name: Bald Martin
chars.desc: A retired knight who lost an arm, now running a tavern. A longtime benefactor and advisor to the Nearl family.

chars.name: Spokesperson Czarny
chars.desc: A spokesperson for the Commercial Federation. He circles around Maria with soft words, sponsorship deals, and quiet persuasion.

chars.name: Platinum
chars.desc: The youngest 'Platinum' of Kazimierz's Armorless Union. She handles back-alley jobs with sharpshooting skill.

chars.name: Shining
chars.desc: A Sarkaz doctor passing through Kazimierz on his travels. He quietly treats the wounded, then moves on.

chars.name: Nightingale
chars.desc: Shining's traveling companion. She looks at the lights of this unfamiliar city with wonder.

chars.name: Senomi
chars.desc: A young messenger from a family of knights on campaign. She has a knack for relaying her grandfather's nagging word-for-word · Operator Gravel

terms.name: Kazimierz
terms.desc: A city-state of Terra where Originium technology meets commercial capital. Its offspring is the gambling spectacle known as Knight Sports.

terms.name: Knight Sports
terms.desc: Kazimierz's national pastime, where corporations sponsor knights and pit them against each other for bets. Buzz and ad exposure matter more than who wins.

terms.name: Nearl Family
terms.desc: Once a storied knight family that represented Kazimierz. Now fallen on hard times, on the verge of losing its noble status.

terms.name: Armorless Union
terms.desc: A secret organization behind the Commercial Federation, handling assassination and surveillance.

terms.name: Commercial Federation
terms.desc: A federation representing Kazimierz's corporations. It effectively controls the nominal Knights' Association from behind the scenes.

terms.name: Eye of Pegasus
terms.desc: A nickname passed down through generations of the Nearl family. It refers to the intuition and Arts sense that sees through a crisis in an instant.

blocks.x: Kazimierz is a dazzling city-state born where Originium technology meets corporate capital. And the most popular entertainment here is 'Knight Sports' — knights beating each other senseless while corporations slap their ads on top.

The Nearl family, once the pride of this nation, is now on the verge of ruin. Six years ago, the eldest daughter Margaret left Kazimierz behind, leaving only the name 'the Radiant Knight.' All that's left is the youngest daughter, Maria, who's good for nothing but fixing motorcycles.
Maria makes up her mind. To protect what's left of the family's pride, she'll step into the arena herself. The problem is, her swordsmanship amounts to nothing more than copying her sister.
1. Hands That Once Fixed Motorcycles Now Grip a Sword

In front of the tavern, while the old knight Poe and craftsman Koval bicker over a motorcycle accident, Maria quietly fixes the broken engine with a touch of Originium Arts. This ridiculous duo has been at it for decades.
A few hours later, Maria is standing in the middle of the Lore Arena. When MC Bigmouth Mob calls out her name, the stands stir — on the strength of the surname 'Nearl' alone.
Word reaches Sophia — the Nearl family's butler and a former Knight Sports fighter known as 'Whislash' — who kicks the door open and grabs Maria. 'Have you lost your mind?!'

But Maria's reasons are firm. Since their grandfather passed, her uncle has refused to have anything to do with the Association, her sister has already been exiled, and the Nearl family will lose its noble status next year.
In the end, Sophia throws up her hands — and volunteers to be her coach instead. Starting the next morning, the weed-choked garden becomes a training ground.

That night, Uncle Młynar leaves just one line before heading upstairs. 'Even if we lose our noble title, our principles won't waver. Don't turn into Margaret.' By the next day, the sports headline was already written: 'Return of the Radiant Knight? The Nearl Family's Rookie Makes Her Debut.'
2. Her Debut Opponent Is 'Mr. Plastic' — But His Armor Runs Way Too Hot
The Nearl family motto... Fear neither hardship nor darkness!

blocks.cap: The Lore Arena, Little Nearl's debut match. The crowd had no idea yet what that name would come to mean.

blocks.x: Her opponent is Ševčík, 'the Knight of Plastic,' of the Lore Knights. In truth, he'd been ordered to stall for time — a promotional stunt for his sponsor Lore Guard Company's new armor line.

The order came from Commercial Federation spokesperson Czarny. 'Tests like this, and the promotion that comes with them, are necessary if we want to land a long-term contract.' Three times his after-tax income from last year was riding on Ševčík.
Early on, Maria is completely outmatched. Her sword goes flying, and his armor doesn't so much as scratch. But instead of fighting back, Maria starts watching.
"That armor isn't venting heat."

She zeroes in on the new armor's cooling flaw and turns the match around in one decisive blow. Neither the crowd nor the sponsor saw it coming. From the next day on, scouting offers start piling up at the Nearl doorstep.
3. A Run-In With the Butcher: A Bloody Second Match Ends in a Draw

blocks.cap: A duel with no rules and no protection. Her opponent picks up an axe, grinning.

blocks.x: Her next opponent is Olmer Ingra, 'the Knight of Rusted Copper.' He's crippled multiple opponents and walked away scot-free every time, thanks to a National Assembly blinded by corporate donations — a true murderer.

blocks.cap: "A knight is a noble being who lights up the whole world."

blocks.x: Ingra toys with Maria, driving her into the ground. Bloodied, a memory of something her sister once said flashes through Maria's mind.
In the end, it's a draw — both of them collapse. When Maria comes to, she finds beside her Sophia's old sword, long unused, left there for her.
At the celebration, Martin raises his mechanical arm to show her. 'This is what recklessness gets you.' He once won in the arena too, but now even washing a single cup is a struggle.

A bit of Sophia's own past slips out too — how nine years ago she was badly wounded by 'the Knight of the Fissure,' and never accepted sponsorship from any corporation for it. Maria falls quiet, hearing the story for the first time.
4. Tangled Up With Two Squirrels
A fifteen-person brawl. Here Maria runs into 'the Knight of the Blazing Tail,' Sona, who grins slyly and says.
"You know what? I'm actually one of the Infected."

Sona and her companion 'the Knight of the Grey Brush-Tail' are knights who came up as Infected. Hearing that they had to survive the Gloomfincer pen just to reclaim status as ordinary citizens, Maria is left speechless.

The Association allows the Infected to compete, but never lets them become a pillar of society. So there's a separate arena just for Infected fighters — nominally called 'bonus matches,' but really just a plaything of a fight.

In the end, Sona lets Maria off the hook. 'Consider it tuition for teaching you what Whislash didn't.' And she leaves her with a warning: the underside of Knight Sports is far scarier than Maria could ever imagine.

blocks.cap: In the hall lined with portraits of champions, there's a place for 'the Radiant Knight' — still unfilled.

blocks.x: Meanwhile, in that same hallway, Armorless Union sniper Platinum receives a new assignment from Spokesperson Czarny: keep watch on the Nearl family's uncle, Młynar. 'Whether this job can be seen through to the end is something you'll need to confirm for yourself, miss.'
5. The Sponsors Come Calling, and She Politely Turns Them All Down

As the wins pile up, scouting offers pour in. From the conglomerate Swarma to Commercial Federation spokesperson Czarny himself, they all come knocking in person. 'What exactly is a knight trying to prove?'

Maria turns them all down. 'I won't let the Nearl crest become some corporation's accessory.' One company man even tears up hearing her answer. 'It's been so long since I've seen good knights get a good ending.'

But the one who actually objects is Sophia. A Swarma employee who'd been championing Maria let slip an unexpected rumor on his way out — that Maria had started catching the eye of the Association's upper echelons.
You could die in that arena!
'That's enough already. Stop here.' Sophia is dead serious. It isn't losing or getting hurt she's afraid of — it's the name Nearl catching the Association's 'attention.'

Maria doesn't back down either. 'I'll keep winning until I can't anymore.' Sophia storms out furious, and that night Uncle Młynar comes by again, snapping coldly. 'You're just like your sister. I can barely stand to look at you.'
6. The Spokesperson Comes Calling a Second Time

This time Czarny comes straight to the tavern. His purpose: announcing the next match — her opponent will be Titus Topola, 'the Left-Handed Knight,' a noble knight who once made it to the semifinals and commander of the Bladehelm Knights.
Old knight Poe gets chills seeing Czarny casually recall his retirement name from half a century ago, 'Batbayar.' 'He's digging into all of our backgrounds.'

Czarny calmly lays out his own logic. 'It's not that I've thrown away my honor. I've simply bought a new television and put the old radio away in a drawer.' To the old knights, it just sounds like sophistry.
That night, Sona and her companion, injured and on the run, knock on the tavern's back door. The Nearl household hides them without a second thought. In that moment, Martin mutters under his breath.
"They're here."
7. The Left-Handed Knight Tries to Snap Nearl's Sword in Two

blocks.cap: The match of the era, the arena packed to capacity. And an emcee being told he isn't even fit to hold the mic.

blocks.x: Titus openly compares Maria to Margaret and mocks her. 'You're just a feeble, shoddy imitation.' Her sword keeps flying out of her hand, and her body keeps hitting the floor.
He doesn't hold back the provocations either. 'The best way to destroy a knight is to strip away her dignity.' Maria's light gets crumpled, broken, sullied.

Guards block Sophia as she tries to rush toward the stands. The tavern regulars can only stomp their feet in frustration, and outside the arena, Spokesperson Czarny watches it all with calm eyes. 'A half-hearted approach like that just gives the opponent an opening.'
8. For Myself...... For My Own Sake
Just as her vision blurs, she hears her grandfather's voice. 'Maria, you carry the Eye of Pegasus. Rise.' Maria grips her sword once more.
When Titus asks why on earth she's holding on this hard, Maria answers, short and simple.
For myself...... For my own sake.

blocks.cap: The moment she raised her sword, even the pain in her arm was drowned out by the crowd's roar.

blocks.x: After an hour and twenty-seven minutes of brutal fighting, a dramatic reversal ends the match. The crowd goes wild and the betting total hits an all-time high. And yet Maria feels strangely empty.

'Who did I actually beat? What did I even gain?' She's won honor, wealth, the family's revival — and yet something feels missing. Only then does Sophia let go of her worry. 'You really are like your sister... you needed to choose your own path.'
Maria shakes her head. 'Maybe I'm really not like her at all.'
9. A Missing Partner, and Two Strange Assassins Behind Snowyhill

On the day of the 2-on-2 match, her partner 'the Knight of Fangs' doesn't show up. The papers report he 'went missing after getting caught up in a fan brawl,' but Sophia senses something's very wrong.

Instead of forfeiting, Maria steps into the arena alone. And the two figures who emerge with the fog aren't from the Snowyhill Knights at all. 'The Corroded Knight' and 'the Decaying Knight' — Sarkaz assassins sent by the Armorless Union.

blocks.cap: 'Place your bets on poor Maria.' Beneath the emcee's banter, the crowd never noticed this was staged to look like an accident.

blocks.x: Neither Arts nor wounds heal properly. Coughing up blood, Maria still won't let go of her sword. The old knights and Sophia, on their way out of the tavern, finally catch on and come running.

Heading for the arena, old knight Poe and craftsman Koval find their way blocked — this time by Platinum herself. 'A former second-tier campaign knight, and a senior master craftsman. Won't you just quietly turn back?'
'There's nothing you can do.' But the threat doesn't last long. An unidentified blue shadow suddenly seizes even Platinum. The unknown voice on the comms says only this.
"Playtime's over, little Pegasus. Fall back."
10. Light Descends From the Sky
Just as the Sarkaz weapon is about to fall on Maria's head — the hammer is knocked away. And a blinding light floods the arena.
Well done, Maria.

After six years, her sister Margaret has returned. Maria hands the shield over to her sister and takes her place at her side instead. 'The weak your sister used to protect aren't here anymore. I'll be your shield now.'

blocks.cap: "A knight is—!" "A noble being who lights up the whole world!"

blocks.x: Caught between the two sisters' combined assault, the Sarkaz assassins finally break. Even after confirming her victory, Margaret still reaches out a hand to them. 'You mustn't give up on your own fate.' The assassins never lift their heads.

As it happens, two Sarkaz strangers passing through Kazimierz — Shining and Nightingale — tend to the assassins' wounds. 'Both of you are hurt very badly, but these scars didn't come from this match.' The assassins only grow more afraid of that gentle touch.

Rushing into the stands, Sophia slaps Margaret across the face after six years apart — then slaps her again, 'this one's for Maria.' Then she grabs her hand and hides her tears. 'I'm so glad you're back.'

Outside the arena, Spokesperson Czarny mutters quietly to himself. 'The Radiant Knight... why come back now, of all times?' That night, after telling his staff not to leave their rooms, he quietly vanishes without a trace.
Epilogue: The Sister Returns, and the Story Isn't Over Yet

Czarny falls from grace, and Malkiewicz, a lowly Swarma staffer, ends up in the new spokesperson's chair almost by accident. The Commercial Federation's orders remain unchanged. 'Make sure the matches keep running smoothly.'
Before leaving, Czarny leaves Platinum with one last request. Ševčík has a son, and that son was present at the scene of the incident. 'Please don't give him the chance to learn the truth. Ever.'
Sona and her companion resolve to expose the Armorless Union's true nature while they're at it. 'We're going to drag their true colors out into the open, aren't we?'
Back home, Maria tells Margaret, 'This time, I'll be by your side. Whatever happens to us in the end, I trust you.' Margaret smiles and replies. 'You've really grown up.'
But Uncle Młynar sees it differently. He demands Margaret draw her sword. 'Falling to my blade would be better than falling in the Armorless Union's shadow.' Margaret answers calmly.
"If that's what you want."

Far off, Senomi, a messenger from a family of campaign knights with ties to the Nearls, brings one more piece of news. After relaying, word for word, the nagging their grandfather once left with the Grand Knight-Commander, she adds one last thing. 'Looks like someone from Rhodes Island has already arrived.'

Kazimierz still makes its money selling off its knights, but this time, something's different. Two Nearl sisters now stand side by side in the same arena. The family motto — 'Fear neither hardship nor darkness' — no longer belongs to the older sister alone. It's become Maria's own, too.

act12d0.tagline: Having her engine stolen turns into an excuse for a visit home, and Gavial ends up swept into the Great Chief's rite, smashing an old friend's giant robot along the way. And even faced with a girl who staged a coup just to keep her there, Gavial still answers with her fists.

chars.name: Gavial
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island medic operator. Hailing from the Sargon jungle of Acahualla, this former fighting fanatic believes fists solve everything.

chars.name: Tomimi
chars.desc: Gavial's junior from back home. Chased after Gavial hard enough to teach herself the Sargon language, and now leads the 'Will of Gavial' tribe.

chars.name: Eunectes
chars.desc: The new chief of the Eunectes tribe, settled near the mines. Once called Zumama as a child, now utterly obsessed with machinery.

chars.name: Ceobe
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator who fell along with Gavial and the Doctor while chasing after them. Overflowing with strength, but always a beat behind on reading the situation.

chars.name: Croissant
chars.desc: An operator who adapted to a land where no one understands her language, purely through business savvy. Already made a killing in the jungle marketplace.

chars.name: Utage
chars.desc: Came along thinking it was a resort trip, and somehow ended up absorbing an entire tribe.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: An operator who jumped into the jungle late, worried about the Doctor. Proves that a good fight is a universal language even in unfamiliar land.

chars.name: Flint
chars.desc: A Tiacauh living alone, roaming all over the jungle, nicknamed Kmar. Develops loyalty out of nowhere upon meeting someone stronger than herself.

chars.name: Lancet-2
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's medical work platform. From repairing aircraft to fielding a strange chief's confession, it handles all the emotional labor.

chars.name: Yi Nam
chars.desc: Sargon's envoy dispatched to the jungle, and the only one who does any trading in this whole area. Loves naps and gossip.

terms.name: Acahualla
terms.desc: Gavial's homeland. A no-man's-land jungle within Sargon territory that the government leaves untouched — the name means 'land thick with forest.'

terms.name: Tiacauh
terms.desc: What the mixed peoples of this land — Adakris, Piedra, and Liberi alike — call themselves, without distinction of race. It means 'one who is brave and fights well.'

terms.name: Mahuijotia
terms.desc: The rite that elects the Great Chief. The name means 'strength and glory,' and the method is simple — knock down every challenger, and you're Great Chief from that day on.

terms.name: Pebble Sickness
terms.desc: What this region calls Oripathy. Treated as a common local ailment, so catching it doesn't get you shunned — but that also means the danger goes just as unaddressed.

terms.name: the Big Ugly Thing
terms.desc: A giant weapon the Eunectes tribe spent years painstakingly building. The High Priest sits in the cockpit.

terms.name: Adakris
terms.desc: The same lineage as Gavial. Proud of their tail's thickness, they even split into thick-tail and thin-tail factions and fight over it.

terms.name: AUS
terms.desc: A mysterious rock band that drifted into the jungle and settled in for a while. Left its music behind in this land even after leaving.

blocks.x: Gavial, a Rhodes Island medic operator, hails from the Sargon jungle. One day, a letter arrives from home. The rite that elects a new Great Chief, 'Mahuijotia,' is being held again.
