Plenty of my comrades are Infected too. Honestly, I can never really tell just by looking, and I've never much thought about who's infected. Well, I do find out at bath time, though.
He'd expected a serious answer, but it ended on a note about baths.
Only once the bandage was done did Big Bob offer a short word of thanks. "Thanks, kid without prejudice."
7. So What Has Rhodes Island Actually Done

In front of the Tarat Mountain mine shaft, Big Bob sharpens his tone again. "You Rhodes Island types raise 'Blood Saints' like Skadi and still have the nerve to call yourselves an organization for the Infected. What were you doing while the Infected were locked in quarantine zones, dying?"

There are things we can face head-on with strength and solve, but there are also things beyond our reach. Still, knowing that, we can't just leave innocent Infected suffering right in front of us. Rhodes Island included.
Big Bob still isn't satisfied. "Hmph, what's so great about Rhodes Island. There's nothing in this world worth more than this little gold coin!" Grani only offers a short apology. "Sorry."
Just then, footsteps sound from the mine entrance. A masked group approaches, leaving the fallen Captain behind. "You're... Reunion?!"
Big Bob freezes for a moment, then sides with Grani to fight together. "Enough talk, let's go!"
With his leg injured, Big Bob ultimately chooses to stay behind on his own two feet. "Take Chloe and go on ahead! Hurry! I'll catch up somehow later!"
Grani leaves with just one promise. "I'll leave your share, so make sure you come back safe."
8. I'll Guard the Entrance
Leaving the forest, Grani asks Chloe if she's scared.
Chloe's answer was short. "I'm terribly scared. But if it's to bring back the village's quiet days and protect our homeland's peace... then I have to do my part too."

Reaching the cave entrance, Grani announces it. "You know the way to the treasure, so go on ahead. I'll guard the entrance." Chloe objects. "Facing that many bounty hunters alone is far too dangerous!"
There's no such thing as a task not worth risking your life for. Even if a barbarian's vicious blade pierces my heart, a royal cavalry officer's spear never falls to the ground.
And she adds one more line. "No matter what happens, I'll protect you. I decided that the moment I saved you in the square. I won't let anyone take so much as a strand of your hair."
Chloe laughs, exasperated. "...Honestly, you... you're still such a little kid..." Grani bristles. "I—I'm not a kid! I'm just a little short!"
Left alone, Grani fends off the bounty hunters swarming in, one by one. In the middle of it, Skadi shows up too, pressing her. "Hand over Chloe. I don't care about anything else."
Grani doesn't back down. "As long as Chloe is my client, that will never happen." Neither of them even knowing what Skadi's mission is, they just keep clashing spear against blade.
Having barely shaken Skadi off, heading deeper into the cave, something strange catches her eye. A hole, blown open as if by an explosion. Not something Skadi had made.
Grani guesses. "So, you've decided what to do." The voice belongs to exactly who she expected.
9. Three-Way Standoff at the Cave Mouth
Waiting inside the cave was Big Bob. Leading his dying brothers, he'd staked everything on one last gambit for the treasure.

blocks.cap: A band of the Infected and the Blood Saint — three forces tangled before the narrow cave.

blocks.x: Big Bob finally reveals the real story. "We can go to Columbia. It's an unimaginably distant land, but there, even the Infected can have their own village and fields. I have to get this treasure for my brothers, no matter what."
Right then, Skadi also breaks through the Reunion group and appears. "Clear the way." With the three of them gathered in one place, the cave mouth instantly turns into a three-way battle.
Skadi speaks as if she couldn't care less about anyone's circumstances. "Reunion, bounty hunters, all the same to me." And she presses Grani again to give up Chloe's location.
Grani refuses. "I can't hand over Chloe or the treasure to any of you, not as things stand. This is the request I accepted." It comes right to the edge of blades clashing.
At that moment, Chloe appears, struggling to drag the treasure chest along. "Grani! I found it! I found the treasure!"
Grani rushes to stop her but is a step too late. An explosive Big Bob threw goes off right beside Chloe, and Skadi wraps herself around Chloe at that very instant.
From that one scene, Grani understands. "You have no interest in the treasure, do you? And you never meant to hurt Chloe either. If you had, you'd have grabbed the treasure and run."
I don't need the treasure. All I want is a certain something this child is carrying.
10. The Key Demands Blood
Skadi warns Chloe. "The moment you use that key to open the treasure chest, you die. If you don't carry Kazimierz blood, the key will tear your hand open and drink it dry."
Chloe's expression was already resolved. "The most important duty of my role as chief is to protect this key, passed down through generations. That's what my father taught me..."
Only the bloodline of Kazimierz, unafraid of danger and undeterred by sacrifice, can remove every obstacle.
Chloe grips the key. "Grani, if something happens to me, please make sure the money from this treasure reaches everyone in the village. I'm sorry, Father..."
Grani stops her hand.
"Chloe, would you show me that key too?" After a brief look, she makes up her mind. "I'll open the treasure chest."
Chloe cries out. "What?! But if you do that, Grani would—" Grani had already made up her mind. "It's fine. Chloe, sorry. I'm opening it!"
Grani leaves Skadi with one line and turns the key. "Skadi, if I fall, catch me properly."
And then, Grani collapsed.
Epilogue. Back to Where Each Belongs
A few days later, Grani woke up safely. In return, she dumps bounty hunter camp cleanup duty on Skadi, snapping at her. "You got the key you wanted, so get to work properly."

blocks.cap: The two who'd had spears crossed not long ago now head out side by side for the final sweep.

blocks.x: Skadi grumbles. "The enemy still outnumbers us. You just barely saved your life, and you're putting it on the line again?" Grani laughs and shoots back. "You're here too, aren't you?"
In the end Big Bob got nothing, and starts with an apology. "Sorry. In the end I couldn't help at all..." Chloe reassures him — it's fine, this can just stay between the two of them.
On their way to part, Big Bob presses an old gold coin into Grani's hand. It was the very same coin she'd gotten from the bounty hunter who'd first picked a fight back in the village.
On the road back to Dewdrop Village, Chloe hands over a letter. It's from Rhodes Island, and the moment Grani reads it, she grins, folds it into a paper airplane, and sends it flying.
"Rhodes Island paper is good quality, so it flies far." The heart of whoever wrote that letter had flown just as far away, it turns out.
In truth, the letter was written by **Nearl**. After rewriting it several times, she finally settled on something short, murmuring to herself. "Grani... please, please receive it safely!"
Sadly, that wish became a paper airplane and flew off just the same.
Around the same time, in a certain room at Rhodes Island, Skadi had come to find **Specter**. Specter catches a familiar scent from a visitor whose name she can't even recall.
"A scent I miss, yet detest." Instead of answering, Skadi only gives her a destination and turns to leave. "A place where a secret sleeps. Very, very far from you."
At the end of that destination was **Kal'tsit**. What Skadi held out was a fragment of the key, soaked in Grani's blood.

blocks.cap: Fair trade. What Skadi held out was the key, soaked in Grani's blood.

blocks.x: There's already blood infused in it. Not yours, I take it — you hate bleeding on solid ground.
Skadi's answer was short. "It's Grani's blood."
What Skadi wanted was the source of the Originium neural medicine she'd given Specter. Kal'tsit sets one condition, and Skadi nods without hesitation.
I only trust people I can trust.
And I always keep my word, once I've given it.
The village kept the legend of a dead knight, and the bounty hunters kept the rumor of the Blood Saint. But neither the legend nor the rumor tells what actually happened within them.

A young spearwoman who chose to bleed in place of a young chief, an Infected who bared his teeth at Rhodes Island for the sake of his brothers, and a Blood Saint who made a fair trade and left without anyone knowing. Behind the names in the rumors, these were the faces.

act51side.tagline: In a world demanding they kneel, children discarded in a junkyard become each other's ramparts and tear down the wall between them. Erase the line dividing 'them' from 'us,' and only one word remains—us.

chars.name: Zima
chars.desc: Leader of the self-government group. A girl from Chernobog whose fists move faster than her words, but who never abandons anyone. Real name in the story: Sonia · Operator Zima

chars.name: Istina
chars.desc: The self-government group's level-headed strategist. Real name Anna · Operator Istina

chars.name: Leto
chars.desc: Heiress of a fallen central count's house. Behind her elegant social graces, she hides sharp judgment. Real name Natalia · Operator Leto

chars.name: Gummy
chars.desc: The innocent youngest member of the self-government group. Real name Rada · Operator Gummy

chars.name: Rosa
chars.desc: A cheerful self-government group member who loves a good fight. Real name Rosalind · Operator Rosa

chars.name: Absinthe
chars.desc: Daughter of an officer who died in the line of duty. A girl who hides warmth behind a cynical tongue · Operator Absinthe

chars.name: Ukusik
chars.desc: A vagrant girl raised in Katorga who once had no name at all · Operator Ukusik

chars.name: Botani
chars.desc: The girl who connects the students by radio · Operator Botani

chars.name: Dur-nar
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island instructor who looks after the self-government group · Operator Dur-nar

chars.name: Crownslayer
chars.desc: A red-haired Rhodes Island operator dispatched to Ursus, carrying a heavy past · Operator Crownslayer

terms.name: Sankt Konburg
terms.desc: Capital of the Ursus Empire. The glittering heart of its nobility.

terms.name: Katorga District
terms.desc: An abandoned industrial zone on the outskirts of Sankt Konburg. In this story, it's the 'Quarantine Zone' where the students are trapped.

terms.name: Student Union Congress
terms.desc: A coalition of student factions from various schools banded together. Its lines are split between groups like the Guard faction and the Study Society faction.

terms.name: The Offbeat Alliance
terms.desc: A youth society seeking to overturn oppression through force.

terms.name: Emperor Igor
terms.desc: The first emperor who founded Ursus a thousand years ago. His majesty remains everywhere in stone statues.

blocks.x: Ursus is an empire that has endured a thousand years of winter. As dazzling as its iron order and noble glory are, in their shadow miners, commoners, and students freeze to death without a sound.

In a hearing chamber in Sankt Konburg. The nobles who gathered to judge the 'Katorga Incident' end up grabbing each other by the collar and brawling instead. Outside the assembly hall, hundreds of students stand silently, without flags or slogans.
And a girl standing in the witness box begins to tell the story of how people abandoned in a junkyard beyond a single wall became 'us.'
The Hearing Where Nobles Started a Brawl
The first witness is **Absinthe** (real name Zoya), daughter of an officer who died in the line of duty. A girl who hides warmth behind a cynical tongue.

What she recalls is the Student Union Congress a few days earlier. Everyone agrees they should rescue their captured comrade Lirea, but they nearly come to blows again over the method—the 'Guard' faction wants to storm the prison, while the 'Study Society' faction urges caution.
Besides her, the vagrant Ukusik and chairwoman Olga take the stand in turn. Each of them a voice that lived through the same winter from a different place.

There, arriving late, is **Zima** (real name Sonia). Leader of the self-government group from Chernobog, a person whose fists move faster than her words. Her level-headed strategist **Istina** (real name Anna) is with her too.

blocks.cap: The nobles' 'solemn and impartial deliberation.' Five minutes in, it turns into a brawl.

blocks.x: Chairwoman **Olga**'s compromise is this: don't strike the prison—instead, surround the winter ballroom unarmed and simply protest. Zima thinks it's reckless, but to protect the self-government group, she ultimately raises her hand in agreement.
They Surrounded the Ballroom, and Found Three Assassins

**Leto** (real name Natalia) infiltrates the ball under the guise of a fallen count's heiress, to dig for information. The elderly noblewoman **Madam Vabrozova**, who dotes on her like a daughter, gently urges her to 'learn politics, and simply enjoy herself.'
Madam's teaching is to 'blend into the crowd.' At the whisper that a single signature could restore her fallen parents and protect her friends, Leto feels there is still much she has to learn.
In that gorgeous hall, Leto sees **Pavlovich**, the privy councilor in charge of Katorga, for the first time. A man who hides calculation behind a friendly smile.

Meanwhile, a ridiculous commotion breaks out below. A clumsy assassin targeting the baron runs into another assassin targeting the duke, and the two bicker with each other. Then there's a third shadow that silently grabs only a kitchen knife and disappears.
At the appointed hour, the entire city of Sankt Konburg loses power. In the darkness, the students' chants erupt.

blocks.cap: 'Down with the Emperor!' At some point, the unarmed protest spreads into the most blasphemous words, spoken from someone's mouth.

blocks.x: The Emperor Vanished from the Ball
In fact, that night explosives were discovered in the ballroom's main hall, and one person quietly vanished without a trace—none other than the Emperor of Ursus.

It's revealed through a conversation between Chairman Vit and the Guard called the 'Emperor's Blade.' The Blade evacuated the Emperor through an underground passage, but deliberately chose not to hold him back—saying, 'let him see the Ursus outside the window with his own eyes.'

Outside, the protest turns into a tragedy. Military police who show up late pour out tear gas and trample the students. **Gummy** (real name Rada) freezes when she spots her missing father's wristwatch on an officer's wrist.
Zima, who rushes in to save Gummy, ends up caught herself—ordering **Rosa** (real name Rosalind) and Istina to take everyone out in her place.

blocks.cap: 'This isn't the leader's order, it's a friend's request.' And so Zima remains behind alone.

blocks.x: Not Enough Prisons, So the Young Masters Get Thrown in the Junkyard

Most of the arrested students were children of the nobility. The troubled police chief, rather than putting them in a proper prison, dumps them all wholesale into the Katorga District, the quarantine zone ruled by Pavlovich.

There, the Offbeat Alliance led by **Antosha** was already fighting. Young revolutionaries determined to burn oppression out by its roots. By his side, his commoner-born friend **Matvey** always stands silently.

There's also **Ukusik**, the nameless vagrant Antosha brought from the police station. They were the first to give this girl a 'place where she wouldn't be driven away' and a name. So to Ukusik, this place was the first 'home' she'd ever had.

Zima and Antosha bump fists once and promise to cooperate. Their paths differ, but to survive this hell, they need each other. Rosa crawls through a power conduit alone to sneak into the quarantine zone and reunite with Zima.
Went to Rescue, Killed an Innocent Man

The gang called the 'Sixteen Heroes,' who control the quarantine zone, sorted and killed students by their price under the name of the 'Auction.' The Study Society's leader Leonid also never returned that way.

Zima organizes a rescue team and brings her comrades back. But in the process, two terrified students mistake a man stripping clothes off a corpse for a robber and kill him. He was an ordinary laborer collecting the belongings of the dead for his starving family.
The students offer everything they have to the wife who collapses before her husband's body, but all that comes back is hollow hatred.
Only Matvey, left standing alone, approaches her and quietly tells her that her husband wasn't a robber, but a man who fought to the end for his family.

blocks.cap: The bonfire on the night of their return. Heavier than the relief of survival is the weight of those they couldn't bring back.

blocks.x: To Antosha, who says 'only fight for those worth it,' Zima answers that no matter their status, she won't abandon anyone who's willing to raise a fist.
Across a Single Wall

Leto enters the quarantine zone on behalf of the children's parents and meets Zima across a single wall. On her way, she sees a beast gnawing on a human finger, and nearly breaks down under memories of Chernobog, but swallows it back down.
Zima quietly asks a favor: to cut off, from outside, Pavlovich's daily heavy weapons supply.

Outside, Leto learns politics. She hears a startling fact from a member of parliament: since the night of the ball, the Emperor hasn't returned to the palace, and might possibly be inside this very quarantine zone.
But along with it came a chilling warning: if the 'special measures' to forcibly return the Emperor to the palace are triggered, the students inside will have to find their own way to survive.
A Noble Betrayal
In the theater where the frightened students are about to scatter, **Istina** takes the stage. She, who is usually so level-headed, raises her voice for the first time.

blocks.cap: 'True failure isn't falling down—it's putting out the fire in your own heart when you could still resist.' Istina takes the stage.

blocks.x: These hands aren't meant to clap and flatter those in power—they're meant to tear down high walls.

Meanwhile, Leto ultimately refuses the 'favorable testimony' offered by Madam Vabrozova, who had doted on her. Instead, she writes a letter of her own 'confession'—to everyone deceived by her scheme, and to the students who were used and discarded.

Not noble blood, but the 'right to choose'—that, Leto says, is the one right everyone should be born with. Olga, who had been confined, also ultimately hands the students the secret passage leading to her father's mansion, breaking with her family in her own way.
The Voice Over the Radio

In the middle of the quarantine zone, the radio girl **Botani** was sending her 373rd distress call into a void where no one answered. The first to reply to that voice was a quiet man who called himself 'Fedya.'
Bolstered by Fedya's calm advice, Botani succeeds in capturing the enemy's accountant, a baron. And we learn Fedya's true identity—the very Emperor who vanished from the ball, **Feodor**.

blocks.cap: 'To tomorrow.' 'To victory.' For the first time, the Emperor clinks glasses with the nameless.

blocks.x: But for Feodor, this tender night is instead a punishment. The student 'Little One,' to whom he'd given a gold coin, had already been killed trying to buy her life with that same money. He can hardly bring himself to look down at these two hands that have both saved and taken lives.
How simple a prayer it is, to say 'still, there is tomorrow.' For the first time, the Emperor truly grasps what he has been neglecting.

Over what to do with the prisoners, Zima and Antosha finally split. Zima says to keep them alive for their usefulness, while Antosha says parasites can't be allowed to live. Even Matvey, who has always stood by Antosha's side, quietly turns his back this time.
The Girl Who Wields the Legendary Hammer

The day of the final battle. Gromov, the boss of the Sixteen Heroes, drags out even a cannon to crush the students, but Zima lifts the legendary hammer-axe of Emperor Igor—the weapon no one was said to be able to wield.

blocks.cap: 'Guess I'm more qualified than you, after all.' A thousand years of weight grows light at last, in a girl's hands.

blocks.x: An ambush luring the enemy into a narrow alley, Molotov cocktails raining from every window, and Rosa and Instructor Dur-nar throwing themselves into the fight. With the workers and citizens Crownslayer brought joining in too, the tide of battle turns.
Zima smashes the giant statue of Emperor Igor blocking the entrance to the quarantine zone with her hammer, bringing down the wall. The rubble buries Pavlovich, who was fleeing to the very end.

blocks.cap: Light pours into the place where a thousand years of majesty crumbled. One person, then ten, then a hundred, cross over the ruins.

blocks.x: They say if you cut out your tongue, you can chew bread with butter. Carve out your knees, and you can get a pardon. But we answer: 'no.'
Before the collapsing statue, Feodor too, without realizing it, mixes in among the students and shouts 'Ura—'
And Then, Matvey
Victory came with a price. The Offbeat Alliance that raided the rear ran into elite military police, and while shielding Antosha, Yura, and then **Matvey**, did not make it back.
Having lost all his comrades, Antosha challenges Zima to a duel. Saying 'step over my corpse and go forth into the new order,' he's trying to flee into a poetic death.

blocks.cap: 'Stop it, Antosha.' Rosa steps in to block him, saying that his ideals are precisely the meaning his comrades protected with their lives.

blocks.x: Zima refuses the duel. Destruction is only a means, she says—throwing away your life doesn't make an ideal shine any brighter.

On the day the two of them planted a cherry tree together in the school garden, Antosha believed an Ursus where no one suffers would come. As the price of that belief, he lost his mentor, and now he has lost Matvey too.

The next day, Antosha alone visits Matvey's home, slips the obituary he wrote for him into the window crack, and turns away as if fleeing. But beyond the window, on his old mother's table sit three bowls of red soup—thanks to a letter Matvey had sent ahead.

blocks.cap: Matvey, reflected in the cart's mirror. On his chest, instead of a bullet wound, a bouquet of cherry blossoms has bloomed.

blocks.x: **Live.** Live, and someday carve me, our mentor, and our comrades into the martyrs' monument. That is how the vision releases Antosha.
The Truth on a Blank Page
And we return to the hearing. In fact, this trial had been a play from the very start. The clerk's record book was pure blank paper, and Zoya's long testimony was bait to stall for time.

In the meantime, the students raided Prison No. 3, which had been left empty of guards, and freed Lirea and all the others held there. The once-scattered 'Union Congress' had, before anyone knew it, become one united force.

blocks.cap: Twelve words came flowing over the radio. Gummy instantly recognizes whose voice it is.

blocks.x: The reunited self-government group hands Zoya a badge with a fist emblem, telling her she's now definitely one of 'us.' The red-haired Rhodes Island operator **Crownslayer** (Lyudmila) sets off again over the radio, chasing the clue left by Gummy's father—'the sarcophagus' and 'the satellite city.'

Emperor Feodor walks with his own two feet for the first time, passing through the snowy streets back to the palace. Only now realizing that snow is equally cold whether it falls on ruins or on a palace.

blocks.cap: Even after decades living in this city, this is the first time the Emperor has walked these streets on his own feet.

blocks.x: But this winter isn't over yet. In the distance, an unfamiliar young man looks up at a Sankt Konburg he's seeing for the first time, and somewhere, a legion is quietly preparing to test a new type of weapon.

Ursus is still in its thousandth year of winter. But through that frozen tundra, a fist that refused to kneel has risen up. Where the wall dividing 'them' from 'us' has fallen, only one word remains now—us.

main_0.tagline: Having lost all memory, the Doctor wakes up in a coffin and, led by the hands of unfamiliar comrades, picks up the baton of command once more in a burning Chernobog. What's lost can simply be found again—what matters isn't the memories that vanished, but now.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Believes in the Doctor to the very end.

chars.name: Dobermann
chars.desc: Captain of Operations Team E1. A strict instructor.

chars.name: Ace
chars.desc: A veteran leader of the operations team.

chars.name: Nearl
chars.desc: A knight of Kazimierz. Holds the front line with her shield.

chars.name: Crownslayer
chars.desc: A Reunion agent pursuing Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Mephisto
chars.desc: Reunion's boy commander, who commands the Infected.

chars.name: Faust
chars.desc: The taciturn sniper at Mephisto's side.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: An organization of the Infected. Handles both treatment and conflict resolution.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that rose up in Ursus.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease caused by Originium. Those afflicted become the Infected.

terms.name: Catastrophe
terms.desc: A natural disaster that strikes Terra. Leaves Originium behind.

terms.name: Ursus
terms.desc: A military empire harsh toward the Infected.

terms.name: PRTS
terms.desc: Rhodes Island's support system. Accessed via neural connector.

terms.name: Kazimierz
terms.desc: Nearl's homeland. A country of knights.

blocks.x: In the world called Terra, a natural disaster known as the 'Catastrophe' strikes periodically. In the wake of a Catastrophe, a mineral called Originium is left behind, and people exposed to it for too long contract 'Oripathy' and become the 'Infected.'
The world treats the Infected not as the sick, but as a threat. Ursus, a military empire, is especially harsh toward them.
This story begins at the edge of a cliff in such a world, inside a single frozen coffin. Sleeping within it is a person who has lost every memory—someone people call 'the Doctor.'

blocks.cap: Somewhere in the fog. An unidentified voice whispers to the sleeping figure. "December 23rd... you must remember, no matter what."

blocks.x: 1. The One Who Woke Inside a Coffin
On a cold operating table, someone restarts a heart that had stopped. Urgent voices go back and forth, injecting cardioplegic solution and beginning the incision.
One of those voices quietly apologizes. "...I'm sorry. I hurt you again." Its owner never gives a name.

When the Doctor opens their eyes, the girl who'd been keeping watch grabs their hand, on the verge of tears. It's **Amiya**. Just moments ago, she'd even been bracing herself for the possibility that the Doctor might never wake up.
The medic beside her marvels—Amiya seems like a completely different person whenever the Doctor comes up. It's clear just how much this reunion means to her.
But the Doctor remembers nothing. Not Amiya, not their own name, nothing about everything that came before.

Amiya doesn't break down in front of them. She introduces herself as a member of Rhodes Island, and tells the Doctor over and over that they are her most precious comrade. She won't ask them to remember it all right now, she says—just give her a little time.
She swallows down just how great a price it took to get the Doctor back. Instead, she only says this: so much has changed, but one thing hasn't—that the Doctor is the person who matters most to her.

blocks.cap: "Take my hand!" — The hand Amiya reaches out pulls the memory-less Doctor out of the darkness.

blocks.x: 2. Handing the Baton to a Stranger
In that brief moment of reunion, an armed group storms the facility. A heavily armed Infected organization—**Reunion**.
Operators shield the Doctor behind cover, shouting urgently. Communication with Kal'tsit is dead—someone is jamming the signal.
Communications are down, and there's no reaching Kal'tsit, the commander for this operation. Even knowing the danger, Amiya asks the Doctor, who has just woken up, to take command.
There's only one basis for it: even having lost their memory, the Doctor was once a comrade who fought alongside them, and her unwavering belief in them.
Putting the Doctor back on the battlefield is agony for Amiya too. Even so, she says Rhodes Island needs the Doctor's wisdom right now, and hopes this battle will help them recover their instincts.
I know you can do it. I believe that. Please... command Rhodes Island...!
Under the Doctor's command, the first battle ends in a flawless victory. The body may not remember, but the fingertips still read the battlefield.

blocks.cap: A crest engraved with a single rook. "Enjoy my life and hone my craft" — this is Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: 3. Three Hours
As the battle ends, **Dobermann**, captain of another operations team, joins up. Her team was also hit by a Reunion raid and just made it over to this side.
Hearing that command was handed to someone who lost their memory, Dobermann is dumbfounded. She's not the type to easily trust a stranger, either.

Still, having witnessed the command with her own two eyes just now, she decides to give Amiya's words a try. She introduces herself to the Doctor as the captain of Operations Team E1, and says she'll escort them from Chernobog to Rhodes Island.

Right then, the neural connector unexpectedly activates, and the support system **PRTS** connects. With radio communication blocked, they still can't reach Kal'tsit, who hasn't made it back to Rhodes Island yet—but at least a channel is now open to tell the Doctor the next move.
Having verified authorization by fingerprint, PRTS offers a calm greeting. "Welcome back, Doctor."
Once preparations for the move are finished, Dobermann quietly lets only Amiya know the truth. In three hours, a Catastrophe will strike this city.
If they can't get out of Chernobog by then, it's over for everyone. Worried the team might panic, Dobermann hasn't told anyone else about the countdown yet.
4. The Ark of the Infected
On the retreat, the Doctor seems to ask again—what exactly is the Infected.
Amiya answers plainly. She herself, Dobermann, most of Rhodes Island, and even the Reunion they just fought—all suffer from the same disease.
It's Oripathy. Those afflicted are hunted everywhere, and Ursus justifies its persecution with reports that stoke fear of the Infected. Citizens even applaud it.
That very oppression is also why Reunion targeted this city. To Infected people crushed under it, Reunion looks like a way out.

Meanwhile, a medic operator wrangles with a Guard trying to dodge an injection, insisting regular treatment is needed to ease the symptoms. This noisy little tussle is just everyday life at a place called Rhodes Island.
Between it all, Rhodes Island carries two burdens at once—searching for a cure for Oripathy, while also cleaning up conflicts the Infected get tangled in.

Because leaning too far to either side means failing to protect the Infected's lives. Dobermann quietly tells the Doctor that since she too is Infected, these are burdens the Doctor might end up carrying from now on.
If a fight can be avoided, hold your tongue... but if it cannot be avoided, fight to the very end!

blocks.cap: Rhodes Island's creed has never once changed.

blocks.x: 5. Chernobog in Flames
Stepping out into the streets, the city is already hell. Explosions, fires, and street fighting all tangle together, and Reunion strikes at Ursus citizens indiscriminately.
Yet the propaganda broadcast calmly repeats, "situation resolved," over and over—even as, off-screen, the military police are actually being overrun by Reunion.

blocks.cap: "The glory of Ursus shall protect His Majesty and the citizens" — as the news of victory plays, the streets are burning.

blocks.x: Rhodes Island clears a path by striking down the Reunion forces using civilians as shields. Along the way, they run into an Ursus military police captain who, despite his contempt for these Infected, lets them pass, saying he doesn't care why they came so long as they're not here to destroy the city.

The captain flatly says he has no interest in what these Infected women are here for. But if they're here to destroy the city, he warns, Ursus's fury won't stop until they're dead. The resolve of a man holding his post.
Then he stays behind alone to defend the street, and falls, leaving behind the words: "An Ursus soldier never shows his back to the enemy."

blocks.cap: As the news of victory plays, there's a child left alone amid the flames on the street.

blocks.x: 6. What's Lost Can Simply Be Found Again

At the next rendezvous point, the veteran leader **Ace** comes out alone to greet them. He'd stationed his team at the meeting point in case of emergency, and came out himself to clear away any dangers directly.
Even hearing the Doctor has lost their memory, Ace isn't shaken. As long as their command ability is intact, that's all that matters to him, and he says he'll follow the Doctor's orders.
What's lost can simply be found again.

Reunion even unleashes infected beasts as soldiers. Dobermann realizes they're not mere beasts, but creatures closer to themselves than not, and Ace says there's nothing to do but release them from their suffering.
The three of them mull over what Reunion really is. Anyone who puts on the uniform and the mark is taken in, an endlessly swelling force fed on hatred and fear.
And a leader who uses and discards that force like chess pieces. Dobermann calls a person like that not a commander, but a 'tyrant'—someone who tramples even his own side to crush the enemy.

Dobermann adds bitterly. Poke just one small hole in the bars of a cage, and those trapped inside will rush out no matter what, even if the outside is a sea of fire. Reunion runs on exactly that kind of desperation as fuel.

Hearing that, Ace recalls something someone once told him. A pawn, if it's a pawn, you just capture it. A castle, if it's a castle, you just bring it down. A crown, if it's a crown, you just topple it. Whoever the enemy is, Rhodes Island simply carries out its mission.
7. The Boy Who Plays Games
Just before the final rendezvous point, an ambush strikes along with the fog. **Crownslayer**, a masked Reunion agent, has caught up with Rhodes Island.
But another figure who shows up right after sends her back. Crownslayer withdraws, leaving behind the words, "I do hope you fail spectacularly."
**Mephisto**. A boy commander who moves the Infected around as precisely as chess pieces. Finding Rhodes Island's way of fighting entertaining, he proposes a 'game'—survive, and he'll let them go.
What he really wants is the Doctor. He presses relentlessly—how did they possibly survive at that facility, who exactly are they—and demands the Doctor be handed over as a gift.
When Catastrophe looms right before your eyes... isn't that exactly the right time to throw a festival?
Mephisto's command is chillingly precise. He moves the frenzied Infected alone like pieces on a chessboard, sealing off one escape route after another, driving Rhodes Island into a corner.
At a single word from him, the Infected shift formation with precision. He hides casters behind defenders, then unleashes them the very moment Rhodes Island's ranks crack.
And die, like sparks in a rainy night!

blocks.cap: Beyond the fog, a commander who moves the Infected like chess pieces closes in on Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: 8. The Knight of Kazimierz

Just as the tide is turning against them, reinforcements who pinpointed the flare's location arrive and smash Reunion's formation from the side. It's **Nearl**, knight of Kazimierz, having already evacuated the citizens in advance.
Mephisto sends his taciturn sniper **Faust** to target Nearl. Faust's sniping is a crossfire raining down from multiple locations at once—enough to wipe out any other team in an instant if hit.
Nearl catches that sniper fire with her shield. Riding the brief opening Ace bought her, she charges straight into the heart of the enemy lines, tearing the encirclement wide open.
If you're seriously thinking of taking down a knight of Kazimierz... come back after a few more decades of training!

After breaking free of the encirclement, Ace and Nearl mull over that sniper. Judging by the minute timing gaps in the blasts, Faust seems to exist not as a single person but split across multiple locations. Reunion is a far more dangerous opponent than expected.

Nearl offers a word to the Doctor, who seems unsure about their own command. She says avoiding danger wasn't down to her own judgment but thanks to the Doctor's leadership, and tells them to have a little more confidence.
And upon hearing the Doctor has lost their memory, she just laughs it off as no big deal. One of her friends has lost their memory too, she says.
What matters most to you probably isn't the memories that vanished, but 'now.'
Epilogue — Unfinished, To Be Continued
As the Doctor's group makes it out of the city, Mephisto withdraws, leaving the pursuit to Faust. He says he needs to report the situation to his 'sister.'

Faust, having failed to catch Nearl in the end, apologizes for his own mistake. Mephisto soothes his sniper, saying the excess excitement was his own fault—and that above all, Faust's own safety matters most.
That sister has already seized Chernobog's central command tower. Whether to let the vermin that is Rhodes Island live or die is now hers to decide.
Comrades. Let us depart now. To greet our era!!

The Doctor, who lost every memory and woke inside a coffin, took hold of a hand that believed in them and picked up the baton of command once more in a burning city. What's lost can simply be found again, and what matters most isn't a vanished past but 'now.' And the time remaining until Catastrophe still hasn't run out completely.

rogue_1.tagline: A story of a fog-locked old castle that turns into a ghostly theater every night, where an actor who once left the stage keeps writing his own revenge play to its final act. If life is one long play, is it a comedy, or a tragedy?

chars.name: Phantom
chars.desc: An actor bound to the old castle. Once a member of the Crimson Troupe.

chars.name: the Playwright
chars.desc: The pinnacle of the Crimson Troupe, who writes with his own blood as ink.

chars.name: Tin Man
chars.desc: A detective with a mechanical face, hunting the Crimson Troupe.

terms.name: Crimson Troupe
terms.desc: A killer organization disguised as artists.

terms.name: the Old Castle
terms.desc: A stage locked in fog. By night, it becomes a ghostly theater.

terms.name: Solitaire
terms.desc: The codename of the traitor who cut down the Troupe's leadership.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: The affiliation of the scout squad trapped in the old castle.

blocks.x: Integrated Strategies is a game where you explore a randomly-assembled old castle again and again. So rather than a fixed plot, the backbone of the story here is the setting laid over that stage and the one truth that keeps repeating. And the name of this stage is none other than 'the Theater.'
A Rhodes Island scout squad loses its way in thick fog. Not knowing whether they're moving forward or just going in circles, they set foot inside an old castle.
The castle welcomes its guests. But once night falls, this place turns into a theater of ghosts.
As both audience and actor, the squad is drawn onto this stage. The bell announcing the curtain's rise has already rung.

blocks.cap: Atop a candlelit staircase, an actor in a red cloak quietly waits for the squad about to take the stage.

blocks.x: 1. The Old Castle Locked in Fog
The Sunset Garden greets its guests like this: Come in, you who have lost your way — the old castle welcomes you. But that sunset glow is not the color of hope.
The old castle unfolds floor by floor, following time from dusk to dead of night. From the Foggy Crossroads to the Sunset Garden, past the Ruins of Home, to where the Nightmare begins.

And then down to the Corridor of the Red Moon, where the clock hands stop at midnight, and finally to the Lobby of Desire, where even time itself is erased. On that final floor, nothing rules over the order of time but a pure hunger for beauty, art, and perfection.

blocks.cap: Vines and blood-red mist climb up the castle's pillars. Darkness always follows, like a shadow.

blocks.x: In every hallway, sweet yet strange incense candles burn. Their thick scent leaves you dizzy, your vision swimming.
It's not hard to see why everyone who's breathed that scent too long has lost their mind. In one room, elegantly dressed artists tear into each other like beasts.
2. The Crimson Troupe
The true master of this stage is the **Crimson Troupe** — a killer organization disguised as a collective of artists.

A detective calling himself 'Tin Man,' who claims to be from the Maylander Historical Society, tips the squad off. A trench coat, a fedora, and beneath it, an oddball with a face made of metal and machine parts.

Years ago, a member codenamed **Solitaire** cut down the organization's leadership, and the Troupe supposedly scattered after that. But everything happening in this old castle proves the Troupe is far from dead.
Still handing out death and suffering to innocent people. Madame Sicilia's token puts it this way: whether truth or lie, kneel before the desire that stirs within you.

All around the castle are people trapped while chasing the Troupe — like Tin Man, who came to investigate the Crimson Troupe's traces and got trapped right along with everyone else. He shares what he knows, then vanishes down the end of the hallway.

blocks.cap: Between the bizarre surroundings and the enemies lurking in the dark, a safe spot occasionally appears. Catch your breath for a moment beside the campfire.

blocks.x: 3. A Night at the Old Castle
The old castle at night bustles with all sorts of characters. There's a miniature theater where, if you drop in a coin, a lone knight replays his proposal, his duel, and his death, over and over.

The play in that miniature theater is a lonely one. The coin tossed to a beggar rolls off the stage, the proposal offered to a lady is refused, and the treasure presented to a king is thrown back. The knight is always left alone.

On the other hand, there's also some silly comic relief. A duck who calls himself 'Duck Lord' struts across the stage, telling the guests gathered for the banquet to go home while they still have the chance.

There's also an old woman who hands out treasure in exchange for stories. She laughs and says she's a Feline who heard the tale from her granddaughter, though something about her feels like she's hiding more than she lets on.

The castle is also full of other lost guests — a Duren rolling a wine barrel, getting drunker by the moment, Liberi fighting over the throne of a fallen empire, even a knight troubled by his failure to draw the holy sword.

There's also an old woman sitting on a pile of crates, reading a storybook. For a few coins, she sells the stories of past treasure owners, and once you hear the tale, the squad becomes the new owner of that treasure.

She laughs and says she's a Feline who heard the tale from her Caprine granddaughter, though something about her feels like she's hiding more than she lets on. As you leave, you notice the book's cover, written in Victorian: 'The New Geoffrey's Timeless Fairy Tales.'

The treasure-hunting Jarak Trio passes by too, along with an old militiaman reciting the fallen glory of Gaul, and a knight still troubled by his failure to draw the holy sword. Each of them wanders this stage, carrying one unfulfilled dream.
The most chilling sight of all is the **Crimson Ball**. Men and women in smiling masks refuse to stop dancing, even as their shoes wear through and their feet bleed.
Someone whispers to the squad that the Troupe leader will surely love their song. Amid all that merriment, the squad feels nothing but dread.
Reaching out because you're curious about the face behind the mask is the greatest taboo at this ball. The instant you lift a smiling mask, all that merriment instantly bares its teeth.

blocks.cap: A dance that never stops. A dancer in a red dress spins and spins alone, upon a stage that never ends.

blocks.x: The castle overflows with people trapped like this, ensnared by something beyond them — a young Feline hiding under a desk, trembling at the thought of being forced to act and sing, and a Sarkaz woman spinning alone in red dancing shoes.
In a spacious hall, statues in stage costumes stand packed together, their eyes closed. Recalling the warning not to wake the sleeping statues, the squad quietly shuts the door.

There's also a vampire girl, bound in chains, begging for blood before finally revealing her true nature. Each of them carries a short tragedy of their own, wandering this stage as one of its characters.

A chatty adventurer by the campfire spins one absurd tale after another — that the President of Columbia is secretly a robot, that Sami's snowy mountains are slowly on the move, that a human-modification experiment lies hidden behind Bolívar's wars.
If life is one long play, is it a comedy, or a tragedy? The old castle keeps asking its guests that same question.
4. Madness in the Name of Art
Deep within the old castle, in an elaborately hidden room, the squad discovers a stone tablet. Carved into its back is this inscription:

In the past I was disheartened by the venom of foolish crowds, yet how could art lose its true meaning simply because it appears coarse and ugly on the surface? If art existed only in appearance, how could one ever tell beauty from ugliness?

Deep within the old castle sits a jewel-studded coffin, and from inside it a voice asks to have the lid opened. Open it, and a handsome youth offers his thanks; walk past pretending not to notice, and you get to keep only the jewels. Whichever you choose, this castle is testing its guest.
To the Troupe, murder is art, and suffering is a script. That twisted belief seeps through the entire castle like incense smoke, turning guest and actor alike into characters upon the stage.

Behind one door, elegantly dressed artists are killing each other, out of their minds from the incense. From further down the corridor, an unknown voice invites the squad to face the actors in a knightly duel.

"I'm rather interested in what you all did back in Kazimierz." With those words, four unfamiliar knights step out of the darkness, saying the squad's participation will make this tedious repertoire so much more dramatic.
5. The Truth Behind the Stage

Behind its glamorous facade, the castle is devouring people. There's a prisoner who flies into a rage if interrupted while carving a story into the wall, a Leithanien Tower caster trying to slaughter every banquet guest for the sake of his own play, and even the oil painting of an actress who perished on stage.

The protagonist in that painting is said to have realized, only at the very end, that she'd been living inside someone else's conspiracy the whole time — and to have thrown herself from beneath the tower to escape every last shackle.
An old wooden puppet with broken limbs sits against the wall. Its features look somehow familiar, and if you open up its chest, inside sits a single red heart.
In a dark room, the squad sees a young man holding a quill pen. Dipping it in oddly colored ink, he's writing something down with great intensity beneath a flickering candle.
As the squad approaches, flames burst from the quill and the young man vanishes into the smoke. All that's left are the mad puppets that rush in to protect him.
6. A Night of Blood
A hunchbacked figure tells the squad about that young man's past. A poor child who dreamed of becoming an actor joined the Troupe, and learned one skill at a time from his masters.

From 'Shadow,' the art of illusion. From 'Sword Dance,' the technique of fighting as though dancing. From 'Windlantern,' the craft of acting. And from 'the Master,' a voice that could steal any heart.
Then one day, the young man learned that every master he'd admired was, without exception, a villain beyond redemption. And that very night, his masters fell one by one, to their own student's blade.

'Shadow,' master of illusion. 'Sword Dance,' the merciless dancer. 'Windlantern,' who was never what he seemed. It was a night of blood indeed, one where the student's blade proved sharper than any of his masters.
That actor's name is **Phantom**, codename Solitaire. Having turned his back on the Troupe and left the stage, he was, in this old castle, finishing the last act of his own revenge play.
A poor child's pure dream of becoming an actor was stained with a killer's skill and his masters' blood. What he earned was not applause, but a single mask he could never take off.

blocks.cap: A crown wrapped in thorny brambles. What was placed upon this stage's protagonist was not glory, but a yoke.

blocks.x: An actor who cut down the Troupe with the very skills it taught him. To him, this old castle is both his masters' grave and the final script of his own that he has yet to finish.
7. Curtain Call — and Four Acts

On the old castle's stage, characters left behind by the Troupe bar the squad's path — the **Playwright**, who writes his plays with his own blood as ink, the crimson-glowing Lucian, 'Blood Diamond,' and even the Grieving Lock and the Troupe's Spokesperson.

The Playwright has already sketched out the final scene with his own hand — an ending where he throws himself into a sea of flame, for himself. Even death on stage is, to him, just another line in the script he must complete.

Lucian, 'Blood Diamond,' is an enemy like a crimson-glowing gem, while the Grieving Lock and the Troupe's Spokesperson are characters who carry, respectively, the castle's seal and the Playwright's will. All of them are gears that keep this ghostly theater running. The squad advances toward the stage's heart, breaking these gears one by one.
Depending on which characters are defeated and which truths are reached, the Crimson Troupe brings down one of four different final curtains for its guests.

**Crimson Finale** — the most orthodox ending. The play ends, the actors step down from the stage, the person you were looking for has been found, and the exploration of the old castle wraps up safely. Leaving the uproar of the crimson stage behind, it's now time to return to ordinary life.

**Grotesque Comedy** — you've certainly found the person you were looking for. But no one can say for sure whether the exploration of the old castle has truly ended here. Only laughter lingers where the curtain has fallen, leaving behind an unease that all of this might just now be beginning.
**Grand Opening** — the Host throws the stage curtain wide open for the audience. He stands shoulder to shoulder with the actors, bows, and declares that every play is now about to begin.

Both the opening and the closing curtain belong entirely to the Host. Since even the guests become part of that endless stage in this ending, the suggestion to hand him a bouquet once the performance ends sends a chill down your spine.
**Chapter of Silence** — the hidden, true ending. Everything the Playwright wrote down was the truth. The blood that was spilled, the tears that were shed, and even life and death itself.
And the play tells you that isn't even the whole truth of the Crimson Troupe. So uncover and seek out that truth to the very end, and break free of this stage.

Whichever curtain the play closes with, the actor who once left the stage never lets go of the final chapter of his own script. With the very blade that cut down his masters, he keeps erasing the Troupe they left behind.

The old castle locked in fog raises its curtain again each time, shuffling its characters and their order anew. The squad that entered as the audience becomes, before they know it, a character on the stage, and the actor who once left the stage keeps writing the final chapter of his own revenge play. With both the opening and the closing still left to the Host, whether life is a comedy or a tragedy gets put off to the next performance, and the one after that.

main_1.tagline: One hour before the Catastrophe hits, they escape a burning Chernobog. To get the Doctor and Amiya to the southern exit, their comrades fall back one by one.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. She can't bring herself to abandon a single comrade.

chars.name: Dobermann
chars.desc: Captain of Operation Team E1. Wants Amiya to learn to stand on her own.

chars.name: Nearl
chars.desc: A knight of Kazimierz. Shields the Doctor with her own body.

chars.name: Ace
chars.desc: Veteran leader of the operation team. Remembers the Doctor.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: The tyrant of Reunion. A flame sorceress who burns the whole land.

chars.name: Crownslayer
chars.desc: A Reunion agent at Talulah's side.

chars.name: Mephisto
chars.desc: A young Reunion commander who controls the Infected.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: An unidentified woman blocking the exit. She's after the Doctor.

terms.name: Catastrophe
terms.desc: A natural disaster striking Terra. Rains down massive Originium.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that has overrun Chernobog.

terms.name: Azazel
terms.desc: A secret clinic that quietly treated the Infected. Now in ruins.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease caused by Originium. Grants power, but ends in death.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: A city in Ursus. A place that has long persecuted the Infected.

blocks.x: Rhodes Island is getting the Doctor — who woke up in a coffin with no memories — out of a burning Chernobog. The Catastrophe is about to strike the city, and there's barely an hour left.
The streets are swarming with Reunion. Destruction, looting, and arson never let up. Overhead, the Catastrophe clouds are already gathering, heavy and ominous.
This is the final stage of the rescue operation. There's only one goal: reach the southern exit alive.
This is the record of that short escape route — of Rhodes Island learning, firsthand, what it means to be Infected, and what it means to protect a comrade.

blocks.cap: The Catastrophe clouds press down on the city, dark and heavy. "It's taking shape. A bad omen."

blocks.x: 1. A Clinic Called Azazel
Even amid the tension, Rhodes Island manages a brief laugh. When Nearl seriously dismisses Reunion as "a bunch of idiots on a picnic," a Medic can't help but burst out laughing.
Nearl belatedly realizes she'd just made a joke and turns awkward. Even Amiya smiles quietly at the clumsy attempt at humor.
We didn't found Rhodes Island to watch you all suffer.
As they move along, the group finds a clinic in ruins — a place Amiya has visited before, the Infected clinic **Azazel**.
There's not even a trace that anyone was here. Azazel was connected to Rhodes Island's communication network, yet it never called for help — and now it's gone, just like this.
One Operator is furious, saying their own arrogance brought this on themselves. But Ace quietly stops him — it wasn't entirely their fault.

blocks.cap: "The Infected can't easily trust others. After everything they've been through, of course they've grown wary and stubborn."

blocks.x: Amiya tells the Doctor what Azazel used to be — the last refuge Infected hiding their identities in the city would secretly seek out, to avoid being exiled to a quarantine zone.

Oripathy grants tremendous power, but in exchange it eats away the body and the whole of a life. It strips away social rights, and death always waits at the end. And the corpse becomes a new source of infection.
That's why people treat the Infected not as the sick, but as something to fear. Chernobog is the very symbol of that discrimination, exile, and extermination.
Azazel must have refused Reunion's demands — and paid the price for it. Amiya doesn't resent their wariness. She says she can understand it, and forgive it.
Very few Infected ever get the chance to join Reunion or take Rhodes Island's hand. Most have already lost everything, and simply wait for death in despair.

To the Doctor, who probably can't fully grasp all of this yet, Amiya calmly adds that a day will come when he'll face it himself — along with the thought that Rhodes Island, too, might end up just like Azazel.
2. Less Than an Hour Left
There's now less than an hour until the Catastrophe. There's no time for a detour — they have no choice but to break straight through the Reunion-held district ahead.

Dobermann leaves the call to the Doctor. Since Reunion's communications are primitive and their formations scattered, the plan is to punch through the front line with a surprise strike and slip into the next district before the enemy can regroup.
Amiya says without hesitation that she trusts the Doctor's choice. But Dobermann doesn't let that answer slide.
Borrowing the Doctor's strategic mind is fine, she says, but Amiya shouldn't lean on him too much. A leader also needs to know how to stand alone.
She says this because she cares about Amiya. Dobermann quietly asks the Doctor, too, to understand her wish for Amiya to keep learning and growing.
Still, Dobermann makes it clear: trust isn't built in a single battle. That said, she adds — more than once — that the Doctor is doing well so far.
At the signal to charge, Rhodes Island breaks through the blockade in one stroke. Reunion, regrouping too late, gives chase, but the group has already slipped into the next district.

Meanwhile, somewhere in the city, the young commander **Mephisto** is reading the situation. Faust is tracking Rhodes Island, he notes, and they're now trapped on the outskirts of the central district.
Mephisto notices they're trying to flee amid the chaos of the Catastrophe. If they might get in the way of his plans, he thinks, ending it here wouldn't be a bad idea.
3. When the Sky Turns Blood Red
Reaching the great plaza, the full horror of the city comes into view. Citizens who couldn't escape in time are pinned down and attacked by Reunion.
But this terrain puts Rhodes Island at a disadvantage. Ace states, calmly but heavily, that they can't afford to rush in to protect the citizens.

Passing through the ruined plaza, Ace seems to recall something — that a city which once persecuted the Infected is now suffering their retaliation. Though she swallows the rest, saying it's all in the past.
Just then, a massive number of Reunion troops pour in from the plaza's exit. Dobermann senses something's off — Reunion has no reason to defend this place, so why are there so many of them?
Far from fearing the Catastrophe, Reunion is exhilarated, shouting, "Our savior of the Infected has come!" Then, for no apparent reason, they begin collapsing one by one.
The sky turns blood red. Nearl murmurs an old verse under her breath. "When the surging clouds swirl within the flames……"

blocks.cap: "Fear steals away their voices, and the earth falls into silence…… as vast Originium comes pouring down from the sky."

blocks.x: Somewhere in the plaza, two figures look down on this scene. Reunion agent **Crownslayer**, and someone with dragon horns.

blocks.cap: "Take care of whoever's left alive." — Above the Catastrophe, two shadows watch over Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: Only then does Dobermann realize: this isn't revenge. Reunion has declared war, aiming to wipe out Chernobog entirely with the Catastrophe — a proclamation that the age of the Infected has arrived.
4. Beneath the Catastrophe
Massive meteorites begin raining down from the sky. Buildings collapse, and Reunion soldiers scream as they're reduced to ash.
Just as falling rubble is about to hit a Medic, the Doctor throws himself forward and shoves her out of the way — putting himself in danger instead.
Nearl rushes in with her shield raised to cover the Doctor. Even as her armor scorches and her shield shakes, she shouts at him to run, to run now.
No matter what happens, I won't allow the Doctor to be hurt. Especially not right in front of my eyes!
Once the danger passes, Nearl scolds the Doctor — don't do something that reckless alone. From now on, take her along at least, she says; she'll protect him.
The first wave of the Catastrophe barely passes. The meteorites thin out, and every Operator gets away with only minor injuries. For a moment, relief that everyone survived washes over them.
But Reunion crawls back out from the ruins everywhere. This isn't pursuit — it's simply the number that survived the Catastrophe.
Even with more than half wiped out, there are still enough left to overwhelm Rhodes Island. Even Nearl is stunned — where on earth did Reunion gather so many Infected?
With numbers like this, they might actually be able to take Chernobog, Dobermann mutters bitterly. Just where did Reunion pull together so many Infected?

From within the encirclement, a single figure walks toward them alone. Nearl catches the scent of steel, sulfur, something burning. If this were meant to be an envoy, the footsteps are far too ominous.
The tyrant of Reunion. Amiya quietly speaks the name from the files.
A flame that will burn the whole land…… is that it.
5. Talulah
Her name is **Talulah**. When she gathers heat at her fingertips, the air around her shimmers like a heat haze, and everything it touches melts away.
Nearl tells Amiya to take the Doctor and flee immediately. Left unchecked, that monster will turn the entire rescue team to ash. But Amiya doesn't back down.
When Talulah quietly murmurs, "Be quiet," Nearl's armor is shredded in an instant. Even so, when Dobermann tells her to fall back, Nearl shouts back for her to fall back instead.
Talulah declares that Chernobog has been liberated. Her goal is already achieved, she says. Resistance may let hope sprout in this land, but it cannot change your fate.
Still, she shows interest in Rhodes Island, saying they have the makings of liberators. But she doesn't like it — they must pay the price for not standing on the true side of the Infected.
Let me give you an ending, in the way I like best. ……Perish.

blocks.cap: "Perish." — Heat that could swallow the entire plaza whole is unleashed from Talulah's fingertips.

blocks.x: Nearl lunges forward to block it, but it's too late. In that instant, Amiya steps in front of everyone.
Amiya raises a barrier of shadow-like power, single-handedly holding back Talulah's Arts to keep it from spilling beyond the plaza.

blocks.cap: "I can't let any of you get hurt. That's…… something I will not allow!"

blocks.x: The barrier begins to burn. Dobermann shouts urgently — at this rate, Amiya's ring, the last seal holding her back, won't hold.
Still, Amiya doesn't let go. Even if she ends up bringing disaster upon them, she can never again lose someone precious right before her eyes — even offering the Doctor an apology in advance.
Talulah murmurs an admiring "Impressive" at the sight. But Amiya's body has already reached its limit.
Nearl also tries to wring out her last strength and charge in, reciting a knight's vow to devote her body to Kazimierz's light. Dobermann shouts at the Doctor to go now — they'll help Amiya.
Then Ace stands beside Amiya. You've already done more than enough, she says — now let us carry this together.
Let us share your pain, too.
Ace tells Dobermann to take everyone and go on ahead, saying she'll follow soon. But no one believes it.
To the Doctor, who has lost his memories, Ace confides one last thing. He doesn't remember her, but she remembers exactly what kind of person he was.
Someday, you and Amiya will have to stand against this cruel land. So watch over Amiya well, Doctor.
6. Those Left Behind
Nearl and Dobermann break through the encirclement with Amiya and the Doctor, carrying the unconscious Amiya on their back.

blocks.cap: A small few who broke through the encirclement. The names of those left behind can't be told to Amiya just yet.

blocks.x: When Nearl tries to say something, Dobermann quietly stops her — Amiya mustn't hear it. She's already carrying far too much.
This is a path their comrades opened with their lives. That effort can't be wasted. Dobermann steels herself — now isn't the time to grieve.
Then she mutters bitterly to herself. Now that she thinks about it, not a single one of the people who ever told her not to worry has come back.

When Amiya wakes, she senses something's wrong. She insists she only blacked out for a moment and can walk herself, but Ace and the others are nowhere to be seen. Still, she doesn't press further. She tells herself Ace will be fine — everyone at Rhodes Island is strong, after all.
The southern exit is right ahead. As she walks, Amiya asks the Doctor if she can lean on him, just for a moment. Just a moment, just for a little while.
7. The Woman Blocking the Exit
They finally reach the exit, only for someone to block the way. Not one of Talulah's subordinates, not a guard — a woman who calls herself **W**.
W says she's been waiting here not for Rhodes Island, but to meet the Doctor. She has something to ask him, she says, and asks that he be handed over — calling him by name.

W mentions offhandedly that she ran into a scouting team on her way here, disguised well enough to fool even Ursusans. Amiya feels a flicker of relief, hoping they're safe — but quickly grasps the real meaning behind those words.
W asks Amiya what kind of magic she used to turn people into subordinates willing to gladly lay down their lives. Whether those people are even worth the sacrifice, to her.
That was the one thing Amiya couldn't bear to hear.
Every human life has worth…… and you have no right to insult that!
Nearl steps forward to protect the Doctor, but W stays relaxed, saying she's no match for Talulah and isn't a defense specialist anyway — as if she just wants to talk with the Doctor.
After a brief clash, W realizes something. "Ah, ah, haha…… so that's how it is…… now I remember, Amiya." Then she steps back, saying they can go now that she's gotten what she wanted.
Reunion withdraws as well, on Talulah's orders. W, saying she can't stand being bored, leaves them with a promise to meet again — to Amiya, and to the Doctor.
Next time, I'll make you tell me the truth yourself, Doctor.
8. The Last Two

Around that time, one Operator left behind in the plaza finally comes to. He blames himself, saying he could do nothing but scream through the hell of watching that monster called Talulah reduce everyone to ash single-handedly.

He'd watched Guard Operators get flung aside like chess pieces, Defenders crushed to death like butter. That monster itself was the Catastrophe made flesh, the subordinate repeats in a trembling voice.
The one who saved him with emergency first aid was Ace, who had already lost one arm. She still has the other one, she says, and she'll use it to go stop Talulah.
The subordinate begs her to flee, but Ace doesn't budge. She can't run until the Doctor and Amiya's safety is secured.
Your life cost me one arm. Even if all I bought you was one more minute, I believe your life was worth that much.
Leaving behind a promise to return soon, Ace walks back toward the monster, telling him to hold on. The Operator left behind closes his eyes, hoping Amiya will fulfill everyone's wish.
In a place where half the street had turned to charcoal and even steel had melted, someone was still fighting. Persistent enough that even Talulah murmured, "I'll remember this, Rhodes Island."
Epilogue — Not Yet, These Tears
Among the group that made it out, a Medic finally breaks down in tears. Amiya sits beside her and quietly passes on words she once heard herself.
Tears…… have to be saved until the war is over. Right now, it's still…… not allowed.
On the way back, Amiya volunteers to escort the wounded Operators at the rear, repeating only that she's fine. Even to a worried Nearl, she just says she's okay.
Amiya still doesn't know the exact names of the comrades left behind. She has her guesses, but can't bring herself to ask — she can only keep walking forward.

In escaping through the last hour before the Catastrophe, Nearl protected the Doctor, Amiya held everyone safe behind her barrier, and Ace and nameless Operators stayed behind — all to get the Doctor and Amiya to the southern exit.
And so Rhodes Island turns its back on burning Chernobog, tears set aside until the war ends, heading toward the place they'll return to.

blocks.cap: "Let's go…… let's go home…… to Rhodes Island."

main_10.tagline: A story of the rootless fighting over Londinium, each staking their own idea of 'home.' The Sarkaz swallow the city to claim a home; the Victorians hold on to reclaim theirs; and Amiya goes to see for herself what anger and grief have gathered at that war's heart. And standing there, at its heart, was someone she thought she'd lost.
