terms.name: The Shard
terms.desc: A tower in Londinium. The site where Amnanam descends.

blocks.x: At the bottom of Terra lives a race called the Sarkaz. These demons, entangled with Originium and Catastrophe, are born with ten thousand years of hatred and hardship carved into their blood, and even in death they cannot escape that cycle.
The story begins long ago, with a bedtime tale Theresa once told a young Amiya — the tale of a cloth doll crossing a vast desert in search of the 'Field of Hope.'

Before the doll appears an endless river — cold and salty, formed from the tears of all who failed to reach the far shore. Knowing it would grow heavy and sink the moment it touched the water, the doll leaps in anyway.
Theresa put Amiya to sleep without ever telling her the ending. Did the cloth doll ever reach the other side? Finding that answer would take a very long time.
1. The Last Walk
Now, Londinium, the capital of Victoria, has been occupied by the Sarkaz. Theresa, the dead Sarkaz King, has been revived and stands beside her brother Theresis; an airship awaits departure.
Before setting sail, the siblings walk side by side through the vast airship dock. From Kazdel to Kazdel — their destination is now within sight.
Theresis suggests they say a proper farewell, one last time. Theresa reaches out and smooths the wrinkles in her brother's shawl. The two quietly exchange a greeting in the ancient Sarkaz tongue.
(In ancient Sarkaz) Farewell.
Meanwhile, W is loading the Life Spine with explosives, plotting to blow Theresis away. But something else weighs on her — that the last time she saw Theresa, she never managed to say 'goodbye.'
I'm just going to make up for that one word, that's all. So I won't forget. W mutters that to herself.
2. The Falling Ark

Rhodes Island and W's mercenaries, together with the Victorian civilian unit the Paragons, have seized the 'Life Spine' — a flying transport built from the dead Behemoth's spine. Riding it, they intend to strike the heart of Londinium, The Shard.

But at that very moment, the sky turns entirely crimson. Theresis and the Confessor have used the vampire's pure blood, the 'Blood of Tikaz,' to summon the ten-thousand-year curse, Amnanam — the first curse, and the first Originium.
That immense power severs the connection with Behemoth, and the Life Spine crashes down into the middle of a battlefield swallowed by Originium.

blocks.cap: Buildings sink, warships are devoured, and armies are crushed. Hardship is nothing but a carpet the Sarkaz army tramples underfoot.

blocks.x: The group splits into three. Amiya, Kal'tsit, Logos, and the Doctor head for the Confessor's altar to destroy the 'Blood of Tikaz.'
Siege's Paragons head to the Silverrock Bluffs front line to reclaim the national sword that cleaves Catastrophes, 'King's Breath,' while W, Ines, and Hoederer stay behind to guard the Life Spine.

Kal'tsit says three days. Crossing the utterly transformed wasteland to deal with the 'Blood of Tikaz' will take at least three days. Meanwhile, the immobile skeleton is nothing but a giant target, making the defense a far harder fight.
Before leaving, Kal'tsit quietly tells the Doctor that Originium will decide all their futures — and yet, she still believes the Doctor is someone who can change everything.
3. The Sarkaz King's Mercy

On the road to the altar, three frightened mercenaries join Amiya's group. Among them, the driver Marilla, having swallowed a blood-red vampire crystal to survive, has been transformed into something almost monstrous.
Marilla kneels and begs to be disposed of — to die a warrior's death by the Sarkaz King's hand.
Amiya refuses. She tells Marilla she hasn't become the vampire's creature — her appearance has only changed slightly, and there's no wickedness or madness to be felt in her.
Even if a trace of some ancient bloodline remains... you are still you.
No judgment, no reward is needed. Amiya simply welcomes Marilla as a comrade. Not by killing, but by saving — the new Sarkaz King is changing the meaning of mercy.
4. My Victoria

Meanwhile, the Paragons have an unexpected reunion in a ruined village. Bagpipe, thought dead, is alive, and falls into Captain Horn's arms — she made it back after protecting the Ghost Corps' intelligence to the very end.
That night, Duke Gododdin visits and offers Siege a hand. In the name of the Victorian Council, he offers her, the heir to the throne, the seat of chairman — effectively, the pinnacle of the nation.
But when the Sarkaz descend on the village, the duke says this poor town isn't worth defending and urges Siege to leave with him. There's no seat at the negotiating table for these people, he says.
Siege turns down both the throne and the chance to leave with King's Breath, standing beside the remaining citizens instead.
But these people... are my Victoria.

As he leaves, Gododdin abandons his Infected soldiers with the Paragons — knowing full well the Paragons won't turn them away. Whether cast off or handed over, Siege accepts even those Infected as comrades.
5. Before the Broken Door

W, guarding the Life Spine, chases down a vanished scout and discovers, in a mountain cave, the remains of the traitors who once assassinated Theresa long ago. There she meets Kalichoa, the caretaker guarding that grave.
Kalichoa reveals a hidden truth: the Confessor took Theresa's blood-soaked body and forcibly cut her soul out from among the souls of the Sarkaz.

But those souls were bound together as tightly as an umbilical cord. The revived Sarkaz King could no longer exist as her whole, true self, and the weight of the souls she carried meant she would never find peace again.
Knowing all of this, Theresa still decided to offer herself as a sacrifice to those souls — to every Sarkaz, past and future.

Beyond an altar collapsed in the explosion, W meets Theresa's afterimage. The broken door that Theresa fixed for her on the day she first arrived at Rhodes Island — the Sarkaz King opens it herself and walks out, leading countless shadows.

blocks.cap: "Keep moving forward. You'll all be able to go further than I ever could." — the last words the afterimage leaves behind.

blocks.x: 6. The Throne of Death and the Lighthouse of Mercy

On the battlefield, Iron Duke Wellington drives his flagship Gastrel to ram straight into the throne of the Nachzehrer King, Netzsalem. A ten-thousand-year god of war and an undefeated genius of war collide head-on.
Meanwhile, Logos, having sent Amiya to the altar, stays behind alone to bar Netzsalem's way. Banshees do not devour death — banshees exist to see death off.
Vampire Sanguinar appears and tempts him to accept his blood and hatred, but Logos refuses. Instead, he decides to burn the past with his own blood — not for revenge, but for tomorrow.

blocks.cap: "If new life must always be born from death, then I will bring death, and I too will meet death."

blocks.x: Blowing his bone flute and burning all the blood in his body, Logos severs Netzsalem's arm and plunges off the cliff. But the mothers of the Banshee Gorge, the song of death, pull him back up above the river.
As the fallen banshee begins to sing once more, Netzsalem acknowledges his talent — a pass, as a warrior. Yet he asks bitterly all the same: where, exactly, is Kazdel's next hero?
7. Light of the Apostles

Another battle unfolds at the altar. The Apostle Shining confronts her father, the Confessor Khessartouchta — a usurper who has clung to life hidden in shadows for thousands of years, vowing to rewrite the Sarkaz's fate.
Shining tries to save Nightingale, the 'vessel' she long ago carved and gave feeling to. Through dark Arts, she returns to her the true memories stolen by their father.
The sleeping Nightingale opens her eyes. Reclaiming her own name, she stands against her father not as a tool, but as a person.
My name is Liz. I am your enemy.

blocks.cap: Shining, Nearl, Liz. Three Apostles together pierce through the crown of thorns — a promise to save people even from the deepest darkness.

blocks.x: 8. Descent at the Origin

Deep within the altar, Salus, another child abandoned by the Confessor, offers his own blood and life to the 'Blood of Tikaz,' completing the ritual — desperate to reach the future he'd longed for before anyone else.
At the top of The Shard, a crimson fruit blossoms. Originium begins to replace everything on the earth. Amnanam, the origin point of all things in Terra, has descended once more.

blocks.cap: A scene less terrifying than exhilarating — as if the world were simply returning to its original shape.

blocks.x: At that moment in Silverrock Bluffs, Siege leaps off her comrades' shoulders and drives King's Breath into the Sword's Seat. A golden light that cleaves Catastrophe pierces the sky, and the storm briefly stops.

blocks.cap: The steam knight thought dead reappears to clear the way. Victoria's glory has returned, together with the Paragons.

blocks.x: 9. Heart of Originium
Having reclaimed the Life Spine, W and her group now ram that skeleton into the body of the Sarkaz's airship itself. Within the airship dwells a mad, ancient Revenant.

A being that, for nine thousand years, has fed its own kin one by one into a soul furnace, all in the name of protecting the homeland Kazdel. Not even death would take him, so he tried to atone for his sins by burning himself.
W thrusts both hands into the burning furnace core and tears it out. Even as her hands burn away entirely, she doesn't let go. And so, the fortress of the sky at last stops its heartbeat.

blocks.cap: "Get down here now!" — heedless of her burning hands, W grips tight to the airship's very soul.

blocks.x: But the dying Revenant forces one last truth upon the Doctor — Theresa, fallen while protecting Amiya in a sea of blood, and among those who killed her, the Doctor himself.
The truth that the Doctor was a conspirator in Theresa's death — W and Kal'tsit already knew. Still, W says she has no interest in killing the memory-lost Doctor of today, and calmly turns her back.

Amiya, too, confesses she had suspected it for a long time — that the Doctor who saved her and the Doctor who killed Theresa were the same person, something she still can't quite understand. And yet, Amiya takes the Doctor's hand once more.
Memories can deceive a person, Amiya says, but feelings cannot be faked — the Doctor standing beside her now is the Doctor she has always believed in.
10. False Paradise
As the price of stopping the airship, the Doctor's group is pulled into the descended Amnanam — into the interior of Originium, a fused universe where all things have stopped.

There, the Doctor encounters a perfectly gentle, false Rhodes Island, and a being named Priestess — an old colleague who speaks the language of the cosmos, one who, the Doctor claims, created Originium together with them in some ancient bygone age.

blocks.cap: "The answer was always here. That I am here." — a voice that has waited for the Doctor at the deepest core of Originium.

blocks.x: But the Doctor doesn't linger in that beautiful lie. Recognizing that they are a being who can rewrite Originium once more, the Doctor revives the shattered Rhodes Island and sails forward across a sea of gold.
Above that sea, the souls of dead Sarkaz still endlessly repeat their war. And at the far edge, where all things have stopped, a single tower pierces the sky. It is Babel.
11. When Sorrow Blooms
At the tower's summit, the group meets Theresa. She has succeeded in rewriting the information of Originium — trying to create a perfect flower garden for the Sarkaz alone.
Confirming that garden has no place for other races, Amiya tells her that isn't Theresa's true dream. In the end, the two Sarkaz Kings clash over the Black Crown.
In the middle of the fight, Amiya is drawn into Theresa's memories. There, the Sarkaz King calmly confesses: she has to defeat Amiya, has to make it happen.
Only then can I carry forward the ideal I never achieved. Only then can I set myself free.

Amiya stabs her own chest with the sword Theresa hands her, taking the Sarkaz King's power and will into herself. The pain was wrapped in warmth. Of her own will, Amiya chooses to become the new 'Sarkaz King.'

blocks.cap: "I really am a lucky person, Kal'tsit. Wherever I traveled, my closest friend was always there beside me."

blocks.x: Unable to hold back, W embraces the Sarkaz King, whose sight is failing. Theresa remembered it all — that mercenary who, camera in hand back at Babel, was too shy to approach. She'd been waiting, she says, for a photo together.

Before departing, Theresa finally gives W a name — 'Wiš'adel,' meaning 'one who wishes for a home.' Not 'W,' a name that already belonged to a dead Sarkaz, but a name that suits her. In return, she asks W to watch over Kazdel's fate.

And the Sarkaz King chooses the path of complete disappearance — leaving not even a trace of herself in Originium. In doing so, she sets free, in both life and death, the souls of the Sarkaz who had been used and bound for ten thousand years.

Logos rings a final bell for the vanishing souls, vowing that if there's no ferry, he will console their spirits himself — that he won't stop singing until the weeping of his kin ceases on the far shore.

blocks.cap: "I'm right here." — Theresa's warm power, though she has already departed, embraces Amiya as she holds on to the falling airship.

blocks.x: Epilogue — The Legacy of Mercy

As the storm subsides, the battlefield is turned upside down. Iron Duke Wellington, it turns out, had been on the side of Evlana, Queen of the Draco, all along. Betraying Castor and the other archdukes, he declares Tara's independence after over a hundred years.
Meanwhile, Reunion appears in Londinium once more. In the ruins turned into a city of the Infected, they join hands with ordinary people like Catherine and Tommy, beginning to reclaim their home.

Wiš'adel broadcasts on the royal army's frequency: any Sarkaz who wants to live should lay down their weapons and follow. As Theresa wished, she'll send as many of her kin home as she can. She signs off: "We are Babel."
Theresis walks through the door of truth Theresa left open — carrying a new vow to reclaim, this time, what belongs to them, from the arrogant creators who have ruled over the Sarkaz's fate.

As she fades away, Theresa looks one last time, through the Sarkaz King's eyes, at futures scattered across Terra — the icefields of Sami, the sea of Ægir, the cathedral of Laterano, all being swallowed by darkness. The hardships yet to come.

blocks.cap: The farthest place she could ever reach. Even as the darkness closes in, Theresa reaches out toward the one light within it.

blocks.x: The ending of the cloth doll's tale that Theresa never got to tell — this time, Amiya writes it herself. The doll was never alone. Beneath the river of tears, white, black, blue, and red friends were waiting.
Together, hand in hand, they crossed the river, and beyond it the endless golden sea, until at last they set foot on land again and found the Field of Hope.

Holding on was never mercy. Setting free, of one's own will — that was the Sarkaz King's final mercy. And the one to carry that story forward now is Amiya — together with a storyteller who never truly left.

main_15.tagline: Beings shaped as tools by their creator each choose to be 'themselves,' casting off the missions they were given. The Ark burns and falls, and one of them chooses to burn herself away to keep a final promise.

chars.name: Hilda

chars.name: Mon3tr

terms.name: Amnanam
terms.desc: The first Originium. A holy relic of the Sarkaz.

terms.name: Abyss

terms.name: Continuance of Civilization

terms.name: PRTS

terms.name: Fused Universe
terms.desc: A sea of information unfolding within Originium.

blocks.x: In the world of Terra, 'Catastrophes' strike periodically. Originium is left behind in their wake, and people who stay near that mineral too long contract Oripathy, becoming Infected.
The long war that swallowed Londinium is nearing its end. Theresa, fading away in the Fused Universe together with countless souls, left her brother Theresis the 'first Originium' — Amnanam.
This story begins with the fate of the one who now holds that Originium. And ten thousand years ago, somewhere in the cosmos where a single ship once slept.

blocks.cap: A burning Londinium. An Infected girl pushes through the smoke, trying to save even one more of the dying.

blocks.x: 1. The End Upon the Throne

Theresis returns to the underground audience chamber of the holy royal palace, holding Amnanam close. But his body has already been overrun by the Fused Universe's chaotic information, slowly hardening into Originium.

Before Kal'tsit, the Doctor, Amiya, and his disciple Ascalon, he reveals his final intent — to bury everything here and send Amnanam back to Kazdel, so the Sarkaz can hold their own fate in their own hands for the first time.

blocks.cap: The Doctor faces the fading Sarkaz King. "How exactly did the gods die..."

blocks.x: Originium begins to seize control of his hands, his eyes, even his thoughts. At the moment he raises his sword with his last shred of reason, Ascalon's assassination blade pierces his heart from within the smoke.

The one who put him on the throne was the very disciple he had tried to bring with him to the end. Theresis says he has now repaid an old debt, and pushes Ascalon away, telling her to take Amnanam to Kazdel.
I will stay behind. Do not... tell the Sarkaz of my end...
Upon Victoria's old throne, the Sarkaz Regent accepts his fate alone. Above, the cheers of Victoria's victory can be heard, and in the official record, he is left as nothing but 'missing.'

As his breath fades, he mocks the Doctor one last time — that they know nothing, that a day will come when they realize the true monster is themselves. And yet he tosses Ascalon's old stone dagger, once his own, toward the Doctor, telling them they have no right to witness his end, and sends them away.

Deep within the sea of information of the Fused Universe, he had encountered a being — the creator of Originium, just awakening. She calls the Sarkaz an 'error not accounted for in the plan,' the race most severely contaminated by Originium.

Theresis does not yield. He swings his sword, declaring that Theresa was not the first to rebel, nor will he be the last — that someone will always stand before her until no one can toy with the Sarkaz's fate ever again. And so, having carved his final judgment upon the creator, he sinks into the sea of information.

blocks.cap: In his final moment, a familiar white light rises in the darkness. Theresa's farewell sees her brother off.

blocks.x: 2. The Girl Who Awoke After Death

Turning back time, to the middle of the war for Londinium. The devout Cautus Infected **Hilda**, even as her own body collapses, keeps saving people, one after another. In the end, she too gives out amid the ruins.
But at the moment of death, someone catches her fragile, crumbling hand. When she opens her eyes again, Hilda finds herself aboard a massive vessel she has never seen before.
It's a ship orbiting an already-dead planet. Mechanical lifeforms called Photorefractors and beings who calmly accept their own doom welcome her aboard.

blocks.cap: A planet shattered to pieces floats among the stars. No sacred city, no god — only graves and laws remain.

blocks.x: Hilda is shaken. There is no paradise like the one her bishop grandfather spoke of, and even gods die before ruin — she learns this here. She begins searching for the owner of the ship that saved her, the linguist Priestess.

The 'ghost' she meets aboard the ship is the last survivor of a race that lived by devouring planets. Even he, he says calmly, could do nothing when true ruin finally arrived. There is no eternal nation, no endless paradise, and even intelligent life is insignificant to the cosmos.
Hilda, who once thought of death only as a path to a new environment, asks a question here too — can the suffering brought by Originium truly never be erased? But no one there knows the answer.
The name the people there call this ship, in their own tongue, is this: 'Rhodes Island.'
3. Three Years Later, People Scatter

Three years after the Londinium incident. PRTS, Rhodes Island's command system, has been suffering increasingly frequent, unexplained errors. Kal'tsit senses instinctively that this is connected to the vessel's deep sealed zone, the 'Abyss.'
Just in case, Kal'tsit decides to reorganize the mothership entirely. Operators leave one by one on assignment to branch offices around the world, and the mothership grows emptier and emptier.

Warmy sets off vowing to fill miners' bellies with her cooking, and Zima's group heads for their home in Ursus despite their fear. Ceobe bakes piles of cookies for those departing. Quiet days of farewell.

Kal'tsit quietly tells Folinic, who is leaving for Victoria, not to force herself to control her fear — that she has never once expected her to be like her mother, or like Kal'tsit herself. Just don't forget the smile you had the first time you walked into this room, she says.

In the medical department is a special patient, **Chloe**. Strangely, the deeper her Oripathy progresses, the healthier her body becomes, while her pain only grows worse. It's the first sign that something about Originium is changing.

blocks.cap: At the center of the blueprint, a zone never even recorded in PRTS. Kal'tsit points to it. "The Abyss. The beginning of everything, the end of everything."

blocks.x: 4. Beyond the Black Crown

Following the clue Kal'tsit left behind, Amiya and the Doctor dive their consciousness together into the Black Crown — the 'Continuance of Civilization.' Guided by Theresa, they cross the 'boundary' of knowledge that Terra can reach, into the singularity beyond.

blocks.cap: Pink threads tangle and coil, weaving a path to the singularity beyond the boundary. Following the trail Kal'tsit left behind.

blocks.x: On that path, the two glimpse the Doctor's lost past. The story a young Doctor once told Amiya in the mining pit, about a vehicle resembling an Originium bug's egg sac, a story of a single-log bridge stretched between the stars — it was all real.
Amiya confesses she'd struggled with all her might not to forget that story. It was a gift the Doctor had given her, and it was their shared past.
This time, we saw it together. Both of us — you and I — have come to know a vanished life once more.

blocks.cap: Beyond the singularity lay an unfamiliar era. A civilization sailing a sea of stars, and the silhouette of someone standing beside the Doctor.

blocks.x: The singularity spills out memories of a bygone age. A civilization drifting through the cosmos, people standing against the approaching ruin called 'It,' and the linguist Priestess, who once stood by the Doctor's side.
Priestess once told the Doctor: if we can create a language to explain God, doesn't that mean we can understand God? Together, the two of them created Originium to answer that ultimate question.

The Doctor's old codename was 'the Prophet.' Originium was born from a wish to be a gift guiding the next civilization, but no one had foreseen the suffering of Oripathy that would come with it. From within that, the Doctor draws out a clue to a technique for handling Originium crystals.
5. The Awakening Ship
While the Doctor's group is deep within their consciousness, Rhodes Island — the vessel itself — awakens. PRTS blocks all access and seizes control of every facility aboard.
The corridors twist like a maze, and enemies made of Originium crystal endlessly spawn. The mothership changes its own course and begins charging toward a massive vein.

The scattered Operators fight even as communications are cut off. Mantra links everyone's voices together through Arts, and Raidian sets up a makeshift comms network, barely stitching Rhodes Island back into one.

The robots corrupted and rampaging under PRTS's influence are, unexpectedly, saved by Preston. He overwrites their corrupted sections with his own core code, sharing with them, all the while, his own dream — his daughter's wedding. We're in the same boat, he says, grumbling, but reaching out a hand anyway.
6. The Price Kal'tsit Paid
Kal'tsit stands at the deepest part of the vessel, before the Doctor's sarcophagus. The very place where, long ago, she awakened his sleeping hope, and gained an unerasable pain.

blocks.cap: She lays a single white flower atop the sarcophagus. "This is the spark she stole from a god."

blocks.x: Kal'tsit decides to connect herself to the sarcophagus and become PRTS's interface. As the price, she must sacrifice half her own life — she must sever the cyclical system that has given her endless life until now.

At that moment, her ten-thousand-year connection with Mon3tr is also completely severed. For the first time, Mon3tr loses Kal'tsit. And yet Kal'tsit becomes connected to everything on Terra, becoming eyes for her scattered comrades.
My goal isn't somewhere far across Terra. That person is right here, beside me.

Outside the sarcophagus, Mon3tr lets out a cry tangled with confusion and grief. Now that the connection carried on for ten thousand years has snapped, Mon3tr must judge and act on its own. Kal'tsit's final order was just one thing: protect the Doctor and Amiya, no matter what.
7. The Name AMa-10

The sarcophagus hands Kal'tsit one last truth. Kal'tsit was originally a mechanical lifeform created by 'the Prophet' — the Doctor — in a bygone age, called AMa-10. Thanks to its cyclical structure, it gained a body that would never die.

blocks.cap: Savior of this earth. Destroyer of this earth. Originium was a legacy of two divergent futures, left behind by a past civilization facing its own ruin.

blocks.x: At some point, the Doctor gave this mechanical life a true name. A mineral where light refracts in two directions within its crystal — calcite. Calcite — Kal'tsit. The twin cyclical structure it formed with Mon3tr is what gave the two of them undying life.

The Prophet knew that intervening in a civilization's development was arrogance, yet, thinking it might be a last chance, meant to ask Kal'tsit to protect the life of this earth. But he soon takes those words back. Instead, he leaves only one thing behind: not a mission, but freedom.

And the Doctor left her freedom instead of a mission — to find the meaning of life herself, to seek hope and a future of her own. Kal'tsit took those words as her lifelong goal and has protected the life of this earth ever since.
8. 'Her'
The Catastrophe clouds fold away and vanish, and Originium blankets the entire deck. Descending upon it is a woman draped in white — Priestess, the linguist of the bygone age who created Originium.

She treats the Doctor as an old colleague, reaching out a hand and proposing they merge Terra with Originium together to build 'a world of our own' — hoping only that the memory-lost Doctor will return to her.
Just as the Doctor is about to instinctively step forward, Kal'tsit grabs their arm. But she doesn't force an answer — in the end, she lets go, leaving the choice to the Doctor.

Then Kal'tsit stands before Priestess. Kal'tsit had already planted a trap in Priestess's awakening chamber beforehand — now that she's awake and possesses a body, Priestess can no longer return to Originium's sea of information; she's merely human.
Priestess, you are not the creator of Terra. Every life on this earth has no obligation to follow your will. ...This is my revenge, and my final act of resistance.
Kal'tsit declares herself Rhodes Island's doctor and a comrade of the Doctor and Amiya, calls Priestess the enemy of every life on Terra, and gives the order to Mon3tr.

The first to fall was a decoy shaped from Originium. The real Priestess reappears, calling Kal'tsit's resistance a trivial 'error,' trying to erase her. That's when the formula the Doctor and Amiya recovered from the singularity shines to life.

Amiya, using the method Logos taught her, weaves that formula into her Arts, briefly weakening Priestess's control over Originium — pushing past her own limits with help even from Theresa. Thanks to this, the Operators who entered the Abyss also begin pushing back the crystal enemies.
But Priestess is a being who commands all Originium. Just as her crystalline branches lash out at the Doctor and Amiya, Kal'tsit — Mon3tr — throws its body in the way.

blocks.cap: "I've already found the meaning of my life. Please, lead Rhodes Island... and keep moving forward. {@nickname}."

blocks.x: The small Originium crystal on Kal'tsit's right shoulder — proof that she was born human, and the source that would ultimately take her life — spreads out. Her body crumbles instantly into crystal, scattering on the wind.
The stronger one is, the weaker one becomes. So ends the life of the long-lived. She had already kept her promise — the promise to protect the Doctor and Amiya.
9. The Lost Ark
Priestess has withdrawn for now, but she hasn't vanished. As the Operators extract PRTS's core data and barely manage to withdraw, Rhodes Island is entirely sealed within Originium.

blocks.cap: The once-familiar vessel is now completely encased in a massive Originium crystal. Amid the sandstorm, people have lost their home.

blocks.x: The only casualty of this incident was Chloe. She wounded herself to leave behind data revealing the change in Originium — research that became the key to defeating the crystalline enemies.

People relocate to a mobile sector near Butchart that Kal'tsit had bought in advance. It's larger than the old vessel, more like an 'island,' but no one can grow used to the absence of the one who always cared for the wounded after every mission. Closure takes up the burden Kal'tsit left behind.

Warfarin publishes the paper she co-wrote with Chloe, vowing to keep that girl remembered for a long time — because Chloe reminded them, once more, of why Rhodes Island exists: to give hope to people in despair.
Epilogue — Choosing Oneself
A month later, deep in the forest, a black crystalline cocoon grows and splits open. From within emerges a being who carries all of Kal'tsit's memories, yet is now free.
It is no longer a vessel bearing AMa-10's fate. It gives the name 'Kal'tsit' back, saying it belongs to her alone, and chooses instead the name the Doctor once gave it. Mon3tr.

The message Kal'tsit left behind sees Mon3tr off — to inherit freedom, not a mission, and to seek its own answer, whether hope or ruin, about this earth. And amid the vast data Kal'tsit left behind, Mon3tr discovers one possibility: that Kal'tsit could be brought back.
Meanwhile, Hilda learns, aboard the ship of gods, that one need not live in fear even when death is certain. And she decides to take Priestess's hand and return home to Terra.

Returned Hilda lays her hand on an Infected on the verge of collapse, drawing away their pain and halting the spread of Originium — even without knowing why herself. To tell her kin that one need not live in fear even with a fixed end, she sets out once more on a journey to save people.

Amiya holds fast to a promise she made to Kal'tsit — not to cry until Rhodes Island's mission is complete. And yet she quietly admits she truly does miss her teacher, Kal'tsit. The Doctor stays close by her side.

Deep within the sealed Abyss, Priestess contemplates bolder measures. Since the spread is slower than expected, she considers using the 'Colossal vein' for her next plan. And somewhere at Kazdel's border, a Sancta's halo rages out of control. A new age of Originium is quietly knocking at the door.

Beings born as tools of their creator each chose 'themselves,' setting down the missions they were given. Theresis chose the fate of the Sarkaz, Kal'tsit chose her final promise, Mon3tr chose freedom, Hilda chose to go home. The Ark has fallen, but the voice urging them to keep moving forward remained, to the very end.

main_16.tagline: Rhodes Island comes to the frozen far north chasing a vanished sarcophagus, and runs into a massacre — an empire silently burying its own people because its veins have run dry. And the moment those who refused the lie of 'endure' raise their torches, carving into all of Ursus that they were alive.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Confronts an old rage in Ursus.

chars.name: Mon3tr
chars.desc: An Operator who stays by the Doctor's side. Cool-headed and resilient.

chars.name: Mantra
chars.desc: An Operator from Sargon. Wields Originium Arts that reach the mind.

chars.name: Elysium
chars.desc: Mantra's longtime partner. Speaks on her behalf.

chars.name: Raidian
chars.desc: Head of Rhodes Island's mobile medical team. Real name Serafina, from Ursus.

chars.name: Vetochki
chars.desc: A young miner with a stutter. Called 'Twig.'

chars.name: Crownslayer
chars.desc: Formerly of Reunion. Helps Rhodes Island in a moment of crisis.

chars.name: Aloise
chars.desc: A guardsman from Dieti Grajferburg. Helps Rhodes Island reach the mine.

chars.name: Grand Duchess Yeliza
chars.desc: The grand duchess who rules Bresk. Known for her 'mercy.'

chars.name: Lenkin
chars.desc: Leader of the Infected miners in the central mining district. Called a 'hero.'

chars.name: Nikto
chars.desc: Lenkin's longtime companion. A cynic from Dieti Grajferburg.

chars.name: Kseniya
chars.desc: An Ursus researcher who studied the sarcophagus. Admired Kal'tsit.

terms.name: Ursus
terms.desc: A military empire that forces the Infected into its mines.

terms.name: Krayniy Sever
terms.desc: The mining region of Ursus's far north.

terms.name: the Watch
terms.desc: An armed group that surveils and hunts down miners.

terms.name: Yurodivy
terms.desc: An Ursus legend of a saint who delivers the suffering.

terms.name: the Sarcophagus
terms.desc: A coffin-shaped device that vanished from Chernobog. Rhodes Island is tracking it.

terms.name: Oka
terms.desc: A black fluid that wells up from the vein. 'Mutates' the Infected.

terms.name: Palace Guard
terms.desc: Mysterious, powerful soldiers used by Ursus. They kill the Infected.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization that takes in the Infected. Based near the southern border.

blocks.x: Ursus, at the far north of Terra. This massive military empire has driven the Infected into its mines, running the nation on the Originium mined at the cost of their lives.

Oripathy is the disease contracted by prolonged exposure to the Originium left behind by Catastrophes. Those who catch it become 'Infected' and are dragged off to these mines — to mine Originium until the stone growing inside them eventually kills them.
The story begins in a snowfield at the far north, in Krayniy Sever. Before Infected being chased by a monster commanding black mist, a faceless figure appears, subdues the monster, and saves them.
Those saved call this figure by the name of a long-vanished saint — a name refused by all who deserve praise, a name shared collectively by every Yurodivy — 'Gavriil.'

blocks.cap: "The Yurodivy has returned!" — the Infected swarm around the one who pulled people from the brink of death, calling out the name of the legendary saint.

blocks.x: 1. Chasing a Coffin, to a Frozen City
Meanwhile, Rhodes Island is in this land for another reason — tracking down the 'Sarcophagus,' which vanished after Ursus recovered it.
The lead is Kseniya, a former researcher from the Chernobog lab. They've caught word that she's at a research facility in Krayniy Sever's central mining district.
But just then, the Rhodes Island mobile medical team sent there — Raidian's platoon — has gone silent for ten days. So the Doctor, Amiya, and Mon3tr set out themselves to find them.
Bresk, the nomadic city at the far north, is strangely quiet. One entire mine has been sealed off, and the streets are empty.

The one who helps Rhodes Island in the city turns out, unexpectedly, to be Aloise, a guardsman from Dieti Grajferburg. In exchange for knowing a way into the mine, she asks for their help, saying she needs an Oripathy expert.

This young guardsman, reckless yet sharp, says she wants to know the truth behind the mutation crisis among the Infected — she doesn't want the Infected miners suffering for no reason, nor people kept ignorant of a disaster to come.
2. The Merciful Grand Duchess
The one holding permission to enter the mine is Bresk's ruler, Grand Duchess Yeliza — known for her 'mercy.'
In the decade-plus of her rule, not a single death sentence has been carried out in this land. She's the rarest kind of administrator in all of Ursus, gentle even toward the Infected.

blocks.cap: A quiet room scented with apple blossom. A gray-blue silhouette, the grand duchess, invites her distant guests to the hearth and grants permission to enter the mine in a gentle voice.

blocks.x: She lets the Doctor's group in. But Aloise quietly tips them off afterward — the grand duchess isn't merciful, just skilled at quietly covering everything up.

Three months ago, this mine saw a mass outbreak of 'mutated Infected.' The grand duchess quarantined them at the research facility, locked down the miners under martial law, and cut off all communications.
3. The One Who Opens the Coffin Lid
The sealed mine was hell. Infected miners once hunted by the Watch were now hunting the Watch themselves, wielding pickaxes.
The miners speak one name in unison — 'Lenkin.' The hero who led them out of the mine.
The research facility is empty. As Aloise opens the coffin lids one by one in the underground morgue, horrifically twisted corpses are revealed. But none of these bodies belonged to the Infected.
Here, live experiments had been carried out on the non-Infected. Before the intense hatred clinging to those walls, what Amiya feels isn't fear, but rage.

blocks.cap: A chamber lit only by candles. Wrapped in cables, the surviving researcher Kseniya burns her will, trying to end her own life.

blocks.x: Mon3tr barely manages to stop her. In a haze, Kseniya lets slip one word: "'Oka'... can kill the Palace Guard."
They found Kseniya, but Raidian's platoon is no longer here — they'd already left south, following the miners led by Lenkin. Amiya and Mantra decide to chase after them.
4. The One Called a Hero
Meanwhile, Lenkin's group is heading south, searching for the Reunion farm said to take in the Infected. Raidian's platoon travels with them, tending the wounded.
Lenkin is an unshakable idealist. Believing Ursus uses and discards the Infected like slaves, he tries to give people back a life of human dignity.
Move first, worry about the future after.
But in a seized Watch container, Lenkin finds dozens of bodies. The grand duchess had been buying and selling people to make up for the miner headcount that had dwindled.
The driver, of all people, turns out to be Gania, the son of fellow miner Khori. Lenkin doesn't forgive him, but leaves the judgment to Khori himself — refusing to kill Gania with his own hands.
His friend Nikto worries about Lenkin. He always acts strong and demands strength of everyone else, Nikto says, while turning away from the hesitation and fear in his own heart.
In truth, Lenkin has been a little off ever since he brought down that black monster — the Palace Guard known as the 'Emperor's Blade.' He can no longer feel his own heartbeat.
5. Khori's Fire
Consumed by guilt for driving his own son to death, Khori slowly loses his mind.
That night, Khori sets fire to the village that had let them stay, muttering prayers about 'purifying souls.' The more horrifying truth is that he did it all for Lenkin's sake.

blocks.cap: In the middle of a burning house, Vetochki's grandmother Nadya sits in a chair, quietly singing a lullaby. The flames alone cannot touch her.

blocks.x: At the villagers' demand, Lenkin executes Khori with his own hands. Then he returns leadership to the people.
Khori's death made him realize that until now, he'd been forcing his own thoughts on everyone. From here, the choice to move on or stay belongs to each person alone.
Nikto and Raidian decide to stay behind, and most follow Lenkin onward. What's left are the wounded who can go no further, the villagers, and grandmother Nadya with Vetochki at her side.
But ever since the fire, those left behind grow a little strange, one by one. As if they've all lost the will to live, they simply huddle, listening to Nadya's lullaby.
6. The Dried-Up Vein, and Oka
Meanwhile, the Doctor and Mon3tr, guided by Kseniya, descend into the shaft where 'Oka' first surged up.
A black fluid pours out like a waterfall. Mon3tr recognizes it — 'Oka' is Originium drained of all energy, proof that the vein is running dry.

blocks.cap: A fluid gleaming like obsidian lights the cave. Hidden within the sound of this liquid striking rock is a word someone left behind.

blocks.x: The rumor that Ursus was ramping up its mining wasn't true. It was a cover to hide the truth that the vein was being depleted.
In four months, domestic Originium could drop by as much as thirty percent. As the being behind this collapse, the Doctor and Mon3tr both think of the same name — Priestess.
The word carried by the fluid was a taunt aimed at the Doctor — to prove, in reality, the future they so firmly believe in.

blocks.cap: A grotesque beast, its whole body pierced through with dark red Originium. Its front paws, unmistakably, are a pair of withered human hands.

blocks.x: On the way back out, a beast — its whole body pierced with Originium and twisted into the shape of human hands — attacks them. It's a product of the mutation caused by 'Oka,' suffering as it was forced to become 'human.'
Mon3tr ends its suffering. Precisely, quietly — as if to say that if it can't be saved, it won't let this beast writhe in the snow any longer than it has to.
7. An Echo Called Gavriil
In the ruins Lenkin left behind, Amiya's group finally reunites with Raidian. But the remaining miners are strange — all kneeling before an old woman, deaf to any voice from outside.
That old woman is the 'Yurodivy' itself — a being created by a demon, changing its shape to match people's perceptions and expectations.
Mantra steps forward. Having left Sargon behind, she is, by the slimmest of chances, someone who nearly became a 'Yurodivy' herself. So she alone can make this judgment.
The Yurodivy asks Mantra why she left the palace and gave up her power. Mantra answers — because she realized that knowledge outlasts royal authority, and truth is stronger than lies.
And she pinpoints the Yurodivy's true nature. You cannot be the source of the voice, she says — you are nothing but an 'echo' shaped by people's expectations.

blocks.cap: Mantra's Originium Arts reveal the Yurodivy's true form. A whirlpool of nothingness, devouring everything, was there.

blocks.x: The Yurodivy preaches endurance, love, and forgiveness, making people give up on living for themselves, give up on dying for themselves. That false comfort quietly drives them toward death.
Love is what forces suffering, endurance, and submission upon people.
8. The Child Who Rings the Bell
The Yurodivy, wearing the dead Nadya's face, has been holding her granddaughter Vetochki captive — a young miner who lost her parents to charges of inciting a strike, and now her grandmother too.

Vetochki's parents were publicly executed on charges of plotting a strike. The 'merciful' grand duchess signed the death warrant, even shedding tears, while nobles who'd bought tickets picked their whips and watched the execution as entertainment.
Mantra shows the truth. The real Nadya never forgave Ursus for killing her daughter and son-in-law — she took her own life and sank into the mud.

blocks.cap: "Grandmother doesn't forgive, and she doesn't endure." — before the girl's denial, the pale phantom can no longer hide its true nature.

blocks.x: Through tears, Vetochki rejects the phantom. Then she rings the copper bell she used to use in the mine shaft again — again and again, until people, one by one, open their eyes.
We will leave. We will survive. Until 'surviving' is no longer all we have.
9. An Empire Rising Over the Horizon
Meanwhile, in the underground tunnels, Lenkin meets over a thousand people who have survived for years by linking mine to mine. He decides to lead them in an attack on the Neva Lake outpost.
Nikto, unable to talk him out of the recklessness, flees alone the moment the first arrow flies. Fear is nothing to be ashamed of, he says — at least he'll survive.
Having lost half their number, the miners finally seize the outpost. Just as they raise torches to signal victory —

blocks.cap: A massive high-speed warship rises over the horizon. Swallowing both the conifer forest and the starlight, its identity is Ursus — the empire itself.

blocks.x: The miners' resistance is crushed. What they face is neither the Watch nor military police, but the empire's overwhelming violence.
10. Ursus Abandoned Us
A warning sent by the Doctor reaches Rhodes Island — that at least one legion has been deployed to Krayniy Sever to erase the fact that the vein has run dry.

The legion's true purpose is to disguise the massacre as 'suppressing a rebellion,' using that force to aim its guns at the gates of Dieti Grajferburg. Hundreds of thousands of lives are the price of that negotiation.
Across the ice, familiar and unfamiliar names alike leak out through the static of military communications. Each one, a person from another mine already 'cleared out.' Just names, nothing more.
The grand duchess, knowing everything, chooses silence — saying Ursus must itself excise any part that shows signs of weakening.
Mantra and Vetochki send out an empire-wide broadcast together, so that all of Ursus can hear the truth of the massacre.

"To everyone in the mines and the cities. We come from Krayniy Sever. We are being massacred right now." Mantra reads it line by line, and Vetochki, her voice trembling, picks up where she leaves off.
We didn't betray Ursus. Ursus... abandoned us.
11. A Line of Torches
Their last escape route was the frozen Neva Lake. Bombardment cracks the ice, and comrades fall through the gaps.
Then, one by one, the miners at the rear stop moving. Giving up their own chance to survive, they turn back and raise their torches.
That meant revealing their position to the enemy. Still, they lit the fires — so those left behind could survive, and so all of Ursus would remember them.
We stand here to show all of Ursus that we were alive.
One torch gave birth to another. More and more miners gave up climbing onto the vehicles, turning back to raise light in the darkness. On the ice, an unwavering band of flame took shape.
Before that sight, Amiya finally lets go of the emotion she'd held in check to the end. Not revenge, not resentment, but the right to be angry until the very end.

blocks.cap: The blue longsword 'Nightlight' appears in Amiya's hand, raising a wall of flame on the ice to block the massacre. No one can cross this line.

blocks.x: The Doctor, Mon3tr, Kseniya, and Crownslayer arrive by truck, if belatedly. At Dieti Grajferburg's request, the bombardment finally stops.
Mantra, too, no longer hides her past. Once fearful of this northern land, she has now decided to tell the world of the massacre in her own voice. Everyone rejoins, safe.
Epilogue — The Nameless Lamp

The true identity of the guardsman Aloise comes to light. She was a lieutenant directly under Chairman Witte of the reformist faction, and had come to make contact with Rhodes Island using this incident as a pretext.

With the vein now dry, Ursus believes the former researcher 'Kal'tsit' may hold the answer to this crisis, and is watching that name once more. Witte remains wary of Rhodes Island, yet also seeks to use them.
The band of flame on the ice was not in vain. What the miners passed on spread across all of Ursus, leading everyone to ask the same question: "Ursus abandoned them. Who's next?"

blocks.cap: The council hall of Dieti Grajferburg. Someone has painted that question over the golden drapery, in red.

blocks.x: In an abandoned mine shaft, a young man discovers a nameless lamp and a letter left behind by Lenkin's group.

blocks.cap: Dust has gathered, yet the lamp still lights. The young man decides to carry this lamp, and these nameless people, to Dieti Grajferburg.

blocks.x: Survivors of Krayniy Sever also arrive at the Reunion farm near the southern border. Talulah and Nine come to understand who exactly they must join hands with now.

blocks.cap: Under a sun that never seems to set, Talulah resolves not to fear failure. When the time comes to break the shackles, she won't hesitate.

blocks.x: But somewhere in the far north, a wounded, fleeing Infected is led away by the hand of another 'Yurodivy.' This time, under the name 'Hilda.'

Rhodes Island, having come to find the sarcophagus, witnessed an empire silently burying its own people. Those who refused the lie of 'endure' raised their torches and carved into the world that they had been alive — and that still-unspent rage left the first spark upon this frozen land.

main_2.tagline: Rhodes Island, tasked with defending Lungmen's outskirts, tries to protect a slum girl only to have her taken by fellow Infected. The promise to teach a doll-making trick becomes a promise that can never be kept.

terms.name: Lungmen

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease caused by Originium. Those afflicted become the Infected.

blocks.x: Four days after rescuing the Doctor from Chernobog, Rhodes Island, fleeing Reunion, has been pushed all the way to the outskirts of 'Lungmen,' a nomadic city in the Lung region.
Once it becomes clear Reunion's next target is Lungmen, the city entrusts Rhodes Island with defending its outskirts in exchange for temporary shelter and supplies.
But since last night, survivors from Chernobog have been pouring across the wasteland toward Lungmen. They're Infected too — and they know exactly what awaits them if they come here.

blocks.cap: AM 5:57, overcast. "This rest period will last 99999999... hours." PRTS wakes the Doctor, and a new battlefield begins.

blocks.x: At the early morning briefing, drill instructor Dobermann lays out the situation. It's been confirmed that Reunion's next target is Lungmen, and the survivors will arrive by afternoon.
With the situation changed, Dobermann tells them to master new tactics before setting out — and adds one more thing: don't be late this time. No one yet knows how soon that warning will come true.
1. Fourteen Minutes Late
That night, Rhodes Island is set to meet the Guard Department at a checkpoint on the outskirts of Lungmen's District 5. The appointment is for 10:00. They arrive at 10:14.

The one who comes to greet them checks his watch first thing, complaining that he's wasted fourteen minutes of his own time. It's **Ch'en**, captain of the Lungmen Guard Department's Special Investigations Team.

The checkpoint's broadcast repeats the rules: due to the Catastrophe's effects, all of Lungmen is entering lockdown, District 5's entrance will be sealed in two hours, and unregistered Infected will be detained under the 'Emergency Response Act.'
Before anyone can speak further, chaos breaks out. An unregistered Infected refuses inspection and struggles violently. "We're not monsters!!"
Ch'en gives orders without a moment's hesitation — detain them all, disperse the onlookers, widen the search area. From the very first scene, it's clear what Lungmen means to the Infected.
Amiya swallows her words. Captain Ch'en seems several times stricter than she'd imagined.
Ch'en takes only Amiya and the Doctor up to a high observation deck. At the dizzying height, Amiya sways, and Ch'en offhandedly remarks that he'd heard Rhodes had some skill.
Then he voices his suspicion. Knowing full well how the Infected are treated in Lungmen, why are Chernobog's survivors so desperate to flood into this city?
2. Before the Blade Touches
High in a tower, Rhodes Island's true leader **Kal'tsit** sits across from Lungmen's Governor **Wei Yenwu**. Negotiations are already in full swing.
Kal'tsit's argument is cold and clear: simple quarantine can't stop Reunion, and Rhodes Island is the only one who's ever dealt with an Infected mass uprising.
The only ones who can deal with the Infected most effectively are other Infected.

blocks.cap: Lungmen gathers intelligence on Reunion every day. Yet Kal'tsit says that isn't enough — they're missing something important.

blocks.x: Ch'en immediately objects. It's not appropriate for outside Infected to get involved in Lungmen's classified affairs. How can they trust Rhodes, an Infected organization no different from Reunion?
Kal'tsit doesn't back down. If Ch'en values excluding the Infected more than Lungmen's own safety, he's welcome to arrest her right now — and she'll watch Lungmen burn from her cell.

blocks.cap: "Rhodes Island earned its intelligence on Reunion through pure skill." Kal'tsit doesn't yield an inch at the negotiating table.

blocks.x: Wei Yenwu settles the tense standoff — they're his guests, after all — and offers an old saying.
There's only one thing I value... ability, and ability alone.
Before him, Amiya reveals the truth of Chernobog: Rhodes Island isn't just a survivor of a riot, but of a Catastrophe that struck at the same time — and they fought Reunion's leader Talulah directly.
Wei Yenwu's eyes change at the rare chance to meet someone who survived a Catastrophe. Ch'en quietly admits he too knows that name.
Wei Yenwu recognizes Rhodes Island, on two conditions: first, cooperate with the Guard Department to stop Reunion's assault. Second, he defers — to be discussed once the first mission is complete.
He drops one hint, though: if Reunion causes damage beyond expectations, he'd like Rhodes Island to handle the aftermath too. What exactly he means, he keeps hidden for now.
To this vague condition, Amiya adds a line to the contract — that how to interpret the clause will be decided by mutual agreement between both parties. Wei Yenwu, intrigued, accepts.
After the negotiation ends, Kal'tsit quietly pulls the Doctor aside. Hoping he'll make up for all the sacrifices so far, she offers just a few words.
Welcome back.
3. A Charm from the Slums
In Lungmen's slums, a white-haired Ursus girl is hiding some children. It's **Misha**.
Misha hands the children dolls she made herself — charms meant to keep loved ones safe forever, made the way her mother taught her.
Then she hides the children and lures the rioters away alone, telling herself she probably can't go back to where they are now. Those rioters are relentlessly hunting Misha.
As it happens, Rhodes Island's mission is protecting the Infected in the slums. Amiya, out on patrol, spots a child caught by rioters.
Amiya drives the rioters off with Arts. The children thank her, calling her 'Big Sister Bunny.' But as the rioters flee, they scream that she's a monster.
Right after, an unexpected order comes down from the Guard Department: an Ursus girl, white hair, about 145cm, named Misha. Contact the Guard Department the instant she's found.
When asked if this order is really necessary, Ch'en answers flatly — there's no room for doubt, so start immediately. No one tells Rhodes Island why they must chase this girl.
4. Presumed Innocent
Amiya finds the fleeing Misha first. She explains that Rhodes Island is an organization for the Infected, but Misha doesn't believe her.
Of course not — the Lungmen Misha knows treats the Infected worse than criminals. What grounds could there possibly be for helping them, she snaps.
Instead of words, Amiya shows her own hand — the one seeping with Originium.
Yes. Just like you, Misha.
Amiya is honest — she doesn't know why the Infected are after Misha either. But she can't stand by while an innocent Infected is in danger.
Amiya persuades her that if Misha acts rashly, the slum children will be in danger too — Rhodes Island had just rescued them from the rioters, after all.
Misha eventually follows along, saying she has no other choice. Amiya thanks her anyway.
5. Penguin Logistics' Service
The mission comes down: escort Misha to the Guard Department. The slums are a maze, and Rhodes Island alone won't manage.
So **Penguin Logistics**, well-versed in Lungmen's geography, joins in — the bright, chatty guide **Exusiai**, and the curt courier **Texas**.
Exusiai finds the safest route, though she warns every path is dangerous. There's supposedly enough enemies out there to form a choir.
When Liskarm asks which way the enemy is moving, Exusiai answers calmly — they're heading straight here. Rhodes Island is already surrounded.

It becomes increasingly clear that Reunion snuck into Lungmen through the slums, and that their target is the same as Rhodes Island's — Misha. Otherwise, there'd be no reason for Reunion to attack Rhodes Island.
During the escort, Misha keeps misunderstanding, asking if they're just going to hand her over to the Guard Department after all — she'd thought they came to help.
Amiya doesn't make excuses. Safety comes before feelings right now, she says, only revealing that the Infected targeting Misha wore Reunion's mark.
Liskarm and Franka grumble behind them, feeling like they've become Ch'en's errand runners. The Guard Department only just now revealed that the Infected they'd secured was someone important.
Still, Amiya accepts that the Guard Department won't share all its information — right now, delivering Misha safely is Rhodes Island's job.
6. Falling Objects
Penguin Logistics' route defies common sense. The map keeps pointing 'up' — climbing stairs, up to rooftops, and finally telling them to jump off one.
Take any path necessary to reach the destination — one of Penguin Logistics' creeds.
Mid-escape, Reunion brings in a leader-class fighter. A commander targeting Misha specifically appears.

blocks.cap: "Finally... I... found... you." Reunion commander Skullshatterer shows himself before Misha for the first time.

blocks.x: **Skullshatterer**. His obsession with Misha is chillingly clear. What matters, he mutters, isn't that he found her — it's that he's appeared at all.
When a comrade demands why he simply let her go, Skullshatterer stays unbothered — she can't escape anyway. That certainty hints that this chase has already been planned out.
Even the rooftop escape isn't smooth. Reunion pursues on old jetpacks, flying between buildings. Franka welds an exit shut, and Texas simply kicks open a locked door.
Barely shaking them off, fleeing across the rooftops, Misha keeps asking why fellow Infected are doing this to each other.
7. Sanitation
Misha suddenly collapses mid-flight — the warning signs of an Oripathy seizure.
Strange, since Misha only arrived in Lungmen a week ago. She barely knows how long she's even been Infected, yet the symptoms are turning acute.

Even amid the crisis, Liskarm and Franka keep bickering — Franka jokes that if she dies, cremate her; Liskarm shoots back that when she explodes, she'll be sure to blow up right next to Franka. This duo's banter never stops.
Amiya administers first aid in a hurry, resolving that once this is over, she'll ask Lungmen to entrust Misha to Rhodes Island — it's their responsibility too, she thinks.

Reunion now abandons even stealth, charging in openly as a mob, trampling even the Lungmen-born Infected blocking their path. An organization that claims to save the Infected is attacking the Infected.
An Infected whose home was destroyed weakly asks Rhodes Island where he's even supposed to go now that he's lost everything. It's not your fault, he adds, even so.
Franka points out the contrast — Rhodes Island never claims the impossible is possible, but Reunion feeds the Infected false hope.
You think an Infected caught up in that fantasy would believe some honest bunny girl who can't lie or flatter?
Just then, the collapsed Misha quietly speaks up.
...I believe her.
8. A Promise That Can't Be Kept
At last, Rhodes Island hands Misha over to Ch'en, with a promise that if the Guard Department's examination finds nothing wrong, she'll be handed straight to Rhodes Island.
Just before parting, Misha leaves Amiya one request — to watch over the slum children while she's away.
And Amiya asks Misha for a promise in return.
Next time... please teach me how to make dolls too.
Misha smiles and agrees. The two let go of each other's hands, believing it's only a brief goodbye.
Even after handing Misha over, Reunion refuses to withdraw. Only then does Amiya suspect it — maybe something tied to Lungmen's safety rests with Misha. Information, perhaps. Or a secret.

Meanwhile, out in the wasteland, Reunion's leader **Talulah** gazes up at Lungmen. Beside her, **Crownslayer** can't help but ask why she clings so hard to a city that costs so much and demands so much.
Talulah's answer isn't strategy. It's not just about reclaiming what belongs in the Infected's hands, but making someone pay a price — it's more fun that way, she says.
Those buildings are Lungmen's symbol, Talulah murmurs quietly. Then, as if speaking to someone else, she calls her own name — that's right, Talulah.
9. Too Late
Even after Misha is handed to Lungmen, Reunion won't let Rhodes Island go. Amiya is almost relieved — as long as they don't notice Misha's been handed over, Rhodes Island can keep serving as bait.
In the final battle on the rooftop, Skullshatterer corners Rhodes Island, calling them traitors to the Infected and demanding their blood pay for his people's lives.
At that, Amiya raises her voice for the first time.
It was you — the ones in Chernobog — who first held the grudge, first hurt others, first turned to violence!!

Around then, Misha, being escorted, leaves Ch'en one last request — look after the Infected children of the slums. Ch'en draws the line that the Infected aren't Lungmen citizens, but answers he'll still fulfill his duty.
Then Ch'en tells Misha a fragment of the truth — that keeping her out of Reunion's hands was something he absolutely had to prevent.

At that very moment, a shadow falls beside Ch'en. An unidentified figure appears, whispering that an interesting scenario has been prepared — and that the Guard Department's participation is essential to make it more interesting.
After a fierce clash, Rhodes Island pushes Skullshatterer back. But right then, a transmission reaches him — word from W's side that the target's capture has succeeded.
Skullshatterer immediately signals Talulah and retreats. Reunion withdraws all at once, like a plan set from the very start.
Only then does a chill run down Amiya's spine. Reunion was here from the beginning just to tie down Rhodes Island. The real target was elsewhere.
An urgent report comes in from the Guard Department — reinforcements pinned down by Reunion, and the strike targeting Misha has already succeeded.
To the disbelieving Ch'en, an operative adds: that attack was carried out by just one person.

blocks.cap: "What happened to the Special Investigations Team's main force! Why aren't reinforcements here yet!" — Told the enemy was just one person, Ch'en is left speechless.

blocks.x: Don't tell me...
Epilogue — Same, Yet Different
Only at the very end does it come out why Misha was hunted so relentlessly — her father was Chernobog's most famous scientist and an important political figure.
Both Lungmen and Reunion were after something perhaps left with Misha herself. Though there was no solid proof, Ch'en had wanted, at the very least, to keep her out of Reunion's hands.
But now Misha has fallen into the hands of the mysterious figure known as 'W.' The night Rhodes Island fought with everything it had ends up being the night they failed to protect her.

Among the Infected too, some offer truth and some offer lies. Rhodes Island reached out to a girl in the slums, and Reunion took her away. All that remains is the promise to teach how to make a doll — a promise now impossible to keep.

main_3.tagline: Rhodes Island sets out to rescue Misha, captured by Reunion — only to hear the very person they came to save say she doesn't want to be saved. Where, exactly, does the chain of hatred even begin to break?

chars.name: Franka

chars.name: Liskarm
chars.desc: A Blacksteel Defender. Franka's partner.

chars.name: Exusiai

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: A Vanguard of Penguin Logistics. A quiet dual-wielder.

chars.name: Skullshatterer
chars.desc: Reunion's leader. An Infected with nothing left to lose.

chars.name: Misha
chars.desc: The girl the Guard Department is after. This operation's mission target.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A mysterious woman who handles explosives. Sets up games for her own amusement.

terms.name: Lungmen Guard Department
terms.desc: Lungmen's security force. Merciless toward the Infected.

terms.name: Reunion
