terms.name: Penguin Logistics
terms.desc: Lungmen's logistics company. Also takes mercenary jobs on the side.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: An Ursus city. Reunion reduced it to ruins.

blocks.x: **Reunion**, the armed Infected organization that brought down Chernobog, sets its sights on Lungmen next. Rhodes Island joins hands with the Lungmen Guard Department to stop them.
But mid-operation, **Misha**, the girl the Guard Department was protecting, is seized and dragged off by a Reunion ambush — into the hands of a woman in red.
This is the story of the chase to get Misha back. But at the end of that chase, what Rhodes Island finds is the back of the very person they fought so hard to save.
1. The Girl Who Must Be Reclaimed
Captain **Ch'en** initially draws a line, saying she has no obligation to tell Amiya Misha's secret. But faced with the argument that information is needed to fight the Infected, she finally speaks.
Misha's father was an important figure in Chernobog, so there may be information locked in her mind that Reunion covets. Just how much Misha herself knows, no one but Misha can say.
With Reunion's next target being Lungmen, they must be stopped from exploiting Chernobog in any way. Losing Misha to Reunion was Ch'en's own failure too. There's no time to spare.
A joint operation begins: the Guard Department handles the assault, Rhodes Island the reconnaissance and strike. Short on scouts, Rhodes Island decides to borrow outside help.
The name Amiya calls is **Penguin Logistics**. Sniper **Exusiai** and Vanguard **Texas** step out on another job together.
The captured Reunion suspects won't talk even under interrogation, seeming like simple puppets who know nothing. Ch'en orders that a confession be extracted by any means necessary.

blocks.cap: A Reunion suspect who won't answer how to use a crossbow, or why they came to Lungmen. "Maybe... they really don't know anything."

blocks.x: **Hoshiguma**, an elite of the Special Investigations Team who joins them, confesses she struggled to handle that single enemy who abducted Misha, even with her whole team thrown at it — the enemy had immense strength, yet seemed as if her mind was somewhere else entirely. Introduced to Amiya as Rhodes Island's leader, she hears the operational plan and acknowledges Amiya's skill.
2. The Man Who Threw Away His Name

The one who appears before the captured Misha isn't the woman in red, but Reunion's leader, **Skullshatterer**. When Misha calls him by his old name, he cuts her off flatly — the person who used that name is already dead.

Skullshatterer tells Misha of his past. In the era when Ursus treated the Infected worse than livestock, military police dragged him from his home, and his mother, beaten mercilessly, never once let go of her son's hand.
All that was left on the snow was a trail of blood. If only they'd been a little faster — if Reunion had been just a little faster — this suffering wouldn't exist, he grinds through his teeth.
Misha still says she hates seeing fellow Infected hurt each other. Skullshatterer answers — when Chernobog let the Infected quarantined in the frozen mines die, who stood up for them?
Who exactly among us is innocent?

To him, Reunion is the first hope the Infected have ever gotten their hands on — because Talulah leads them against every cruelty. Rhodes Island, meanwhile, is nothing but a traitor: an Infected organization that sides with Lungmen to kill its own kind.
Misha slowly opens her heart to his pain, too — thinking he must have suffered so long, that everyone carries painful memories. If Reunion really can protect everyone, she begins to waver.
And yet he's gentle with Misha alone. Just as Reunion protects everyone, he will protect her without fail — never letting her get hurt again.
3. Warm-Up

Meanwhile, Exusiai and Texas make quick work of Reunion's sentries. No personal grudge, but a contract is a contract, they say — a logistics company's basic job is to fulfill its contracts faithfully.

Twenty Reunion members fall in an instant before Exusiai's explosive rounds and Texas's dual blades. That combination is still something else. This commotion signals Rhodes Island's main force to launch its raid.
Meanwhile, in the abandoned mine, Skullshatterer teaches Misha how to fire a gun — pulling the trigger, gripping Originium, focusing her mind to detonate the ammunition with Arts.

Misha's palm grows steadily warmer. Skullshatterer is delighted — he says he senses something similar between them, that she must surely be his... but before he can finish, word arrives of Rhodes Island's raid.
Misha pleads with him — Rhodes Island would help the Infected, let's at least talk to them. It falls on deaf ears with Skullshatterer. Talk with the ones who've killed countless of his people?
Do the Infected really have no other path than killing each other, and dying by each other's hands?
He leaves for the battlefield with a promise to return without fail. Let's go home together, he says.
4. The Hooded Target

The enemy proves stronger than expected, so Skullshatterer calls for reinforcements — the mysterious woman who handles explosives, **W**. She says merely protecting Misha isn't enough, and lays out one more game.
The target W points to is the command post directing all of Rhodes Island's battles — the hooded figure sticking close to Amiya's side. None other than the **Doctor**.
Kill just that one, and pop! Rhodes Island's whole head comes right off.
While W's Casters provide cover and her subordinates draw attention as decoys, Skullshatterer himself decides to go after the Doctor, leaving behind a request to take care of Misha.

Sand blows across the wasteland, blocking visibility. Franka and Liskarm circle around to scout the high ground, but despite it being perfect ambush terrain, they can't find a single footprint or scent. Something's off from top to bottom, Franka clicks her tongue.

Franka's blade instead cuts down Reunion fighters lying in wait beneath the ruins to ambush them. Disappointed to find nothing but incompetent cowards, Franka is scolded by Liskarm not to let the enemy slip away. While they bicker, Reunion pulls back and starts forming ranks.

Then they put forward one massive fighter. Liskarm flinches, wondering if it could be a Sarkaz, but with the joint operation underway with the Guard Department, Amiya stops him — sensitive matters can wait for later.
5. A Blade From Below
That massive enemy was bait. After dealing with one squad, all those Infected vanish without a trace — no footprints, no sound, no scent. Something is badly wrong.

The strangeness was underfoot the whole time. The enemy had been targeting the Doctor from underground. Exusiai shouts a warning, and Hoshiguma shields the Doctor, blocking Skullshatterer's surprise attack with her shield, 'Hannya.'
But Skullshatterer says he doesn't need to break through the shield. His weapon's temperature spikes rapidly — he intended to self-destruct and take the Doctor with him.

blocks.cap: "I'll finish you off!" — A self-destruct no shield can stop. Death closes in on the Doctor.

blocks.x: At that instant, Amiya steps in front — she can't let the Doctor be hurt. And a power no one has ever seen erupts from her fingertips.

blocks.cap: The urge to protect the Doctor awakens something within Amiya. Skullshatterer's strike is turned right back at him.

blocks.x: Skullshatterer falls to his knees. He curses that someone with this much power turned a blind eye to his people's suffering, and that people like her will never be forgiven — then collapses.
Watching from a distance, Misha lets out a short, quiet scream.
6. I Didn't Want You to See This
The dust clears, and the Guard Department pushes for the next operation. But Amiya can't compose herself before the Doctor — she just took a life with that power.
What upsets her isn't hurting someone in itself, but that with the Doctor so close by, she showed him herself taking another's life.
If it's power I use for the Doctor's sake, I won't regret it — even if it means taking the life of someone with their own story.

Still, what pains her is never finding a better way. She always says she wants to save the Infected from suffering, yet when it truly matters, she talks herself into a choice that takes their lives instead.
She's truly weak, Amiya confesses. But no matter what happens, no matter how much time passes, she promises to protect the Doctor.
7. I Hear Them, These Voices
At Skullshatterer's death, Reunion surges back to reclaim him. The battlefield turns to chaos again, and amid it W appears, pinning Hoshiguma down with long-range bombardment — purely for fun.
Ch'en's reinforcements surround W, but she stays perfectly at ease even when told to drop her weapon. She's not here for Ch'en today, she says, and hands Amiya a phone — someone wants to talk to you.
W leaves a flashbang as a parting gift and slips away in an instant. Amiya answers cautiously, and Misha's voice comes through from the other end.

blocks.cap: Through the receiver come the cries of Reunion's Infected — pleas for blood transfusions, for the critically wounded, screams not to die.

blocks.x: Misha recalls a memory she'd forgotten — no, not forgotten, just something she didn't want to remember. The day Reunion tore down her home and took her father away, her little brother cried out her name in front of her, and she turned away, hiding in her room.

The boy she met again had already given up on so much, yet loved his sister just the same. Misha, afraid of that, ran away again — from the innocent child who'd just started asking her for help with homework.
Who in this world was ever innocent to begin with?
Amiya holds on desperately — it wasn't her who hurt innocent citizens, and it's not too late to change now. But Misha's answer is already set.
8. Don't Come
Misha declares she's Infected now too, and will stand with the Infected. Returning to the uninfected exactly what they've done to the Infected is the punishment they deserve.

Yet Misha still questions herself — if it's all just mutual revenge, where does this tragedy even begin? If Ursus hadn't treated the Infected worse than livestock, no one would have ever hated Ursus in the first place.

Amiya tells her Skullshatterer is already dead, and that Rhodes Island will protect her without fail if she comes back. Misha answers only with a quiet line drawn between them — you're Infected too, so you should understand.
Amiya acknowledges it — everyone at Rhodes Island understands her pain, but becoming Infected doesn't change anything. She says she's coming right now.
Don't come.

Misha says she's just pathetic and weak. Still, she wants to take even the smallest first step forward. Reunion is Infected, and so is she, she repeats, apologizing again and again before the call cuts off. No matter how many times Amiya calls her name, there's no answer.
9. What Was Called a Miracle
Misha won't leave Skullshatterer's body. W pushes her to leave, mocking that she's only being kept around for her use — but Misha doesn't waver.

W states coldly that she has no strength left to carry Skullshatterer back to Chernobog. This person called Ch'en shows no mercy to anyone, she says, so let's go now together. Misha refuses, saying it gives her chills — don't think you'll get anything from me.

Misha repeats that she'll bring Skullshatterer back. This boy can protect everyone, she insists, he'll surely do it — everyone, just like her, are Infected who've lost their place, people who deserve protection.
And in the middle of the all-out battle that follows, the dead Skullshatterer rises again. Reunion cheers, calling it a miracle — their leader has returned.

blocks.cap: "Skullshatterer has returned!" — A miracle to the cheering Reunion, but Amiya, watching blankly, can't find her voice.

blocks.x: The returned Skullshatterer tells his comrades urging him to go back to Ursus together to think of themselves first, to hurry and leave. Then he stands alone against the enemy, saying he'll protect them. Amiya realizes this is no miracle at all.
Why...... why are you doing this...... don't......
10. The One Who Picks Up Masks
Facing Skullshatterer again, Amiya freezes. This time her hands won't move. All she can manage is: Doctor, what should I do?
Then Ch'en steps forward — enough, it's time to end all of this. She finishes Skullshatterer in Amiya's place, then quietly offers a word to the frozen girl.
Fate is unfair by nature. If you want to resent someone, resent me.

After the battle ends, Ch'en speaks to Amiya about the mask Skullshatterer left behind. Misha didn't do anything wrong, and this isn't punishment for a sin, she says. Misha simply chose that outcome herself, and no one has the right to stop her or blame her for it.

blocks.cap: "Power drives people mad, and desire corrupts them." Ch'en shares the rules of the world she knows with Amiya.

blocks.x: Amiya wavers. She always believed she had a duty to help everyone, so why did it end this way — she no longer knows if what Rhodes Island does is truly right.

Ch'en admits the Guard Department could never do what Rhodes Island does — recklessly taking in the Infected of their own will, trying to save them by means that aren't harsh. That's something only Rhodes Island tries to do.

Then she brings up the mask again. If Amiya, who isn't even one of their own, picks up their masks, someday a room will be filled with masks like this. Even so, if she's willing, she should take it — this is her own matter, no one else's.
If you're prepared to bear the weight of a leader's duty, then make your own choices, and take responsibility for their outcomes.
Epilogue — Fates Entangled
Amiya thanks Ch'en and takes Skullshatterer's mask with her, believing there's one more way to give the Infected hope besides eradicating Oripathy — breaking this very chain of suffering and hatred.

Ch'en leaves Amiya with one last piece of advice. If Reunion is willing to listen to her words, help them — but don't hesitate before those who'd devote the rest of their lives to madness. The Guard Department will clean up the scene, she says, and pass the news to that doctor.
Unable to save either Misha or Skullshatterer in the end, the operation draws to a close. Watching it all from a distance, W hums to herself — that people's fates always end up tangled together.

blocks.cap: "So even the finest player can get checkmated from a place they never expected." W looks forward to what's coming next.

blocks.x: The story of hearing, from the very girl she set out to save, that she didn't want to be saved. No one yet knows where to begin breaking the chain of hatred, but that day, for the first time, Amiya carries someone else's mask back to her own room — taking on the duty of a leader, and its weight, as her own.

main_4.tagline: A story where Amiya's helplessness — feeling like salvation is just out of reach, yet unable to save anyone — is tested in a frozen, abandoned city. A vow to never abandon a single comrade, and a Snow Demon that emerges from the cold.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island, crushed by guilt over those she couldn't save.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's supreme commander. Keeps a close watch on Amiya's condition and her ring.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: Team leader of the Lungmen Guard Department. Cold toward the Infected.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: An oni of the Guard Department. Ch'en's dependable partner.

chars.name: Meteorite
chars.desc: A Blacksteel sniper. Leader of the scouting team.

chars.name: Frostleaf
chars.desc: A scout who wields cold Arts. Quiet and swift.

chars.name: Jessica
chars.desc: A Blacksteel rookie. Timid but earnest.

chars.name: Mephisto
chars.desc: A boy commander of Reunion who commands the Infected.

chars.name: Frostnova
chars.desc: A Reunion frost caster who leads the 'Snow Demon' squad.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that rose up in Ursus.

terms.name: Lungmen Guard Department
terms.desc: The law enforcement agency of the city-state Lungmen.

terms.name: The Abandoned Nomadic City
terms.desc: A dead city that broke away from Chernobog. Destroyed by a Catastrophe.

terms.name: Snow Demon Squad
terms.desc: A frost unit that remains a legend in northwestern Ursus.

terms.name: Calamity Messenger
terms.desc: A special type of Infected who senses and interprets Catastrophes and Originium.

blocks.x: The Catastrophes that strike Terra leave Originium behind wherever they pass, and anyone who stays near that ore too long contracts Oripathy and becomes Infected. In the incident where the Infected organization Reunion burned Ursus's city of Chernobog, Rhodes Island barely managed to pull out and return.
But Amiya's face is shadowed since her return. The memory of watching people she could have saved vanish before her eyes, unable to do anything but watch, won't let her go.
And out in the wastes beyond Lungmen, a nomadic city that broke off from Chernobog is discovered. This frozen, abandoned city is the next stage.
1. Free Hugs
Kal'tsit examines Amiya's returned hand. There's no scratch or discoloration on the ring, which is a relief, but she warns Amiya to take care of herself, since no one knows what might happen next.
Back in her room, Amiya forces herself to act fine. She says she's used to this by now, that she shouldn't burden others with it. But eventually her true feelings slip out.
The people we could have saved... disappearing right before our eyes... Is all we can do just watch?
It feels like she could reach them if she just stretched her hand out a little further. Amiya says it's really too much for her right now, and asks to be left alone for a while.

Kal'tsit quietly asks the Doctor for a favor. She worries that Amiya's shell is too hard, and that one day it will shatter under too much pressure. If that happens, she asks the Doctor to help her even a little.

She adds one more request: if Amiya gets caught up in a fierce battle, check the state of her ring right after it ends, and report immediately if there's any change at all. She says she can't tell the Doctor the reason yet.
Meanwhile, in the abandoned city, the scouting team begins to move — Blacksteel's sniper **Meteorite**, the quiet frost-wielder **Frostleaf**, and the timid rookie **Jessica**.

Their mission is to search for survivors and investigate the true state of the city. But they've been ordered to contact Kal'tsit immediately and retreat if anything related to Reunion turns up. They were told it wouldn't be a dangerous mission — but that promise doesn't last long.
2. Walking in the Rain

Before the Penguin Logistics and Blacksteel members step away for a while, they all worry about the exhausted Amiya. Texas says calmly that life goes on no matter what happens, and tells her to take care of herself.
Left alone, Amiya heads for the slums on the outskirts of Lungmen, thinking she might be able to help there. There, she meets a child picking up a phonograph.

The child has gathered a pile of broken phonographs and records. Even though they've never heard them play, they say they'll fix them someday and let others listen. Then, without hesitation, the child reveals they're Infected.
Amiya laughs softly and says she is too. Infected or not, the fact that this is a good child doesn't change. She shares everything she has with the hungry child.
After the child leaves, Amiya stands alone and asks herself — if even a child like that doesn't know how things will end, what can she possibly do?
In the rain, an inner voice pushes her to the edge. It says all that awaits the Infected is hunger, persecution, and death; that she can do nothing, and will eventually be swallowed by rage.

Amiya pushes back. She insists Rhodes Island is becoming a source of strength for the Infected, that everyone is working toward the same goal. She must never be swallowed by despair, she tells herself, and holds on to the promise she made to that child.
Just then, she spots a familiar figure from behind — someone receiving a handmade doll from a little girl.

blocks.cap: "They say this doll only means something if you make it yourself. So, here — this is for you!" — In an alley, a handmade doll changes hands.

blocks.x: That person was none other than Ch'en. Amiya sees a look in her eyes she's never seen before.

blocks.cap: "This has nothing to do with you. I'll handle anything concerning Lungmen myself." — Ch'en's unfamiliar face.

blocks.x: Ch'en pushes Amiya away, saying this has nothing to do with her. Amiya keeps thinking to herself that she's never seen Captain Ch'en look like that before.
3. Artificial Cooling

The scouting team in the abandoned city runs into something strange. The weather hasn't changed, but the walls are absorbing heat, dropping the surrounding temperature sharply. Frostleaf recalls a legend he once heard back at the barracks.
During their third skirmish with Reunion, they find black Originium embedded in a corner of a wall — the ore from which an ice pattern spreads visibly, the source of the cold.

Frostleaf continues the legend. In northwestern Ursus, there was once an Infected guerrilla unit that opposed the government, and among them were monsters like iron towers and a squad that was like the cold itself. Wherever they passed, he says, nothing was left but frozen corpses and lifeless clearings.
Right — I'm saying those guys might be the ones from that legend... The legendary 'Snow Demon Squad.'

Meanwhile in Lungmen, Amiya reports the scouting team's findings to Ch'en. Hearing that Reunion is planning an attack on Lungmen and has already secured an infiltration route, Ch'en scoffs, saying they must think little of the Guard Department.
Amiya suggests that the Guard Department and Rhodes Island run a joint operation to wipe out Reunion inside Lungmen first. But Ch'en's answer is firm.
You're Infected. If you go on another mission in the slums, it will only cause more chaos.
4. Don't Be Afraid
At that moment, an urgent transmission comes in from Jessica. She's spotted a Reunion leader inside the abandoned city — no, two of them.

Somewhere in the ruins, two Reunion commanders sit facing each other — the boy **Mephisto**, who lost his limbs to a bombing, and **W**, who's needling him. They cackle that their target has taken the bait, and since a big fish has been hooked, today they'll play nice.
The hidden scouting team is soon discovered. Mephisto releases his subordinates, saying it's impossible to hide from him. He tells them to eat the appetizer before the main dish arrives.

The scouting team barely manages to take down their enemies, but the defeated Reunion soldiers rise again. Mephisto's staff glows, reviving the dead and drawing in even more reinforcements — and he says he hasn't even lifted a finger yet.
Just then, Frostleaf senses cold approaching, and says the truly terrifying ones have arrived. He has Meteorite fire at the western building to draw attention, then retreats with Jessica.
While fleeing, Frostleaf sends a desperate transmission to Amiya.
Listen carefully, Amiya. ...You must not come here. This abandoned city is...
5. Bureaucracy

The transmission cuts off there. One by one, the other scouting teams report retreating and then go silent. In the control room, Kal'tsit sharply asks Ch'en just how long Lungmen intends to keep up this pointless stubbornness.
Amiya formally requests that the Guard Department cooperate with Rhodes Island's rescue mission. If they refuse, she says, Rhodes Island will move on its own.

But Ch'en's answer is unexpected. Of course the Guard Department intends to go too, she says — only their goal isn't rescue, but thoroughly eliminating Reunion. She makes clear they'll lend what strength they can, within limits.
My apologies. I have my own duty to carry out.

Kal'tsit sends a rescue team to support the other scouting teams, but the location where Meteorite's group is stranded is too deep to reach. In the end, that most dangerous place can only be left to Amiya.
6. Ignorance Breeds Wonder

Entering the abandoned city with Ch'en and Hoshiguma, Amiya shows her skill as a Calamity Messenger. Using observation data, she determines that this city moved this far before a Catastrophe destroyed it.
But in front of an area with a heavy concentration of Originium, Ch'en suddenly freezes. She sees something, and without a word, dashes off alone.

The shadow looked like it might have been Talulah. Neither Hoshiguma nor Amiya can be certain, but one thing is clear — they have to chase after Ch'en. At that moment, Reunion troops and exploding Originium creatures block their path.

Even after the fight ends, there's no sign of Ch'en, not even a shadow. Hoshiguma tries to push in herself, but Amiya stops her. Beyond this point, Originium has taken root, and anyone healthy who goes in too deep risks contracting Oripathy.

Hoshiguma laughs, saying it's cute how hard tiny Amiya is trying to persuade her, and sets the Guard Department up as Rhodes Island's shield. She'll hold the entrance, she says, and leave the Infected problem to the Infected experts.
Go on ahead — I'll leave Ch'en to you. Just make sure you bring her back safe.

Around that time at Rhodes Island, Kal'tsit examines the rescued scouting team. Swollen skin, dropped body temperature, and necrotic tissue — all of it frostbite. Whatever this cold really is, it does this to people.
7. Mutual Benefit
Deep in the abandoned city, Amiya meets Ch'en again. Having taken down countless Reunion soldiers alone, Ch'en apologizes for the first time, saying she let her emotions get the better of her.

Asked whether the shadow she saw earlier was Talulah, Ch'en holds back, saying it was probably a mistake. The mission comes first right now, she says — if she doesn't lead, the Guard Department can't properly conduct the investigation either.
Amiya reaffirms Rhodes Island's policy. Protecting Lungmen is part of the mission, she says, but the most important reason they came here is to rescue their comrades.
Rhodes Island never abandons a single comrade.

Ch'en only says she'll lead her unit and join up later, then leaves alone, saying she doesn't need an escort. Amiya realizes once again that Rhodes Island must fight this alone — but there's no time to dwell on it, so she moves forward.
8. Stress Response
Following the smell of burning, Amiya reunites with the wounded Frostleaf. He tells her that Meteorite and Jessica are still in the plaza, and that the longer this drags on, the worse it gets.
They find Meteorite's group, but something's wrong with Jessica. She's frozen, gripped by trauma from something she saw.
Amiya makes up her mind. She asks Frostleaf to have Jessica grip her wrist tightly, then uses the ability she's kept hidden — taking on and sharing what Jessica saw.
An overwhelming sorrow washes over her. When Jessica comes to and sobs an apology, Amiya quietly stays by her side.
We're here now. You're not alone. We'll always be by your side.

Amiya understands all too well that feeling of wanting to run but being unable to move your feet, wanting to scream but having no voice come out — she's been through the same thing herself. Jessica finally manages to stand.
When Frostleaf asks what that power just now was, Amiya only asks him to keep it a secret from Dr. Kal'tsit.
9. Cutting to the Bone
Mephisto appears again to mock the scouting team, saying he has something to show them. He casually describes what he did to the residents who fled the city.

As revenge for Ursus's persecution, he says, he turned the captured residents into a single monument — one meant to show that the Infected's fate ends only in miserable death. Then he orders the tower set alight.

blocks.cap: A bonfire born of sin and vicious madness. Amiya finally faces the truth of the light illuminating the city.

blocks.x: Mephisto reveals his reasoning. If a tenth of them writhe in the agony of death, he says, the remaining nine-tenths will tremble in fear — and that's the best way to minimize damage.
The Doctor tries to cover Amiya's eyes, but she asks him to lower his hand. Their wrongdoing, she says, is something she has to face head-on eventually.
Amiya tells herself the frightened, weak person she once was is gone now — she's seen countless horrific tragedies. Even so, she vows she must not look away, that she can't back down yet.
10. A Candle in the Wind
Frostleaf cuts through a shield with a blade of cold and charges at Mephisto, saying anyone who mocks life like that doesn't deserve to keep his own. But at that instant, his legs freeze solid.
Mephisto introduces the true star of the show — the princess of the Snow Demons, **Frostnova**.

blocks.cap: "First, I think I'll throw a beast like you, who's not even fit to be called a murderer, out onto the snowfield and freeze you to death." — The princess of the Snow Demons descends.

blocks.x: Before Frostnova, who drains the heat from the entire area, even the cold-wielding Frostleaf freezes up, saying he's never seen anyone with a lower body temperature than himself. He shouts for Amiya to take everyone and run.

Amiya refuses. She won't abandon Frostleaf and run, she says — she won't abandon a single comrade. She takes on the job of holding back the enemy's movements herself, and leaves Frostleaf's rescue to Meteorite.

As Frostnova chants a spell like a lullaby, black crystals fall to the ground, freezing everything they touch. Amiya catches the moment the cold wavers and realizes Frostnova is using Originium buried by her soldiers to generate the cold.

Once the buried Originium is destroyed, fog rises, and Meteorite uses the opening to rush to Frostleaf, shouting at him not to play the hero — did he really think she was stupid enough to leave him behind?

Frostleaf stays calm, asking if saving several lives with one child's life isn't more worthwhile. Meteorite warns him she won't let it slide if he pulls a stunt like that again, then hauls him up onto her back and retreats.
Amiya orders the snipers to destroy Reunion's monument entirely. Using the fog thickened by the explosion as cover, Rhodes Island barely manages to break away from the plaza.
As Amiya leaves, Frostnova murmurs that it's a good look in her eyes — definitely the eyes of a warrior. Then she offers a brief apology. Perhaps even this cold-blooded princess has her own reasons.

In the plaza after Rhodes Island withdraws, Frostnova is left alone, coughing. Someone beside her warns that her own body must be starting to fail as well, but she says she doesn't need to be told, and tries to pursue Rhodes Island anyway — if she deals with them all, she says, she can head to Lungmen.
Epilogue — Lungmen Is in Danger
As she retreats, Amiya receives an urgent transmission from Ch'en. A voice, barely audible through the static, delivers the worst possible news.

blocks.cap: "I'm unharmed. But..." — Through the static-filled transmission, Ch'en's voice wavers.

blocks.x: Reunion has seized the Guard Department's key strongholds, and is forcing open a path from the slums to the outskirts of Lungmen — all while Amiya was pinned down in the frozen, abandoned city.
Amiya... Lungmen... is in danger.

blocks.cap: While Rhodes Island was held captive in the abandoned city, the real target was Lungmen all along.

blocks.x: It was all a trap. While W drew the attention of Lungmen and Rhodes Island and lured them into the abandoned city, Faust and Crownslayer had already brought down Lungmen's key points. Mephisto laughs, saying he'll paint every road leading to Lungmen from here with their enemies' blood.

blocks.cap: And then, the one who drew this entire picture finally speaks.

blocks.x: Beside him, the silent leader of Reunion finally makes her declaration — that she will burn this rotten city to ash.

Amiya, who once felt she could almost reach out and touch salvation yet save no one, kept her vow in this frozen city not to abandon a single comrade. But as the price for that, while their eyes were held fast, the true Catastrophe was already turning toward Lungmen. And a name that had stayed silent for so long had just begun to move.

main_5.tagline: A story of Lungmen's cold-blooded blade, who abandons a comrade and even cuts through her own old home to drive Reunion wholesale into a trap. Yet the one who drew up that perfect plan wasn't the one holding the sword.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: Special Inspection Team Leader of the Lungmen Guard Department. An upright dragon.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: Ch'en's partner. A horned oni police inspector.

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: A Guard Department commissioner from a wealthy family. Ch'en's rival.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island Operator who wields fire.

chars.name: Mephisto
chars.desc: A boy commander of Reunion who commands the Infected.

chars.name: Faust
chars.desc: A quiet sniper at Mephisto's side.

chars.name: Wei Yenwu
chars.desc: Lungmen's Director. The one who lays out the plan.

terms.name: Lungmen
terms.desc: A city-state in Higashi. Ch'en's homeland.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: The armed Infected organization that swallowed Chernobog.

terms.name: Dragonslayer Sword
terms.desc: The sword Director Wei lent to Ch'en. Must not be drawn carelessly.

terms.name: Special Inspection Team
terms.desc: An elite Guard Department unit led by Ch'en.

blocks.x: Having swallowed Chernobog, Reunion sets its next sights on Lungmen, the city-state of Higashi. Bursting out from every corner at once, they seize control of the city in an instant, even snatching away the Guard Department building — once a symbol of law and order — in its entirety.
The protagonist of this story is the blade that guards Lungmen — Special Inspection Team Leader **Ch'en** of the Guard Department. A cold, upright dragon.
But this time, Ch'en keeps swallowing something down — Director Wei's classified orders, the comrade she left behind, and a very old version of herself.
1. A Reunion That Begins with a Bucket of Water

Ch'en wakes up drenched in water. She'd been talking in her sleep, caught in a childhood nightmare. The one holding the cup is **Swire**, a young lady from a wealthy family — and this is a cabin aboard Rhodes Island.

Forty-five minutes earlier, Ch'en had been caught in an explosion, knocked unconscious, and led her team fleeing to this place. If not for Swire and Rhodes Island, the entire team would have fallen to Reunion.

blocks.cap: "Still talking in your sleep? And looking like a mess, too." Swire's cup of water pulls Ch'en up from her nightmare and back into reality.

blocks.x: Swire is itching to tear into Ch'en. The Guard Department has already fallen into Reunion's hands, and she pushes the blame entirely onto Ch'en for being away — demanding she step down at once and hand over the Special Inspection Team.
Ch'en doesn't back down either, snapping back that Swire doesn't have what it takes to lead the Special Inspection Team. Their argument turns ugly enough that Lungmen curses start flying.

But there's a wound on Swire's back that Ch'en has never seen before. While carrying the unconscious Ch'en into a bunker, a caster's Arts detonated just three meters away, blowing up the wall behind her entirely. For rivals, she risked her life to save Ch'en. Hoshiguma scolds Ch'en, telling her to be a little kinder sometimes — there's no need for the two of them to be at each other's throats every single day.
2. A Team Leader Playing a Role
Ch'en's longtime partner is the horned oni inspector **Hoshiguma**. After Swire leaves, Hoshiguma senses that something's been off with Ch'en lately.
To Ch'en, who coldly lays out Lungmen's losses in front of the Rhodes Island Operators, Hoshiguma quietly says one thing: that Ch'en isn't a politician.
You're a team leader and a commissioner, not a politician. This kind of act doesn't suit you.

Ch'en only says it's all for Lungmen's sake. The operation followed orders, she says, and the contents of those orders are classified. Hoshiguma quickly realizes that the only person who could give the Guard Department such orders is Director Wei.

Above all, Ch'en left Amiya, the Doctor, and the others behind in that abandoned city. Hearing the circumstances, a Rhodes Island Operator grows furious, asking if the Guard Department always abandons comrades like that.

Ch'en says it's entirely her own responsibility — that protecting Lungmen was the top priority, and she judged she couldn't take on that risk. Still, Hoshiguma notices a corner of her heart that remains uneasy.
3. A Severed Transmission
Just then, a transmission comes in over a classified channel — news that Ch'en's informant, XR02, has been surrounded by Reunion.

blocks.cap: The final transmission from the Mukwo warehouse. "Once these guys have all left, please come back here... I'll leave the information on my body..."

blocks.x: Already hit by several shots and unable to hold out any longer, the informant says he'll leave the information on his own body. Ch'en shouts at him to survive, but the transmission cuts off just like that.
XR02! Don't you dare die! That's an order! Survive!
Ch'en formally requests help from Rhodes Island. Punching through a breach, they head straight for the Mukwo warehouse.
4. Can a Thug with Oripathy Be a Good Person Too?

Hoshiguma already knew the informant's true identity — **Apot**, a friend of ten years. He used to be a bar owner polishing glasses, but Ch'en had secretly groomed him into an informant. She should have at least told me, Hoshiguma quietly resents.

Hoshiguma volunteers herself as bait. She charges alone into the ambush site and wipes out every last Reunion soldier in just two minutes and thirty seconds — enough to make the Guard Department troops shudder and say she's no different from a demon.

Deep inside the warehouse, Apot waits for Ch'en, blinded and pierced through by an arrow. He hands over the coordinates of the intel he obtained at the cost of his life, worrying to the very end only about the oni sister he calls Big Sis.
He asks her to stay by his side, so the rich don't get to look down on her for where she came from. Then he asks Ch'en — can someone like him be called a good person, even a thug with Oripathy?
Ch'en tells him to ask Lungmen itself. Then she answers on Lungmen's behalf.
"It doesn't care." Apot, you've always been a person of Lungmen, and you always will be, forever.

Only then does Ch'en call over Hoshiguma, whom she'd brought along and kept standing behind them all this time. Held in her arms, Apot asks that his remains be buried with the others, even in their old, now-derelict hideout, as long as he's with them.

blocks.cap: "Big Sis is holding me up... All my effort until now was worth it..." In the arms of the oni he once feared, the thug's life quietly comes to a close.

blocks.x: Big Sis... We were always afraid of you, but honestly... we were always rooting for you...
5. The Empty House

The coordinates Apot left point to a Reunion stronghold in a wealthy residential district. But it looks like they aren't trying to build a solid base — they seem to be secretly searching for a 'high-value target.'

The mansion Reunion was trying to break into is, of all places, the home of Ch'en's birth father's family — a place once called the 'Ch'en household,' where she lived until she left to study abroad in Victoria.

blocks.cap: There's no key. "You saw Reunion punch a hole in the wall earlier, right?" Without a second thought, Ch'en cuts down the door of her own old home.

blocks.x: Inside the house, the rules of her childhood rise up like walls — voices telling her to put her toys away neatly, never to leave the house, never to look an adult in the eye without permission.

Ch'en's room is still kept spotlessly clean. Was it left this way for her, or simply left untouched because she was seen as an ill omen? Ch'en laughs bitterly. And from beyond the wall, old words steeped in someone's hatred come drifting out.
You will never earn my love. Not even a scrap of it.
Ch'en finds an old photograph. Then it suddenly dawns on her — could this be what they were looking for?

blocks.cap: An old photo she thought would have been thrown away by now. Ch'en quietly speaks to someone in the photo.

blocks.x: Ch'en quietly speaks to someone in the photo, promising she will surely help them. But why now, of all times — why here, of all places? Of course, the photo doesn't answer.
6. Decoy Operation
Along the road to the Guard Department stands Daigu Plaza. A Reunion caster there senses something is off — Lungmen is completely empty, and every expensive item has nothing left but its price tag.
It's as if someone cleared everything out beforehand. What's more, Reunion units in other districts are disappearing one by one, without a trace, their microphones still switched on.

Before that unease can fully take root, Ch'en's Guard Department rappels down from the rooftop and crashes through the windows. Ch'en orders them to blow up Reunion, cover and all — even knowing full well that Daigu Plaza belongs to Swire's family.

Swire blows up over the comm, and a piano worth 1.8 million Lungmen dollars gets used as cover and smashed to pieces. Ch'en's real goal lies elsewhere — she waits for the enemy to finish their report, letting word spread across all of Lungmen that 'the Guard Department is here.'
If you've finished your report, then your job here is done.
7. Life and Death Together
Then explosives hidden on the third floor detonate right beneath Ch'en's feet. Hoshiguma shields her with her shield, and the two of them tumble down the stairs together.
With one arm broken, Hoshiguma bears the weight of two floors' worth of debris with just her shield. Then she pushes Ch'en up, saving her life once again.
But this time, Ch'en stubbornly refuses to leave Hoshiguma behind. The old Ch'en would have weighed the costs and put the team first — but not now.

blocks.cap: The dragon who always stood behind the shield now stands in front of her wounded partner instead. "Just let me be your shield sometimes."

blocks.x: For the first time, Ch'en lays her true feelings bare. She says Hoshiguma is no longer just Inspector Hoshiguma to her — she's Hoshiguma, her friend and partner.
Your shield has always held me up until now. Let me be your shield sometimes too. Hoshiguma.

Hoshiguma laughs, saying Ch'en can deliver a line like that without even blushing. Then she gives Ch'en, who's heading for the Guard Department, just one warning — never get lost in some thought and act on stubbornness alone. Don't fight for anything other than who you are right now.

In place of the injured Hoshiguma, who drops out, Swire calls in over comms with some silly joke. She lightens the tense mood with talk of a sandwich from Lang Yam Restaurant, then leaves one last line — that life matters most, so don't go risking it over whatever's in front of you.
8. To Reclaim the Guard Department
In front of the Guard Department building, Ch'en stands before her officers. We are neither rioters nor criminals, she tells them — remember your obedience, your duty, your resolve.

blocks.cap: Facing the building that once symbolized safety and law, the Guard Department forms ranks. "Lungmen must never again have to worry because of us."

blocks.x: As the Lungmen Guard Department, let's take back what's ours. That alone is enough.

But the inside of the building is strangely quiet. There's not a single enemy on the lower floors, and while Reunion numbers grow the higher they go, there's no sign of an attack — as if they're all taking a nap. The command post sits on the rooftop, an obvious trap.
9. Devils
Waiting on the rooftop are Reunion's boy commander **Mephisto** and his quiet sniper **Faust** — a man who commands the Infected with the precision of chess pieces.
When Mephisto uses his Arts, Originium grows through the bodies of fallen Reunion soldiers, piercing through them. Their wounds heal and they feel no pain — remade into 'undying bodyguards.'

But Ch'en was prepared too. Four of the twenty-one Infected in the Guard Department are spies she planted, and one of them leaked Mephisto's entire plan to her. Even Mephisto's belief that he'd rescued Faust was part of Ch'en's setup from the start.
Mephisto tries to win Ch'en over, telling her she too will eventually be used up and discarded by Lungmen.
Do you know that you'll end up being used like a chess piece by those people, and then thrown away?
10. The Sword That Won't Draw

Ch'en carries a sword lent to her by Director Wei — the **Dragonslayer Sword**, codename Crimsonnight. An extraordinary weapon, she'd been told never to let it exude killing intent except when it was truly worth drawing.
Ch'en tries to draw it, but Crimsonnight refuses to leave its sheath. Caught in Faust's crossfire, Ch'en can only draw the blade halfway, barely deflecting arrows to hold her ground.

blocks.cap: "This sword isn't meant to be used on the likes of you." Even so, Ch'en grips the hilt, vowing that he will pay a price no matter what.

blocks.x: Mephisto mocks Ch'en as a mere game piece, but Ch'en cuts him off flatly — she says she's nobody's pawn.
This kind of game board doesn't suit me. Kid, I'm not anyone's game piece — I'm Lungmen's blade.

But Mephisto knows Ch'en's weakness precisely. He says the 'Leader' told him, seeing right through the fact that Ch'en can't draw her sword. In the end, Ch'en is thrown back by a shockwave, her ribs broken.

Mephisto's Originium Arts only work on the Infected. Yet seeing the dust clinging to Ch'en, he mutters something meaningful, as if he's noticed something. What exactly Ch'en is — the story never gives a clear answer.
11. Reinforcements from the Sky
Just as Ch'en braces herself even to die there, Rhodes Island's aircraft, the 'Bad Guys,' appears in the sky.

blocks.cap: "There's no need to go that far, Captain Ch'en!" Rhodes Island, once left behind, now comes cutting back through the sky.

blocks.x: Fire-wielding Operator **Blaze** drops in, and **Amiya** uses her Arts to restore the erased emotions of Mephisto's bodyguards. The emotionless Infected suddenly begin to weep, silently, all at once.
Amiya declares to Mephisto that she won't back down. But she says he isn't her enemy — the only thing she means to destroy is his grudge and his cruelty.
Mephisto, the one standing alone on this battlefield right now — that's you!

Ch'en apologizes to Amiya. When Amiya plays dumb, saying the battlefield was too loud to hear anything, Ch'en once again swallows a sincere apology. This time, she decides to hand command over to the Doctor, saying she'll trust him, just once.
12. Be Dyed Crimson

Blaze catches Faust's invisible crossbowmen, the 'Phantom Crossbowmen.' With a technique that sprays her own blood and sets it alight, she burns away their air-refraction camouflage entirely, exposing them.

With the Doctor's command and Rhodes Island's covering fire, an opening finally appears. Ch'en doesn't miss the moment — she draws the Dragonslayer Sword and cuts down over a dozen bodyguards in a single stroke.
Be dyed crimson...

blocks.cap: The rooftop collapses under a single blow. Amid the crumbling ruins, Mephisto and Faust barely manage to pull themselves free.

blocks.x: Mephisto and Faust retreat under cover of the chain of explosions. To the very end, Mephisto can't understand it — why haven't the twenty squads and two battalions of Reunion he mustered shown up yet?

Betrayal? A leak? Mephisto tries to reassure himself that Big Sister Talulah couldn't possibly have orchestrated something like this. That name is the first hint, slipping out, of this night's true weight.
13. Targeted Treatment
Over the comm, the voices of **Wei Yenwu** and Kal'tsit overlap. And it's revealed that all of this was Wei Yenwu's plan from the very start.
Leaving Amiya in the abandoned city, deliberately isolating the Guard Department — all of it was bait to make Reunion let its guard down and draw out its full forces.

Herd the scattered Reunion forces into one place with a citywide blockade across Lungmen, then wipe them out entirely with elites lying in ambush. Cutting out only the diseased tissue from the wound that is the city — that's precisely 'targeted treatment.' This was the true identity of the classified order Ch'en had been swallowing all along.

Rhodes Island knows they were used. But evidence is lacking for now, and it's also true that Swire helped hold off the pursuers together with them, so Amiya swallows her anger. Wei says calmly that as long as the result is satisfactory, that's all that matters.
Lungmen's problems must be something Lungmen itself can resolve entirely on its own. That is what matters most of all.

Then he adds, speaking to the Doctor, that though they may not be friends, at least they aren't enemies yet. Their cooperation remains valid until Reunion is completely wiped out, he says — and so Rhodes Island and Lungmen's precarious partnership continues, teetering along.
Epilogue — A Night That Was Only the Beginning
In the Director's office, Wei Yenwu sits across from his longtime companion Fumizuki. Even though it's a night of victory, Wei's expression is dark.

This was clearly an all-out assault by Reunion — so why didn't their leader, Talulah, show up in Lungmen at all? A suspicious air hangs over the entire Ursus Empire. A feeling that all of this is merely the beginning of something.

Fumizuki brings up an old name. That battle twenty years ago that stopped Ursus's conquest — if they had lost then, the person sitting in Wei's seat right now would have been someone else. Duke Kashchey.

blocks.cap: The long night ends, and dawn breaks. But this victory is only the first piece of a far greater picture.

blocks.x: Fumizuki says she somehow senses that person's presence in Talulah. Wei's answer quietly lays down this night's final truth.
But he's already dead. Killed by Talulah's own hand.

Lungmen's cold-blooded blade abandoned a comrade and even cut through her own old home to drive Reunion into a perfect trap. But the one who drew up that perfect plan wasn't the one holding the sword — and the orders, the guilt, and the old wounds she had to swallow still remain inside Ch'en. And as Wei Yenwu sensed, this long night had only just begun.

main_6.tagline: In Lungmen after Reunion is driven out, the very people who claimed to protect the city end up cutting into its own flesh. What the Infected with nowhere left to go held onto until the end wasn't ideology or revenge, but the right to choose for themselves.

terms.name: Lungmen

terms.name: Guard Department

blocks.x: Reunion's invasion of Lungmen is nearly over. Rhodes Island has joined forces with Lungmen's Guard Department for one final joint operation, to sweep out the last Reunion remnants left in the city.
On the surface, it's a mop-up operation whose outcome is already decided. And yet, this story is titled 'Necrosis'.
The old saying that rotten flesh must be cut away to save the rest — it was aimed at Reunion and Lungmen alike.
1. How About a Sauna? (Instructor: The Blazing Cat)
Before Reunion, cornered by the blockade, an Operator drops straight out of the sky. A cat who wields heat, the Elite Operator **Blaze**.

Blaze introduces herself on the spot and suggests they quietly lay down their weapons. Since they're all Infected, she says, she's giving them a chance. And if they resist, she'll warm up the air around them first — all with a smile.

blocks.cap: "Whose side's air is hotter, mine or yours?" — Blaze's invitation to the sauna is one you can't refuse.

blocks.x: This chaotic cat has been put in charge of the Doctor — carrying them around bodily across a battlefield with no comms.

Amiya quietly asks the Doctor for a favor. Not to protect Blaze by force, but to look after her heart, since she has to keep fighting fellow Infected. This is where the Doctor's long journey to earn an Operator's trust begins.
2. Ch'en of the Guard Department, and a Secret Order

The other flank of the encirclement is handled by the Guard Department. Special Inspection Team Captain **Ch'en** and the merchant house's young lady **Swire**, bickering all the while, drive Reunion's main force into a fixed position.

On the surface, it's a smooth joint operation. But behind it, Chief Administrator **Wei Yenwu** quietly gives someone an order — don't contact him for three weeks, and hand over all information to 'Guillory's daughter'.
And a voice from some night long ago overlaps with it. A man who called Wei his 'sworn brother' and asked him to look after his woman and daughter. He said he'd already picked the daughter's name.
……Talulah.
"Don't take it too much to heart. I know it all already." The man says that and vanishes. Beneath Lungmen's clean-looking victory, something was already festering.
3. The Commander Who Turns His Own Men into Monsters

Cornered, Reunion's commander **Mephisto** resorts to the worst move to survive. He uses his own Arts to force his comrades' infections into runaway overdrive, turning them into monsters that can't even feel pain.
Those monsters don't distinguish friend from foe. They tear into people who were their own squadmates moments ago, devour them, and multiply, creating more monsters.

blocks.cap: "All of Lungmen will become your soil." — Mephisto plants catastrophe in the city, using his own kin as seeds.

blocks.x: The sniper at his side, **Faust**, freezes in front of the sight. She cries out that these aren't monsters, they're living people.
And Faust silently turns around, like someone who already knows which side she has to protect.
4. Two Snipers, Back to Back

Faust, separated from her unit, is caught by **GreyThroat**, Rhodes Island's non-Infected sniper. Arts that erase sight, and a piercing arrow — both are lone snipers, so if they pull the trigger at the same time, they both die.

blocks.cap: "If we pull the trigger at the same moment, we both die." — In the fog, two snipers aim their barrels at each other's backs.

blocks.x: GreyThroat lowers her weapon, saying she saw Faust carry off a wounded Infected, saw her not shoot a straggling Guard Department officer. You're doing the right thing, she says.

Faust answers coldly. A person who only follows orders and leans on someone else's judgment ends up becoming something terrifying. And yet, since neither of them fired, she lets GreyThroat live this time.

For GreyThroat, so used to orders that she can't judge for herself, that one line left by an enemy lingers long after. Having given her parents to Oripathy research and lost them in the clash between the Infected and the authorities, she belongs fully to nowhere, and still hasn't decided whether to stay with Rhodes Island or leave.
To a GreyThroat like that, this invasion is full of question marks. What is Rhodes Island even fighting for — and if it helps the Infected, why does it have to aim its guns at other Infected?
5. People Erased in the Slums
Around that time, something strange happens in the Slums. Half the residents of one district vanish outright, and someone has already cleaned away the Reunion corpses without a trace.
Ch'en spots Black Hats slipping across a rooftop — an unidentified unit that erases everyone in sight, Infected or citizen, without distinction.
The truth that emerges after her pressing is grim. That special unit wasn't Reunion — it was Lungmen's own hand, unleashed by Wei Yenwu to gut the Slums outright.
Even **Lin Xiya**, who cherished the Slums, is complicit in this. What the citizens fear is the Infected, she says — whether that Infected is Reunion or not doesn't matter.
Ch'en forces the special unit to withdraw, but the answer that comes back is a single chilling warning: don't try to find out what happened here, and 'don't investigate the drainage system'.

And the Infected informant 'Nine', who had been attached to Ch'en, quietly leaves the Guard Department after seeing all this. Because she's learned Lungmen has no intention of protecting the Infected. No matter where they live, she says, the Infected are all in the same position.
6. Eno and Sasha
Mephisto and Faust both had names, once.
In the sewers of Chernobog, there was a child who always smiled, **Eno**. A child who, even while enduring abuse, sang songs for his only friend, **Sasha**.

Eno believed that if he just did as people told him, everyone would be happy. So he moved the children who bullied him like chess pieces, burning away everything that had hurt him — thinking Sasha would be pleased.

Sasha said through tears — not what you're forced to do, not revenge, just do what you actually want to do. The two children swallowed Originium together and became Infected together, vowing to survive as one.
It was Reunion's leader who gave Eno a new name. She told him the past-him no longer existed, and to seek his ideal along with the name 'Mephisto'. She called herself **Talulah**.
But Sasha notices. That after passing through that village, Talulah had changed completely. That she'd become someone who even exploits Mephisto's trust.
7. Sasha's Last Arrow

Once it becomes impossible to bring their transformed comrades back, Faust has her unit retreat toward the Slums and decides to stay behind as bait. Since those monsters won't attack her, she says, they can buy time together.
Mephisto cries out — we promised to survive together! Faust answers quietly. If you come to choose for yourself and want something, she says, whatever it is, I won't blame you for it.
And she uses her final Arts, knowing that using it again will destroy her infected organ. There's no reason left for me to hold back now, she says.
Even alone, that's fine — just live. Don't die. This is my… last wish.

blocks.cap: "Farewell, Eno." — She nocks the arrow, draws the string, and takes her final aim.

blocks.x: The Phantom Crossbowmen hold nothing back with high-explosive rounds for her — exactly Faust's own way of saying no tribute to a fallen comrade is ever too much.
8. Just Falling Out of the Sky
Meanwhile, Amiya's group is surrounded by the Snow Devil Squad in the ruins. That's when Blaze drops from the sky with no rope at all, and clashes head-on with Reunion's frost Arts user.

blocks.cap: "When have you ever seen me use a rope?" — Blaze jumps down recklessly again today.

blocks.x: That opponent is **Frost Nova**, commander of the Snow Devil Squad. A dying Infected whose whole body is wrapped in cold colder than winter itself. Blaze, showing respect for her strength, urges her not to push herself any further.
During the fierce battle the ground collapses, and the Doctor falls into the ruins below along with Frost Nova. Blaze uses compressed air to slow the Doctor's fall, protecting them all the way down.

The remaining Snow Devils make an unexpected offer — a ceasefire. Aren't both our commanders rabbits anyway, they say. These people, who seemed like they'd be as cold and merciless as ice, turn out to value their comrades' lives above all else.

It was actually them who came here to hunt, they say. They'd long wanted to clean out a Reunion that hurts the innocent, like Mephisto does. And so Rhodes Island and the Snow Devils begin digging through the ruins together.
9. Kal'tsit and Crownslayer
Elsewhere, Rhodes Island's doctor **Kal'tsit** captures the Reunion agent **Crownslayer** in the Slums, with the wolf-scented girl Red holding her down.

blocks.cap: Neither the fog nor her codename is any use. Kal'tsit stands before the captured Crownslayer.

blocks.x: Crownslayer curses Kal'tsit as a traitor, saying it's Kal'tsit's fault that Ursus slaughtered her father and the other scientists. Kal'tsit calmly tells her about her father Illya, and about Crownslayer herself as a child.

The real traitor wasn't Kal'tsit, but a greedy empire that trampled on the scientists' devotion. Kal'tsit says she's no Caster, summons Mon3tr, and lets her go alive even though she could have killed her.
Leave this place. And find another path — the true path that can change this world.
Kal'tsit believes she will someday become a 'seed of unrest' that wakes from Reunion's madness. We don't stand in for the law, she says, and we have no right to judge anyone.
10. Beneath the Ruins, Facing the Frost
To the Doctor trapped beneath the ruins, Frost Nova tells her own story — a kind of thank-you for the Doctor not killing her while she couldn't move.

She was born at a mine in the tundra of northwestern Ursus. Her parents and grandmother were taken and killed one after another, and at eleven, her turn came. That's when Arts burst out of her and froze her executioners to death.

blocks.cap: Just before the final massacre, a band of guerrillas arrives — Patriot's unit, from Bulldrokarsty.

blocks.x: The one who took her in was Bulldrokarsty, an old war hero of Ursus. Even with an armorless arm about to go necrotic, he wrapped the ice-cold child in a tight embrace — she called him father.

Unable to let anyone touch her because of the cold, only able to feel warmth from hot drinks, a single spicy candy is her one pleasure. She plays mischievous pranks on the Doctor, then quietly laughs and apologizes.

She doesn't hate the people of Ursus. Even those raised to hate the Infected were, before hatred blinded them, just ordinary people. Her only enemy is the 'Ursus Empire' that drove the Infected to the brink.
She says a person who doesn't fear death is someone with no attachment left to the world. And yet, she quietly admits, she herself has failed to find the answer to that question for over a decade.

Now she thinks she has to stop even Talulah, who seems to have deceived the Infected, with her own hands — she has to keep, within whatever time is left, the promise to give her brothers and sisters a place to live.
11. The Snow Devils' Last Toast
The squad knows Frost Nova doesn't have much time left. Her Arts have been eating away at her body, so she grows lighter every time they hold her.
They decide to entrust their sleeping commander to Faust's unit, to be sent to her father Patriot's side — and to stay behind themselves to hold off the pursuit.
Warriors are bound to die someday, they say, sharing vodka they'd smuggled along, even passing a sip to their youngest member. Dying without ever knowing the taste would be too sad, after all.
When we're free, that's when we'll cry out loud… We haven't won yet, so we've no right to cry!
The last sip is offered to that cat who once said she'd drink with them — praying that everything works out for those kids. Blaze rushes over too late, and in the end can't break their resolve.
12. Winter Falls
Having lost all her brothers and sisters, Frost Nova waits for the Doctor's group beneath the ruins. With no one left beside her now, she says, for the first time she can give it everything she has.
She gathers the cold of the whole world to bear down on them, freezing all ten of Amiya's rings solid, shattering GreyThroat's arrows in midair. Asked why she goes this far, she answers.
As a commander, she's already lost, she says, but as a warrior, she hasn't lost yet. If I can't even beat myself, how could I face Talulah — beat me, and show me hope.
Amiya reads her rage and weeps, sensing that what Frost Nova endures — the pain in her heart — is many times greater than the pain of drawing out an Originium crystal.
So they don't hold back, and fight to the end. And they win. Frost Nova smiles and lets her head drop.

blocks.cap: "From now on, I'll… be by your side." — She leaves one last promise, that she wants to enter Rhodes Island.

blocks.x: She asks the Doctor a favor. Stop the Talulah of now, she says, but help the 'real Talulah' who once walked that muddy road with her. And she wants to die at her father's side, to pick with her own hands the fruit of what she planted.
The rotten part has to be cut away. Defeat me, and defeat Talulah. And the body that was colder than winter grows quiet and cold at last.
13. Don't Investigate the Drainage System
After the battle ends, Ch'en keeps hearing the same warning — 'don't investigate the drainage system.' Swire tries desperately to stop her, but Ch'en opens the door in the end.
The smell tells her where the erased people of the Slums went. Ch'en heads straight for Wei Yenwu.

Ch'en confronts him — that he erased the very Lungmen citizens who trusted and supported him, wholesale, just for being Infected, to protect his own position. And she brings up a secret long buried — that it was Wei who killed Talulah's father, and Wei who entangled Ch'en's mother.
At the same moment, Amiya visits Wei and voids the successful contract herself, because Lungmen deceived the Guard Department, the citizens, and the Infected alike, using people as game pieces.
You must not treat living people as game pieces. A person is a person.
Rhodes Island doesn't fight only for the Infected. Clearing the shadow of Oripathy from this world, while never using anyone as a tool — that's the principle Amiya upholds.
Epilogue — A City Draws Near
But the real crisis hasn't come yet. Through the entire annihilation of Reunion in Lungmen, its supreme commander never once showed her face.
Talulah had been in Chernobog the whole time. From the moment the fire started to the moment they caught their breath, she had always been watching Lungmen.

blocks.cap: 36 hours later, the core of the nomadic city Chernobog collides with Lungmen.

blocks.x: Kal'tsit reports it. Chernobog's core is approaching Lungmen, endlessly broadcasting to the whole city. The message that leaves even Wei Yenwu speechless is a single sentence.
"This city is the territory of Ursus."

In Lungmen after Reunion was driven out, those who claimed to protect the city cut into its own flesh, and the Infected with nowhere to go closed their eyes not for ideology or revenge, but by their own choice. And where winter had fallen, an entire frozen city now comes bearing down. Necrosis is not over yet.

main_7.tagline: A dead city rolls whole toward Lungmen, and those who crawl into its coffin-like Core to stop it betray one another again and again. In the end, an old warrior who fought fate for two hundred years turns his back on it for the first time.

chars.name: Nearl
chars.desc: The Radiant Knight of Kazimierz. Holds the line with her shield.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary captain from Victoria. Specializes in bombs.

chars.name: Wei Yenwu
chars.desc: Lungmen's ruler. Head of the Guard Department.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: Leader of Reunion. Is driving the Core toward Lungmen.

chars.name: Patriot
chars.desc: An old warrior of the guerrilla unit. Called the shield of the Infected.

terms.name: Chernobog's Core
terms.desc: The heart of the nomadic city Reunion is driving. About to collide with Lungmen.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that rose up in Ursus.

terms.name: Lungmen
terms.desc: The nomadic city of Yan. The end of the Core's path.

terms.name: Infected Guerrillas
terms.desc: The Elite unit led by Patriot. Strict in discipline.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: A persecuted, wandering people. They call themselves demonkin.

terms.name: Wendigo
terms.desc: A branch of the Sarkaz. The bloodline closest to death.

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: Ch'en's dragon-slaying sword. Forged in fire, cuts through Arts.

blocks.x: Chernobog, a city swallowed by Catastrophe and left for dead. Reunion has revived that city's heart, the 'Core', and is rolling an entire nomadic city toward Lungmen.
Thirty-two hours remain before the Core collides with Lungmen. A collision would mean war between Ursus and Yan.
And the world will remember only one cold fact — that "the Infected started a war."

blocks.cap: "Not a bad opening for a farce." — The story opens at the heart of Reunion, the Core.

blocks.x: 1. An Improvised Explosive Suicide Act

At the top of the Core, the Sarkaz mercenary captain **W** comes to see Reunion's leader **Talulah**. She approaches under the pretense of gathering intel, but her real goal is to stop the Core's march on Lungmen.
But Talulah reads through W's intentions from the very start — down to the things W thought she'd hidden even from herself.

Talulah is someone who hides herself behind every kind of face. A fighter before Reunion, a business partner before W, a gentle daughter before Patriot. Each mask, once peeled away, is only another mask.
As talk of W's intangible Arts unfolds, the hundred-odd bombs W had planted in the steel base melt away without a trace, entirely consumed by Talulah's heat.
Cornered, W turns her own body into a bomb and detonates, falling into the Core district. Preparing something worse than dying is W's way.

W laughs even as she dies. She thinks she was actually afraid of this dragon-horned woman — not that she disliked her, but that she feared her. In front of a monster steeped in lies, someone who plans ahead always keeps a final move in reserve.
I hope it was a sufficiently painful death, W of Victoria.

Talulah goes on to win over the Sarkaz mercenaries by promising them 'an age where war becomes king', pulling them into her grip. And to Patriot, she entrusts the key that can stop the Core, still playing the role of a gentle leader.
2. A Night of Farewells — The One Who Leapt from the Window
Meanwhile, in Lungmen. In Chief Administrator Wei Yenwu's office, Kal'tsit, Amiya, and Guard Department Captain **Ch'en** discuss the situation.
The truth comes out. Ursus handed Chernobog over to Reunion in its entirety, letting the Infected flow into Lungmen — the whole scheme laid out to unfold like flowing water.
Wei Yenwu makes his decision. He will accept war and strike the Core preemptively — intending to pay Lungmen's debt with his own death.
Ch'en can't bear that approach. The moment Wei Yenwu calls the Infected of the Slums a 'mistake', Ch'en reveals that she, too, is that 'mistake'.
Because I'm Infected too. If there's no place for the Infected in this city, then I shouldn't be here either.

Ch'en, Infected since three years ago, returns her Guard Department badge and turns her back on Lungmen. When her mentor and uncle Wei Yenwu blocks her path with black-cloaked soldiers, Ch'en draws Sui and cuts a way through.
Then she breaks a window hundreds of meters up and leaps straight down, leaving Wei Yenwu's pleas behind her.

blocks.cap: "Now we owe each other nothing." — The parting of mentor-and-uncle Wei Yenwu, and niece-and-student Ch'en.

blocks.x: Ch'en heads to the Core alone for one reason — Reunion's leader Talulah is none other than her older sister.
3. Blade of Betrayal
On the path out of Lungmen, **Hoshiguma** is waiting for Ch'en. The Guard Department has already put out a warrant for her.
The broken-horned oni trusts Ch'en — and that's exactly why she can't let her go. Standing alone against Reunion, she says, is throwing your life away for nothing.

blocks.cap: "You are not leaving Lungmen." — A friend blocks a friend's path.

blocks.x: Hoshiguma is someone who's had precious things taken from her, one by one, by time. She says she's crushed her own comrades' dreams with her own hands before, and that she can't afford to lose Ch'en too.
Even after Ch'en confesses that she's Infected, that her sister is Talulah, Hoshiguma's answer stays the same — that it matters, and that's exactly why she can't let her go.
You're Infected? I couldn't care less.
In the end, the two cross blades. And for the first time, Ch'en's sword leaves a wound on the oni.
Hoshiguma finally steps back, accepting Ch'en's request to keep, in her place, the promise sealed by the teddy bear left behind by the Ursus girl Misha — a promise to the children of the Slums.

blocks.cap: "Go! And don't ever come back." Then, left alone, she mutters, "Liar… who else worries about this city as much as you do?"

blocks.x: 4. Leave It to the Real Experts

At the Lungmen residence, Wei Yenwu's wife Fumizuki holds her husband back. Does becoming Infected mean Ch'en and Talulah stop being your nieces, she asks — are you really going to let them die in a foreign land like this?
That's when Kal'tsit steps in. The true experts for handling the Core aren't Lungmen's army, she says — it's Rhodes Island.
Only the Infected can face the Infected.

After also pointing out that Talulah may be Duke Kashchey's heir, Kal'tsit secures two things from Wei Yenwu: a promise not to interfere with Rhodes Island's operation, and the keepsake of Edward Artorius, who died in Lungmen twenty years ago — Talulah's father.
Meanwhile, in the biological processing room of the Rhodes Island vessel, the Feline girl Rosmontis quietly sends someone off — Frost Nova, a Reunion commander who fell in Lungmen.

Sending someone off, she says, is untying the thread that connected you to them. You have to get used to it, or the pain hits you all at once, all of a sudden — and only at the very end does Rosmontis ask the name of an enemy she never even knew.
Frost Nova… what a pretty name. Hello, Frost Nova. …Farewell.
5. A Promise Kept Together
Before departure, Amiya broadcasts to the whole ship. Not a speech to raise morale, but a broadcast that lays out the reality as it is.
If they can't stop a war that will be recorded as one the Infected started, everything Rhodes Island has built will vanish. The odds are slim, she says, but defeat is not something they can accept.

blocks.cap: "We will face madness with reason, and we will never give up." — Amiya's first declaration of war as leader.

blocks.x: Amiya is no longer the girl who only received orders. She says she can now hold back the storm herself, even without the Doctor and Kal'tsit — that she's finally standing shoulder to shoulder with them.
The operation is precise. A sandstorm erases their tracks, and Dobermann lures the enemy's eyes with an aircraft. Five teams have to climb up the underside of the moving Core.
When the sandstorm grows weak enough to risk exposure, Nearl steps in. The Radiant Knight bends the very angle of sunlight to her will, lighting up the whole underside of the Core.
If it will lessen this world's hardship… even the sun's own light will follow my will, if only for a moment.

blocks.cap: Amid the sandstorm, they climb onto the moving city with only a single wire to hold onto. Kal'tsit wraps an arm around the Doctor's waist and pulls them up.

blocks.x: 6. The Girl of a Forgotten Land
Having entered the Core's underground, Rhodes Island witnesses Rosmontis in battle — a chilling display of power, a massive sword floating in midair, shattering enemies to pieces.
This fourteen-year-old girl chose to fight on her own. So that no one else has to live a life like hers, she says, the world is calling her name.

Rosmontis holds Arts that gather up the emotions of the dead in their stead, but she's forgotten where those emotions actually come from. Tears flowing without any memory behind them is what hurts her.
If you don't look back… you forget. I will never forget. Not anyone's death.

Kal'tsit calmly explains — Rosmontis has almost never directly killed an enemy; that seemingly brutal power is actually used to disarm foes. It wasn't the lab that broke her, she says — she broke the lab, and so Rhodes Island helped her find the discipline to govern that power together.
Amiya stays by her side. Whatever emotion suddenly bursts out is entirely Rosmontis's own, she says — no one should take it from her — and holds her hand.
7. Reunion in the Warehouse
In the Core's warehouse district, Ursus Infected are barely clinging to life — citizens of Chernobog massacred and abandoned by Reunion.

One Reunion member caring for them turns out to be an old comrade Rhodes Island had planted undercover — an Operator who slipped into Reunion, carrying on the will of a Scout who died protecting them.

blocks.cap: A reunion of comrades in the middle of enemy territory. "It's been a while, Miss Amiya, Dr. Kal'tsit. And you, Doctor."

blocks.x: The Ursus Infected woman Tatiana curses him as Reunion. Kal'tsit holds Mon3tr's blade to her comrade's throat and asks Tatiana: has this man truly protected you?
Frightened, Tatiana finally admits — this one man has never done wrong by them. That answer alone proves her comrade's innocence — a cold, Kal'tsit-style psychological maneuver.
Through him, Rhodes Island approaches the truth. The Infected of this region are still breathing only because the old warrior **Patriot** and his guerrillas protect them.

The guerrillas are different from Reunion's rioters. They call the Infected by name instead of number, and stand as a shield for those forced into labor. Her comrade decides to stay in Reunion, believing in that conviction.
But Kal'tsit sees an even more terrifying picture — Talulah is engineering the Infected to kill each other, trying to bring down Reunion from within, in the most dishonorable way possible.
8. The Wrath of the Silent
The central district holds an altar for the Sarkaz's ancient rite of 'cannibalism' — an old ritual, no longer reproducible in the modern age, that tears life apart regardless of friend or foe.

Rhodes Island fights back with an information war. Rosmontis expands the ritual to locate every enemy messenger, Raidian scrambles the signals with her Arts, and Amiya holds Rosmontis's crumbling mind together.

As Rosmontis's mind stumbles and loses its way mid-search, Kal'tsit and Amiya place their hands on her face and bring her back to the deck. We're here beside you, they say — you just have to move forward.
The guerrillas, dependent on primitive megaphones and security directives, fall into chaos as their communication network is torn apart entirely. Rhodes Island's decapitation strike tightens the net.
At the end of that net, he finally appears — a mountain of a giant, deflecting Rosmontis's sword with his bare hand.
9. Death of a Patriot
Patriot. True name Bulldrokarsty. A Wendigo who has lived through long ages — one who left Kazdel to become a warrior of Ursus, and is now the shield of the Infected.
He and Kal'tsit have met before. Remembering Kal'tsit as a 'Knight' of the Kazdel Queen, he raises his sword against Rhodes Island, blaming them for Frost Nova's death.
But it isn't revenge. His daughter's death was a path his daughter chose herself, he says — he believes only in the outcome of war.
War does not ask right from wrong. Step onto the battlefield, and it decides itself who dies, and who was wrong.

As the fight continues, Amiya's Arts reflect his whole life — his wife Helen; his son Grobezijl, who himself requested to be put down while protesting the anti-Infected laws; and his daughter Yelena, raised amid the ice.

In the ice, young Yelena once placed a hand on her weeping father's shoulder and said — good person or bad, whatever I have to become, just let me stay with my family forever. That daughter was Frost Nova.

He has betrayed his convictions three times in his life. He left Kazdel, he opposed Ursus, and now he is about to betray Reunion. Each time he lost something precious, and each time he walked back toward fate all over again.
And yet he doesn't stop walking. If he stops, he says, the deaths of those who died beside him become meaningless — so, his body in ruins, he keeps pressing forward.

The reason he tried to keep Amiya alive was his dead daughter's words coming back to him — that they have no right to kill the Talulah of today, that whatever the future holds, today's me is me and my father is my father, Yelena had said.
Finally stopped by his accumulated wounds, he hands Kal'tsit the key to stop the Core, accepting his defeat as the victor's spoils.
Only I… need to die. I've found a place worth dying in.
