But at that moment, an old Sarkaz prophecy comes alive before his eyes — a vision of a 'Demon King' wearing a black crown, enslaving every race.

And that Demon King's face is none other than Amiya's. "The last Wendigo dies by the Demon King's hand." Realizing he is, as prophesied, dying at the Demon King's hand, he reaches out toward Amiya with his last strength.

blocks.cap: The Caprinae girl doesn't flinch. She doesn't step back, doesn't scream — she simply looks the giant straight in the eyes.

blocks.x: But the hand never reaches the girl. Death stops the Wendigo's long march before it can — a march that had never once retreated a single step.
Amiya tries to show him one last illusion, but he refuses. However miserable it is, he says, it's the result of how he's lived and struggled, and the ending he rightly deserves.
Young Demon King, everything comes down to action. If this is fate… I refuse to believe it.
10. The Cradle Crumbles
Patriot's death spreads through the whole Core. His guerrillas turn their blades not toward Rhodes Island, who killed him, but toward Talulah, who drove him to this death.
For Patriot!
The shieldbearers leave Amiya a warning too — we don't believe in the prophecy, we believe in the Captain; and if you ever become a tyrant like Talulah, we will never forgive you.
Talulah's scheme backfires on her instead. Now that the Infected and the citizens have had time to get to know each other, the facade called Reunion begins to crumble from within.
W, buried under a heap of rubble, is ironically rescued by Kal'tsit's own hand and heads for the command center, declaring that she's coming back to Talulah.
And Ch'en finally reaches the Core, holding onto nothing but an old promise — to stop her sister.

blocks.cap: "Talulah… a promise is a promise." Wounded all over, Ch'en takes her last steps toward her sister.

blocks.x: Old wounds surface too. Twenty years ago, Duke Kashchey held out the shackled infant Talulah before Wei Yenwu to test him. What Wei Yenwu cut down that day with his dragon-slaying sword Sui was none other than his own brother, Edward.
A sword meant to slay dragons, in the end used to slay his own kin. That tragedy was the beginning of the sisters Ch'en and Talulah.
Epilogue — Snow, and Someone Already Prepared
As Frost Nova's remains are processed, the cold they release blankets Chernobog's sky in heavy snow. The guerrillas speak of it — is Yelena coming to greet the Captain?
Mephisto hums, clumsily, the lullaby Frost Nova used to sing. Frustrated at how badly he sings it, only an empty song lingers in the falling snow.
And in the darkness, another scheme takes shape. The Sarkaz Regent and the Confessor, chasing clues about the 'Demon King', bring out 'her' — already prepared and waiting.

blocks.cap: "I'm back." — Where one tyrant has fallen, other hands in the darkness quietly begin to move.

blocks.x: A dead city rolled toward Lungmen, and inside it people betrayed and were betrayed, losing each other along the way. An old warrior who fought fate for two hundred years turned his back on it for the first time at the very end, entrusting the future to a young Demon King, while one woman walked into the snow-covered Core holding onto nothing but a promise to stop her sister. The key to stop the Core now rests in Rhodes Island's hands — but what this cradle of death will bring forth, no one yet knows.

main_8.tagline: The story of an abandoned girl who, carrying the curse of a black serpent, burns herself out trying to tell every Infected on a frozen continent 'you are not alone.' The snow won't melt and spring won't come — but don't hate. Only rage.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: Leader of Reunion. A Draco Infected who commands fire.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island. Fights with her ring and Arts.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: An officer of the Lungmen Guard Department. Wields the sword Sui.

chars.name: Rosmontis
chars.desc: An Elite Operator of Rhodes Island. A Feline Caster.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's doctor. A cool-headed commander.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz arms dealer. Commands mercenaries.

chars.name: Frostnova
chars.desc: Squad leader of the Snow Demons. An ice Caster.

chars.name: Patriot
chars.desc: The towering Sarkaz warrior who leads the guerrillas.

chars.name: Alina
chars.desc: Talulah's friend. A teacher to Infected children.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization that resists Ursus.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: Ursus's massive nomadic city, occupied by Reunion.

terms.name: Infected Guerrillas
terms.desc: Infected warriors who follow Patriot.

terms.name: Watch Team
terms.desc: An Ursus organization that hunts down and persecutes the Infected.

terms.name: Emperor's Blade
terms.desc: The Ursus Emperor's assassination corps. They slash faces.

blocks.x: The far north of Terra, the Ursus Empire. Here, the Infected aren't treated as the sick, but as beasts. The Watch Team hunts them down and drives them into the mines, into death.
Reunion is the armed organization the Infected formed to resist that oppression. Once, Reunion swallowed Chernobog, Ursus's massive nomadic city, whole.
This story begins after that Reunion has fallen apart — with Rhodes Island heading into the burning Core of Chernobog, on their way to confront Reunion's leader, Talulah.
And meanwhile, an unsent letter is read aloud. Addressed to 'Huijie,' sent by Talulah. In it, Talulah confesses just how she ended up this way.
So this story moves between two timelines side by side — a girl's past, which began in a snowfield, and the present, where they're on their way to face that same girl amid the flames.

blocks.cap: A scabbard covered in scratches. This story flows toward the moment two sisters stand face to face once more.

blocks.x: 1. The Child Found in the Snow
A few years ago, in a small Ursus village. An elderly couple takes in a girl they find collapsed in the snow, raising her as their own daughter. The blood-soaked child's name is Talulah.
The couple already knew Talulah was Infected, and sheltered her anyway. The grandfather himself had caught Oripathy in the mines and was Infected too.
Then one day, the Watch Team raids the village. When Talulah teaches one of their men a lesson, the entire village becomes a target for retaliation.
The grandfather confesses to being Infected in Talulah's place and is dragged off by the Watch Team, slitting his own wrist to prove it — his blood scattering like mist.

blocks.cap: "Thank you for your cooperation, Infected." — In his granddaughter's place, the grandfather bares his own Infection before the Watch Team's blade.

blocks.x: When Talulah returns after burning the Watch Team to ash, she learns that it was her own grandfather who'd informed on the village — throwing himself away just to make the search stop.
And that day, Alina, another Infected in the village, reveals her own identity for the first time. "I'm Infected too," she says.
2. Daughter of the Black Serpent
Talulah tells Alina about her past. She was originally raised as an 'heir' by an Ursus duke, Kashchey.
On the surface, Kashchey was a benevolent ruler, but underneath, he was a monster who tamed people through inequality and fear. He taught Talulah the arts of slaughter, scheming, and domination.
One day, Talulah refuses Kashchey's order and drives Originium into her own arm, becoming Infected — to break free of her fate as his heir.
Instead of raging, Kashchey offers a strange 'wager' — that the wisdom and grudges he'd taught her would someday make Talulah just like him, and that this was a blessing, not a curse.
Talulah, don't forget. In the end, you will become me.
Talulah runs Kashchey through with a sword right then and there. But the seed of Originium Arts he'd planted had already begun quietly taking root inside her.

blocks.cap: "You're not even worth hating." — The day she cut down the black serpent who raised her. The seed had already taken root.

blocks.x: 3. "You Are Not Alone"
After leaving Kashchey, Talulah wandered aimlessly until she reached the old couple's village, where she met Alina. That's when her real journey begins.
Talulah takes in hunted, starving Infected one by one. She believes the Infected need a base to join forces and decide their own fate — and a center to rally around.
Her message was simple. A single line that would come to echo the very creed of Reunion, the organization Infected in another city had founded.
You are never alone.
Alina became a teacher for Infected children — protecting people not with fighting, but with knowledge, vegetables, fruit, and storybooks.
4. The Snow Demon and the Wendigo
Talulah sets out to find the legendary Infected guerrillas of the tundra. Guarding their gate was an ice Caster — the Snow Demon squad leader, Frostnova.
Talulah earns her trust by melting Frostnova's ice with her own fire. The two become comrades who bicker constantly, yet respect each other.
The true master of the guerrillas is a towering Sarkaz warrior, Patriot — a legend even the Ursus Empire can't forget, a being called a 'Wendigo.'
Patriot dismisses Talulah's plan to head south as an idle fantasy and objects every time. And yet, he quietly watches her push forward, unshaken.
If fate laughs at me, I'll burn it down without fail.

blocks.cap: The Wendigo beside the campfire. Before this living symbol of the Infected, Talulah speaks of her future.

blocks.x: Then one day, the Ursus Emperor's guards appear before Talulah — the 'Demons,' the Empire's assassination corps, said to slash faces to make nameless corpses.
Realizing Talulah is Kashchey's daughter, they try to win over even Patriot — proposing to put the Infected at the vanguard and restore Ursus's old glory, conquering the world together.
Patriot refuses flatly. He is a traitor to the Empire, he says, and the commander he follows now isn't the Emperor — it's Talulah.
It's this woman's cause. ... Because I know better than anyone what that means.

blocks.cap: Before the Emperor's Blade, the Wendigo chooses one girl's cause over the Empire's glory.

blocks.x: 5. Every Meeting, For the Sake of Parting
The unit inches slowly southward. Alina teaches the children, Talulah gathers more Infected, and Frostnova sings the children to sleep. Brief but warm days.
Then one day, Alina, on her way back from trading for supplies in a village, loses her life at the Watch Team's hands.
Talulah carries Alina's blood-soaked body on her back, walking through the endless snowfield. That light, small body feels as heavy as the earth itself.
With her last breath, Alina begs her — never hate anyone. What deserves hatred isn't people, but Ursus, and a world that made people this way.
Every meeting... exists for the sake of parting.
Talulah doesn't remember what happened after Alina closed her eyes. All she knows is that wherever she walked, the snow-covered trees burned silently in her wake.

blocks.cap: "The only thing you're allowed to hate is what they did." — Her friend, to the very last, begs her not to hate.

blocks.x: 6. Into the Burning Core

Now, the present. Reunion has already collapsed from within. Sarkaz mercenaries are massacring the Infected, and Ursus regulars have infiltrated wearing Reunion's uniforms. Talulah herself set out to wipe Reunion out here.
Under Kal'tsit's command, Rhodes Island enters the Core of Chernobog. They have to stop this massive hunk of steel before the city comes within range of Lungmen's cannons.

Amiya and Rosmontis fight their way toward the command tower. Along the way, they join hands with the shield-bearers of the guerrillas who once followed Patriot — even though each side has killed the other's comrades, for the sake of a bigger goal.
Rosmontis nearly gets swallowed by destructive Arts, but holds on, gripping the hands of her teammates and Amiya. To stand not as a weapon or a soldier, but as a 'warrior' — as herself.
The shield-bearers keep pushing Amiya toward revenge and hatred — hate the ones who made you like this, they say, because hatred is the very force that drives them.

But Amiya doesn't waver. The injustice done to the Infected is real, she says, but that's not the same as using it as fuel for hatred. We don't lay down our lives for anyone, she declares flatly — there's no such thing as a necessary sacrifice.
7. A Physician's Choice
Deep within the Core, Kal'tsit faces a horrifying sight. An Infected, mutated inside a sarcophagus, has become a beautiful yet lethal white creature, scattering Originium dust.
That dust was the seed of a large-scale catastrophe that would infect even ordinary people. As a physician, Kal'tsit decides to stop the creature.

Chernobog's Core was originally some unidentified facility, unearthed long ago. Ursus scientists Kal'tsit had once taught uncovered its power, but one by one, under the military's greed and threats, they perished inside the sarcophagi. Kal'tsit has carried the guilt of failing to protect them for a long time.
Even as it dies, the white creature sings — that being alive is good, that even when things are hard there's a friend who'll stay by your side, and please, tell people that.

And Kal'tsit reveals a truth long hidden from the Doctor — that three years ago, this sarcophagus was meant to treat the Doctor, and that the being who once used the Doctor's body had killed Kal'tsit's friend, Theresa.

Kal'tsit is torn between her promise to protect the Doctor and an anger toward him she can't erase. Even so, she says the answer is something the Doctor must find for himself, and leaves the judgment for later.

blocks.cap: An Infected, mutated within the sarcophagus. This creature, the starting point of the catastrophe, sings to the very end, even as it dies.

blocks.x: 8. Two Sisters
At the top of the command tower, someone was already standing against Talulah — Ch'en, an officer of the Lungmen Guard Department, sword Sui in hand.
Ch'en and Talulah grew up together as children. Twenty years ago, on that rainy night, Ch'en let go of her hand out of fear, and Talulah was dragged off to Kashchey alone.

Ever since that day, Ch'en has lived with a single goal — to find her sister again. She trained in swordsmanship in Victoria, returned to Lungmen to become an officer, and chased the trail of a girl who'd vanished ten years earlier.

The one who taught her swordsmanship was none other than Wei Yenwu, the man who'd driven Kashchey out of Lungmen — the one who, back when Ch'en was a child, told her that "righting a wrong is worth risking your life for."

Amiya reads the memory lingering in Ch'en's scabbard and joins her. Then, the moment she looks into Talulah's emotions, she realizes something bone-chilling — the one speaking from inside the Talulah before them is Kashchey, who should already be dead.

Ch'en makes it clear — she didn't come here to prove her sister's innocence. If it's a plot that treats human life as worthless, she says, she'll stop it with her sword, even if it's her own flesh and blood behind it. That's exactly why she staked everything to get here.
Kashchey's curse was trying to swallow Talulah whole — making her kill Ch'en with her own hands, erasing even the last trace of herself that remained.

blocks.cap: A life-or-death struggle between blood kin. The moment it touches Sui's blade, the Draco's flames die down.

blocks.x: 9. The Right to Rage
With his mask off, Kashchey reveals himself as the 'undying black serpent' — a being who claims to have watched over everything in Ursus for a thousand years, never dying.

He says he loves every single person in Ursus — strong or weak, good or evil, all of them. That's why he'll temper people through war, he says, and restore dignity to the Infected. The small sacrifices in front of them are nothing, he says, next to the glory that awaits a hundred years from now.
Talulah's flame is hot enough to swallow Amiya and Ch'en whole. Inside that fire, hot enough to burn away even the oxygen, Amiya closes her eyes and walks into the memories of countless others.
At the end of those memories, she meets the rage of a Sarkaz king who was betrayed and slaughtered — not revenge, not a grudge, but pure rage aimed at injustice itself.
Even without taking revenge, even without holding a grudge, I will always have the right to rage!
Hatred can't erase a grudge, but rage was different. Ch'en's swordsmanship and that rage weave together in Amiya's hands, becoming a blue blade that looks just like Sui.
That sword finally splits Talulah's flames apart — a blade carrying the rage of countless generations, one that can cut through even Arts.

blocks.cap: A blue sword, the very image of Sui, slices across the night. What cut through that Arts was the rage of an entire age.

blocks.x: 10. "Because I Am Fire"
Amiya and Ch'en don't kill Talulah. No one has the right to judge her, they say, and instead try to hold onto her — not for a verdict, but for accountability.

Once the summoned memory has circled long enough through Talulah's mind, she herself finally rises up against Kashchey — against the black serpent mocking a comrade who wouldn't bow her head, even in death.
You're the seed, and the vine, and the towering tree too. But it doesn't matter, Kashchey. Because I am fire.
Talulah drives Kashchey out of herself. It was the earth, the snowfields, and the comrades who chased the light that taught her who she is — the black serpent, she says, was never her.

Just then, the arms dealer W appears, searching for the key that can stop the Core. To the very end, Kashchey tried to talk Talulah into destroying that key, but failed — Talulah still had a conscience that not even Arts could erase.
With the key Talulah hands over, W enters the code. Chernobog grinds to a halt with a disgruntled roar, just outside the range of Lungmen's cannons.
W regrets her own mistake — hurting the boy Misha just to stop the city. Only now does she realize that she, Patriot, and Frostnova had all been played by Kashchey's schemes.
When the fighting ends, W hands 'freedom' to the surviving Sarkaz mercenaries. Live for yourselves now, she tells them, not as someone's killing machine.

blocks.cap: "Get up, Talulah!" — At Ch'en's cry, the Draco who'd fallen to her knees rises, very slowly, back to her feet.

blocks.x: 11. An Ending, or a Beginning
The Chernobog incident is quietly sealed away as 'an accident caused by the Infected.' Neither Ursus nor Lungmen wanted the truth to come out.

Rhodes Island takes Talulah with them. They hand her over neither to the shield-bearers who want revenge, nor to Ursus, nor to Lungmen — because there is still no place in this world that can judge the Infected fairly.

Talulah chooses not to die. If she dies, it would only be playing into Kashchey's hands, and the names only she remembers would vanish along with her. She decides to live — until, someday, she earns the right to die for them.
Amiya, who held back her tears to the very end amid the flames, is barely saved by Kal'tsit's hands. And then she breaks down crying — every tear she'd been holding back, all at once.
Forty-one Rhodes Island Operators fell in Chernobog. Kal'tsit calls each of their names, one by one, vowing never to forget.

Rhodes Island didn't throw themselves into this for the sake of results. It's only because they believe a life's worth isn't just about surviving, and that the wounds this world has suffered can be healed, that they keep moving forward.

Ch'en doesn't return to Lungmen. She stays for the sake of what she has to do — building a place that can judge the Infected and ordinary people fairly, together. That, she says, isn't a dream. It's a responsibility.
The embers left behind by Reunion's fallen members don't die out either. Wherever they scatter, those who believe the Infected will rise again even after falling keep that fire alive.
One quiet postscript, meanwhile — a newly developed standard medicine for the Infected is given a name: 'Frostnova.'

blocks.cap: Sweet dreams, then. — A farewell shared by comrades beside the campfire, on a night when it was still winter.

blocks.x: In her letters, Talulah always closes with the same words. The snow won't melt, and spring doesn't seem like it's coming. Even so, her fire won't go out — not before she makes it through the winter.

An abandoned girl, carrying the black serpent's curse, burned herself out trying to tell every Infected on a frozen continent that they weren't alone. Hating no one, holding onto nothing but the right to rage.
And at the end of the burnt-out city, in a world buried under ash and snow, Amiya reaches out her hand to the Doctor, who has lost his memory. So that this time, no one lets go of that hand.
Welcome back, Doctor.

blocks.cap: "Welcome back, Doctor." — At the end of the world, once again.

main_9.tagline: A story of soldiers chasing one stolen weapon who get trapped in a city where more people turn to ash the more everyone chooses sides. Those who took no side at all, and simply tried to save people, force a dawn out of the pitch-black night.

chars.name: Horn
chars.desc: Commander of Victoria's Storm Assault Force. A principled noble officer.

chars.name: Bagpipe
chars.desc: Horn's subordinate. A stubborn, hard-headed Wyvern warrior.

chars.name: Outcast
chars.desc: An Elite Sankta Operator of Rhodes Island. A master of the revolver.

chars.name: Jenny
chars.desc: A Victorian honor guard stationed in Hillock County. She lingers close to the residents.

chars.name: Colonel Hamilton
chars.desc: Commander of the garrison in Hillock County. He despises the Taran people.

chars.name: Mandragora
chars.desc: A Caster executive of Dublin. She commands stone.

chars.name: Nine
chars.desc: A Reunion commander pursuing Talulah.

terms.name: Hillock County
terms.desc: An ordinary mid-sized nomadic city near Londinium.

terms.name: the Taran people
terms.desc: The native people of this land. Discriminated against in Victoria.

terms.name: Dublin
terms.desc: The 'Ghost Legion.' An unidentified armed organization claiming to fight for Taran revival.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: An organization that treats the Infected and mediates conflicts.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization of the Infected that once rose up in Ursus.

blocks.x: Victoria is a nation that outpaced the world with steel and capital. But in the shadow of that prosperity live people who called this land home long before the city was ever built.
Victoria calls them the 'Taran people,' handing them low wages, scarce medicine, and worn-out protective gear. Oripathy, strangely enough, spreads only through their alleys.
This story begins in just such a city — Hillock County. Chasing a stash of stolen military-grade Originium goods, a small squad from Londinium sets foot here.

The stolen weapons are enough to arm an entire small town, or blow apart a nomadic city's caterpillar treads. Only a well-trained unit could have pulled off such a flawless heist. People call that ghostlike organization the 'Ghost Legion.'

blocks.cap: A nomadic city that looks perfectly ordinary. But the more ordinary it looks on the surface, the deeper the undercurrent running beneath it.

blocks.x: 1. The Thread That Started in a Potato Warehouse

Storm Assault Force commander **Horn** and her subordinate **Bagpipe** raid a warehouse on the outskirts. But there are no stolen weapons — only crates of potatoes and carrots. All they catch is one terrified young man, Damien.
Just then, garrison Captain Kelly shows up and demands the suspect be handed over. Jurisdiction belongs to Hillock County, and rules are rules. Horn reluctantly hands Damien over.

The next day, in the interrogation room, the garrison deliberately gives Horn's group the wrong time, then claims the questioning is already over. Then, without a trace of concern, they announce it: Damien resisted during transport and was 'shot dead.'
There was no judgment, no trial. It was simply an execution. Bagpipe explodes with fury, but just like that, another lead turns to ash and disappears.

In truth, Captain Kelly, who handed Damien over, was the boy's own uncle. A man who had watched his nephew grow up ended up pushing him to his death in the face of orders from above. A child screams at him: "Traitor!" The captain turns away, the word echoing over and over in his mind.

To Horn and Bagpipe, Damien and his weeping family looked like nothing more than ordinary people — nothing about them resembled trained soldiers. And that's exactly what unsettles Horn. If ordinary people take up arms, Victoria will face a catastrophe unlike anything it has known in a long time.
2. A Morning With Milk Tea

Meanwhile, a visitor comes to the Rhodes Island Hillock County office every single morning — **Jenny**, a Victorian honor guard who slips out of camp in secret. She bakes pies, brews milk tea, and hands out smiles to everyone.
Jenny doesn't hesitate to towel-dry the hair of Craig, a boy who lost his father to Oripathy — a child everyone else is too afraid of infection to even touch.
Into that peaceful morning, someone slips in without anyone noticing — **Outcast**, an Elite Operator of Rhodes Island. A dessert-loving Sankta who eases tension with card tricks.

blocks.cap: Beneath that easygoing exterior, Outcast can read a person's origins and true feelings from a single turn of phrase.

blocks.x: Outcast notices Taran language cropping back up throughout the newspapers. Someone, he realizes, is using the bond of language to gather a scattered people back around a single spark.

A bond forged through language is stronger than almost anything else. It doesn't thin out over the years like blood ties, and it isn't weighed and traded like self-interest. Outcast senses it coming: the greater the force applied, the more the reaction will exceed all expectation.
3. One City, Two Victorias

Bagpipe digs for leads out on the streets, getting pelted with rotten vegetables for her trouble. Jenny, staying by her side, explains: the residents call themselves 'the Taran people,' the true owners of this land who have lived here for centuries.
Jenny says carefully that Victoria has never once treated everyone equally. Bagpipe, in turn, encourages her — even an honor guard can change what's right in front of her.
At the same time, Horn sits down across from **Colonel Hamilton**, commander of the garrison. To the colonel, this isn't an incident. It's a 'war' that has raged on for centuries.
If a person refuses to make their name known in the Victorian tongue, then they are no longer Victorian — they are nothing but an enemy threatening the safety of the Empire!

The colonel hardens his resolve at the sight of bloodstains on a Taran poet's collection of verse. Horn realizes: the real danger in this city might not be the enemy hiding in the dark, but the ally standing in the light.
4. The Banquet, and Martial Law

A discreet banquet among nobles. On the surface, they praise Taran culture, but underneath, each is quietly calculating the 'technical support of a distant friend' and their own share of the spoils. Amid it all, Horn shares a quiet conversation with the poet Seamus Williams.

The poet says quietly that he has neither the power nor the intent to change this land — he is merely someone who plants feathers. Thought is free, and so the bird that grows in each person's heart is different every time.
Just then, the door is kicked open. Colonel Hamilton storms in with armed troops and places the entire banquet hall under arrest. He tailed Horn's group to find their hideout.
The colonel even moves to lay hands on an innocent servant, and the poet steps forward to protect the others. At that exact moment, a ball rolling in from outside the window starts to make a sound.
5. Dublin's Fire
An explosion. The poet was far too close to the bomb. The courage he showed, throwing himself in harm's way for others, was no less than any soldier's. Horn can only pray his death wasn't in vain.
And out of the darkness, a unit steps forward — an unidentified army led by a Caster, **Dublin**. The world calls them the 'Ghost Legion' for how ghostlike they seem.
Their 'Leader' can turn an entire street to ash with just a flick of the fingers. No fire is even actually burning, yet every living thing, from statues to the town hall, silently crumbles into ash.

It was the oppressed young Taran men and women who lit this fire. Believing the Leader to be their savior, they fire off the signal flare of rebellion with their own hands. The Leader answers under his breath: "My fire… spread faster."
6. A Poem in the Ashes

A girl is dragged before the Leader — **Sielsha**, who leaked word of the banquet to the military. She's Damien's cousin, and Jenny's closest friend. The executive Mandragora presses for her execution.
Trembling with fear, Sielsha still asks: will there ever come a day when the Taran people live better lives? The Leader answers, "There will." The girl says she wants to believe those words.
Ah… hah… I wonder, once spring comes next year, will willow shoots sprout even on this land that's been burned to nothing…

Fire surges from the tip of the Leader's spear. Sielsha turns to ash. Every time an execution ends, the Leader's chest burns with pain, because this girl was not the first of his own people that he had killed.

The Leader picks up a half-scorched poem from the ruins, its lines burned before the ink could even dry, lit by his own fire. The poem speaks quietly: on the other side of the scale, it saw the soul of a person.
That soul was, in truth, also his own — the Leader who hid beneath the shadow using him, wanting nothing more than to run away.

The Leader was 'a shadow pushed onto the stage.' What moved him was someone who shared his own blood. And beneath the dazzling light, he himself was quietly falling apart, counting every person he had killed.
7. The Innocent
The residents, having lost a friend, blame Sielsha's death on Jenny. Jenny is driven out under a hail of stones. Even Craig turns his back on her. "We don't need you!"

Outcast offers a hand to a broken Jenny, and tells her his own story. Decades ago, a village where his mediation failed fell the very next day, and the people, even as they died, still believed he would protect them.

When Jenny says that at least he did his best, Outcast asks her back: could she then just stand by and watch evil repeat itself? Jenny says she couldn't. No matter how many times she's asked, she says, she doesn't regret it.
Saving people isn't something you divide into sides.
8. The Real Thief, and the Black Flower

The stolen weapons never turn up on the battlefield after all. A scout team led by Triangle, tracking their whereabouts, sends back one final transmission: the weapons have been moved to the garrison's artillery camp.

And they've been converted — into 'dirty bombs' that scatter active Originium. With her comrade Snare, an arrow through the heart, lying beside her, Triangle keeps the transmission going. Who exactly is their enemy, she asks — they even use command codes she recognizes, tied to the same weapons.

Horn tells her that doesn't matter right now, and orders her to come back alive, no matter what. Triangle answers with a laugh and cuts the transmission. That team, having uncovered the truth, never makes it back.

This was Hamilton's plan from the very beginning: if the spies can't be sorted out, burn the enemy and the civilians together, all at once. And Oripathy makes for a convenient excuse to clean up afterward.

What sounded like thunder was cannon fire. Shells pour down like rain, blooming black Originium crystals across the streets — like black flowers bursting open in a muddy downpour. Chef, the courier carrying vital information, falls beneath it too.

Bagpipe finds Chef's body in the collapsed rubble. The evidence he had risked his life to protect to the very last moment is still clutched in his arms. Bagpipe makes a quiet promise to this comrade whose name she never even knew: your courage will not have been for nothing.
9. The Flag That Flies

In the middle of the bombardment, Jenny carries the wounded and helps with disease control — for the very same people who drove her out. Just then, Ronan, the man who sold out Damien, appears and tries to chase off even the injured children.
Jenny blocks his way. She doesn't speak Taran, and she isn't infected with Oripathy — but walking these streets for over two years has taught her one thing: we are all, equally, living people.
Jenny tears the Victorian flag apart and binds Craig's wounds with it, saying she has nothing cleaner to use. Then she tells Ronan she'll hold him accountable for Sielsha.

blocks.cap: "At least, for this one moment, we're on the same side." Behind Jenny, gripping the flag, the residents gather one by one.

blocks.x: Behind Jenny, ready to fight alone if she has to, the less severely wounded residents begin to gather. It's the moment 'the same side' is born for the first time, outside the bounds of any faction.
10. Just Before Dawn
Outcast saves a dying Infected man. He was the mob's leader, but to Outcast, he's simply one more Infected person collapsing, swept up in a struggle for power.

Leaving the man in Bagpipe's care, Outcast stays behind to wait for their pursuers. Six of Dublin's executives step out of the darkness — the 'Accountant,' the 'Arsonist,' the 'Plunderer'... each one carrying a name as ominous as a catastrophe.
If justice unfolded on its own, would my gun ever need to do anything at all?
Outcast breaks a promise he made to a close friend — a bet that he would never fire all six shots rashly. He loads the last bullet into his revolver and fires all six at once.

All six are reduced to dust in a burst of sacred light. The blinding flash that erupts from the muzzle pierces the clouds and lights up the whole city. And as the price of that judgment, Outcast himself scatters into drifting ash.

People out on the streets who see the light mistake it, for a moment, for daybreak. The rain hasn't stopped, yet the sky has brightened. To the very end, Outcast never managed to follow his own rule — to look after your own safety before saving someone else.

Meanwhile, at the collapsing command center, his subordinates beg him to flee, but Hamilton stays behind alone. He, too, the man who burned an innocent city, dies in his own way in the end — straightening his collar, drawing his cavalry saber, and walking straight into the swelling light.
11. The Final Order

Horn and Bagpipe clash with Mandragora at the communications base. She's powerful with her command of stone, but every time she uses Arts, she leaves an opening. The two of them try to bring her down by targeting that weakness.

Mandragora taunts them — they haven't lost because they lack skill, but because Victoria abandoned them. There are people helping Dublin even among the military's upper ranks, and Hillock County was under surveillance from the very start. Horn edges closer to a chilling truth: the Dublin Leader's true goal might be the throne of Victoria itself.

But at that very moment, a broadcast pours out from communication towers across the city. Dublin declares itself a friend of the oppressed, carving its name into all of Victoria. What's truly frightening is that most of what it says is true.

Horn has always valued Bagpipe's life above everything else. She drove the young recruit especially hard back in the day, worried that a girl who arrived carrying meat pudding and a blanket from home might never let go of the past. But that same stubbornness made Bagpipe into a warrior who surpassed her own commander.
Now there's only one way left to get the truth out — someone has to escape this city alive. Horn shields Bagpipe with her half-shattered shield and gives her final order.
Bagpipe... you have to get out alive! This is our... last chance!

Bagpipe cannot refuse an order from the person she trusts most on the battlefield. With reddened eyes, she fires her Castlebreaker and launches herself into the sky, carrying with her the order never to look back.

No matter what happens to this land, dawn always comes. And if it doesn't come on its own, people will burn their own lives to bring the light of dawn into being. Ahead of Bagpipe, the real morning sun was rising.
Epilogue — Rhodes Island on the Eve of the Storm

What Outcast left behind was an urn of ashes and an empty magazine. Misery asks that, just this once, she be the one to send off her comrade. And she makes a decision: Rhodes Island must head to Londinium right now.

Jenny survived and joined Rhodes Island. Her name now is that of an Operator — **Saileach**. Aboard the ship, she crosses paths, in passing, with the red-scaled dragon Infected she once saved. He doesn't recognize her.
Trapped in thick fog, Rhodes Island receives an email from an old friend, Dijkstra. Along with his greeting, the ship's systems go dead. And then Reunion strikes.

Their target is **Talulah**, asleep deep within the ship. This is a raid led by commander **Nine** and Guard, an old comrade who turned her back on Rhodes Island. The Infected want the truth behind the betrayal, and answers from their leader.
Amiya, Kal'tsit, and Mon3tr try to stop them, but Talulah decides to go with Reunion of her own will. There is still a reckoning she has to face, and a reason she has to survive.

It was Closure's old friend Dijkstra who helped with the raid. He apologizes, yet feels no regret. From Columbia to the Sarkaz homeland, he has seen the eyes of Infected everywhere across this earth, burning with anger and conviction. He says that, before anything else, he is a 'rebel.'

In Londinium too, the Sarkaz royal court moves in secret, and factions everywhere angle for the peak of the chaos. The Dublin Leader already stands in the middle of that board. The spark lit in Hillock County has begun to spread across the whole earth.

Months later, Bagpipe wanders the wasteland with her old comrade Ch'en, believing that Commander Horn is still alive, waiting for her, trusting her to come. Officially, the Hillock County incident was covered up as a 'military training accident,' but Bagpipe remembers everything she saw. And she knows that an enemy who caused such an uproar and then vanished into quiet is exactly the shadow of a far greater conspiracy.

The fire sparked by a single stolen weapon didn't end with the one city of Hillock County. On that night when the innocent turned to ash the more people chose sides, some stood beside their fellow human beings, beyond any side at all, and some burned their own bodies to force the dawn into being. And now that spark has begun spreading toward all of Victoria.

rogue_2.tagline: A story of the Doctor and Mizuki descending into the coastal waters of Iberia, swallowed by the Profound Silence, to seek an answer to evolution at Caerula Arbor, the Seaborn's origin. Whether to remain human or become Seaborn — four different seas each give a different answer.

chars.name: Mizuki
chars.desc: A boy of the Abyssal Hunters. He heads into the deep sea together with the Doctor.

chars.name: the Deep Sea Bishop
chars.desc: A figure who studied the Seaborn. He dreamed of fusing humanity with the Seaborn.

chars.name: the Knight
chars.desc: A madman who has made the sea his enemy, standing alone against the waves.

terms.name: the Profound Silence
terms.desc: The great catastrophe that destroyed Iberia. The sea swallowed the land.

terms.name: the Seaborn
terms.desc: A creature born of the sea. It erodes the land through evolution.

terms.name: the Abyssal Hunters
terms.desc: Warriors of Ægir who fight the Seaborn.

terms.name: Caerula Arbor
terms.desc: A Seaborn hatchery within Terra's mantle. The remains of a 'Firstborn.'

terms.name: the Many
terms.desc: The collective consciousness shared by the Seaborn.

terms.name: the Inquisition
terms.desc: An Iberian religious faction that fights the Seaborn with fire. Opposed to the Church of the Deep.

blocks.x: Integrated Strategies is a game where you sail again and again across a sea that's freshly assembled each time. So rather than a fixed plot, the backbone of the story here is the setting laid over that voyage and the one question that keeps repeating.
The wind blows past your shoulder, heading toward the far side of the sea. Your destination lies there, but you haven't set out yet.
Pack your things and finish your preparations. A vast sea is waiting for you.
The sea the Doctor and **Mizuki** are about to set out onto is the coastal water of a nation once called Iberia. And that sea no longer belongs to people.

blocks.cap: Atop a pile of stones on the night sea, Mizuki holds an umbrella and waits for the Doctor beside two weapons half-sunk in the waves. Far off, the last Eye of Iberia glimmers faintly.

blocks.x: 1. The Sea After the Profound Silence
This nation collapsed under a catastrophe called the **Profound Silence**. The sea swallowed the land, and the brilliant golden age Iberia once took such pride in sank away into dust.

Back when the rinsu-fishing boats used to set sail, village children would make wishes to their parents, and the adults would hand the biggest rinsu to a child. Even that tender custom vanished completely after the Profound Silence.

The sea begins at a sky-blue coastline, passes through a remote coastal island and fierce breakers, and leads on to a secluded hotbed. At its end wait a fluorescent-lit deep-sea forest, and a deep blue cradle that bears all things within it.

blocks.cap: Waves break against a black sand beach, and pale light seeps down over a crumbling cliff. Iberia's sea is beautiful, but it no longer accepts people.

blocks.x: In the cemetery atop the cliff, people burn their dead and let the ashes scatter on the sea wind. Burial at sea used to be the custom here, but now people refuse the sea.
The Corrosion consumed even the light this nation once took pride in. Even the last Eye of Iberia corroded and collapsed, and its once-brilliant history sank away into dust.
You can see why if you look down from the cliff. The Corrosion, a trace left by the Seaborn, covers the sea and is spreading toward the cliffs.
It isn't that people rejected the sea. The sea rejected the people.

Before the Profound Silence came, the sea used to buzz with tales of pirates who seized the king's ships and roamed every corner of it. Their stories, of wealth rivaling an entire nation, vanished along with Iberia's golden age. That there are no pirates in this era now is simply a fact.

In the middle of the empty city stands a museum honoring nameless kings. Yet inside, there are no statues, no names, no titles for those kings at all. Iberia's kings have been lumped together, and have come down to this day as nothing more than 'the nameless kings.'

Still, there's somewhere to rest your weary steps. In a safehouse where the sound of waves can't reach, the light is warm and the food steams invitingly. A bigger wave waits ahead, so rest well here and get ready.

blocks.cap: "Sleep well — you won't hear the sound of the waves here." A modest shelter built by Rhodes Island. Here, you can forget the sea, if only for a moment.

blocks.x: It's Rhodes Island that protects the Infected in this sea. Tulip, the officer in charge of the Iberian outpost, mobilizes every resource she has to keep the Infected alive, and somewhere nearby, a squad is always standing by at the Doctor's side.
2. Fire, Faith, and the Sea's Outsiders
It's the **Inquisition** that guards fallen Iberia. Its inquisitors and missionaries believe fire alone can burn the Seaborn born of the sea, and they revere lanterns like sacred relics.
But that faith in fire often spills over into madness. Some missionaries drag around a Seaborn nailed to a brazier, and the moment a hymn ends, they set fire straight to an infected operator's body.
At the opposite extreme are the **Church of the Deep**. They worship the Seaborn as gods, living in a delicate coexistence where, as long as enough food is offered, even the Seaborn pose no threat.

Survival gets even more brutal than that. Some locals lead the Doctor into a maze-like cave, where enormous shellfish are piled up, and people silently collect, filter, and package the secretions oozing from their shells.

Those shellfish are actually Seaborn eggs. A coating made from the eggs' secretions throws off a Seaborn's tracking. But in exchange, those who go to collect them often don't come back, and every time one of the fleshy masses shrivels up, a sob seems to echo from somewhere.

Atop a reef, a lone Seaborn plucks a human instrument with its tentacles and sings its final song. A grating noise mixes with a sweet melody, and that song soon calls its kin. It isn't a sound a person makes, so it's best not to listen at all.
And there are people of the sea being slowly forgotten. Ægirians, washed ashore not once but twice, still keep watch over the waves, waiting for the day they can reach their homeland again.

One last wave-watcher, counting the fifth tsunami in half a year, still won't step back from the cliff. Someone, she says, absolutely has to record what happens at the sea's center in that final moment.

Atop a reef, an inquisitor who had lost her lantern stood propping herself up with her sword, breathing hard. Seaborn corpses lay scattered around her, and her other hand was struggling to hold down something writhing inside her sleeve.
When the Doctor hands her the lantern she'd lost, she manages a strained smile. "Go, quickly. I'll light your way ahead. One last time." A flame of faith, fading away, lights a stranger's path.

blocks.cap: Two weapons sleeping in a crate beneath a lantern's light. Relics of the Abyssal Hunters, most skilled of all at breaking the Seaborn.

blocks.x: An old craftsman in the cave is maintaining two weapons. They were made for the **Abyssal Hunters**, but now there are no Hunters left in the sea, nor any owner left to wield them.

When the Doctor lifts the weapon, the old man turns away bitterly. "How could a landsman ever wield an Abyssal Hunter's weapon!" The true owners of that weapon are already asleep somewhere in the sea.

Somewhere in the sea, there's also a madman who believes himself a knight. He races across the waves, shouting for the sea to be slain. Civilization despises him and nature spurns him, but the Knight's head will never bow.
3. The Seaborn, and an Evolution That Cannot Stop
The **Seaborn** are a race that pursues nothing but survival. Since that survival is itself hardship, they evolve endlessly to overcome their flaws and stand against nature.

The new trick born of that evolution is Erosion Dust. The dust methodically breaks down cities and civilization into useless white powder, and uses it as nourishment to raise the next generation. The whole land is turning, bit by bit, into a Seaborn field.
A dried-up Seaborn corpse on the shore was clutching something tightly. The moment the Doctor touches it, a flood of images flashes through their mind.

Kin evolved in a forest over the ruins to digest metal, humans who offer food and humans who become food. And the first words the Seaborn learned after evolving their vocal organs — those were 'gift' and 'friend.'

blocks.cap: Dark clouds cover the entire sea, Originium dust swirling within them. In the face of nature's threat, what can a living thing possibly do?

blocks.x: In a certain dream, the Doctor stands among a crowd of Seaborn packed inside a sunken ship. They communicate without a sound, new limbs sprout from severed ones, and some individuals even redirect the flow of the water.

They communicate without making a sound. Neither fast nor slow, like a thousand years of ebb and flow, mournful and plaintive syllables simply flow between their exchanges, like the cadence of a poetic drama.
And then it dawns on them — that in this dream, they too are a Seaborn. Strangely, the Doctor adapts well to that identity, and the others make room, accepting them as one of their own.
Evolution. The process of surpassing every known form, on the way to the perfect answer. This is an instinct that cannot be stopped, and the only 'path' there is.

It isn't the environment that changed them. They are the ones changing the environment. Standing before the singularity of their evolution — beings who will one day surpass this land and sea to reach somewhere new — they ask themselves a single question: "Who am I?"
Before a stone tablet assimilated by the Corrosion, the Doctor reads letters that never stop shifting. "You will either lead humanity in resisting the Seaborn, or become a Seaborn and lead your kin."

Mizuki stops the Doctor. The Corrosion is still analyzing information even now, and whatever the Doctor has read, the Many will soon know as well. And yet the tablet drags the Doctor's consciousness into a moment in time the Seaborn are about to experience.
4. The Doctor and Mizuki, into the Deep Sea

The terminal rings with nothing but bad news. Seaborn are heading for land, a punitive fleet has been wiped out, the Church of the Deep is attacking a fortress. But what worries them most are the Abyssal Hunters who entered Ægir aboard the Stultifera Navis.

The document Mizuki found laid out a plan to deceive them, lure them back into the sea, and revive them there. And another document, 'Caerula,' held the records of a bishop who spent his whole life studying the origin of the Seaborn and a single place.

There's no time to tell Rhodes Island, or their Iberian allies. The Doctor decides to infiltrate the deep sea together with Mizuki, using the secret the bishop left behind to defeat the Seaborn and save the Abyssal Hunters.
In the name 'Caerula,' the Doctor senses an inexplicable familiarity. Mizuki even tries to show the Doctor that he himself can assimilate 'its' core organ. Even so, neither of them stops walking.

The two of them keep descending, down and down, all the way to **Caerula Arbor**, the experimental hub seated within Terra's mantle. It's a Seaborn hatchery, and naturally, a Seaborn can become part of it too.

Where the map pointed lay a single colossal set of remains. Countless Seaborn were feeding on the endlessly regrowing wreckage, and the two of them slip through the gaps to enter a laboratory sealed away for ages.

Not even Mizuki could open the tightly sealed gate. But the moment the security system caught sight of the Doctor's face, the lock released on its own. This strange laboratory in the deep sea had recognized the Doctor as 'authorized personnel.'

In the middle of a garden, a colossal tree rose up, its branches stretching out beyond the laboratory itself. Mizuki says this is 'its' core organ — a tree that is, in theory, dead, yet still grows on survival instinct alone, feeding the Many.
The moment Mizuki touches the bark, he's slowly drawn into the tree. Before long, 'it' awakens, and the entire laboratory trembles with that revival.
Ishar'mla, I, stop it.

These remains are the very 'Firstborn' that swallowed its own birthplace. Only a Seaborn can assimilate with it, and only a human can understand the sorrow held within it. Mizuki means to become one of the Seaborn through that body.

The bishop's ultimate wish was to fuse humanity and the Seaborn, evolving them into a stronger form of life. To him, the Seaborn were nothing but a means to an end. To tear down that scheme from the inside, Mizuki climbs onto the experiment table himself.

After sending Mizuki into the transfer chamber, the Doctor heads for the main console. This is an automated process that takes an extremely long time. A hundred years? A thousand? Not a single figure shows on the console.
Outside the glass, stem-like tendrils endlessly stretch and contract, drawing energy from the magma, and part of the experimental hub's structure had already been completely assimilated.

The Doctor could have stopped this dangerous experimental process. But if they choose not to stop it, only one thing remains: to stay by Mizuki's side until his life comes to its end within this experimental hub.

Beside its dwelling lay a pristine area the Seaborn's touch had never reached. There was nothing there but a broken statue, and two beautiful women asleep with weapons still clutched in their hands. Right up to the final moment, the Abyssal Hunters had slept in the shape of humans, not Seaborn.

Even after the Doctor leaves, the Seaborn never encroach on that place. Their kind doesn't even remember what the Abyssal Hunters looked like, but they know, at least, that they must never enter there. Forever.
One day, while wandering the deep sea, the Doctor comes across a half-human, half-Seaborn mutant. Its badly wounded body still had scraps of a missionary's uniform draped over it.

The Doctor suddenly recalls a missionary they'd met before, who'd said he was heading back to Iberia. Hoping this creature won't die alone and without dignity, the Doctor sends its remains back to the sea instead of leaving it in the pitch-black cave.

Even just recalling the laboratory's research sends a chill down your spine. Perhaps, in order to survive that era, someone who held the same status as the Doctor once did something unforgivable. Caerula Arbor, holding even that memory within itself, is still growing toward the planet's core even now.
5. Precious Everyday Life
The goal set at departure is fulfilled at the journey's end. That being, poised between Seaborn and human, finishes a delicious meal and prepares to greet one more tomorrow.
The everyday is the greatest kindness fate grants to any living thing. This ending quietly tells us that the ordinary is itself a joy.

blocks.cap: At a sunset shoreline, two people sit with a campfire between them. There is no longer a knight in the sea, and Mizuki too has returned to his own journey.

blocks.x: 6. An Age of Silence
A soundless wave rises high. The Knight who made the sea his enemy roars out in fury, but even that cry vanishes into the silence.
The catastrophe that destroyed Iberia, the Profound Silence, returns once more. And this time, it will never go away again.

blocks.cap: Beneath a yellow sky, a lone knight holding a spear stands face to face with a colossal beast risen from the sea. "The sea is the knight's enemy."

blocks.x: 7. The Price of Peace
The conflict surrounding the 'Firstborn' reaches a resolution. The one that was human has beaten the one that wasn't. The Profound Silence recedes, and the waves grow calm.
But as the price for that, Mizuki can no longer return to land. And beneath the waves, an undercurrent still stirs — as the 'Firstborn's' blood guides Skadi toward the Many.

blocks.cap: Mizuki turns his back on the red land and walks into the blue sea. The Profound Silence has lifted, but there is something he's left behind too.

blocks.x: 8. Stella Caerula
The heir of evolution rises up from Terra's waves. Piercing the clouds, it presses onward into an endless sea of stars.
Now there is no more hardship left in the world, only the joy of a new life. The age of humans has ended, and the age of the Seaborn is soon to come.

blocks.cap: In the middle of a sea where fluorescent jellyfish drift up into the sky, a single figure stands alone atop a seashell. This is the landscape of the 'perfect answer' that evolution finally reached.

blocks.x: 9. Who Am I
Four seas mean four answers. Mizuki sometimes remains human, sometimes becomes a Seaborn, and sometimes leads his kin soaring up into the starry sky.

This race pursues nothing but survival, and survival itself is hardship. So they evolve without end, believing there is no problem repetition cannot solve, no wall evolution cannot break down. When the time comes, they say, the answer appears on its own.
Mizuki's human light has not faded, even now. Since he accepted the Seaborn as a human, some believe he will surely find his way back from Seaborn to human again.
But Mizuki himself is neither human nor Seaborn. He's simply doing what he believes to be right.
The Many is not me, but I am the Many.

The wind blows past your shoulder, heading toward the far side of the sea. The sea of Integrated Strategies changes its course and order each time, stirring up its waves anew, and each time, the Doctor and Mizuki pack their things again and descend into the deep sea — searching for the answer to "who am I" in this sea, and in the next one after that.

rogue_3.tagline: The story of an expedition that sets out fully prepared for the far edge of Sami's icefield, only to come face to face with a prophecy sleeping beneath the snow. The pursuit of knowledge never ends, but misfortune has only just begun.

chars.name: Valarqvin
chars.desc: A Cyclops who sees prophecy and fate — the expedition's guide.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: The Rhodes Island leader overseeing restoration of the Stargate.

chars.name: Magallan
chars.desc: An expedition member who scouts the icefield by drone.

chars.name: Surtr
chars.desc: Sami-born, carrying memories of flame and war.

terms.name: Sami
terms.desc: A frozen land of the far north, where nature itself is both god and power.

terms.name: Tribe Tree
terms.desc: A giant tree that carries a tribe as it walks — home to the Samifolk.

terms.name: Foldartal
terms.desc: A tablet inscribed with nature's revelations, known as the Foldartal.

terms.name: Cyclops
terms.desc: A Sarkaz clan who see the future, rumored to bring misfortune.

terms.name: Stargate
terms.desc: A ring-shaped gate at the icefield's edge, leading elsewhere.

terms.name: Andskotarnir
terms.desc: A corruption that stains the snowfield black, consuming beast and human alike.

blocks.x: Integrated Strategies is a game about exploring a randomly-laid icefield over and over. So rather than a fixed plot, what forms the backbone of the story is the world laid across that snow, and the one prophecy that keeps recurring within it. And the stage's name is none other than the silver-frosted edge of Sami.

Supplies and personnel — the preparations are perfect. The expedition sets out from Sami's southernmost city and heads north. Once they cross Sami and the icefield beyond, the veiled unknown will soon be revealed.

And yet this journey is a little strange. Without knowing whether they're moving forward or just circling in place, the expedition keeps repeating the same course. Nature has not yet accepted them as friends.

blocks.cap: Pure white snow falls onto the lake, and a cold wind carries the boat along. It's a land where, wander long enough, you always lose track of time.

blocks.x: 1. From the Silver Lake to the Eternal Dust

The icefield unfolds in layers, from the snow-falling **Silver Lake** to the lightless **Silent Forest**. A curled-up Hornbeast invites visitors to rest, but a pitch-black shadow silently stains the snowfield.
Past the forest lies the **Dawn Tundra** — a cold that bites to the bone, trees growing ever sparser. The map is blank, and now, who is there left to trust?

Beyond that are the **Frozen Rock**, where ruthless warriors have raised towering walls, and the **Flawless Garden**, where every trace of life has vanished. Solid ice has sealed away countless lives from ancient times to now, blossoming into beautiful crystals.

At the very end lie the **Foreseen Sphere** and the **Eternal Dust**. An inescapable fate stands at the icefield's edge, visible to everyone in this moment. A single grain of dust is still too heavy — the expedition neither hopes nor regrets.

blocks.cap: Sturdy Sami has raised its mountains high. The explorers' gaze, turned north, is blocked by that multitude of peaks.

blocks.x: This snowfield has one line that nature keeps repeating. Even if you fall and become nourishment for this land, nature will never interfere — neither welcoming nor refusing.
2. The Tribe Tree and Nature's Code
People live on the icefield too. When the giant **Tribe Tree** settles down in the middle of a bog, Sami merchants gather along its trunk, roots, and shoreline to open a lowland market.

Branches move stalls on their own, wooden puppets carry goods back and forth between the bog and the flatland, and animals announce the news at the top of their lungs. It's all so bewildering that the expedition takes photos first, just so no one back home calls them liars later.
To the Sami, nature is power itself, and gods dwell within all things. The very first code, they say, was a cry that once echoed across the mountains and fields.

Follow the beast trails, and sometimes the one walking beside you turns out to be a Rock Hornbeast, not a fellow member. At the end of that trail, a pure white, colossal Hornbeast sleeps soundly in a flower bed, with a clear spring trickling gently beside it.

It's less that the beast lives here and more that the forest seems to exist for its rest. Offer it tribute, and it grants your wish; take a sip from the spring beside it without waking it, and the sorrow and pain in your heart are washed away.

The northland shaman games, where many tribes gather, are worth seeing too. Grotesque 'giant' war golems march out of the forest at the high priest's command, and you choose which champion to bet on. When Beast Hornfell knocks out Giant Moss in a single blow, the once-solemn arena erupts in cheers.

That revelation is carved onto the **Foldartal**. Called the Foldartal, this tablet forms when an ancient tree's bark splits open with a roar, surfacing in intricate patterns. A shaman reads those patterns to convey nature's will.

blocks.cap: The old tree's bark splits open and symbols surface. Linguistics can decipher the tablet, but no one knows where it truly came from.

blocks.x: The shaman shares one old prophecy: that Sami will one day manifest as mountains and icefields melted by flame, and that before all things awaken, their war will break out once more.
Among the expedition members is **Surtr**, Sami-born herself. When she brings an irresistible flame before the ancient tree, scorched Foldartals fall to the ground.
Flame, war, and my memories……
She watches the flame die out in the blizzard, takes out a notebook and writes something down, then crosses some of it out again.
3. Those Who See the Prophecy
At Sami's northernmost edge live the **Cyclops** — a Sarkaz clan who see the future and fate, trailed by rumors that they bring misfortune.
Ask a silent group walking north through the blizzard where they're headed, and they answer: toward the next thousand years.

Among that group, **Valarqvin** becomes the expedition's guide. She 'sees' things like a faint mist, a formless needle, a black key. Their meaning is unknown, but she draws a clear route on the map all the same.
In a cave she leads them to, there's an altar. A single flower blooms there, rootless yet impossibly sharp, and countless Cyclops watch it in silence.

blocks.cap: Ground without substance, a rootless flower, a key from beyond. It's an altar the Cyclops watch over in silence.

blocks.x: Reach out and you still can't touch the flower. Try to focus and dizziness sets in, until the flower finally takes root inside your own mind. Only once that coordinate is fixed can you finally 'take' the flower.
The result is the purpose, and the purpose is the cause.

When the mountains' shadows overlap and blur your sight, a woman dressed as a fortune-teller may even offer you a card to draw — odd, since neither the Sami nor the Cyclops are known to read the future by tarot.

Inside the crystal ball she holds out, only a black mountain range appears, as if it weren't your fate at all. She's wandering these mountains trying to recover an old companion swallowed by a great shadow, and by the time you part ways, you notice Originium crystals have grown thick across her own body too.
Don't fear fate, one fortune-teller says, and don't fear turning your back on it either. On the day you meet the prophecy, you finally realize it had always been waiting for you in the future.
4. What Stains the Snowfield Black
This journey isn't all mystery and wonder. At some point, black footprints begin spreading across the snowfield.
Before the countless animal carcasses buried beneath murky snow, the expedition's legs freeze in place on instinct. This threat has already spread hundreds of kilometers — perhaps farther still.
The Sami warriors seem to know what this is, but for some reason won't say a word about it. What did they go through, to make them so wary of every outsider?

This is **Andskotarnir** — corruption. Corrupted Sami catch arrows with their bare hands, and Originium Arts vanish without a trace before them. It's also the true face of the great shadow that has haunted the tribespeople's dreams for years.

blocks.cap: A river that never freezes. Ice crystals fall onto the back of your hand, and if you glance down without thinking, a starry sky is reflected in the churning water.

blocks.x: Reflected in the never-freezing river are eroded earth, vanished beasts, and your own body growing inward. Pull a drowning person out of the water, and even they can't remember whether they leapt in out of terror or were strangled by the river itself.

A Sami warrior known as 'Treescar' leaves their helmet behind in the camp and sets off into the snowfield alone. For a Sami warrior to leave their helmet behind means they've given up any thought of returning alive.

The funeral procession moves to a steady drumbeat, each person kissing the water's surface and praying for the protection of all things and the sun. Such healing rites are said to steady the heart and give courage to face evil spirits — but no one knows just what evil spirit they claim to have seen.

"We don't recognize this as Sami. This has only borrowed Sami's shape." So says a shaman who carries a withered branch with them. Nature is power, she says, and once you've gained power, standing against a threat is only natural.

Still, if you don't press forward, fear will catch up eventually, and the end of flight holds only emptiness. The old tree's attendant reveals the truth of this land as disturbed by Andskotarnir, and thankfully, the expedition's companions agree to see this long, nearly hopeless journey through to the end.
5. All Sorts of Guests on the Snowfield
It isn't all heavy stories, though. This icefield is also crawling with plenty of absurd guests.

Off away from the campfire, there's a Sami man drinking alone, talking to a tree. He asks the tree how it'd feel about befriending a stranger, and when it seems fine with that, he nods to himself, satisfied.

The tribal tales he tells don't quite add up somehow. A warrior said to have sacrificed himself turns out to be alive and well; the drink tastes off, and when you come to, you're curled up on the floor. This place has its own rules, and guests simply have to follow them.

In a cave that looks like a Mangler's nest, drunk Ursus folk throw a party and insist that since only Ursus people are here, this is naturally Ursus land. They hand over a vodka wrapper, claiming this 'official colonization permit' is all you need to get the nation's full backing.

A duck calling itself 'Duck Lord' causes no end of trouble too — gathering corrupted Sami for a ritual one moment, luring Columbian tycoons into danger the next. As it turns out, that 'Sinister Fortress' is actually a Rankwood film crew's shooting set.

An outdoor survival expert everyone thought had gone missing is overjoyed to receive a few coins, saying it's plenty to bike back to his warm apartment, and slips you some expedition data he calls Rhine Lab classified intel.

Bob's General Store, selling canned vegetables and beer, is so popular locally it can't keep stock. Even so, the owner has a quiet regret — that he shouldn't have sold this modern technology to the Sami people at all.

blocks.cap: A convoy of extravagant vehicles parked in the middle of the icefield. The culprit who lured people out even here is, of course, that duck.

blocks.x: There's also a courteous Sarkaz woman living alone in a small cabin on an icefield with not a soul in sight. But be rude to her, and the cabin splits into four rotten branches. As it turns out, she's an important figure in the Náchtcherer royal house, which is why she builds her home out of such things.

Watch out, too, for the crimson tent pitched on the silver-white icefield. Open the curtain and a play is in full swing — an actor aims a weapon at the audience, and dancers step down from the stage. Turn around, and the tent is suddenly ablaze, with no survivors. A troupe that's made its way even to remote Sami is best avoided.

Find a clearing that glows when stepped on, and after a long while, your radio picks up a signal again. The stripes on the ground light up brightly, and you hear the voice of a companion far off at the southern camp. But once the stripes lose their glow, the transmission cuts out at once.

In the middle of the icefield there's also the Hundred-League Garrison, where exotically dressed Kuranta soldiers are stationed. What looked like an empty encampment suddenly fills with soldiers the moment a horn sounds, and that vast camp stretches endlessly to the north — like the footprints a lone traveling knight left across the earth.
6. The Great Structure at the Icefield's Edge
At the icefield's far edge stands a ring-shaped **Stargate**. It was discovered years ago already.

Much has happened since. A so-called Black Hole Accord was struck to research and restore the structure, the Behemoth departed Sami, and Collapsal research sent technology leaping forward explosively. Reaching this northern land that changed everything has now become every explorer's dream.

Then one day, a stranger appears at the camp. Invisible to the other members, he takes the invitation you hand him, nods, and departs. On his back, something like Rhodes Island's logo and insignia flashes past.
The next moment, the expedition finds itself standing in the middle of Sargon's desert. The others fall into panic, but the Doctor knows this is a test. It's time to prove yourself.

Ask for help before the ring-shaped gate, and interesting things happen — a squad hauling camp-building materials might emerge, or the operator you need might simply walk out. But sometimes, an unfamiliar enemy that clearly didn't come from the icefield's edge bursts through instead. Where does this door really lead?

blocks.cap: Starlight glimmers in the garden of rootless flowers, and a deep abyss is revealed within the great ring. The door has already opened.

blocks.x: The gathered materials are handed over to **Kal'tsit** at camp, and restoration work officially begins. First the Collapsals are cleared out, then the spatial stabilizer once used against Crazelyseon is powered up.

Researchers from Columbia, casters from Leithanien, Sami shamans, Sarkaz well-versed in sorcery, and even the Elves' ancient civilizational knowledge all gather in one place. They stabilize the disordered flow of space, and at last every facility is restored.
The great structure powers on once more, waiting quietly for a visitor. And the one to take the first step inside is none other than the Doctor.
7. Five Branching Ends

Depending on what truth the icefield yields, this journey closes on five different ends. Some end in dialogue with the mountains, some in frozen blood and stillness, and others lead into a new journey beyond the desert.

**To Talk with the Mountains** — the end beyond 'Sami's Resolve.' The mountains are already conquered, and the explorer's footprints now mark the endless icefield. The cracked earth speaks with you, asking and promising in turn: that the pursuit of knowledge never ends, but misfortune has only just begun.
**Winterfall** — the end that comes from felling 'Treescar' Eikthyrnir. Fate draws near at the edge and lowers its own curtain, and the warrior's last drop of blood freezes before it can even fall.
In another branch, though, the northern fortress allows the expedition passage. Then the warrior climbs atop the high walls, watching over Sami's frontier day and night.

**Long into an Abyss** — the end beyond 'Crazelyseon of the Endless Toll.' You see a darkness too deep to cross, and feel the heavy weight of snow settling on your shoulders. And now, only stillness remains.
At the far edge of that stillness, the Cyclops shares everything they have seen with you. Now is the time, deep within the icefield, to reveal the prophecy in full.

blocks.cap: A darkness too deep to cross, snow piling layer upon layer onto your shoulders. And in the end, only stillness remains.

blocks.x: **A Ray of Light** — the hidden end. Wrong deductions, wrong blame, wrong sacrifices. And yet you still manage to find a path that lets those who come after reach this far. Still, the depth of fate remains, even now, unknowable.

**From Start to End** — the end that goes farthest of all. In Sargon's desert beyond the open door, the expedition endures endless yellow dust and war, surpassing 'the Scourge of the Land' Kharanduu Khagan and 'the Overlord of Ages' Lugalszargus.
And you return beneath another great structure, weathered by sandstorms, identical to the one on the icefield. This is the end of a long journey, and the beginning of a new one.

Following in your footsteps, explorers will press on into the vast land behind the structure. And someday, they'll come face to face with the truth. The ring-shaped door waits in the cold wind, for someone to open it once more.

Changing its order and scenery each time, the icefield welcomes the expedition once more. Those who set out fully prepared come face to face with a prophecy sleeping beneath the snow, and nature watches on still, neither welcoming nor refusing. The pursuit of knowledge never ends, but misfortune has only just begun.

rogue_4.tagline: Drawn in by the Revenants' voices, the Doctor takes up the pen beside the Sarkaz ancestors' Furnace, continuing an unfinished old tale. Imagination becomes history, and the story's ending splits six ways.

chars.name: Nymph
chars.desc: A Sarkaz storyteller who spins stories out of imagination.

chars.name: Tin Man
chars.desc: A detective who gathers scattered perceptions to reconstruct stories.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The leader of Rhodes Island, who rejected the Doombringer's power and set out on her own to save people.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: A doctor of Rhodes Island who established a shelter for the Infected.

terms.name: Kazdel
terms.desc: The Sarkaz homeland city, powered by the Soul Furnace.

terms.name: Soul Furnace
terms.desc: The heart that burns Revenants' souls to keep Kazdel running.

terms.name: Revenant
terms.desc: The ghosts of Sarkaz ancestors dwelling within the Furnace, craving ever more thrilling stories.

terms.name: Tikaz
terms.desc: The primordial ancestor of the Sarkaz — a hunter shaped by Originium.

terms.name: Anasa
terms.desc: A Sarkaz who attained enlightenment in the East — the path of the lotus and the Dharma.

terms.name: Sankta
terms.desc: A race bearing halos and wings.

blocks.x: Integrated Strategies is a game about exploring randomly generated paths again and again. So rather than a fixed plot, what forms the backbone of the story is the world laid along that path, and the one question that keeps recurring within it.
This time the stage is "Sarkaz's Furnaceside Fables." And the question that keeps recurring is this: can a story be rewritten? Can even an already-fixed ending be overturned by imagination?
The Revenants' voices pull the Doctor out of reality. One ritual, one exchange — and an imagining that transcends the earth soon begins.

blocks.cap: Atop a shattered Kazdel, a red-horned storyteller stands amid scattered sheets of paper. Off in the distance, a detective in a bowler hat watches her.

blocks.x: 1. The Storyteller by the Furnace
Sarkaz ancestors don't vanish completely even after death. They become **Revenants**, dwelling within Kazdel's Soul Furnace, endlessly craving stories.
By their side is a storyteller named **Nymph**. She tells the ancestors stories — true stories, adapted stories, and even wholly imagined ones.
But what happens when there are no more stories left to tell? The Revenants circle around Nymph, digging up even the thoughts she'd rather avoid and reworking them into new stories.
The Doctor is a guest drawn into that imagining. Walking here is less an act than a thought. When a thought dissolves, ripples spread across the surface, and the story's boundary widens once more.

The Doctor isn't alone. The detective **Tin Man**, met before at the old castle, shows up here too. He gathers scattered perceptions and weaves them into new stories, and even negotiates on the Doctor's behalf with rampaging Revenants.

The ancestors keep tumbling about, craving ever more thrilling experiences. Even when Tin Man helps trap them inside a story and turn them into beads for the backpack, they're still watching for their next chance at 'free expression.'

blocks.cap: Two young Sarkaz stand atop an open book. Far beyond the thorn thicket, a path leads on to a world of light where spires rise.

blocks.x: 2. Kazdel, the Heart
The stage is named **Kazdel** — the homeland the Sarkaz built at the cost of their lives. What keeps the city moving is the Soul Furnace, beating like a heart.
Throw a bone into the Furnace, and discontent, rage, calm, and shame all burst out at once from the tangled Revenants within. Those emotions become the very power that runs Kazdel.

blocks.cap: At the center of the crimson-black crystal, the Furnace's core burns like a sun. Here, the ancestors' emotions are converted into the city's power.

blocks.x: Through imagination alone, Kazdel is reborn from an empty shell with nothing but a furnace into a thriving city, filled out with details and history that differ, bit by bit, from reality.

So Kazdel's structure defies common sense. Roads stretch out according to intuition, and the scenery shifts to match the subconscious, so people inside the city often lose their way. The city simply watches those bewildered travelers with interest.
3. The Path Down Through Eight Zones
The story continues layer by layer through eight zones — from the Dawn of Soul Fusion, to the Root of Forged Iron, to the Dust-Laden Ruins.

Next comes the Fork of Plausibility. The Doctor crosses boundary after boundary, choosing one Kazdel after another, and every Kazdel accepts him. Ending, unification, stability — here they're all just splits on the way to a new chapter.

Crossing the boundary of the false and the real to reach the resplendent sky, the Doctor collapses before the truth. Its resistance is so overwhelming that fleeing from the truth is the only option left.
And then, the Aranya of Freedom. The precepts have already been passed down, and keeping them, it's said, leads to enlightenment. But can truth really be followed, or sought after at all?
Just as a lotus leaf falling into water does not harm its root and stem, do not cling.
The last is Eternal Respite. Every story stops here. When the light before you dims and the sound of the Furnace fades, the storyteller falls quietly silent too.

blocks.cap: An underground Kazdel stained red. The wreckage of a city that once soared into the sky and then fell lies scattered like blades.

blocks.x: 4. The Rule Where Imagination Becomes History
Here, the Doctor's choices become history exactly as they are. Depending on which thought is offered to the story, Kazdel splits into an Age of Hope, an Age of Resentment, or an Age of Wishes.

A scrawl from the Age of Hope reads like this: a Tikaz orphan who lost their parents to a Catastrophe was taken in as family by the neighbor, Uncle Pillain. I am, proudly, a descendant of the Ancients.
