The hatred of the Age of Resentment is the exact opposite. My little sister was bitten to death by a vampire, my father was dragged away — so even if there's repentance, it'll only come after the Sarkaz are extinct. Two ages, crossing paths like this on the very same earth.

A Lich mistakes the Sarkaz book of prophecy for her own fortune book. Since it's filled with nothing but the ruin of her homeland and the cruelty of her enemies, she wonders — if she writes in something good, might her misfortune finally end?
……And in the end, we all lived happily. The End!

blocks.cap: One page shows a burning ruin, the other a field in bloom. Within the same book, ruin and hope sit side by side.

blocks.x: 5. The Characters Wandering Through the Story

Kazdel bustles with all sorts of characters. At Scar's shop, chained above the lava, any mercenary can be hired as long as you have resources — and it later gets reborn as 'Scar International,' led by figures like Clip Cliff.
'Duck Lord,' a duck who considers himself the master of a commercial empire, stirs up the auction house and gets into a pride-fueled bidding war with the Doctor over an Originium pyramid.

There's even a tourist spot labeled 'the Lost City of Kazdel' over the fallen city. A mud-caked Sarkaz swindler sells rocks as royal treasure, and teases the Doctor by demanding three 'Crunches' as the price of admission.

There's the Burden Beast 'Carrot,' who drops a notebook stamped by the Snowy Mountain Post Office, and even the arthropod cousin 'Slugkaz,' who supposedly lives in waste inside a pipe. Each carries a small vignette of its own as they wander through this story.

Then there's a Cyclops who tears through a rift, and she's dead serious. She asks the Doctor to hand over the 'key that changes the future,' saying she means to erase from time the miserable future she has foreseen.

At a gallery, a painter draws and sells portraits of an old sovereign, and if the Doctor listens to the backstory of a 'Lost Treasure,' he becomes its new owner. This is how Kazdel runs — on the buying and selling of stories.
Among them, the 'Garden of the Departed' stands apart. It's a memorial park the two Doombringers built at the heart of Kazdel to honor friends who have passed on.

Guard captain Ascalon, the medical expert Touch who passed away from overwork, the merchant Fitzroy who moved Columbia through commerce, and the chronicler Erikson who lived out his full years — each has a monument standing quietly in a row.
And one monument carved with ten rings. The name engraved there is **Amiya**. Before the Doctor knows it, those ten rings are somehow already on his fingers.
For some reason, those rings don't seem to belong only to the Amiya standing before him. They're the Doombringer's, they're Rhodes Island's, and they're also that Cautus's, the one the Doctor knows.
6. The Ordeal of the Last Bloodline

The stories also retrace, here and there, the path the Sarkaz have walked. When only the last bloodline of the Doombringer clan remained, a black procession writhed across the land in search of a way to survive.

Following the sound of a Banshee's flute deep underground, a Lich offering up her own lifebox to let the tribe pass, or settling in Sami by following a Cyclops — whichever path is chosen, the dream of founding Kazdel still remains far off.

Later, the Sarkaz get hold of Nasti's designs and take to the sky entirely. On the night Kazdel first rises, the engineers burn their blueprints, sneering that reconciliation with other races will never come.

And then the War for the Homeland. When General Pillain draws in the Coalition of Four Nations, the new Doombringers and the Six Heroes drive back the invaders, and Theresis beheads the general herself. It's a history all too familiar to the Doctor — one already woven into his own story.
7. The First Hunter, the First King
At the very bottom of the story lies a primordial scene. An exiled hunter, dying in the Silver Mountains, sees a crimson pattern surface on a black box.

A pure white light rises up behind his head. The moment the pitch-black hunter first awakens, order arises and a paradise is founded. On the spot where he received his revelation, a sacred horn is later enshrined.

But the Doctor senses that the horn isn't the Sarkaz's own. It was the first Doombringer, called 'the Exile,' who exchanged the relic for him — and the hunter later dons a crown, becoming king of all life and souls.

blocks.cap: A statue of the king wearing a horned crown, holding spear and bow, bathed in light. It is the sanctuary of the sacred city the hunter finally reached.

blocks.x: In another primordial scene, a black beast and a golden being meet for the first time. As the years pass, the two races stop distinguishing themselves from one another, and it becomes common knowledge that everyone survives only by cooperating.

Tikaz hears a wish that the being before him not go hungry, and gives up its food. Sadly, later people would remember only that Tikaz grants wishes, and would pray to it endlessly — until it destroyed both Tikaz and themselves in the end.
How could the Sarkaz be Sankta? This question sinks to the bottom with its answer deferred, only to later shake one entire ending to its core.
8. An Ending Split Six Ways
Depending on how far the story is pushed and what ends up in hand, the Fables offer different endings — five completions, and one collapse.

Either way, the Doctor runs into trouble on the last page. The more perfectly he revives the imagined Kazdel, the more he becomes, to the two Doombringers, an anomaly absent from the record of 'civilization's continuity.' And they are duty-bound to eliminate that anomaly.
**The End?** — An unfinished story gets cut short by Fremont, the Lich's Master. Known as 'the answer to every thought,' he'd rather the Doctor lay bricks for the present than soothe the dead.
No matter how perfect the imagined Kazdel is, it's ultimately a fabricated story, he says. So the story should stop here, and the Doctor should return to the real earth and build a real city.

The moment you think the ancestors are sealed away, they may instead slip out through the Doctor's own story. Watching a pale-faced Fremont and an Ermengarde wiping sweat again and again, you can't help but feel uneasy about whether this ending is really the end.
**Twin Crown** — The former Doombringer dies, and the black crown passes to Theresa and Theresis. But the crown splits in two, floating above both their heads side by side.

blocks.cap: Two Doombringers reach out toward the black crown, split in two. It is an age unprecedented in Sarkaz history — a time when two rulers govern together.

blocks.x: Thanks to the twin kings, the land regains its peace, and wounds carried through two centuries finally heal. It is an age for humankind, and a dreamed-of homeland the Sarkaz are willing to share. The Doctor's bold hypothesis and imagination gave rise, in the end, to this very ending.
**City of Angels** — an ending guarded by the Holy Guard's Grand Knight, Buldrokasti. Here, the question that had sunk to the bottom finally bears fruit.
The Sarkaz are Sankta.

blocks.cap: A Sankta nun kneels before a holy statue. In her hand, appearing here in Kazdel, rests a clay figurine of a Sarkaz.

blocks.x: Beside Kazdel's Furnace lies an old guardian gun. There's a sense that if this gun fires, heaven and earth will turn upside down. The instant the trigger is pulled, the once-hazy sense of connection sharpens, and a pure white, sacred city unfolds before your eyes.

The Doctor is drawn so deeply into this strange tale that neither the Revenants' rage nor the intervention of the Lich's Master can pull him back out. When the gunshot finally rings out, the two words 'Sarkaz' and 'Sankta' vanish at once, and only the single, bright and sacred word 'Tikaz' spreads across the whole world.
**The Wisdom of Kashnyana** — an ending opened by Kolam, an incarnation of the Mahasattva. He is a Sarkaz who journeyed east, attained enlightenment, and became one of the Anasa.
Kolam shares countless stories with the Doctor. Today they enlightened one another, but tomorrow, he says, they will all enter 'Kachidiya' together — the Anasa's homeland, the Dharma-abiding realm.

blocks.cap: A Sarkaz bearing a halo walks quietly through a reed field lit by the setting sun. It is the tranquil shore finally reached after passing through the Age of Resentment.

blocks.x: The crystal shines, yet the water lies still. Karma has connected, the Dharma is fulfilled, and the gate to Kachidiya has opened.
**A Perfect Day** — an ending where Amiya stands at the core's final movement. The Revenants believe the Doombringer destroyed everything, but this story imagines a different answer.

A blind craving for an ending summons a being called 'Finality.' It emerges from the void wearing the guise of 'Amiya.' Despair, salvation, and stillness make up, respectively, its beginning, its turning point, and its end.

When every act of medicine, amid catastrophe and war, becomes an accomplice to hatred, Amiya seals suffering people one by one inside a 'cocoon.' She stops their time entirely, so that Originium can no longer take their lives either.
And Amiya alone remains within time, setting off to find an antidote that can cure the root of every illness. Until then, only absolute solitude and an endless, unending journey await her.

blocks.cap: Two silhouettes stand facing each other amid cocoons of light and scattering keys. The 'Eternal Key,' forged by Nymph out of an imagined Doombringer's power, stands against Finality.

blocks.x: If time is long enough, and if Amiya truly finds an answer that can save everything — then every living soul caught in stillness will someday be saved.
Whether it has ended or not, the suffering of every story will end on this day.
9. Missing — A Story That Collapses
There are also stories that never reach completion. Grab only a thread and chase the ending blindly, and all that's left is contradiction that doesn't add up.

By the time that's realized, it's already too late. The ground beneath Nymph's feet turns to chaos, and the threads holding the story together snap one by one. The very impatience for an ending is what breaks the skeleton that had been holding the story up.

blocks.cap: A void where stars and ancestors drift about like beads. In the storyteller's hand, one black story shatters into pieces.

blocks.x: "All suffering has vanished." It sounds kind at first, but it isn't salvation. Companions give no answer, the road ahead is nothing but emptiness, and even meaning itself is lost to me.
Only you remain. The story has collapsed. Thought. The three phrases cut off one after another, and the imagined earth scatters without a trace, leaving no ending behind at all.
Epilogue — Once More, From the Start

Out in the reality beyond the story, those who inherited the two Doombringers' dream are still building Kazdel. The Lich's Master, Fremont, and Ermengarde watch over the Doctor's imaginings from that bridge.

Sometimes a single 'Gate' opens and carries the Doctor to 'Rhodesdel,' a shelter for the Infected. There, Theresa and Kal'tsit, whom he meets, tell him that the connections between stories run far tighter than he thought.
Beyond the Gate, in Rhodesdel, Kal'tsit says this: if you want to, coming back here again won't be hard at all. Between one story and another, there's always a door left open for returning.
When the Revenants' voices call the Doctor once more, the fable by the Furnace begins again from the start — imagination becoming history, and that history becoming, once again, someone's story.

Can a story really be rewritten? Even after six branching endings and one collapse, Kazdel still waits for the last story yet untold — for that Eternal Respite, when the sound of the Furnace fades and the storyteller falls silent.

rogue_5.tagline: A story of a scenic tourist spot that turns into an altar for lulling a god back to sleep each night, where twelve Emissaries and humans repeat the same ritual every year around a sleeping old god. And the one who laid out that whole game, from the very start, was Wang.

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: An Emissary of Sui. A red-horned Behemoth. Loves mahjong.

chars.name: Ling
chars.desc: An Emissary of Sui. A Behemoth who delights in games of wine cups and coins.

chars.name: Shu
chars.desc: An Emissary of Sui. Cultivates wishes in the fields and reaps their karmic fruit.

chars.name: Chongyue
chars.desc: An Emissary of Sui. A Behemoth who champions martial valor.

chars.name: Leizi
chars.desc: A member of the Envoy Bureau. Calls down thunder with her sword to aid the Ascension rite.

chars.name: Raidian
chars.desc: An accompanying operator. Skilled at negotiating even with grotesqueries.

terms.name: Sui
terms.desc: An ancient, colossal Behemoth that has slept since time immemorial. Its mausoleum is its very body.

terms.name: the Garden
terms.desc: A tourist attraction that gathers Yan's finest scenery in one place. By night, it becomes an altar that holds Sui in check.

terms.name: Sui's Emissaries
terms.desc: Twelve Behemoth gods awakened from Sui's own thoughts.

terms.name: the Pacification Rite
terms.desc: A ceremony that puts Sui back to sleep every single year.

terms.name: the Four Generations
terms.desc: An organization that runs the Garden by day and watches over grotesqueries by night. Descendants of the Great Hunt.

terms.name: the Great Hunt
terms.desc: An age of high antiquity when humans and Behemoths joined forces to hunt gods.

terms.name: the Remnant
terms.desc: A world formed from Sui's lingering consciousness, centered on Sui's Mausoleum.

blocks.x: Integrated Strategies is a mode where you explore a stage that's freshly assembled each time you play. So rather than a fixed plot, the backbone of the story here is the setting itself and the one truth that keeps repeating on that stage. And the name of this particular stage is none other than 'the Garden.'

Near Baizhao, in a place blessed with fine mountains and clear water, stands an attraction that never runs short of visitors. But one day, this place clears out its guests and locks down under tight security.
At last, the Pacification Rite begins in earnest. With the Four Generations' approval, the Doctor and the operators step deep into this strange tourist spot.

blocks.cap: A great hall lined with ink-painted landscape screens. A figure with a long golden mane stands upon the water's reflection, waiting for guests.

blocks.x: 1. By Day a Tourist Spot, by Night an Altar

The Garden gathers all of Yan's finest scenery into one place — waterfall-draped foothills, treacherous cliffside paths, and a rock mountain built from bizarre, jagged stones. Every kind of scenic wonder is packed in.
Tourists burn incense and pray for luck, stamp their passports, and buy souvenirs. By day, the Garden is as peaceful as any ordinary amusement park.

Except the stamps are a little strange. Each time one is pressed, the character on it shifts to follow the flow of feng shui across the land — yet no one actually knows what that character means. Like a footnote whose original text has vanished.

blocks.cap: A tea-scented veranda overlooking a waterfall. By day, the Garden is no different from any other tourist attraction.

blocks.x: But once business closes, the Garden reveals an entirely different face. Staff who were tour guides, gardeners, and security guards become lantern-keepers and candle-keepers.

They watch over the uncanny, record any changes, and guide the **grotesqueries** wandering the Garden by night. Every one of these human staff actually belongs to an organization called the **Four Generations**.
The Four Generations' roots run very deep. The organization arose from survivors of an ancient war against a Behemoth, who passed their experience and skills down through later generations.
2. Those Who Light the Candles
Near the Garden's entrance stands a candlelit shrine. By day it's where tourists burn incense, but once business closes, it becomes the headquarters from which the Four Generations manage the Garden.
Beneath the light of candles gathered from every corner of Yan, the **Candlebearers** each light their own candle and recall their duty. They have come to carry out the Pacification Rite.

blocks.cap: The shrine after nightfall. Rows of red candles flicker behind the lattice doors. This is where the Four Generations' real work begins.

blocks.x: The Pacification Rite is a ceremony repeated every year. It means entering the Garden, establishing authority in Sui's name to subdue him, and soothing him through thought.
Through battle with Sui's Emissaries, human authority is driven through the Garden straight into Sui's vital point. The Candlebearer's duty is passed down generation after generation.
Not even fierce beasts can hunt the Fowlbeast of the sky, so how could a human possibly harm the master of the Garden? And yet, repeating this ritual carries its own meaning all the same.

blocks.cap: Beneath the shrine's eaves, a Candlebearer presses both hands together and looks up at the sky. Today, the rite begins once more.

blocks.x: But what exactly is being subdued? Beneath the Garden's mountains, waters, and buildings, something has lain sleeping since long, long ago.
3. Sui, the Sleeping Old God
**Sui** is an ancient, colossal Behemoth unchanged since time immemorial. His form is majestic, and Sui's Mausoleum, the deepest place in the Garden, is his very body.
Long ago, godlike beings walked this land, and humans joined forces with Behemoths to hunt them down. That was the age of the **Great Hunt**.

blocks.cap: A colossal Behemoth spreads its wings over a ruined battlefield. A memory of the Great Hunt, when humans no bigger than specks of dust advanced upon a god.

blocks.x: In those days, countless households handed over half their fortune to the court, sometimes all of it. Fathers and sons stood shoulder to shoulder, mothers and daughters lined up together, all trying to shake a being that seemed like a god.
Not a single soul from those countless households survived. The coins that had been their share were melted down together into the Martyrs' Memorial of Baizhao.

The fireworks launched into the sky from the Garden every year-end were originally a custom of mourning that grim, costly victory. Mourning turned into remembrance, and remembrance into celebration — only the meaning changed, never the custom itself.
Sui did not die — he sleeps. Even in that sleep, he refuses to let his body be trampled, and will not allow his memories and thoughts to be disturbed.

Every time Sui stirs toward waking, crystals of rage sprout like thorns, watching for a chance to break the human heart. The Garden is nothing less than a colossal apparatus built to prevent that awakening.

The **grotesqueries** wandering the Garden at night are objects that have taken on a spirit under Sui's influence. Stand beside a suppression pillar and the five senses fall away as consciousness expands — just like that ancient Behemoth looking down upon the earth from above the clouds.

Magil, a teacher at the academy, instructs these grotesqueries in how to live as people. He tells them that Sui's influence is merely the condition for becoming a grotesquerie — whether one actually becomes one is a matter of each individual's nature and choice.
4. When the Coin Falls to Earth, Heaven and Earth Shall Change
The Garden by night is full of stories steeped in human years. And every one of those stories begins, without exception, with a single **coin**.

The rock mountain built from Guwu stone is also called 'Old Man Stone' or 'the Stone God of Fortune.' A pagoda crowns its head and untold wealth wraps its waist; struck day after day, it never grows angry, and simply wishes good fortune upon everyone.

blocks.cap: Among mist-wrapped, jagged stones, coins spill down the mountainside and glitter. The Stone God of Fortune simply greets the coins as they come, and sends them on their way as they go.

blocks.x: Throw a coin, and the passage of time transforms it. A coin falls to the ground and becomes a shrine, becomes an academy, opens up a whole new world. Human years lie hidden in every single coin.

All across the Garden wander those who carry their own tragedy and turmoil. There is a pair of lovers trapped in a spinning bridal chamber split in two, who have lived over eighty years separated by a single wall. Once able to meet for only a single day every ten years, they now wish for nothing but to be together.

There is also the orchard of a farmer who, murdered by his landlord, now tills the field himself, and the amber of the Eastern Sea, which burns and weeps in a repeating four-hundred-year cycle. Each of them is bound fast by the years, lonely things with nowhere left to go but this Garden.

The little village of 'Innocence' inside a crystal ball is chillier still. Villagers who fled war and prayed for an eternal paradise now live trapped inside that orb for generations, by the grace of a 'god.' As long as they never wish to leave, the orb 'protects' them forever.

And then there's foolish laughter too — a duck calling itself 'Duck Lord' who won't stop hyping its 'Garden Paradise' business venture, a chain restaurant called 'Boss Bob's' supposedly opened by a Westerner, and even Jarak, who tries to solicit investment from the grotesqueries.
The Corridor of Greed makes you want more coins the moment you pick up just one. That endlessly swelling desire is exactly the meal this corridor enjoys most.
5. The Twelve Emissaries
The true masters of the Garden are **Sui's Emissaries** — twelve Behemoth gods awakened from Sui's own thoughts, each ruling over one domain.

Red-horned **Nian** is a gambler who stakes her life on a single round of mahjong. "We're one player short, hurry up! Fair warning, the stakes are pretty high — that's what makes it fun." If she loses, she sweeps the table clean with her tail.

**Ling**, who tips back wine cups like a waterfall, invites you to test your luck by tossing coins; **Shu**, who wraps around the fields, takes people's wishes as seeds and harvests them into every kind of fruit. **Chongyue** stands alongside them, urging on martial valor.

Among them, the master of the Garden is **Yi**. A skilled craftsman, he's also known as 'Master Yi,' who loses all resistance the moment a fine item is put in front of him. Show him something he likes, and he is never stingy in return.

blocks.cap: A crystal pagoda, a pearl gourd carved with a dragon, a scale weighted with coins. Rare treasures shaped by the Garden's master, Yi.

blocks.x: Yi's younger sibling **Zhi** is a merchant who's always bickering with his brother over how to split the profits. From a wandering young physician who heals wounds, to a taciturn law official, to a painter who lives only inside their own painting — each Emissary has a personality all their own.

The painter Emissary, who splashes ink and sweeps the brush, lives only within their own painting. They scowl at the Doctor, who intrudes into the painting like an unwelcome guest, but upon recognizing a token left behind for Yan's elders, they reluctantly see the matter through to its end.

The Doctor borrows the power of these Emissaries to paint Sui's form, stroke by stroke. Like the finishing touch that brings a painted dragon to life, each added stroke draws a little closer to the sleeping Sui's body and mind. To subdue a sleeping god, one must first render that god whole.

And among those twelve, there is one whose very name the years have erased. Like a shrine whose plaque cannot bear even a single carved character, this Emissary has had their entire existence wiped clean. Such an erasure is nothing a human hand could ever accomplish.
6. The Bridge to the Remnant

The Envoy Bureau masters a technique called Ascension. When a candle burns without flickering, consciousness can slip free of the body and enter the **Remnant** — a world formed from Sui's own lingering consciousness.

Within the Remnant remain the war camps of the Great Hunt, the last memories of humans and Behemoths who once fought shoulder to shoulder. Shake those memories, and Sui in the waking world might shake too.

**Ryua** of the Curiosity Workshop is an inventor who studies the Remnant and the coins alike. A petal-shaped shadow occasionally flickers behind her back — it is an afterimage of her own future self.

The Four Generations hold one unspoken rule: face your own illusion, and the future becomes fixed fact. So the shadow only signals for silence, straightens the collar of her past self, Ryua, and then scatters away.

Step across the border of the Remnant and it's easy to lose all sense of what is real and what is not. The vivid sensation that the mausoleum is Sui's very flesh, the ancient war camp stretching for a hundred li, and the shimmering illusion of a Behemoth far in the distance — all of it leaves a person adrift.

In that final memory within the Remnant, a single scout keeps looking back. Where he looks, Sui is there, his shadow swallowing the camp and those about to set out. The scout hurries to break free of that shadow.

Every exploration the Doctor has carried out in the Garden turns into ink and gathers onto a single scroll. This accumulated trace is enough to reveal the secrets of the Garden and Sui's Mausoleum — and enough, further still, to awaken an unfinished relic.

blocks.cap: An ink-stained hand unrolls a scroll bearing not a single character. The instant a drop of ink spreads across it, something is born where there had been nothing at all.

blocks.x: 7. Wang's Go Board
Somewhere in the Garden sits a game of Go that has only just begun — a match left behind by Wang, one whose depths even Ryua, for all her long study, could never fully grasp.

**Wang** is also one of Sui's Emissaries. A being who sees in every direction, he holds black and white, right and wrong, all within a single hand-span of board. And the opponent he has chosen is none other than the Doctor.

blocks.cap: A Go board floats among mist-shrouded mountain peaks. The match Wang set in motion has, in truth, been underway since long, long ago.

blocks.x: Is the Doctor meant to play on a board already rigged in advance? The moment the Doctor flips the board without hesitation, it spins in midair and transforms into a printing plate of movable type, with the character for 'Sui' set right at its center.

A thought that never reaches a solid character becomes chaos; an ideal never carried into action is nothing but empty fantasy. With the Go board as heaven and the printing plate as earth, an entrance takes shape just outside the mausoleum's gate.

blocks.cap: A single character carved into empty air by a giant brushstroke. A character is the medium through which everything born of this land exists — humans and Sui alike.

blocks.x: Sui's awakening carries countless variables in tow, so Wang had to prepare a way to meet every one of those changes. Having already placed humans as pieces on his board, he bears the duty of preparing the surest possible plan for their sake.
It was Wang who devised the scheme, but it is humans who see it through. Now, what remains is up to you.
8. Five Endings — the Close of the Pacification Rite
Depending on which trials the Doctor passes through and which truths they touch, this recurring ritual closes with one of five different endings.

**Pacification by the Rites (依法鎭武)** — the most orthodox ending. It closes with the ancient guardian beasts, the Monkey Guardian and the Rat Guardian, subdued, and the rite completed exactly as tradition demands.

The Garden's Pacification Rite goes on year after year, and the Candlebearer's duty passes down generation after generation. This finely honed blade, just like the Garden itself, holds Sui firmly in check. Nothing changes at all, and the world simply lives out one more day.
**A Scroll's Lingering Trace (掌卷遺痕)** — with one missing from the twelve, things can never be complete. This ending follows the trail left by that erased Emissary.
What was seen at the shrine may have vanished, but one thing is certain — she herself has not vanished yet. Through a scroll steeped in ink, someone may yet reveal that erased past again, someday.
The Emissary who lost her name remains, then, not as a name but as a trace — like a set of twelve cups that is only complete when exactly one of them stands empty.
**The Mystery of Black and White (黑白玄妙)** — it was humans who drew the Pacification Rite into Sui's Mausoleum, but in the end, this ending is one that Wang led them toward.

Just as though he were playing Go. Black and white, right and wrong, all tangled together on a single board. Just when will this vast match he has set in motion ever end? The Doctor, it turns out, had already stepped inside Wang's board.

Sui has no interest whatsoever in the clarity of right and wrong. No matter how cleverly the world is rearranged, only by winning Sui's favor can one set foot in that mausoleum. And Wang had already calculated even that favor as one more move on the board.

**Something from Nothing (無中生有)** — an ending in which something is born where nothing was before. The unfinished relic that the Garden's master, Yi, had long kept watch over, is at last completed by human hands.

The Doctor witnessed a fragment of the Great Hunt within the Remnant, and a faint trace of ink spreads across a scroll bearing not a single character. With this, characters are formed, books are written, and learning itself is established. And so, both grotesqueries and people alike can, at last, become truly human.
The thin barrier that had stood between reality and Sui's Remnant shatters, in that very instant.

blocks.cap: Amid shards of light, god, humans, and the Emissaries stand face to face. The rite that has repeated so many times finally moves toward its last act.

blocks.x: **No Retracting the Play (落張不入)** — the hidden, true ending. This is the one where a human carries an Emissary's future out beyond Sui's Mausoleum.

At that, Wang gives his all, becoming able to shatter every one of Sui's delusions within a finite infinity. Under Wang's scheme, the Doctor cleaves through Sui's rebirth, extinguishing even the flames of Yan and the illusions of the Emissaries.

A ritual that had only ever put a sleeping old god back to sleep advances, for the first time, all the way to ending that god for good. Of course, even that was likely just one more stone Wang had laid down long, long ago.

Whichever ending the ritual closes with, the Garden opens its doors again each time, shuffling its zones and its order anew. Someone who entered as a daytime tourist becomes, before they know it, a nighttime Candlebearer, and the sleeping god waits once more beneath the mausoleum for the moment of awakening.

The Garden, blessed with fine mountains and clear water, still welcomes tourists by day and lights its candles by night. The twelve Emissaries each hold their guests with their own games, and the sleeping Sui waits beneath the mausoleum only for the moment he wakes. And with both the opening and closing moves still left to Wang, just when this vast match will end is put off to the next Pacification Rite, and the one after that, and the one after that.

act19mini.tagline: The tale of one who stole a martial arts manual just to leave — only to learn he was no man at all, but an object shaped by someone's longing. A quiet garden's record of how you must let go before you can truly receive.

chars.name: Yun Qingping
chars.desc: A scribe who records the grandmaster's martial arts at his side. He himself, though, is a scholar who never learned a lick of martial arts.

chars.name: Lin Qingyan
chars.desc: Heir to the Thunder Arts of the Celestial Master's Manor. She has come to Mangshan Village in search of the missing Yun Qingping.

chars.name: Zuo Le
chars.desc: A young official of the Sishi Bureau. He calls Lin Qingyan 'auntie' and sent her a letter of request.

chars.name: Waai Fu
chars.desc: A girl versed in martial arts. She is chasing after her father, who vanished without warning.

chars.name: Lee
chars.desc: A seasoned tracker who set out with Waai Fu to find someone.

chars.name: Hu Jun
chars.desc: A waiter at the teahouse in the south of the village. He longs to leave this place.

chars.name: Teacher Bo
chars.desc: An old teacher who instructs the children of Mangshan Village in their letters.

chars.name: Mo Yi
chars.desc: A clever girl who wanders the jianghu. She stirs up chaos using the Martial Codex as bait.

terms.name: Mangshan Village
terms.desc: A remote village near Baizhao. A strange place found on no map, where not even a communications signal reaches.

terms.name: Changgui
terms.desc: A conscious being born when a person's obsession lodges itself in an object. It vanishes if the object breaks or the obsession fades.

terms.name: Sui's Mausoleum
terms.desc: Where the sealed Behemoth 'Sui' slumbers. The power leaking out turns objects into Changgui.

terms.name: The Martial Codex
terms.desc: A martial arts manual left behind by a grandmaster. Rumor says it holds the secret to turning a Changgui into a human.

terms.name: Ji Garden
terms.desc: A garden built atop Sui's Mausoleum. It belongs to the collector 'Yi.'

blocks.x: On the frontier of Yan lies a place called **Mangshan Village**. Though not far from Baizhao, it appears on no map, and the moment you step inside, not even a signal will reach you — an eerily hushed little village.
In that village's little teahouse, though no customer had come for the longest time, the waiter kept scrubbing the floor until it shone. Just who could he be waiting for this badly?
At last a customer walked in. Not to drink tea — but to kill someone.
A teahouse that sells neither tea nor wine — but poison, apparently

The two robbers who came were the **Lu brothers**, once known as the 'Three Storm Bandits.' The third had already died; what the surviving eldest and second brother were after was the peerless manual, the **Martial Codex**, held by a certain **clever girl**.
The girl pretended to hand over the manual obligingly, but the price the brothers named was the girl's own life. Just then, all three of them keeled over — the tea had been poisoned.
The one who poisoned it was the waiter, **Hu Jun**. A captive guest, the scrawny scholar **Yun Qingping**, tried to stop the killing at least, but Hu Jun tied him up too.

Hu Jun says he simply wants to leave this village, and that is why he needs the manual. In a village you can loop in 2,900 paces, he had been worn down by scenery that never changed no matter how many years passed.
Meanwhile, the Celestial Master **Lin Qingyan**, who had just entered the village, carried a letter from **Zuo Le**, a nephew of sorts. It was a plea to find her missing friend Yun Qingping.
Something about the village was off. An old man who pulls a freshly baked flatbread from his robes, and a girl named **Xiaoxu** who cannot tell time and so counts numbers all the way to 9,000.

Xiaoxu was on her way to meet Hu Jun. But she had lost the letter she had been asked to deliver, and failed to keep his secret too. Hu Jun said that if things had gone well, he had meant to take Xiaoxu and leave together……
"Farewell, Xiaoxu." After those few words, all that remained in that spot was Xiaoxu's umbrella, left forlorn.
When Lin Qingyan followed the clues to the teahouse, a smear of paint on the front of Hu Jun's robe caught her eye. It was the very color she had seen on Xiaoxu's umbrella.
His identity exposed, Hu Jun fled to the second floor, where some swordsman stood holding the manual. And a moment later, Hu Jun tumbled out the window.
What was left on the ground was no corpse — only a teapot smashed to pieces.
The schoolmaster who asked her to steal the dead waiter's 'remains'
Over the teapot shards, the village's **Teacher Bo** steps forward. This old teacher, who instructs the children in their letters, insists he must have Hu Jun's 'remains' back.
Lin Qingyan goes to him and hears the truth: a great many of this village's people are in fact **Changgui** — beings that gained consciousness when a person's obsession lodged in an object.

Nearby is **Sui's Mausoleum**, where the sealed Behemoth 'Sui' slumbers. Even after a thousand years sealed away, the power leaking out was more than enough to turn objects like teapots, fans, and pebbles into Changgui.
Teacher Bo was once a Behemoth scholar. Pitying the Changgui who would perish for causing a scene, he founded the **Academy of Objects** to teach them benevolence, righteousness, and propriety.
Hu Jun was the Changgui dwelling in the teapot of a tea farmer who set out across the southern sea in search of fine tea and never came back. That is why he always longed to leave the village.
But a few days ago, someone spread a letter carrying a rumor of a **Martial Codex** that held 'the secret to turning a Changgui into a human,' throwing the village's hearts into turmoil.
The man who spent a whole lifetime coming to buy a single pebble
Long, long ago, a **gentle man** asked a child by a river to sell him one pretty stone. He would pay whatever price the child named. The child refused.
From that day on, the man kept returning — as the child grew into a youth and then an old man — sometimes after ten days, sometimes after three or five years. Yet not once did he take it by force.
That child is now **Elder Man**, lying on his sickbed, staking half his fortune on a Celestial Master to find the stone he had lost.

Lin Qingyan quietly reveals that the thief's true aim was not the stone but the inheritance in the storehouse, and that the culprit was the elder's second son. And the stone itself, as it turned out, had become a Changgui by the riverside.

blocks.cap: The stone thought lost at last returns to Elder Man's hands.

blocks.x: At the elder's side, that gentle man had come to visit once more. Asked why he kept coming when he went home empty-handed every time, the man said only that he simply loved this stone, and loved the wind and the people along the road here.
To the elder, who now offers to give him the stone, the man answers that they should let the stone choose for itself — and that it is all right, now, to finally let go.
The elder closed his eyes in peace, and the stone by the river, too, slipped quietly down into the water.
The man who, for a hundred years, has tried to win an unwinnable game of go
On the hill behind, a boy named **Lanke** had been locked in a single game of go for over a hundred years. A certain immortal had promised, "Win this game and I will grant your wish."

His wish was to be reunited with his dead lover, **Baijin**. The trouble was that this game could be neither won nor lost — a draw, a triple ko. The more he obsessed over winning, the more hopelessly tangled it became.
Yun Qingping, climbing the hill, took a look at the board. Then he abandoned the decisive point and placed a single stone somewhere unexpected, breaking a hundred years of deadlock in one move.
First you let go, then you look, and only at the last do you gain.
The immortal kept his promise. Lanke reunited with Baijin and departed without regret — for Lanke, in truth, had died long ago as well.

But the very Baijin who had climbed the hill alongside Yun Qingping was…… the joss paper, soaked in Lanke's tears and longing, that had been laid before Baijin's cenotaph — that paper had become a Changgui.

The 'Baijin' whose wish had been fulfilled left a plea to be sure to meet again in the next life, and returned to being a bundle of joss paper. On that night of disaster 120 years ago, Lanke was swept away while saving others, and Baijin drew his last breath pulling him free.
The lost sword was the girl who came to him every single year
The swordsman **Zhengxiu**, bynamed the 'Soul-Severing Blade,' lost his sword in this village seven years ago, and taking it as a warning from Heaven, means to wash his hands of the jianghu.
But ever since, every autumn, a **woman bearing a sword** comes to pester him to teach her swordplay. Over seven years of such meetings, the girl grew astonishingly strong on her own.

That very girl was, in fact, Zhengxiu's lost sword turned Changgui. Having only ever watched the jianghu alongside her master, never walking it on her own feet, she wanted to live the jianghu under her own name.
But a Changgui cannot cross the invisible barrier that wraps around the village. And so that girl had tried to seize the Martial Codex to break through the barrier.
Before Lin Qingyan, who says a killer must be punished, the swordsman shields her. Yet the girl was already slipping into the grip of a savage Changgui frenzy.

blocks.cap: At the Celestial Master's command the sky darkens, and thunder magic falls upon the sword.

blocks.x: As the thunder magic struck, the girl vanished, and all that was left in her place was a single plainly bound book — the 《Martial Codex》.

The swordsman, his sword lost, resolves to turn himself in. And before him appears a woman who remembers nothing, saying she "wants to go forward." The swordsman hesitates a moment, then asks if she will not come along with him.
To become the greatest under heaven, he went so far as to summon a 'god'
Meanwhile, there was a martial artist who had broken into Yumen, stolen the Martial Codex, and vanished — **Waai Fu**'s father, the man people called 'the Great Hero,' **Waai Tin Pui**.
His daughter Waai Fu and the seasoned tracker **Lee** give chase. Waai Fu simply could not understand why her father, always so honest in his martial arts, had come to this.

At the center of the uproar was a clever girl named **Mo Yi**. Using the Martial Codex as bait, she had summoned the people of the jianghu and the Changgui to the mountaintop, staging a 'feast of heroes' to make them fight one another.

Mo Yi had Yun Qingping appraise the manual's authenticity. But Yun Qingping told the truth before everyone: this book holds no mysterious power at all, it can be printed and handed out endlessly, so there is nothing to fight over.
As the crowd turned its back on her, Mo Yi played her last card. Using the power dwelling in the Martial Codex, she summoned the avatar of the sealed Behemoth 'Sui.'

blocks.cap: The colossal avatar of 'Sui,' projected by the Martial Codex, looms over the summit of Mangshan.

blocks.x: Before that phantom, against which even thunder magic passed as if striking empty air, one man stepped forward — Waai Tin Pui, gripped by the obsession to once more defeat the grandmaster he had dueled half a year before: that very 'Sui.'

blocks.cap: Waai Tin Pui advances alone toward 'Sui,' one step at a time.

blocks.x: Some thirty exchanges. Driven to the very edge of the cliff, he would not retreat. Then his daughter's cry brought him to a halt, and Waai Tin Pui reclaimed what he had forgotten — a forty-year promise, a daughter to protect, and his own name.
The only one who can contend with me is myself. If there is no road ahead, then I must look back.
With a final blow he erased 'Sui,' and with that he fell down the cliff. Zuo Le hurriedly ordered a search below the mountain.
This village was something someone had built for them

Mo Yi is caught in the end, but there was a reason behind all this uproar. She had set the whole scheme in motion to find a certain object that could stop a war — by meeting 'Yi,' the master of **Ji Garden**.
Ji Garden is a garden built directly atop Sui's Mausoleum. Its keeper, **Liang**, tells a story from long ago.

Once, Liang was ordered to dispose of every Changgui wandering near the mausoleum. But upon learning that they held emotions and longings just like people, instead of disposing of them he built a village for them atop the ruins.
He had made up his mind after seeing a returning villager who insisted on coming back to till the fields again even though his hometown had been razed. Because a hometown, even ruined, is still home.
In that village was also the vase Changgui that Lanke and Baijin had shaped together as the first object for their new home. For a hundred years it has been waiting for the two of them to return.
Teacher, I am waiting for someone.
And so **Mangshan Village** was born — a place where things shaped by obsession wait and long, living side by side with people.

Do you remember that teahouse waiter who scrubbed the floor though no customer came? In truth, the whole of this village was that teahouse. Sweeping quietly again today, for someone who will come one day.
Liang, sweep the garden clean and make ready to welcome our guests.

act18mini.tagline: A story where everyone heads home to end the war, only for that journey home to become the beginning of yet another war — victory was a cold sunrise pouring down like blood over a mountain of corpses.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: Leader of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Reed
chars.desc: A Draco who leads a band of Tarans.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: A titled dignitary of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Talulah
chars.desc: The Draco who became Reunion's banner.

chars.name: Nine
chars.desc: An Infected who leads the new Reunion.

chars.name: Nowell
chars.desc: A man who entered blockaded Londinium in search of something.

chars.name: Entelechia
chars.desc: A vampire of 'Rose Riverside' who reads the blood of the dead.

chars.name: Hoederer
chars.desc: A Sarkaz who leads the Sarkaz withdrawal.

chars.name: Ines
chars.desc: A Sarkaz who commands shadows.

chars.name: Wiš'adel
chars.desc: A foul-mouthed Sarkaz who broadcasts in the name of 'Babel'.

chars.name: Delphine
chars.desc: A member of House Windermere in Victoria's Paragon unit.

chars.name: Paprika
chars.desc: A Sarkaz girl trying to return home.

chars.name: Vina
chars.desc: Victoria's returned monarch.

chars.name: Margaret
chars.desc: A knight who protects the wounded on the front line.

chars.name: Nightingale
chars.desc: A Sarkaz medic who soothes pain.

terms.name: Tara
terms.desc: The homeland of the Draco. The motherland of the sisters Reed and Eblana.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An Infected organization that began in Chernobog. It is now seeking a new meaning.

terms.name: Rose Riverside
terms.desc: An organization of vampire 'Cups' who handle information through blood.

terms.name: Nachzehrer
terms.desc: Sarkaz who wield decay. Their king is Netzsalem.

terms.name: Amnamn
terms.desc: A Sarkaz crown power that summons catastrophe clouds.

blocks.x: This is the tail end of the war, where over Victoria's capital Londinium the Grand Dukes of Victoria and the Sarkaz army of Kazdel faced off across a single wall.
Everyone thought that once the war ended, they would all go home. But for some, that journey home was another beginning.

Some crossed these ruins to give orders, some to atone for their sins, some to meet the person they had sought their whole lives — accompanied by a certain voice that vowed to **end everything in fire, in the end**.
We, feebler than a single mote of dust, gave an order to a Grand Duke
**Reed** is the small leader of the Tarans who have followed her all the way from the Scáthana fields. Yet at the Londinium battlefield, she says she will leave them all at the camp and go alone.
Even though the people who came saying "We came to fight" feel hurt by it. Reed knows that her own strength is feebler than a mote of dust. Still, there was someone she had to face. Her sister.

Where Reed headed was the Duke of Wellington's high-speed warship, the 'Gastrell.' But instead of her sister, the **Duke of Wellington** came out, treating Reed — who spoke of peace — as a mere canvasser, and cutting her off with "On the battlefield, a plea means nothing."
So Reed set aside her plea and, for the first time in her life, gave an order.
In the name of Tara, I command you. Save the Victorian citizens within the city.

The Duke obeys without resistance. He says the reason he had stayed outside Londinium all this time was not because of Reed's sister **Eblana** — the 'leader' of Dublin — but because Reed had shown the will to give a command.

blocks.cap: Eblana standing at the prow of the 'Gastrell.' From the tip of her spear, purple flames of death spread toward the walls of Londinium.

blocks.x: While Eblana's purple flames swallow the wall and clash with the Nachzehrer King **Netzsalem**, Reed turns her back on that grand 'performance' and returns to the people waiting for her. "Let's go home," she says.
To atone for her sins — and of all places, back to that pure-white snowfield
Meanwhile, in the back alleys of Londinium, there was an Infected unit sheltering Sarkaz with nowhere to go. It was the new **Reunion** led by **Nine**.

Nine wants to overturn the injustice before her eyes right now, while **Ch'en**, the operator who came to keep watch over them, clashes with her, saying "This is Victoria, so use a safer method." The two had different ideas of what 'fairness' meant.
The one who had long kept silent between them was **Talulah**. To the Infected who don't know the truth, she is a Draco revered as a hero.

blocks.cap: Talulah's blade blazes and traces a mark on the ground. It is the brand of Reunion, a symbol of a dark past.

blocks.x: Talulah rarely opens her mouth, but she sets their destination. Ursus. She says she will return to that snowfield where she committed her sins and face true judgment.
I will throw this body into the true flame and let it burn away — until the cruel snowfield melts.
She tells them to witness Reunion's end, and the something new that will be born from its corpse. Ch'en decides to accompany them as a watcher, and the three head for Ursus.
The person he had sought his whole life — he lost her just before they could meet

There was a man who entered blockaded Londinium to search for someone. It was **Nowell**. He was searching for the being who had made his body undying — the legendary 'Lord Protector' said to grant wishes.

While covering the evacuating citizens, Nowell meets the vigilante leader **Clovisia**. But then a cave-in happens. Clovisia, holding the red-haired child Olli in her arms to the very last moment, falls into the earth.

Nowell digs through the rubble and rescues Olli, but from within the darkness that voice echoes. Speak your wish, it says, and I will grant you a reward. Nowell refuses. Getting the child out comes first, he says.

Only after coming outside does Nowell learn the truth. That Clovisia had already breathed her last in the ruins. And that the being he had devoted his whole life to finding... was the very Clovisia he had just lost.
From that moment on, Nowell's wounds no longer healed. Though his body had once been undying.
Even as he pours out blood and falls to his knees, instead of tending to his own wounds Nowell says this: right now, he must save more people.
From the blood of the dead, she read a final farewell
A guest arrives after a long while at the Rhodes Island safe point that Kal'tsit guards. **Entelechia** — a vampire of 'Rose Riverside' who reads the blood of the dead to draw out their memories.

In exchange for opening a passage in and out of the city, she wants clues about her long-lost sister 'Noble.' And with her companion 'Brainy,' who moves alongside her, she watches over her the old way, across the radio.
But when she returned after clearing the command center, 'Brainy' was lying in a pool of blood, clutching the receiver. Someone had preserved at least her final dignity.

blocks.cap: Placing her hand on the blood of the departed 'Brainy,' Entelechia shares a final conversation with a consciousness that has not yet scattered.

blocks.x: As the blood cools and the memories fade, something hard touches her fingertips. Entelechia's own blood-colored crystal — the starting point and the vow of Rose Riverside. It was something the vanished Noble had returned to her.

Perhaps Noble had come not because of this war, but simply to say goodbye. Entelechia entrusts 'Brainy's' petal to Kal'tsit to send it to her homeland Kazdel, and departs once more into the river of blood in search of her sisters.
The god of war chose to die by his own hand, within the war he himself had raised
After the wall was breached, the battlefield moved to the western hall of the Sacred King's Palace. For Netzsalem had turned the entire plaza into a swamp of death with a thick fog of decay.

Within the corroding fog, everyone endures in their own way. **Margaret** and **Nightingale** save even one more person to cut off decay's nourishment, and **Vina** — Victoria's returned monarch — leads the people.

On the Sarkaz side too, **Hoederer**, **Ines**, and **Wiš'adel** seize the command center in the name of 'Babel,' and **Delphine** of the Paragon unit clears the way, saying "These Sarkaz have the same goal as us."
And Netzsalem, it turns out, had not come to fight.
It matters not. This war — the one that raised me, and the one I raised — has already ended.

He says the purpose of the war repeated over a thousand years has been fulfilled, and for the first time the Sarkaz hold their own destiny in their hands. For the lingering echo that remains — the war itself, now without even a purpose — he offers himself up to death.
What rose where the fog cleared was not victory, but a cold sunrise pouring down like blood over the mountain of corpses.
Over the road home, a catastrophe never seen before rose into the sky

blocks.cap: The shadow of the colossal skeletal vessel 'Life Spine' falls over the column of withdrawing Sarkaz.

blocks.x: With the war over, the Sarkaz begin a large-scale withdrawal toward Kazdel, taking even the elderly, children, and wounded. Hoederer knows that the moment this column looks weak it will be torn apart, so he says they must show strength.

That means was terrifying. The Sarkaz crown power '**Amnamn**' raised catastrophe clouds over the capitals of every nation. Zwillingstürme, Laterano, Montelupe... all of Terra looked up at that shadow. It was both a threat and a demonstration.
But before the fleet that came to stop that catastrophe-bearing column, a small Cautus girl steps forward. **Amiya**, the leader of Rhodes Island.

blocks.cap: Amiya draws a black line and raises a barrier that splits the earth. Above the girl's head floated a black crown.

blocks.x: It was the moment Amiya, who inherited the black crown from Theresa, revealed her true identity for the first time. Even as the fear and madness of the Sarkaz surged into her mind, she held the barrier.
The cycle of resentment has come to its time to end.
One fleet finally turns away... and at that moment Amiya hears a voice, strangely vivid.
A revelation not yet unveiled

blocks.cap: Laterano's Pope, Evangelista XI, who felt the trembling of the Halo.

blocks.x: It was Laterano's **Pope Evangelista XI**. Sensing that a being resembling himself had made a choice of her own, he poses his first question to Amiya. "Who are you?"
The two speak of a long journey. Letting go of resentment will take a very long time — but what if Terra does not have that much time left?

blocks.cap: Someone arrives too late, within a collapsing tower, before a solitary rhombus floating in a golden sea and sky.

blocks.x: At the same time, a person follows certain footsteps into a frozen void. But the one he sought had already departed to a place beyond his reach. He had to accept that he was too late.

The voice that had vowed to end everything in fire vanished along with the battlefield. But catastrophe clouds trailed above the column heading home, and the Pope said that a crisis unlike any faced before had already arrived.
It has drawn near, right before our eyes.
In the place where one war had barely died down into ash, the next ember — one no one had yet given a name — was just beginning to flare up.

act17mini.tagline: A tale of chasing after fallen sparks — and running into all sorts of city folk, and ancestors who have burned for 200 years, along the way. The kindest Diyel of all asks: is there really no road to a better Kazdel?

chars.name: Nymph
chars.desc: Said to be the kindest and warmest Diyel in all of Kazdel. Recently became a district warden.

chars.name: Poul
chars.desc: The 'Advisor' who speaks for the people of the slums. Easygoing on the outside, but a timid Sarkaz youth within.

chars.name: Ermengarde
chars.desc: Nymph's Lich friend, 'Ermie'. Run ragged with chores under a demanding mentor.

chars.name: Crowny
chars.desc: A Sarkaz designer who makes clothes and souvenirs from scrap. Always elegantly dressed.

chars.name: Mudrock
chars.desc: The one who led Infected from outside the city back into Kazdel. A member of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Kalaisha
chars.desc: Nymph's neighbor. A gentle soul who serves herbal tea and strawberry cookies.

chars.name: Mostima
chars.desc: A Sankta courier from Penguin Logistics.

chars.name: Fiammetta
chars.desc: A Sankta keeping watch over Mostima.

terms.name: Soul Furnace
terms.desc: A colossal furnace that has burned Revenants for 200 years to supply Kazdel with energy. Also called the Great Furnace.

terms.name: Revenant
terms.desc: The souls of ancestors burning within the furnace. When they dwell in a shard, they stir up the emotions and senses around them.

terms.name: Diyel
terms.desc: A branch of the Sarkaz. They use Arts to read emotions and cast suggestions. Nymph is a Diyel.

terms.name: Lich
terms.desc: Sarkaz who guard the halls of knowledge. They craft objects with their formidable Arts. Ermie and her mentor are Lich.

terms.name: Requiem Rite
terms.desc: The Lich's ritual to summon the Revenants from within the furnace and, at long last, set them free.

blocks.x: Kazdel is the homeland of the Sarkaz. For 200 years now, this city has run on the souls of ancestors burning within a furnace — the Revenants. Their heat travels through pipes to the citizens all across the city.
But one night, the Lich hold a grand ritual to summon the Revenants from the furnace at last and set them free — to finally let the ancestors, sealed and burning for ten thousand years, rest.

But the ritual goes awry, and shards like sparks pour across the night sky. A few of them carried Revenants inside. And the one who ends up chasing after those shards is a single Diyel — said to be the kindest in all of Kazdel.
The Furnace Blew Up, and Nobody Ran

**Nymph** is a Diyel who recently became a 'district warden'. She uses Arts to read emotions and cast suggestions, but what she's truly best at is looking after people. She even promised a friend that she'd keep her district's residents safe while the ritual was underway.
So Nymph tries to evacuate the people in the plaza past the cordon. But the mercenary sharpening his blade, the vendor collecting stall fees, the wandering singer — nobody listens to her.

Far off, the ritual is in full swing at the Great Furnace. Then a Military Commission airship's cannon, under the influence of a Revenant shard, charges of its own accord — and fires straight at the furnace.

blocks.cap: The sparks that poured across the night sky — in truth, they were furnace shards with Revenants dwelling inside.

blocks.x: A burning rain poured down on Kazdel. A glittering shard fell right onto Nymph's head, too. A shadow seeped out of it — an old Revenant grandfather who had burned for 200 years, no, ten thousand years.
When Nymph shows him her neighborhood, the old man grumbles that it's a sorry state, that nothing has progressed at all… and then, after a long silence, he leaves behind just one quiet word.
Rest……

Around then, Nymph's Lich friend **Ermengarde** — 'Ermie' — gets in touch. Fewer than ten Revenant shards scattered by the blast fell across the city, and since they're no better than ticking time bombs, she asks Nymph to recover every last one.
Following the Smell of Roasting Potatoes, She Found a Gang War
Carrying a detector bag Ermie modified for her, Nymph heads into the slums to search for shards. There she meets **Poul**, a laid-back young man out shopping for sweet potatoes.
A huge chunk of metal with a shard embedded in it had crashed into the slums, and gangs like Broken Horn were on the verge of drawing knives over turning it into food.

But as it turned out, Poul was the 'esteemed Advisor' whom all the slum's people looked up to. He mediates the meeting and defuses the fight — outwardly composed, though drenched in cold sweat within.
Having spirited the shard away safely, Nymph decides that, since it's come to this, she'll search for the remaining shards together with Poul.
The Monster Draped in Bedsheets Turned Out to Be a Runaway Golem

A rumor spreads through the neighborhood that a monster draped in a bedsheet — a 'vengeful spirit' — is on the prowl. The timid Beansprout, and little Cherry who is learning her letters from Nymph, both quake with fear.
But that 'monster' was **Mudrock**'s giant stone statue. Possessed by a Revenant shard, it had run amok. Strangely, it attacked only Sarkaz and never touched outsiders.

Mudrock is the one who led her Infected comrades from outside the city back into Kazdel. As she pulls out the shard, Mudrock tells of why she went out of her way to bring her comrades of other races inside the walls.
You can steer clear of what's bad, but what's good is good in the end, no matter what anyone says.
When Nymph says Kazdel has grown far too weak, Mudrock answers that the Kazdel of today has never been so weak — telling her, along with it, the story of Rhodes Island and of Amiya.
Why She Guarded a Brooch That Wouldn't Even Sell as If It Were Her Life
The next shard was in the hands of an elegant market vendor. **Crowny** — a Sarkaz designer who makes clothes and souvenirs from scrap — had a Revenant dwelling in her purple gemstone brooch.
Unable to get the brooch, Nymph chases after Crowny, takes to the sky with her clumsy Arts, and promptly plummets. That's when the Sankta courier **Mostima** catches her.
Mostima belongs to Penguin Logistics. She'd come all the way outside Kazdel to deliver expensive silk to Crowny. And another Sankta, **Fiammetta**, lay hidden, keeping watch over Mostima.

Crowny says she was once driven out of Kazdel and wandered Ursus. There, she came upon an old sketchbook belonging to some Sarkaz designer — all that remained was a half-erased phrase, 'For AM,' and the outline of a single dress.
To understand that feeling, she learned how to make clothes, and crafted tourist souvenirs that wouldn't sell. Saying she no longer needs it, Crowny hands the brooch over to Nymph.
Someday, Kazdel too will welcome tourists from across Terra… I believe that.
She Dropped By for a Cup of Tea and Got Trapped in Someone Else's Memory

The home of her neighbor **Kalaisha** is filled with the scent of herbal tea. She's a gentle soul who bakes strawberry cookies — but she earnestly warns Nymph not to touch anything in the house carelessly.

Of all places, where the shard detector reacted sat a wreath woven from dry branches. Drawn by the shard's power, Nymph's hand picks up the wreath of its own accord — and she is pulled into Kalaisha's memories.

Within the memory, Nymph becomes a Nachzehrer who has left the battlefield. She meets a deserter dying while longing for snow, a witch immersing herself in an ice spring, and even the legendary bell-toller Netzsalem.
Pain is the shadow of power, and life is the burning sun.

Kalaisha pulls Nymph out of the dream. That wreath was an as-yet-unfinished device she'd made to heal Nachzehrer tormented by the trauma of the battlefield. In exchange, the shard's rampage sent Nymph's Arts wild, and Kalaisha's sturdy house collapsed flat.

blocks.cap: The house with its roof blown off. Even so, Kalaisha calmly offers tea.

blocks.x: Around then, a strange wanderer was roaming the city — one made entirely of tin, carrying the largest Revenant shard of all.
The Biggest Shard Walked Around Smoking a Cigarette
The wanderer Nymph and Poul catch calls himself **Tin Man**. A tin-bodied stranger from Columbia — and astonishingly, he could converse with the Revenants inside the furnace.
Tin Man says he wants to go inside the Great Furnace to meet an 'old friend.' Though reluctant, Ermie takes Nymph's group — who gathered up all the shards — to the deepest part of the furnace.
When They Told the Ancestors About Kazdel's Future
At the furnace core, Ermie tries to complete the requiem rite her teacher never finished. But far from answering, the Revenants move to tear Ermie to shreds. Tin Man barely holds them off.
So they change tack. Everyone writes down the future of Kazdel they each envision on paper, and decides to send it off by burning it in the furnace fire — telling their story to the ancestors.

blocks.cap: Each writes down the Kazdel they envision, and sends it off in the furnace's fire.

blocks.x: Nymph and Mudrock envisioned a Kazdel with no monarchy and no discrimination; Crowny wondered about the legendary Kazdel said to have sunk beneath the earth; Poul earnestly forecast the future… each a different story.

At last the ancestors manifest, shaking the furnace. They rage at these presumptuous tales of the future, yet grow intrigued as well. To an ancestor who asks what they'll do if they take a wrong road, Nymph answers.
Even if it's a rough, narrow road of mud, isn't that better than standing at a dead end on the edge of a cliff……?
The ancestors reach their verdict. If they truly believe such a Kazdel is better, then let them live it out and prove it — bearing both success and failure on their own.
We shall watch and see whether you are worthy… of forging a new road for the Sarkaz.
So, Where Are You Trying to Send Us?
Nymph asks urgently — where are they going? The ancestors' answer was brief.
Into the story you told us.
The story stops here — at the threshold of a new road, right on the cusp of the next chapter.

On a sleepless night, there was someone who wanted to try something against the pitch-black sky — like, say, catching the fireworks that burst so loudly. Nymph had believed in the Revenants since childhood, truly met an ancestor that night, and now, before the watching eyes of that ancestor, she walks into the story they told.

act16mini.tagline: A decrepit transport's final run is upended by a hijacking — but what the hijacker is after isn't treasure, it's the light that saved her as a child. In the end, everyone was searching for a home to return to.

chars.name: Pot Lid
chars.desc: The young Cautus who guards the transport's kitchen. She calls herself a 'six-star chef.'

chars.name: Alanna
chars.desc: The transport's driver, known as 'Alanna of the Wasteland.'

chars.name: Ray
chars.desc: A taciturn mine prospector who carries a crossbow in her arms.

chars.name: Savage
chars.desc: A woman who wields a massive hammer and travels alongside Amiya's group.

chars.name: Asbestos
chars.desc: An explorer who writes travelogues.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The leader of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Jerry
chars.desc: A timid Sarkaz insurance salesman.

terms.name: Rim Billiton
terms.desc: A barren union of autonomous states where rusted factories and mine shafts stretch on without end. Catastrophes and mining accidents are frequent.

terms.name: Greening Valley
terms.desc: The destination Ray is bent on reaching. It lies deep within the Greenmeadow autonomous state.

terms.name: Prospector
terms.desc: A temporary mining worker who descends into the shafts ahead of full-scale excavation to check air quality and the risk of collapse.

terms.name: Mobile Platform
terms.desc: A mobile city that roams the wastelands of Rim Billiton.

blocks.x: Rim Billiton is a barren land where rusted steel frames and mine shafts stretch on without end. For years now, a single aging transport had been crossing that wasteland.
Aboard this vehicle, besides the driver and passengers, were two people who had made it their permanent home. And today was this route's final run.
It would have been nice if it had ended without incident... but of all days, today is when this vehicle gets hijacked.
A Six-Star Chef Lives on This Transport
**Pot Lid**, the young Cautus who guards the kitchen, calls herself a 'six-star chef.' She's a kid who takes cooking so seriously she'll quietly reheat a cold stew with Originium Arts.
The driver, **Alanna**, is known as 'Alanna of the Wasteland.' She's full of bluster and pride, but she's also the guardian who has secretly raised this child aboard the vehicle for two years now.
Beneath Pot Lid's sleeve, black crystals have begun to sprout. This child is Infected. Even the bracelet Alanna fastened on her can't fully hide that glow.
Once the route is cut, this vehicle — this home — will disappear too. Even so, the child makes a point of smiling brightly.
Because Warmy has never once been gloomy.

'Warmy' is the name Pot Lid uses for herself. That claim of never being sad somehow sounds a little sad. Saying she's doing the final tidying up, Alanna sends the child off on an errand — to buy a safety valve and daily necessities, and to sell off their old household goods. The two of them called this 'division of labor.'
The Kid Runs an Errand — but No One Will Take Her Goods

Out on the streets for the first time in ages, Pot Lid loads a cart high with cargo and goes rolling down a slope, only barely coming to a stop thanks to a customer. It was a woman of few words who'd come to buy a Sandbeast, a crossbow in her arms.

The general store owner refuses to deal at first, accepting the goods only once he hears Alanna's name. At the government office the line is so long she's at her wits' end — until some spirited lady offers to submit the paperwork on her behalf and presses a business card into her hand.
With the errand done, the transport takes the returning child aboard and begins its final run. But then—
This transport has been commandeered. From this point on, this vehicle's destination is Greening Valley.
The one holding the broadcast mic was that woman of few words from earlier in the day. In her other hand was a crossbow, its point trained on Alanna's head.
A Robber With a Crossbow to Your Head — yet the Conversation Keeps Flowing

The vehicle hijacker — this woman later called **Ray** — is oddly composed. She says no one will get hurt as long as they reach the destination, and that she'll even pay. Alanna keeps talking nonstop, watching for an opening.
But a checkpoint trips them up. With refugees flooding in after a recent mine accident, inspections to root out the Infected have begun.
Pot Lid's inspection device goes off. For the first time, Alanna bows her head and pleads, but the inspector remains coldly unmoved.

In that instant, Ray looses an arrow to hold the guards back and pulls Pot Lid out. Alanna rams straight through the gate, breaking through the checkpoint. Now everyone aboard this vehicle is an accomplice.
The reason Ray is willing to endure all this chaos to reach Greening Valley is—
I'm going to find a giant shadow. A shadow that glows in the fog, huge as a mountain. It can even speak.
A Man Who Fled His Engagement — Only to Fall for the One He Fled

One more passenger had ended up stranded aboard by chance — **Jerry**, a timid Sarkaz insurance salesman. Pushed into an arranged engagement by his 36 siblings, he'd hated it and was in the middle of running away.

While on the run, Jerry met a woman named Keira who was play-acting at running away from home, and together they weathered a crisis. Then the two felt a connection forming between them... only to discover that they had, in fact, been each other's arranged fiancés all along.

Meanwhile, to learn the truth about the 'Behemoth,' Alanna pays a visit to travelogue writer **Asbestos**. Asbestos tells her the Sargonian word for that giant shadow, but admits that even he doesn't know what it actually is.
And out in this wasteland, there was also a girl who had returned to the hometown where she spent her childhood — **Amiya** of Rhodes Island, along with the Doctor and **Savage**.
A Child Who Used Arts to Cool the Heat — Collapses Because of Those Very Arts

The incident erupts with Pot Lid. Wanting to be useful, this child had secretly been using Arts to regulate the reactor's temperature. Then her Originium Arts run out of control, and her whole body turns into a ball of fire.
Alanna is frantic, even knowing folk remedies are useless. Knowing full well that Oripathy is incurable, there was nothing she could do but believe that she and the child would recover together.

That's when Savage — the owner of that business card — appears, along with Amiya and the Doctor. Amiya uses Arts to suppress the crystals' activity and saves the child. The truth behind that card: it was Rhodes Island, the ship that rescues the Infected.
That night, they all sit together out in the wasteland. Beside the Doctor gazing up at the stars, Savage quietly begins to share stories she'd long kept buried.

blocks.cap: The Doctor keeping watch beside a sleeping Amiya, with Savage seated close by.

blocks.x: She Finally Meets the Light She'd Sought for So Long — but It Says It Isn't the One

Ray had originally been a prospector who checked the air in the mine shafts. The Sandbeast that had warned her of toxic gas through the color of its dorsal fin died in a recent gas accident. Before its empty cage, Ray resolved to once again seek out the 'light' that had saved her as a child.

Walking on in a daze, Ray discovers her own hand clutching the Doctor's hood, having mistaken it for her dead Sandbeast. As it turned out, the Rhodes Island group had been following behind them the whole time.

blocks.cap: Ray, dazed, still gripping the Doctor's hood.

blocks.x: Where the group arrived was a rock fortress that shielded them from sandstorms — an oasis teeming with healthy Sandbeasts. Delighted, Pot Lid hands out carrot cookies to the Sandbeasts.

blocks.cap: The oasis of the rock fortress, brimming with Sandbeasts.

blocks.x: But then a Catastrophe rolls in. As everyone evacuates, Ray falls behind while trying to protect the remaining Sandbeasts. That's when the Sandbeasts, sounding a strange bone-flute cry, lead Ray into a cave deep underground.
There, a giant being had been trapped in darkness for hundreds of years, unable even to move.

blocks.cap: The giant being, encountered in the heart of the darkness beyond a split rock wall.

blocks.x: 'Behemoth'? The one I was looking for... was you?

But the Behemoth speaks calmly. It asks how it could ever have saved her when it can't even move, and says none of its kind are here. It drives the point home: what you're looking for isn't here. Then it asks in return — so what was it that brought you all this way?
The Truth of the Light

In the end, the group never reaches Greening Valley, only gazing at it from afar before turning back. It's confirmed that what Ray was searching for isn't there. And yet, strangely, Ray didn't look disappointed.

Back at Rhodes Island, Ray joins as a prospector and, for the first time, receives an ID tag with her own number on it — because temporary prospectors weren't even given a number. And Ray asks the Doctor: what was that light, back then?

In that moment, a scene flashes before Amiya's eyes. The newly excavated Rhodes Island crossing an Originium wasteland, someone reaching out a hand to a band of stranded hunters. The 'light' that, over a decade ago, saved the young Ray among that pack of hunters — was this very ship, and that person aboard it.
Because I wanted everyone to have a home to return to.

Pot Lid learns how to handle an Arts staff from Savage. She keeps fumbling and bursts into tears, but Alanna tells her she can cling to her sleeve and cry all she likes. Because at least at the end of this journey, the child wasn't empty-handed.
The two of them now head to Rhodes Island to search for a cure for Oripathy — for the sake of the day they'll one day reunite with the father they lost contact with in an accident two years ago.

The aging transport whose final run ended in a hijacking thus completed the longest, unplanned journey of all. Someone found a home to return to; someone else chose to become that home. And the child who made a point of smiling even in the dark was, this time, truly not empty-handed.

act15mini.tagline: A story of setting out for the icefield in search of a missing mentor, and ending up crossing Sami alongside the 'witch' that Ursus so feared. Even in a land where words don't reach, someone will always insist on reaching out a hand.

chars.name: Magallan
chars.desc: A young Columbian explorer surveying the endless icefield. She belongs to Rhine Lab's Scientific Survey Department.

chars.name: Santalla
chars.desc: A Sami shaman who commands the blizzard. Magallan's icefield best friend, affectionately called 'Sister Simone.'

chars.name: Typhon
chars.desc: A Sarkaz hunter who carries a black bow. He was raised by the hand of the Cyclops Arges.

chars.name: Gitano
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island fortune-teller from the Sami marshlands who divines fate with a crystal ball.

chars.name: Oakcup
chars.desc: A talkative apprentice shaman left behind in the Primeval Forest. She's fluent in many languages.

chars.name: Eikthyrnir
chars.desc: The most authoritative warrior of the Northern Front in all of Sami.

chars.name: Arges
chars.desc: A Cyclops who foresees the future. He raised Typhon.

chars.name: Supervisor Miriam
chars.desc: The mentor Magallan looks up to. She's the leader of the missing Scientific Survey Department expedition.

terms.name: Sami
terms.desc: The land of snow and ice at the far north of Columbia, and its native people. Rather than civilization, they follow nature and fate, and their shamans read the 'will of Sami' from the cryptic script that surfaces on trees.

terms.name: Calamity
terms.desc: A Demon-born disaster that spreads out from the icefield. The Sami call this enemy 'Anskotarnir,' and believe it grows by feeding on people's fear.

terms.name: High Priest
terms.desc: The supreme shaman who leads a tribe. They foresee calamity through divination, and a tribe without a High Priest cannot notice the Calamity even as it approaches.

terms.name: Ancient Tree
terms.desc: The trinity tree of Sami that encompasses past, present, and future. Distinct from the 'tribal tree' each tribe venerates, it is an existence that transcends Sami itself.

blocks.x: Sami is a land of nothing but snow and ice at the far north of Columbia. People from the south call it a land of barbarians, but the Sami live by following nature and fate rather than civilization. It's a place where shamans read the 'will of Sami' from the cryptic script that surfaces on trees, and so avert disaster.

The young Columbian explorer **Magallan** learns that the mentor she reveres, **Supervisor Miriam** of Rhine Lab's Scientific Survey Department, has gone missing along with her entire expedition while surveying the 'endless icefield.' But of all times, Ursus has sealed off every route to the icefield.
So Magallan decides to enter the icefield by crossing through Sami. But along that road, she comes face to face with the 'witch' that Ursus so dreaded.
The kindly guide Ursus assigned turned out to be an informant

At the Ursus border, the courier **Ivan** checks Magallan's papers. Though he says all icefield expeditions have been canceled this year, upon hearing her plight he offers to accompany her himself. On the surface, he's the most courteous of men.

But out of Magallan's sight, Ivan reports to someone in Ursine—he's passed along the Columbian's coordinates and orders her kept under watch.
