Every Sami tale he tells is about nothing but 'savages' and 'witches.' He claims that vengeful spirits wandering the border isolate Ursus soldiers in blizzards and kill them.
At the Ancient Tree outpost, Ivan stepped out saying he'd go check on the blizzard—and never came back.

blocks.cap: A blizzard advances slowly, as if obeying a command, and before it stands a figure holding a staff.

blocks.x: And then a blizzard approaches the outpost, as if someone were commanding it. The one who led that blizzard was none other than Magallan's icefield best friend, **Santalla**—the Sami shaman who commands blizzards, whom Magallan calls 'Sister Simone.'
The witch Ursus spoke of was her sister all along. Since Santalla, being Sami, can open the way to the icefield, the two set off together.
A mountain where you must hold moss under your tongue to survive
As they enter the Winterthus Mountains, Magallan begins to hear her mentor's voice calling, 'Magallan…' Just as she moves to run toward it in delight, someone clamps a hand over her mouth.

It was **Typhon**, the Sarkaz hunter with a black bow—a child raised in the cave of the Cyclops Arges. Typhon tells her the voice is a hallucination, the calling of the 'Calamity.' If you hold moss under your tongue, he says, that noise will vanish.

Typhon joins as their guide, and the three encounter Sami warriors guarding the Northern Front. Santalla even invokes her former standing as a shaman to ask for passage, but the warriors won't budge. Santalla, it turns out, is an exile who was nearly made High Priest but abandoned the duty and left.
Frustrated, Magallan blurts out an explanation in a loud voice—and in that instant the warriors' faces turn grim. She has brought with her an omen of the Calamity.
You fool, run! You've just brought an omen of the Calamity down on us.
The shadow she was told never to touch—she finally touched it

Fleeing along a side path Typhon knows, the three reach a tribe in the forestlands. As it happens, this tribe too had received a revelation from Sami and was preparing to migrate. Santalla deciphers their genealogy, which not even the tribe's own people can read.

That night Magallan is tormented by a nightmare in which she becomes a hunted beast. When she wakes, a huge shadow—part human, part tree—is silently pacing the room. A shadow that shows up in no photograph, visible only to Magallan's eyes.

blocks.cap: Typhon first dips his hand into the shadow, then gently rests it on the back of Magallan's hand.

blocks.x: Typhon says it's just a shadow, and that to overcome fear she should touch it herself. Surviving despite the fear is the hardest thing of all, he tells her—and Magallan is already doing exactly that.
When Magallan stretches out a trembling hand and puts it into the shadow, she truly feels nothing at all. She gives the shadow a name: 'Mr. Solitude.'
The next morning, the tribe's awakening ritual is held. Thanks to Santalla's decipherment, the ritual succeeds—

blocks.cap: A great tree bearing the weight of ages and memories pulls its roots from the earth and slowly rises.

blocks.x: The tribe's great tree uproots itself and rises, beginning to walk southward together with the tribe.
The chatty guide they met in the forest was an apprentice shaman

The party enters the Primeval Forest, said to be the heart of Sami. There they meet **Oakcup**—a talkative apprentice shaman whose master, a High Priest, left her in the forest telling her to 'grasp the will of Sami.' Oakcup volunteers to be their guide and joins them.
That night Magallan dreams again. This time a pure-white Hornbeest sleeps soundly at her side—a strangely wistful dream.

blocks.cap: A pure-white Hornbeest sleeps quietly within Magallan's dream.

blocks.x: The next day the party finds a mother Rock Hornbeest tainted by a Demon, along with her calf. The mother is already beyond saving. Oakcup sings a Sami funeral hymn to lay her to rest, and takes in the orphaned calf, resolving to wait for her master. Santalla asks Oakcup to tell her master that Ursus's 'Blackmark' is entangled in all of this.
A brother and sister returned home—one of them lying upon a boat

Coming out of the forest and reaching the marshland tribe, Santalla receives an unexpected request: to examine the bodies of the warriors who have returned. Among them was the elder brother of **Gitano**, the Rhodes Island fortune-teller. Having foreseen her brother's death and come home, Gitano was being called by her old name, 'Ragna.'

Years ago, during a disaster of snow, the siblings chose opposite paths. Her brother went to the Northern Front to try to stop the disaster, while Gitano led the people south to safety. Over that, the two turned their weapons on each other and never met again.

Santalla finds black blood in the bodies—the taint of a Demon. She realizes too late that the water-burial rite, which sinks these bodies into the water, could be dangerous. But the funeral has already begun.

blocks.cap: Boats bearing the dead drift along the waterway, as the living too bid farewell to their homeland.

blocks.x: Gitano divines whether the dead wish for a funeral. Most answer yes, but one alone—her brother—remains silent to the end.
Brother, why is it that you will not answer me?
As the funeral proceeds, the taint of warriors long accumulated in the marsh awakens and takes physical form. Typhon raises his bow to block the way, and Santalla freezes the marsh solid to buy time.

blocks.cap: Typhon draws his bowstring toward the seemingly empty marsh.

blocks.x: Just then **Eikthyrnir**, who received Gitano's distress signal, appears leading the warriors of the Treescar tribe. The most authoritative Northern warrior in all of Sami, he scatters the shadows of the warriors—now transformed beyond recognition—with a single swing of his hammer.

To the tribe about to retreat south, Eikthyrnir declares that Sami has no need to flee. Fear itself becomes the breeding ground of the Demon, he says, so they must overcome that fear and stand firm against the Calamity.
The client waiting for Typhon was his master Arges himself

The party at last reaches Chapart, the southernmost city of Sami—a tourist city covered in steel and concrete, where Columbian corporations have set up their offices. Here Santalla goes on ahead to attend to her own business.

The client who had been waiting for Typhon in Chapart was, astonishingly, the Cyclops **Arges**—that is, the adoptive father who raised Typhon. Arges, who foresees the future, hands Typhon a box and asks him to deliver it to Rhine Lab.

The box belonged to Supervisor Miriam. Arges recounts that he entered the icefield together with Miriam's expedition, encountered a Demon, and that only he and Miriam survived. Miriam then ventured alone into the deeper reaches of the icefield.

Inside the box was a single flower with neither color nor roots. But the moment Rhine Lab's researchers open the box, the flower stains the entire lab with an invisible color. The Calamity—Anskotarnir, which means 'enemy' in the Sami tongue—has opened its eyes.

Typhon immediately reseals the flower and holds it back. Only then does Magallan recall the last words of her mentor that she had heard as a hallucination: 'If the box is opened, Anskotarnir will take notice.' Though it had already been opened.
The witch who returns into the blizzard
The researchers are giddy over their new discovery, but Typhon quietly warns Magallan: Arges's prophecies always end in misery, and if anything happens, she must come find him.

Meanwhile, Santalla had captured and was interrogating an Ursus spy who had infiltrated Sami. The man confesses that 'Blackmark' is a guardsman of the Ursus emperor who lost his nation—one wholly consumed by a Demon. He still defiles the land of Sami to this day, yet no living soul knows where he is.
We are the very fangs of Sami that bite down on the Demon's footprints. And you… you aren't even worthy of being the 'enemy.'
Having finished with the Ursus man, Santalla once again calls to mind her own mission—the tainted land of Sami, and the blizzards of the icefield beyond it.

Magallan never did come to understand why people must clash when they could cooperate instead. Even so, in this land where words don't reach, there were friends who reached out a hand to her. Somewhere in the icefield her mentor still remains, and the journey of searching is not yet over—so Magallan turns once more toward the blizzard.

act14mini.tagline: The story of a boy who left home to make a name for himself, only to nearly be turned into 'a dead man' the moment he returned to his hometown. A quiet struggle waged by the nameless over the weight of a name.

chars.name: Qiubai
chars.desc: A wandering swordswoman of the jianghu. Someone who never takes what belongs to others and always pays her way in full.

chars.name: Bang Dolsoe
chars.desc: A boy from Moxian Village who left home vowing to make a name for himself.

chars.name: the Hunter
chars.desc: Bang Dolsoe's father. A sick, hunched old hunter.

chars.name: the Old Chief
chars.desc: The aged village head and clan chief of Moxian Village.

chars.name: Saga
chars.desc: A wandering mendicant monk from Higashi.

chars.name: Mulberry
chars.desc: A member of the 'Chunken' disaster relief squad.

chars.name: Wind Chimes
chars.desc: A Catastrophe messenger who travels the mountains alone.

terms.name: Moxian Village
terms.desc: A poor mountain hamlet tucked deep in the northwest of Yan, home to some hundred villagers all bearing the surname Zhu. Legend says their ancestors carved it out of the mountains a thousand years ago.

terms.name: Yishan Shrine
terms.desc: Moxian Village's ancestral shrine, dedicated to the forebear who moved a mountain with a single hoe to found the village.

terms.name: Chidao
terms.desc: A road network laid down by Yan to reach the lands that mobile cities cannot. Whenever a Catastrophe destroys it, it is built again.

terms.name: Mobile City
terms.desc: A colossal city that migrates across the land to escape Catastrophes.

terms.name: Jianghu
terms.desc: The world through which martial artists wander—a place where knights-errant, bandits, and swordsmen mingle.

blocks.x: This is a tale of a remote mountain backwater in the northwest of Yan, so far off that not even the mobile cities reach it. Catastrophes gnaw away at the land, and its people scrape by day to day, their lives entrusted to the mountains and the sky.

On one such mountain path, a swordswoman corners a fleeing boy at the edge of a cliff. The boy blusters that he is the boss of a bandit gang called the 'Shanhai Gang,' but he turns out to be no more than a kid barely in his teens.

But from the boy's torn pocket fall an old protective talisman inscribed with 'Moxian Village' and a house key. The swordswoman makes up her mind: she will take this child home—without any idea what kind of village it is.
The runaway 'boss' who left to make a name for himself—and reports his rescuer as a kidnapper over a single meal

The swordswoman's name is **Qiubai**—a wanderer of the jianghu whose name means 'vengeance and snow.' Her martial skill is formidable, yet she never takes what belongs to others and always pays in full for what she buys.
The boy's name is **Bang Dolsoe**. He is the son of the one household in the village with a different surname, and three years ago he left home vowing to become a 'renowned knight-errant.'

Their journey home is an utter mess. Bang Dolsoe secretly slips an inn clerk a note saying he's been 'kidnapped' to summon the guards, then bolts off under the excuse of a bathroom break. Yet as she chases when he flees and he hides when she fumes, the two spend two weeks together and, oddly enough, fall into perfect sync.

When a Duskbeast attacks out in the wastes, Qiubai takes a claw across her body shielding the boy behind her. Bang Dolsoe offers a salve his father taught him to make and, fearing she won't trust it, cuts his own arm to demonstrate it on himself.

At the pass leading into the mountains, the two spot a gray road—a **Chidao**, one of the highways Yan lays down in lands the mobile cities cannot reach. Whenever a Catastrophe brings it down, it is built again and again, and Qiubai remarks that few people realize someone is out there endlessly keeping up that work.

Bang Dolsoe keeps asking: why did she bother saving him, why does she keep trying to send him home? Qiubai answers curtly—a child with a home to return to is better off there than wandering the wastes alone. Even as she says it of herself, 'I no longer have a home.'
A homecoming after three years—and yet a fresh grave stands at the village gate
The boy's hometown is **Moxian Village**, a poor mountain hamlet said to have been carved from the mountains by an ancestor wielding a single hoe a thousand years ago.
But the villagers react strangely at the sight of Bang Dolsoe. 'You... you're alive?'—not with joy, but with dread. They scatter as if fleeing, saying they must tell the village head.
At the village entrance stands the relocated ancestral shrine, the **Yishan Shrine**, and beside it a freshly made grave. Not knowing whose it is, Bang Dolsoe bows his head to it in respect.

His father isn't at the house. Instead, the sick and hunched **Hunter**—Bang Dolsoe's father—pushes his way out through the crowd of villagers. Reunited after three years, father and son can hardly even meet each other's eyes.

The village's aged **Chief Zhu Shun** welcomes Qiubai warmly. Thanking her for bringing the boy home, he confides the village's plight: failed harvests, drought, and then torrential rains… It seems spring has yet to come to this mountain hollow at all.
But after a talk with the Hunter, the chief puts a strange request to Bang Dolsoe.
Dolsoe, my boy... for the sake of this village, couldn't you just once let yourself be a dead man?
The village that would pin a living boy's name onto the corpse of a child killed in a landslide
A few days earlier, beside a Chidao washed out by the downpour, the villagers found the body of a strange boy. Unidentifiable, the child was clutching a heavy box in his hands—a camera.

The chief had meant to report it to the authorities. But someone hatched a scheme: if they pinned onto this corpse the name of Bang Dolsoe—presumed dead these three years—they could pass it off as a death in the line of duty during Chidao construction and claim a hefty compensation payout.

The village was desperate—its irrigation, its seed grain, its stored food all run dry. The chief prostrates himself before the ancestral statue in the Yishan Shrine and prays, saying he never wanted to become a wicked man, that this truly was the only way left.

blocks.cap: The wandering monk Saga opens her eyes, her back resting against the ancestral statue. She had been listening to the chief's prayer from the very start.

blocks.x: Just then, someone dozing against the statue opens her eyes—the wandering monk **Saga**. A cleric from Higashi, she is an odd sort who declares that 'karmic bonds make no distinctions' and addresses the clay statue as a 'benefactor.' She heard the chief's confession in full.
Meanwhile, Bang Dolsoe is locked in a storehouse. A village woman who brings him food tries to soothe him—what's the big deal about lending out a single name? But the boy will not back down.
I don't care how you people live. But I want to live like a human being!
The father who said he was making a bow was really carving his son's gravestone
Qiubai, whom everyone thought had left the village, had in fact not gone—now that she had brought the boy here, she meant to see his safety through to the end.
She returns to the Hunter's house. He is whittling a piece of wood, claiming to be 'making a bow,' but Qiubai sees through it at once—it is no bow, but his own son's gravestone.

The father had believed his son already died out in the wastes three years ago. He'd planned to take the compensation money, leave the village, and set out to find him. And then, of all things, the son he'd thought dead came home alive.
At this point two more visitors arrive at the village: **Mulberry**, a member of the **Chunken** disaster relief squad, and the **Messenger** who travels these mountains alone.

Fearing their scheme would be ruined, the villagers even set hunting traps along the mountain path to stop the Messenger—only for Mulberry to let her through by sheer accident. Mulberry, who has come to investigate the cause of the debris flow, happens to discover on the mountainside the residue of homemade explosives and unfamiliar wheel tracks.
The day a gravestone left half-inscribed was cleaved in two by a single stroke of the sword

When the government inspector arrives a day ahead of schedule, the village scrambles. The Hunter is dragged out and made to stand before the grave, clutching his son's gravestone with its inscription still unfinished.

blocks.cap: As a flash of the blade streaked through the air, the gravestone about to be driven into the ground was split in two.

blocks.x: The instant the gravestone is about to be planted in the earth, the blade flashes—Qiubai has cut it clean in two.

In truth she had never trusted them from the start. And now she speaks of herself for the first time: how her father, a river pirate of Jiangji, was killed by government troops, and how she alone fled when the rest of her comrades were slaughtered. She, too, is a person without a name in this country.
A corpse, a name, money. Can your way of survival only be bought by trampling another person's dignity?

And then, too late, a cruel piece of news arrives. The subsidy the village applied for two years ago reaches them on this very day. With that money, they'd never have needed to do any of this in the first place. In that moment, the chief felt as though the mountain said to have vanished a thousand years ago were bearing down on his shoulders once more.
And then word comes down from the mountain: Bang Dolsoe has... thrown himself from the cliff.
The father who insists 'Bang Dolsoe must not be alive,' and the son who flings himself from the cliff
Up on the mountain, Qiubai had already subdued the two villagers driving Bang Dolsoe toward the cliff's edge. Between the Hunter, who arrived after her, and his son, one last conversation remained.

The father tells his son to lay down his name, stay in the village, and live in peace. The son begs him to leave for a mobile city instead, promising to learn martial arts and cure his father's illness. It is here that the father finally speaks of the mother who ran away: she had longed to leave this mountain and live 'a different life,' but passed away a few years ago—leaving behind, in a thin envelope, the money from her own death benefit, addressed to her son.
Even so, the father does not relent.
'Bang Dolsoe' is not allowed to be alive.
The boy throws himself off. And as if the wind itself catches his back, Qiubai seizes him for a third time.

At that same moment, in the Yishan Shrine, Saga stops the chief as he clutches the last of the explosives and tries to take his own life—telling him not to flee into death, but to confess everything to the authorities.
An evil thought and an evil deed differ by but a single word, yet their consequences are as far apart as heaven and earth.

blocks.cap: To the boy with nowhere to go, Qiubai said, 'Come with me for now.'

blocks.x: To Bang Dolsoe, now awake, Qiubai says: if he has nowhere to go, then come with her for now. A person without a name can sometimes raise an even louder voice. But should he gain power and stray onto the path of evil, then she will kill him with her own hands.
Who, then, was that boy who died without a name?
One question remains. Who was that nameless dead boy, the one who nearly had Bang Dolsoe's name forced upon him?

The footage recovered from the repaired camera gives the answer. The boy was a director shooting a documentary—an Infected with Oripathy and little time left, who had come all the way to these distant mountains hoping to record 'the real land and its people' one last time.

He had followed this mountain path's Messenger with his camera in hand. The Messenger's name is **Wind Chimes**. She, too, is Infected—bearing a broken horn, she shoulders others' letters and parcels and walks dozens of li of mountain road even on rainy nights.

The two had quarreled. When the director said he 'wanted to change this place,' the Messenger sent him away, calling it nothing but pity steeped in a sense of superiority. Not long after, the director was swept away by a debris flow, alone.
The people who stayed here are doing their very best to live, each in their own way.

Wind Chimes never does manage to learn his identity—not his name, nor where he came from. But she resolves to carry on the journey left in his camera, to finish in his stead the documentary he never could.
Two months later, in a village with more wells than before

Two months on, Moxian Village had changed. Wells had multiplied, new irrigation had come in, and people gathered along the paddy ridges. With the chief gone, there was much to do in the village, yet everyone was pulling their own weight.

Two children, Xiaoping and Xiao'an, chatter about building a tractor out of abandoned Chidao construction tools and riding it all the way to Lungmen. Wind Chimes takes a photo of them—because a photo is something you can carry, so that even if they're far from here someday, they'll still be able to remember home.

In the director's camera there also remained the face of a boy he had happened to film along his journey—that boy, gleefully running away, hollering that a terrifying swordswoman was chasing him from behind.
I am Bang Dolsoe! A man who aims to be the greatest knight-errant of all! Remember my name.

The boy who left home to make a name for himself nearly had that very name stripped from him in his own hometown. Yet beyond the lens, his face still cries out his name, clear as ever. A forgotten village, a person who has gone—both will one day quietly fade away, but someone, in just this way, leaves behind a trace that they were once here.

act12mini.tagline: A knight who laid down his sword and wandered in search of his vanished family draws his blade one last time to stop an old friend bent on starting a war. A victory that is no victory, and a single line of answer handed to him after twenty years.

chars.name: Młynar
chars.desc: A knight who laid down his sword and wanders Kazimierz in search of his vanished family. A member of the Nearl family.

chars.name: Deschi
chars.desc: A high-flying lawyer with an undefeated record in the Grand Knight Territory.

chars.name: Margaret
chars.desc: The Nearl family's 'Radiant Knight.' Młynar's niece.

chars.name: Toland
chars.desc: The chief of a band of Sarkaz bounty hunters. An old friend of Młynar's.

chars.name: Tsishbor
chars.desc: The commander of the silver-armored Expeditionary Knights, and an old friend of Młynar's.

chars.name: Zeno
chars.desc: An Infected foreman working at a Zbonek construction site.

chars.name: Shevchik
chars.desc: A retired arena knight once known as 'the Plastic Knight.'

chars.name: Flametail
chars.desc: A knight from Pinus Sylvestris. She now works alongside Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Ashlock
chars.desc: Flametail's fellow knight, known as 'the Grey Brushtail Knight.'

terms.name: Expeditionary Knight
terms.desc: Knights who patrol Kazimierz's frontier and keep order beyond the cities.

terms.name: Grand Knight Territory
terms.desc: The heartland where Kazimierz's noble power and the Adjudication Council are seated.

terms.name: Adjudication Council
terms.desc: The governing body of Kazimierz's nobility, whose gaze reaches into every corner of the nation.

terms.name: Zbonek
terms.desc: An old fortress city on Kazimierz's southern border, called 'the place where the caravan bells fall silent.'

terms.name: Pinus Sylvestris
terms.desc: A knightly order whose founding creed is to fight for the Infected.

blocks.x: Kazimierz is a nation of knights. But the place those knights level their lances has long since ceased to be the battlefield—it's the arena, glittering with money and advertising.
And here is one knight who has laid down his sword: a man who wanders the Kazimierz countryside alone, with no retainers, no armor, and not even the will to use his own blade.
This is the story of the few days leading up to the last time he drew his sword.
A knight who won't use his sword, yet brought the sword along

Late autumn, in a rural village of southern Kazimierz. **Młynar** is a member of the Nearl family and uncle to the 'Radiant Knight.' He withdrew from the battlegrounds of the powerful long ago, so there's no one left to count him as an enemy anymore.

An old man in the village recognizes him: a former teacher who fled Leithanien, cut off his own horns, and spent twenty years in hiding. He still remembers the kindness Młynar once did him, quietly slipping him in among a band of wanderers. Młynar himself doesn't remember it, though.

What Młynar is searching for is people—his brother and sister-in-law, who vanished fifteen years ago: the two missing Nearls. Without a single word of news, he retraces, one by one, the roads he once traveled.

blocks.cap: A massive transport vehicle of the Commercial Association rumbles past kicking up dust, and the knight who brought his sword faces that towering steel in silence.

blocks.x: At the same time, on a hill overlooking a valley, two old friends meet. One is **Toland**, chief of a band of Sarkaz bounty hunters. The other is **Tsishbor**, commander of the silver-armored Expeditionary Knights. Both have known Młynar for a long time.
Tsishbor says he's investigating the raids troubling the area, and warns Toland to stay well clear of Gale Industries. But what he really wanted to talk about was something else.
A few years ago, Selina—a knight Tsishbor loved—was falsely accused, dragged off to the Grand Knight Territory, and killed. At the time, he wrote to Młynar begging for help, but no reply ever came.
Toland asks cautiously: does he want Młynar as an ally, or… does he want revenge?
A lawyer who came to fleece the village saved it instead, then left

A companion falls in with Młynar on his travels: **Deschi**, a high-flying lawyer with an undefeated record in the Grand Knight Territory. When her rental car breaks down, she ends up relying on Młynar to guide her.

The job she's taken on is to stand with Gale Industries and beat down the relocation compensation owed to the villagers of Raystone, who are about to lose their land. She crushes the village's lawyer, Ham, with sheer logic, and wrangles a settlement for a truly rock-bottom sum.

But when night falls, Deschi grows strangely quiet. Looking up at a star-filled sky for the first time in ages, she remembers her rookie self, who once vowed to uphold the dignity and fairness of the law.

And the next day, she quietly 'loses' a document to Ham: the decisive proof that Gale Industries' land contract was void in the first place. The lawyer who was paid a million marks to fleece the village had just handed the village its winning card.

When Ham asks why, Deschi laughs. Not because she's some great person, she says—just because she'd like to keep drinking this village's beer in the future. Then she quits her job and heads for Zbonek.
The man who confessed to starting the riot was with us that day

The Zbonek that Młynar heads toward is an old fortress city on Kazimierz's southern border. Lately, Infected riots have been breaking out one after another at the construction sites of its new district.

The news names an Infected foreman called **Zeno** Shelvin as the ringleader. He turned himself in, they say. But two people simply can't believe it: **Flametail** and **Ashlock**, two knights from Pinus Sylvestris who now work for Rhodes Island.
When the riot broke out, Zeno was with the two of them—in fact, it was Zeno who rescued the pair from danger. There's no way he's the culprit.

Only after Młynar persuades Gale Industries to drop the charges and the two knights spirit Zeno away does Zeno finally speak. He says an Expeditionary Knight asked him to take the fall for the riot—and that he needed the money for his family.
Pin the crime on a single Infected, let the press stoke the fear… someone was painting a bigger picture in this city.
An actor in fake armor becomes a real hero
That night, an explosion tears through an abandoned street in Zbonek. In a slum where shipping containers are stacked like mountains, children are buried under the rubble.

There's a man with a past on this street: **Shevchik**, a retired arena knight once known as 'the Plastic Knight.' Because he wore plastic armor he made himself instead of taking corporate sponsorship, he now hides his identity and lives quietly with his family.

As it happened, his son had been playing on that very street, and Shevchik plunges into the flames to find him. In the end, he saves more than twenty children and becomes a 'righteous man' who refuses to give his name.
But when his son says he wants to become a knight just like his father, Shevchik flatly refuses to allow it—the more beautiful the dream, he says, the more it will disappoint you.

Meanwhile, saving children amid those flames, Młynar comes face to face with a noble who had come from Leithanien—someone who, envious of Kazimierz's vitality, had come to see the slums for himself in hopes of reforming his own domain.
In a room the two of them finally break into, a shattered communication device is still leaking sound—military intelligence about preparing for war with Leithanien.
The Leithanien noble, who heard those words right alongside him, goes white as a sheet.
Someone believed this city needed a war
Late that night, Młynar and Toland piece together the scattered fragments.

A land contract signed without any authority by the son of the dead noble Marek; the knightly order standing behind it; Gale Industries, which recruited the Infected and bribed the press to stage the riots. All of it was aimed at one thing: kindling the sparks of war between Kazimierz and Leithanien.
At tomorrow's opening ceremony celebrating friendship between the two nations, the Leithanien noble would be killed. That was the final scene of this plan.
And the one directing all of it was Tsishbor.

But Młynar's own sword wasn't in his hands—Tsishbor had taken it to a knightly order's craftsman under the pretext of having it serviced. So the knight who had laid down his sword borrows another man's blade to go and stop his old friend.

blocks.cap: Beneath the factory lights across the valley, the swordless knight holds out his hand. "…Would you lend me your sword?"

blocks.x: He won a duel he didn't want to win
The dawn of December 1st. Abandoned even by his own subordinates, Tsishbor sets out alone to kill the Leithanien noble. And Młynar steps in to bar his way.

The duel in the rain was really a conversation. Tsishbor keeps asking Młynar: how, after seeing all this rot, could he not draw his sword? When he could have saved Selina, why did he not even spare her a glance?
A knight must never bow his head to power. But neither should he refuse to kneel before the suffering of others.
Młynar refuses to grant his conviction any validity. A creed that stakes one's life like a wager, even knowing there is no hope, can change nothing at all.
In the end, Młynar wins. But he sheathes his blade—he didn't want it to end in death like this; he only wanted Tsishbor to stop.

But Tsishbor asks in return: to whom would Młynar have him say he lost? To the nobles and their base scheming, to the merchants peddling a false peace…? He says he will accept defeat at Młynar's hands, and his alone.
And just before he falls, Tsishbor hands Młynar the answer he has waited twenty years for: that a few years ago, when he had slipped into Leithanien, he happened to meet the two missing Nearls.

blocks.cap: Through his fading sight, Tsishbor gazes one last time at the Nearl looking down at him.

blocks.x: When Młynar asks whether he told anyone else this, Tsishbor slowly shakes his head. Then, leaning on his lance, he falls as silent as one of the countless empty suits of armor he has seen out in the wilderness.
Młynar kneels in the rain and pays a knight's respects to the one who once walked the same path as him.
He sets out to find people he may never find—and goes anyway

The case was dug up. Countless corporations and old noble houses were implicated, and even a verdict that could not be overturned years ago was reopened. But by now, neither those who committed the crimes nor the people who remember what happened are much left.

Meanwhile, back in the Grand Knight Territory, **Margaret** is little by little learning the weight she must bear as the Nearl family's 'Radiant Knight'—visiting the grave of her grandfather Kirill, and standing up to the threats of the merchants who take aim at her. The old craftsmen talk of the long-gone Snitz and Młynar, wondering where the two Nearls might be now.
Toland hands Młynar an invitation from Rhodes Island—it seems the Doctor there has something to say about his brother and sister-in-law.
Młynar sets out once more to find the two people about whom there is still not a single word of news—even as Toland teases him that it's a hope with nothing to stand on.
Even if I added up all the hope I held in the Grand Knight Territory across ten whole years, it would come to no more than this.
And so the knight who had laid down his sword departs once again in search of his vanished family. Every road already points to its destination—but what he seeks lies beyond those roads.

act11mini.tagline: From snowy peaks to prison cells — treated like a country bumpkin, swapping out a face, penning letters behind bars: six stories from across the continent. People who live in utterly different places, in utterly different ways, yet who all longed for one and the same thing — a slightly better life.

chars.name: Sirius
chars.desc: A young lady sent to Victoria to represent the Browntail family. Hot-tempered and fiercely competitive.

chars.name: Yucatan
chars.desc: Sirius's husband. He looks after her at her side.

chars.name: Ratatos
chars.desc: The young head of the Browntail family and Sirius's elder sister.

chars.name: Yang Xian
chars.desc: The young magistrate who governs Shangshu in Yan.

chars.name: Miss Ying
chars.desc: An official who grew close to Yang Xian over shared cups of tea.

chars.name: Miss Du
chars.desc: An innkeeper's daughter preparing to set out for Yumen City.

chars.name: Lee
chars.desc: An old friend of Yang Xian's, come from Lungmen.

chars.name: Capone
chars.desc: A mafioso driven out of Siracusa.

chars.name: Gambino
chars.desc: Capone's longtime partner.

chars.name: Lappland
chars.desc: The Siracusan who orders the two mafiosi around.

chars.name: Rat King
chars.desc: A seasoned power broker of Lungmen's back alleys.

chars.name: Roy
chars.desc: An assassin of Kazimierz's Armorless Union.

chars.name: Hermann
chars.desc: Mayor of Siesta, the seaside city soon to become a mobile city.

chars.name: Euril
chars.desc: President of Mountaindash Logistics, a Lungmen freight company.

chars.name: Bison
chars.desc: Euril's son and an executive of Mountaindash.

chars.name: Jesselton
chars.desc: An agent of the Parasol company locked up in Mansfield Prison.

terms.name: Kjerag
terms.desc: The nation of snowy mountains. Browntail is one of its three great families.

terms.name: Shangshu
terms.desc: A riverside region within Yan. It is right in the middle of a wave of change.

terms.name: Armorless Union
terms.desc: An assassins' organization of Kazimierz. It trades its knights and assassins around like merchandise.

terms.name: Mountaindash Logistics
terms.desc: A freight company that does business circling the outskirts of Lungmen.

terms.name: Parasol
terms.desc: A Columbia company. The place Jesselton belongs to.

blocks.x: The continent of Terra has an awful lot of nations. From the snowy peaks of Kjerag to the arenas of Kazimierz to the prisons of Columbia — where people live differs, and so does how they live.
But look closely at their stories, and you'll find they're all wishing for the same thing. A slightly better life. Just that.
Here are six stories that played out across the continent. There's a negotiating table, a prison, a riverside teahouse. Let's hear them one by one.
Shall I teach you how to use your cutlery? — That country bumpkin was one of the three great families

**Sirius** came to Victoria representing Browntail, one of Kjerag's three great families. She's a hot-tempered, fiercely competitive young lady, and her husband **Yucatan** stays at her side, fetching her water and snacks.
But a Victorian merchant magnate named **Harrison** looks down on Sirius with a smile. Offering to teach her how to use her cutlery, and repeatedly getting 'Kjerag' wrong by calling it 'Kjeragg.'
Sirius bites her tongue and endures it. This is the very first time Browntail is joining hands with the outside world. Instead, she blows up where no one can hear — 'Aaargh, so annoying!'

Still, Sirius knows the truth. Victoria has trains, has smooth roads, has electric lights instead of candles — things Kjerag doesn't have. We need these things too, she thinks, and we can just build them all ourselves.

Her elder sister and family head **Ratatos** comes up too. The one who took the family onto her shoulders at a young age when their grandfather fell ill, pushing herself alone. And how, at the news that Enciodes of the SilverAsh family had laid everything down and left for Victoria to study, Ratatos quietly grew wistful.
That's when the banquet hall starts to fall apart. A noble called **Viscount Evans**, having fallen out with Harrison, has stormed in with his men.

Sirius and Yucatan quietly cut down the Viscount's men. Once the Viscount withdraws, Sirius approaches Harrison. Now that you've fallen out with the nobility, she says, wouldn't you be needing a new partner?
About the cooperation between us…… won't you sit down and talk it over with me one more time?
The old friend who came for one last drink, and why he never stepped forward in the end
Shangshu, in Yan, is right in the thick of change. **Yang Xian**, the young magistrate, must set out for the capital tonight, following the **Grand Tutor**.

Before leaving, Yang Xian watches a play with **Miss Ying** halfway up the mountain. They share tea; he takes note of which candies suit her taste. In his pocket he clutches a case holding a knotwork ornament, unable to bring himself to take it out.
If you had answered back then that you'd go without a single moment's hesitation, perhaps I too would have been a little upset.

The innkeeper's daughter **Miss Du** is getting ready to leave for Yumen City. Her father, **Boss Zheng**, presses into her hands an itinerary marking out the hard road, and urges her: once you've taken the lead, don't turn back.
And there's someone watching this scene from afar. Yang Xian's old friend, **Lee**, come from Lungmen.
Lee only tips his teacup, saying now isn't the time to step forward. He'd wanted one last drink with his old friend, he says, but it seems that won't be happening after all.
The letter said to bury you — so I burned that letter
In Lungmen's back alleys are two mafiosi driven out of Siracusa. **Capone** and **Gambino**. These days they're stuck running errands under 'that crazy woman,' walking on eggshells.

Their job was just to fetch a single letter, but things went sideways. Chased by the Guard Department, they barely got away thanks to rubber arrowheads. The letter in their hands bore the seal of Madam Sicily.
'That crazy woman' was **Lappland**. The letter was written with instructions to bury these two sorry fools out in the wilderness.
Lappland burns that letter in the fire. The order's become ash, so there's no reason to follow it, she says — and tells the two there's still work left for them to do.
Meanwhile, **Rat King** takes tea with Cadedu, Madam Sicily's envoy. He has no intention of meddling in Siracusa's affairs, but he makes it plain: in Lungmen, you follow Lungmen's rules.
Once business is done, Rat King quietly tells his subordinate to book a table for two at the hotel this evening. He's just having a meal with an old friend, he says.
If I change my face, will a better life come?
**Roy** of Kazimierz is an assassin of the Armorless Union. He used to be a country hunter, but he threw himself into this line of work to earn bigger money.

Roy and his comrade **Monique** decide to leave the organization in exchange for helping a Blood Knight escape. Together they erase their identities by faking their deaths, and Roy even changes his face to become 'Joel Tyrell.'
The night before he changes his face, Roy happens to drink with two people on the street. A weary corporate employee who's played hooky from work, and an infected knight who lost his match today.
The three of them get drunk and talk. A world where knights are merchandise and employees are expendable — and yet everyone just wishes for a slightly better life.
As they part, Roy quietly tells the infected knight to stay somewhere crowded tonight.
But when day breaks, the knight heads home alone.
And in a deserted alley, the Armorless Union's blade was waiting for him.
Roy starts a new life with a new face. Wondering what it'd be like to become a salesman, where he should go on his vacation.
The negotiator who gave a watch, and the mayor who worries for his daughter

The seaside city of Siesta will soon become a mobile city. To arrange logistics cooperation, Mayor **Hermann** welcomes **Euril**, president of a Lungmen freight company, along with the son **Bison** he's brought along.
Euril gifts a wristwatch and starts playing hardball. Let's grow Siesta into a neutral trade hub, he says; let's found a freight company led by Mountaindash. His ambitions are formidable.

But as the conversation flows on, the two find themselves talking as fathers. Hermann of the days his daughter — who kept him worried — studied abroad in Victoria; Euril of the days his ailing wife would scold him by letter.
Watching the sea beneath the setting sun, the two raise their glasses.
This liquor you brought from Lungmen has a truly fine taste. …… I believe it will suit not only Siesta, but the palates of people from other nations as well.
The company will surely come to save me — stay tuned to this channel
In Columbia's Mansfield Prison there's a prisoner named **Jesselton**. He's an agent of the Parasol company who infiltrated disguised as a prison guard, got caught, and ended up locked away instead.
Jesselton writes a letter to 'Mr. K.' Firmly convinced that, as an important employee of the company, they'll surely come to save him soon.
He tries to use a naive prisoner named **Rick**. By forging a letter supposedly sent by Rick's wife, he means to stoke the flames of revenge.

But the plan goes completely off the rails. Rick's wife really did sell the house to raise bail, and Rick is set free. The guards had known this a full week in advance — and they'd simply sat back and watched Jesselton flail about, trying to buy connections with pocket change.
Even so, Jesselton picks his letter back up and keeps writing. Signing it, 'Your most faithful servant, Jesselton Williams.'
But when the shot pulls back, all of this turns out to have been *True Story*, a program on Channel 58. People on the street laugh, calling Jesselton an actor who plays his part well.
…… But what if he isn't an actor — what if he's a person who truly exists? To that question, no one gives an answer.

From snowy peaks to prison cells, the six stories end like this. Where they live, and how they live, are all different. But the one thing they all had in common was this: every one of them wished for a slightly better life.

act10mini.tagline: The story of the day she finally decided to buy her very own shop with a lifetime's savings — only for that very shop to be swept up in someone else's scheme and reduced to ashes. And yet the green flame is not so easily snuffed out.

chars.name: Susie
chars.desc: A clerk at the 'Green Spark.' A cheerful, ever-smiling Feline Infected girl with sparks flying from her fingertips.

chars.name: Quercus
chars.desc: The manager of the 'Green Spark.' A courier who dashes tirelessly across the wastes.

chars.name: Haze
chars.desc: A Feline witch who wanders Caladon. She dotes tenderly on stray cats and Infected children.

chars.name: Grani
chars.desc: A mounted officer temporarily put in charge of the Infected district. One of the few peacekeepers willing to help the Infected.

chars.name: Skyfire
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island specialist who proposes Infected-relief measures to the city council. A young lady of the Mountvellan family.

terms.name: Caladon
terms.desc: A mobile city of Victoria. It has an 'Infected district' that takes in Infected from other cities and gives them work.

terms.name: Green Spark
terms.desc: A bar-cum-botanical-lamp craft shop in the Infected district. One of the few havens for Infected laborers.

terms.name: Rhodes Island
terms.desc: An outsider organization that treats the Infected and cooperates with the city council. It secretly helps the Infected district.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed Infected organization. After a great upheaval, it has shed its emblem and quietly supports the Infected.

terms.name: the Witches' Forest
terms.desc: An organization that once took in Infected Casters. It was burned down by the Victorian army five years ago.

blocks.x: Caladon, a mobile city of Victoria. In one of its corners lies an Infected district where the Infected live gathered together. The chimneys of the Originium factories belch smoke all night long, and workers coming off their shifts drift toward a single old shop.

That shop is the 'Green Spark.' Everyone takes it for a bar, but it's really a botanical-lamp craft shop, and behind the counter stands a Feline Infected girl, **Susie Glitter**. She has an Arts that sends sparks flying from her fingertips, and she smiles easily.
Susie has held a dream for a long time: to buy this shop and begin a life under her own name. And now, at last, that dream is almost within her grasp.
6,500 pounds — that scrap of paper was a bridge across to her dream

Fried fins, bland potato soup, and flavorless fruit wine are all the shop has to offer. Even so, it had its regulars — the Infected Stone and Bean, and Jims, who isn't Infected yet dropped by every day. Even on days when she was beaten in the streets for being Infected, in this shop at least Susie could smile.
For the Infected, a dream is a luxury. Most just fill their bellies however they can and live waiting for their illness to deepen. Even so, Susie scrimped for years and saved up 6,500 pounds.

On the appointed day, the shop's owner was supposed to come. But who showed up was a regular — **Bitter Root**, a 'specialist' from Rhodes Island. As it turned out, the Green Spark was a Rhodes Island asset, and he was its owner.
Susie said she wanted to open a hair salon. Her father had been a barber.
Cutting hair may seem like a trivial thing… but someone who wants to make themselves look nice surely still believes in life.
The contract was signed without fuss. The regulars crowded in to celebrate, and Susie ended up in tears. Tomorrow she'd be the manager. For an Infected, this was almost too luxurious a happiness.
But the next morning, smoke rose into the sky
A great fire shattered the peaceful morning.
The sign reading 'Green Spark' was left as nothing but a charred fragment, and the walls and roof had melted away. The place that until yesterday had been her dream and her future was now a ruin.
Someone had set the fire, and no one even knew why.
The will that had held the girl up could no longer support her frail body. Susie sank down where she stood.
To the girl standing at the building's edge, a stranger — a witch — spoke
A few days later, in an abandoned district on the city's outskirts. Susie stood precariously at the window of a collapsed building.
**Haze** appeared before her — a Feline witch who wanders Caladon, doting tenderly on stray cats and Infected children.
In truth, Haze was in the final stage of Oripathy. She had come to this deserted place to quietly end her life. And of all things, she had run into a child about to jump.
Haze scolded Susie. Someone else destroyed your dream — so why are you trying to ruin the last thing you have left?
If you've lost something, go take it back. The one who ought to fall off this building is the bastard who wrecked your dream. Isn't that right?
The moment Susie finally changed her mind, Haze collapsed. Dark red blood ran from her mouth and nose. Any Infected knows exactly what that means.

Susie hoisted the witch onto her back and ran into the city, crying out for anyone — anyone at all — to save her. Just then, **Red**, an Infected laborer who liked to sit alone in the shop reading books, appeared.
Burning down the shop wasn't an accident — it was the crumb of a certain plan
In front of the burned shop, the mounted officer **Grani** clenched her fist. She was one of the few peacekeepers willing to help the Infected district.
At the scene were fragments of a military incendiary shell. The guard corps wanted to write it off as an accident and bury it, but Grani dug in.
The incendiary shell came from the military factory of **Count Bishmar**. And the captain of the guard corps was Bishmar's nephew.

Around the same time, there was a kidnapping aimed at the aged **Councilman Angst**. Rioters disguised as Infected seized the Rhodes Island specialist **Skyfire** instead of the councilman and dragged her off to an empty factory.

That was a grave mistake. Skyfire was a Caster of the Mountvellan family, and after incinerating the entire stockpile of explosives with her flames to neutralize them, she subdued the rioters outright.

The pieces fell into place. Bishmar had siphoned off the factory's equipment with the city council's money, then meant to blow up the factory and pin it on 'the Infected' to cover up his embezzlement.

The fire at the Green Spark was an incendiary shell hurled by two of the rioters preparing that plan as they chased the fleeing Haze. Susie's dream, which had nothing to do with any of it, had been caught up in someone else's scheme and burned away.
In an underground passage, sparks flew
In the meantime, Susie had gone missing. To press her about the 'witness' he'd written into his report, the captain of the guard had dragged Susie into the underground logistics passage.
The shop owner **Quercus** made her move. Known as the 'Druid of Gododdin,' she was in truth a courier of Rhodes Island.

Just then an explosion far off shook the underground. The hideout where Grani had been cornering the rioters had blown up entirely, together with the Originium explosives piled inside. The collapsing structural floor came down on the guard captain.
In the chaos, Red appeared and flung Susie to safety. As it turned out, he was a formidable man who wielded a flaming sword.
And then Susie came face to face with Bishmar, who had survived.
A man who had burned down a shop and tried to harm countless innocent Infected, all for mere money. Sparks flew from every inch of Susie's body. She seized the councilman by the throat.
Who are you… to ruin my everyday life. Who do you think you are……
As Bishmar foamed at the mouth and his breath grew faint, Quercus rushed over and threw her arms around Susie. Even as the sparks seared her flesh, she would not let go.
Someone like this… isn't even worth killing. Let's go home.
There was no Green Spark left to go home to, Susie sobbed quietly.
Even in the ashes, a flame remained
Bishmar was taken into custody, and the guard corps was handed over to the mounted police. Councilman Angst's proposal passed by a margin of exactly one vote.
The 153-year-old Councilman Angst said he would step down next year. Saying he had no attachment left to his title or his station, he left behind just a single line.
The times must change, Mountvellan.
Susie became a 'city hero,' but all she held in her hands was one large certificate. Haze survived — Rhodes Island had treated her acute attack.

blocks.cap: The Green Spark may be gone, but there are still plenty of Infected who need a drink, Quercus tells Susie, urging her toward a fresh start.

blocks.x: Quercus asked her: how about making drinks somewhere else, and trying her hand at cutting hair? Wouldn't she like to pay Rhodes Island a visit?
Looking back, this city was full of hidden hands — Rhodes Island's Bitter Root and Quercus, and even Red and Bean of Reunion, who had shed their emblem to quietly prop up the Infected district.
Far out in the wastes, a suspicious merchant known as 'the Canned One' was extending a hand to Reunion, saying the world was falling apart. For better or worse, someone had to do something, he said.
At the end of the black storm clouds, a flame still glimmers.
The 'Green Spark' was reduced to ashes. But the green flame of that name has not yet gone out — neither on Susie's fingertips nor in this dark city.

act9mini.tagline: The story of people who, drawn to the dazzling arenas of Kazimierz, are instead swallowed by the city's shadows. And yet, each in their own way, they never stop asking: what am I really fighting for?

chars.name: Sona
chars.desc: A Zalak knight who's always giggling. She gathers Infected knights and leads a knightly order.

chars.name: Greynutty
alias: Knight of the Grey Brush Tail
chars.desc: A blunt but honest Zalak knight who wields a cannonlance. Sona's partner.

chars.name: Iwona
chars.desc: A loud, battle-crazed former expeditionary knight.

chars.name: Justyna
chars.desc: A taciturn sniper. She only ever says half of what she means.

chars.name: Margaret Nearl
chars.desc: A star knight who has returned to Kazimierz after a long journey.

chars.name: Maria Nearl
chars.desc: Margaret's younger sister. A knight determined to protect the Nearl family.

chars.name: Młynar
chars.desc: Margaret's uncle. A former knight who now runs the Nearl family's company.

chars.name: Malkiewicz
chars.desc: The Commerce Union's newly appointed spokesman. He was originally just an ordinary employee.

terms.name: Kazimierz
terms.desc: A nomadic nation where the entire country is obsessed with knightly sport. Knights are stars and commodities alike.

terms.name: Grand Knight Territory
terms.desc: The central city of Kazimierz. It holds the glamorous arenas and the Commerce Union's headquarters, while the Infected hide away in its darkest corners.

terms.name: Commerce Union
terms.desc: The massive commercial power that effectively rules Kazimierz.

terms.name: Armorless Union
terms.desc: The 'Knight Killer' outfit that disposes of disobedient knights in the shadows on the Commerce Union's behalf.

terms.name: Appraisal Council
terms.desc: A faction entangled with the Kazimierz Knights Association. It opposes the Commerce Union, yet takes an ambiguous stance on the issue of the Infected.

blocks.x: Kazimierz is a place where the entire country goes wild for knights. The roar of the arena, the flood of sponsorship money, the cheers of the fans... Beneath those dazzling lights, knights become stars by striking one another down.
But there are places that light never reaches. Because they carry Oripathy, Infected knights bleed in underground arenas just to scrape together a single knight's title.
This is the story of the people caught between that light and shadow. People who, each in their own place, keep asking themselves, "What am I really fighting for?"
She founded a knightly order made up entirely of the Infected
**Sona** is the Knight of the Flame Tail. She's a Zalak Infected, an optimist who's always giggling and acting laid-back. But beneath that smile lies a rock-hard resolve.

Just before a battle royale in the underground arena, Sona quietly extends a hand to an Infected knight she's never met before — to secretly form a team. Not to win, she says, but "because I don't want to let anyone else win." In a melee, if someone survives, someone else has to die.
Winning or losing doesn't matter — but if someone survives... it means someone else has to die, doesn't it?
The one who took her hand was **Greynutty**, the Knight of the Grey Brush Tail. A Zalak who holds grudges and comes off gruff, but is honest at heart. And so the two became a duo.
And then they gather Infected knights and found a knightly order. Its name: Pinus Sylvestris. Unlike other orders out to make money, it's about the Infected helping one another.

They pour every bit of their knightly prize money into hiding and feeding ordinary Infected who have nowhere to go, tucked away in the darkest corners of the Grand Knight Territory. But the more they do so, the tighter the Armorless Union — the ones who dispose of knights in the dark — closes in.
Better to be fertilizer for a new sprout than a rotting old tree, she said
Greynutty was originally of the Kaliska family — a knightly house once renowned for forestry in the south.

When she was little, her grandmother placed a cannonlance in her hands and taught her: "The cannons roar, and victory is at hand." Her grandmother, who'd said she would rather become fertilizer for a new sprout than rot away, called Greynutty the family's fine new sprout.

But once she was infected, that same family cast her out by unanimous vote. Knowing full well it had nothing to do with the disease, they pinned the whole decline of the house on her. The only one who took her side that day, they say, was Sona.

In the arena, Greynutty faces Ingra, the 'Knight of Rusted Copper' — a butcher who calls the Infected trash and jabs only at their sore spots. Greynutty puts on an act of being enraged, drags the fight into a long game, and utterly crushes his arrogance.
Then she trains her muzzle on the fallen Ingra... and quietly fires her last shot into the sky.
This shot I dedicate to the audience. You aren't worthy of it.
She'll bet her life for three days' worth of meals, apparently

**Iwona** is the Knight of the Rough Mane. Loud-mouthed and battle-crazed, she's a simple soul who'll do anything as long as her belly is full. She once aimed to become an expeditionary knight — a bereaved survivor of the Ursus war who lost her whole family to it.

Iwona had been held by the Commerce Union, and Sona and Greynutty secretly paid her bail to get her out. Iwona, none the wiser, joins up lightly, saying, "Give me three days' meal money and repair costs for my weapon, and I'll put my life on the line."
But later, learning the truth and not wanting to be in their debt, she chases after the two. Along the way, she sees Sona shielding an Infected child from hunters with her own body.
In that moment Iwona grins and says: why turn her down? She's damn cool.
The truth is, Iwona's compound lance has never once broken in the arena. Because she never truly intended to take anyone's life.
She promised to return home once she became a knight
**Justyna** is the Knight of the Fang, a taciturn sniper. She only ever says half of what she means — a country girl who used to play a grass whistle.

She left after promising the folks back home she'd return in glory, but on the road to the Grand Knight Territory she ended up infected. Refused knight registration for being Infected, she wandered for three weeks and barely scraped together the registration fee through black-market matches.

After joining Pinus Sylvestris, Justyna smuggles the wounded 'Knight of Plastic' out of the hospital — even though the two don't even like each other. Because he has a home to return to, and a son waiting for him.
Justyna has none of that. The knight Justyna has a hometown, but the Infected Justyna has nowhere to go.
I won't make any sound but the melody of a grass whistle. Because I want to keep Justyna as she was — a pure, ordinary girl who simply dreams of becoming a knight.
The Knight of Light returned, breaking through the arena wall
And then a star long absent returns to Kazimierz. **Margaret Nearl**, the Knight of Light.

The moment she's back, she crosses blades with her uncle, **Młynar**. The Nearl family motto is "Fear neither hardship nor darkness." Yet her uncle is a man who gave up glory long ago and threw himself away as an ordinary company man.
Her younger sister **Maria** offers to hand over her own points for her sister's sake. She admits she never wanted to be a knight — she only wishes for this house to still be their home.

blocks.cap: For what? For the family, for oneself, for a knight's glory.

blocks.x: Even so, Margaret decides to enter the tournament again — even if no one understands the choice. Because light isn't about driving out the darkness; it's about lighting a new path for those who've lost their way.
Even if this is what Kazimierz has become, so long as we don't lose our faith, that too is something meaningful.
He just happened to be there, and yet he became a big shot
**Malkiewicz** was, until yesterday, an ordinary employee fretting over his rent. He was a nameless liaison who connected Infected knights to knightly orders.

But on the whim of some big shot, he gets a single phone call and overnight becomes the Commerce Union's spokesman — a 'big shot' who wears a tailored suit, carries an invitation, and walks into the press conference.

He himself insists that no matter how he looks at it, the outfit doesn't suit him, but the people around him already see him as a big shot — regardless of what he thinks. Malkiewicz, who couldn't take off the suit even when liquor spilled on it, finally mutters this:
By now... somehow it seems I can no longer take this suit off.
She dreamed of knights, and ended up a Knight Killer
The last story belongs to an archer whose future once looked bright. A good shot, pretty of face, obedient to company policy — a promising talent about to receive her knight's title.
But with match-fixing, baseless scandals, and schedules changed at whim, she'd already grown sick of being a knight. At least a commodity is done with once it's sold — so what is she, she wondered.

Then she gets a phone call — pass a simple 'test' and she'll be given a better job. The test was to snipe a past opponent, and she pulls the trigger. And so she becomes a member of the Armorless Union.
Not long after, the wavering previous 'Platinum' dies trying to protect a target. Lazurite presses the badge into her hand and says: you be Platinum.

She silently disposes of one of the directors and looks out at the Grand Knight Territory beyond the window — realizing that even the directors are replaceable puppets, and that an unseen hand keeps stacking up the towering spire that is Kazimierz.
I want... to rest......
So then, whose light is it for?

The grinning optimist, the exiled noble, the starving warrior, the taciturn girl, the returned star, the accidental big shot, the weary killer... all of them are caught in the same machine of the same city.
And that machine hasn't stopped yet. Even the Infected hideout Sona tried so hard to protect is discovered, and the story halts right in the middle of it all.
The arena lights won't go out today either. And beneath that dazzling glow, people are still asking: this light — just who is it for?

act8mini.tagline: A memory-lost Doctor wanders every corner of Rhodes asking 'who was I before?', only to face the fact that they themselves are the greatest mystery of all. They say hope is cruel — but we walk on anyway.

chars.name: Doctor
chars.desc: The commander of Rhodes Island. Returned from Chernobog having lost nearly all of their memories.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The leader of Rhodes Island. A young Cautus girl.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's chief physician and most senior member. The Doctor's personal doctor.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: A Feline operator of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Canot
chars.desc: A slick merchant roaming the Gaspar Wilderness. Insists on being called 'Mister Good Enough.'

chars.name: Earthspirit
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's Disaster Messenger.

chars.name: Eyjafjalla
chars.desc: A young volcanologist newly arrived at Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Closure
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's chief engineer. Of the Vampire clan.

chars.name: Beagle
chars.desc: A Perro operator who tends the memorial storage room.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary entangled with the Doctor's past.

chars.name: Theresa
chars.desc: The Sarkaz king who led Babel. She appears in flashbacks.

chars.name: PRTS
chars.desc: The terminal service system that runs Rhodes Island — the ship's brain.

terms.name: Mamazons
terms.desc: Tikaronto's largest local conglomerate. It reportedly started out as a doughnut chain.

terms.name: Disaster Messenger
terms.desc: A profession that predicts Terra's natural disasters — 'Catastrophes' — and guides people to safety.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease caused by Originium infection. The body eventually hardens like stone.

terms.name: Babel
terms.desc: The organization the Sarkaz king Theresa led in Kazdel. The predecessor of Rhodes Island.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: The city where the Doctor was found inside a sarcophagus, with almost all memories lost.

blocks.x: Terra is a land scoured by natural cataclysms called 'Catastrophes,' where a mineral called Originium spreads wealth and power while at the same time sowing an incurable disease, Oripathy. Rhodes Island is a pharmaceutical company on wheels, sailing the desert with a vow to cure those infected.

The commander of that Rhodes Island, the **Doctor**, was ambushed in Tikaronto, a metropolis of Columbia. The group was scattered, and the Doctor ended up dumped in the middle of an unfamiliar wilderness together with the leader, **Amiya**.
But the real problem lies elsewhere. The Doctor right now barely even knows who they used to be — they lost almost all their memories not long ago.
And every time they fall asleep, a certain voice keeps whispering.
To hold on to hope is a very cruel thing.
Ever seen a cop who fires a crossbow before asking questions?

What remained in the raided city were **Dylan**, a low-altitude aircraft pilot, and **Blaze**, a Feline operator. Dylan, more at home in a cockpit, ended up scrambling through back alleys clutching a crossbow that had snapped in half.
The fallen attacker wore a 'police' uniform, but the ID badge was fake to anyone's eyes. Blaze remarks, dumbfounded.
You really think there's a cop who fires a fully automatic crossbow the moment they ask a question?

The ones chasing them this relentlessly were mercenaries hired by **Mamazons**, Tikaronto's biggest corporation. Their target: the Oripathy medicine samples Rhodes Island had brought. They had already seized a good portion of them back at the hotel.
I woke up in the wilderness, and there was a guy in an iron bucket helmet

Having barely escaped the city, the Doctor and Amiya landed in the Gaspar Wilderness. When the Doctor came to, a man wearing a crude iron bucket helmet stood before them. A self-proclaimed merchant, **Canot** — he'd like to be called 'Mister Good Enough.'

In exchange for two Originium Primes as a guide fee, Canot agrees to take the two of them to the nearest **Disaster Messenger** outpost. A little farther south, he says, and they'd have plunged straight into a poison-gas swamp.

When night fell, Amiya deftly picked out wild greens and mushrooms and stirred a mess tin. Most people of the mobile cities can't survive outdoors, but Amiya was different. It was the Doctor, she said, who had taught her directly a few years ago.
The Doctor has no memory of that. Amiya smiled a little wistfully, then went back to stirring the mess tin as if nothing were wrong.
As long as you're here like this, Doctor, someday everything will be all right.
The 'suppressant' handed out to the Infected was actually just a painkiller

Arriving at the outpost, Canot takes the Doctor's group to a Mamazons pioneer corps office. There, the Infected who were pioneering the wilderness were being issued 'Oripathy suppressant' every month. But the medicine Amiya was handed wasn't suppressant at all — it was a painkiller and a nutritional supplement.

Mamazons, it turns out, was originally a doughnut chain. With no pharmaceutical technology of its own, it bought up others' production lines and passed painkillers off as suppressant. And if it got hold of Rhodes Island's samples too, it could manufacture an even more convincing fake 'hope.'
Canot says it plainly. Even if you tell people the truth, in the face of immediate pain relief, the truth doesn't matter — the very same words as that voice in the Doctor's sleep-talk.
People come to Columbia carrying hope, create value within hope, and die within hope.
Even so, Amiya said she couldn't just stand by and watch this. And the Doctor quietly asked Canot for something.
Instead of taking the goods from the enemy, the Doctor walked into a jail of their own accord

A few days later, the Doctor walks out of a Columbia detention center. What the Doctor had done was this: turn themselves in to the Border Administration, confessing that 'our company smuggled Oripathy medicine.'

In Columbia, Oripathy medicine is the most strictly controlled of all. The Bureau, by regulation, confiscates and destroys all the contraband — that is, the samples. And so the path for Mamazons to upgrade its fake suppressant with Rhodes Island's technology was blocked. The price was three days' detention for the Doctor.

Before leaving, Canot bids farewell, saying it had been a delightful journey. But this slick merchant had another face: what he had with him was a band of wilderness outlaws, the 'Rusty Hammers.' Even that, the Doctor had seen through long ago.
Steering a ship into the eye of a storm — isn't that just suicide?

blocks.cap: The main ship of Rhodes Island accelerating toward the heart of a Catastrophe.

blocks.x: Back on Rhodes Island, the Doctor is met by the physician **Kal'tsit**. Over a month, Kal'tsit had scanned the Doctor's brain seven times. Her conclusion: while the memories of the Doctor's own self were lost, the procedural memory ingrained in the body was rapidly returning.

Around that time, the Disaster Messenger **Earthspirit** picks up anomalous data. A massive cyclone was gathering ahead and forming a core. To hold course meant colliding with hundreds of ore bombs, and it was already too late to retreat.

blocks.cap: The Catastrophe cyclone, now right before their eyes.

blocks.x: To the Doctor who appears on the bridge, Amiya hands over all command authority of Rhodes Island without hesitation. In that brief window, the Doctor calculated a safe route and drove the ship right through the storm. The result: less than 5% deck damage, and zero casualties.
The closer we draw to despair, the more magnificent and beautiful the scenery becomes when gazed upon with hope.

That formula was, in fact, made by the Doctor of the past themselves. Kal'tsit says: not for a single moment did you ever forget that you are an Originium researcher. **Eyjafjalla**, the young volcanologist who first pinpointed the anomaly, said too that people like the Doctor kept showing her the hope to keep moving forward.
The red safe the whole ship couldn't open — turned out to be full of beer
And so three months passed. The Doctor wandered every corner of Rhodes Island, picking up fragments of who they once were. Every one of them was nothing but a sensation of 'unfamiliar yet nostalgic.'

In the memorial storage room, the Perro operator **Beagle** was polishing a badly broken shield — the shield of Ace, an old comrade no longer at her side. In that room were also piled the keepsakes of the Reunion members who had fallen at Lungmen.
There were enemies, and there were friends. The Doctor may not know each person's story, but the suffering of the Infected — that, they know. Beagle said the operators sometimes ask themselves.
Was all of it — truly worth it?

Amid the heavy questions there was a rather absurd incident too. A red machine in a corner of the warehouse — something the chief engineer **Closure** had failed to open even after wrecking three cutting machines — slid open the moment the Doctor gave it a tap. Inside was a beer vending machine. Around that time, the Sarkaz mercenary **W** was quietly leaving the ship, heading for Londinium.
It wasn't Her Majesty they betrayed, but the Her Majesty they'd painted in their own mind
The rising fragments of memory keep taking the Doctor back to **Babel**, to the days when the Sarkaz king and leader **Theresa** was there.

On the day Theresa, pushed out by the Military Commission, decided to withdraw from Kazdel, a Sarkaz soldier raids the Doctor's camp — furious that a member of another race stood by Her Majesty's side. But the Doctor stops the veteran mercenary scout who tries to kill him, and instead says this.
What you don't believe in isn't me — it's your own Her Majesty.

As the Doctor saw it, that soldier was less a traitor than a man shifting the blame because he was terrified of himself for no longer being able to believe in Theresa. In the end, he called himself 'a Sarkaz of Kazdel' and said he would accept Her Majesty's judgment; not long after, unwilling to be a burden to his king, he took his own life.
Theresa received the news with a sorrowful face. The road is long, she said, and that is exactly why people crave answers they can see right now. That Theresa is no longer by the Doctor's side.
So — what is it you fight for?

blocks.cap: The deck at dusk, the Doctor and Kal'tsit standing side by side.

blocks.x: On the deck as the sun sets, Kal'tsit asks the Doctor: how must one live to truly be alive? If you've submitted to another's will and lost your reason to move forward, is that being alive?

blocks.cap: Kal'tsit gazing at the Doctor with meaningful eyes.

blocks.x: And finally, she asks them to look her in the eye and tell her: just what is it you fight for? In that moment, every memory since waking in Chernobog surged up, and the Doctor blurted out an answer almost unconsciously. What they said, though — we couldn't hear it.
Because even if no one understands, we have to walk on like this.
Insufficient authority — one question no one can answer

On the last night, the Doctor goes to the ship's brain, **PRTS**. Just moments earlier Closure had been telling PRTS all about the Doctor she'd been watching all this time — that Doctor who, before and after losing their memories, was clearly the same person and yet somehow different.
The Doctor poses PRTS a single question. But instead of answering, PRTS only sounds a warning tone.
Among the members currently registered, no one holds the authority to answer that question.

Instead, in the middle of a self-check, PRTS turns up an old recording that wasn't even password-protected. Date: unknown. Title: 〈PRTS First Function Test〉; filename: 〈Onboard Video Record 000000001α〉.

blocks.cap: The oldest record, unearthed by PRTS.

blocks.x: 'I have provided the answer to your question, Doctor.' So says PRTS, and three seconds later it begins counting down, announcing it will lock the authority. 3. 2. 1. There the story stops.

The voice heard every time they fall asleep still whispers that hope is cruel. Still not knowing who they once were. And yet the Doctor — and Rhodes Island — walk forward again today. Because even if no one understands, this is the road they must take.

act7mini.tagline: Six short chronicles about people who meant to run, to hide, or simply to pass through — yet kept reaching out to grasp another's hand. A story about how what we were really looking for was never a 'place to belong,' but 'someone to stay by our side.'

chars.name: Ila
chars.desc: A former Reunion warrior who has left Ursus behind and set out for the wastes.

chars.name: Gareth
chars.desc: Ila's comrade-in-arms who fled alongside her; a former Ursus soldier.

chars.name: Heavyrain
chars.desc: A young Kuranta mercenary who crossed over from Sargon.

chars.name: Pallas
chars.desc: A priestess whom the villages of Minos call their 'goddess.'

chars.name: Kirara
chars.desc: An operator who loves horror games but is painfully shy around strangers.

chars.name: Bena
chars.desc: A Victorian kid who loves to show off her storytelling skills.

chars.name: Blue Poison
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator who wields poison.

chars.name: Glaucus
chars.desc: An engineer operator from Iberia.

chars.name: Aria
chars.desc: An apprentice lighthouse keeper guarding a lighthouse whose flame has gone dark.

chars.name: Carnelian
chars.desc: A Sargonian attendant who serves Count Hyacinth.

chars.name: Cliffheart
chars.desc: The youngest of the SilverAsh family and a mountaineer operator.

chars.name: Courier
chars.desc: A dependable retainer who serves the SilverAsh family.

terms.name: Rusted Hammer
terms.desc: A band of those who have turned their backs on civilization and chosen to belong to the wastes. As promised, only those who survive a month in the canyon are accepted as brethren.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization of the Infected that arose in Ursus.

terms.name: Minos
terms.desc: A land at war with Sargon. In the villages of the Akroti region, the priestess Pallas is revered as a 'goddess.'

terms.name: Iberia
terms.desc: A nation whose coastline was swallowed by darkness in a Seaborne Catastrophe decades ago. Thousands of lighthouses once guarded its seas.

terms.name: Leithanien
terms.desc: A nation ruled by its empresses. It is a land where the crimson terror of the Witch King once blotted out the sky.

blocks.x: Across the land called Terra, every nation carries its own tale. This record isn't one grand event tying everything together — it's six short stories about people living in six different places.
One leaves home, one runs away, one just means to pass through. And yet, strangely enough, every one of them stops in their tracks and ends up grasping someone's hand.
Perhaps what each of these six was searching for was, in the end, 'a place where I'm allowed to be.'
To Prove You're No Beast of Burden, Just Survive a Month in the Canyon

**Ila** is a former Reunion warrior who has left Ursus behind and is heading into the wastes. Together with her comrade **Gareth**, she was walking in search of an organization called the 'Rusted Hammer,' rumored to be a band of brutal marauders.

Rumors are just rumors, Gareth says. He'd seen it once, back in his army days: an Ursus unit that had crossed into Kazimierz was wiped out by that very band — a horde that freed the Infected from the mines and smashed every trace of Ursus as it charged in like madmen.

A rusted hammer stood driven upside-down into a mountaintop. The man guarding it tosses the pair a shovel and a pickaxe: survive one month down in that canyon. That, he says, is the condition for joining the 'Rusted Hammer.'

A month of hunting burdenbeasts, scattering bone meal to plant barley, and wandering in search of water. But on the way to find water, they spot the bootprints of Ursus soldiers — the pursuit party of Colonel Lomono, come to capture Gareth.
Gareth says they should pack up and flee. But this time, Ila won't budge.
I won't run today. If I die, I'll die in this canyon. Everything here is all I have — it's our blood and sweat, and this is our new home.
