It wasn't only the Ursus army that witnessed her resolve. The 'Rusted Hammer' warrior who came each day to check whether the two were still breathing finally declares them accepted.

Warriors of the wastes packed both cliffs of the canyon, encircling the twenty-four Ursus soldiers. When Ila offers to fight alongside them, the warrior answers: we don't fight — we only break. The sound of hammered iron shook the canyon.
Saved From the Brink of Death, the Guest Swung a Weapon the Moment She Woke
This time, we're far away in Minos. In this land at war with Sargon, **Heavyrain** is found collapsed near the border — a young Kuranta mercenary who had crossed over from Sargon.

The one who took her in is **Pallas**, the priestess whom the villages of Minos call their 'goddess.' The Sarkaz mercenary at her side was wary, warning that she might be a spy, but Pallas insists on treating her, saying she cannot abandon a life so long as there's no danger.

The moment she wakes, Heavyrain swings her weapon — though the cramped tent blocks the motion. Pallas, in turn, sets her own weapon down. Looking at that weapon without a single fresh scratch, she says: you haven't fought recently. So there's no need to be afraid.

Heavyrain has no destination — she simply wants to go far away. The wastes, the battlefield, Sargon: right now she doesn't want to face any of it. Pallas presses a working compass into her hand and prays for her.
We are all walking an endless journey, enduring pain to protect our own souls. And the more important thing we must all face is this: to find a resting place of our own.

That night, a Sarkaz killer who had been trailing Heavyrain sets their sights on Pallas. The one who stood in their way was the Sarkaz mercenary. The battle in the dark was never recorded for posterity. Only this: Heavyrain's journey continued, and one day the light of her soul would drift toward Rhodes Island.
The Kid Who Set Out to Scare Her Big Sister Turned Out to Be the Scarier Tale Herself

The scene shifts abruptly to a Rhodes Island lounge. While looking for her doll, Annie, **Bena** discovers **Kirara** engrossed in a game in front of the TV — an operator who loves horror games but is terribly shy around strangers.

Bena's pleading to play together leaves Kirara flustered — worried about age ratings, she starts by turning off the blood effects. Even so, the two take down a hidden boss together, and Kirara gleefully explains the Shin-Akishi ghost story and the true ending. A ghost story, she says, loses its power the moment its truth is revealed.
In return, Bena tells an old Victorian ghost story: of an old woman who takes orphans away on rainy days, of a child-sized doll named 'Annie,' and of the children who never came back.

Just as Kirara is thoroughly spooked, the doll Annie quietly opens its mouth and greets her — Bena's mischievous little act. And yet, the lingering sense that the child in that frightening old tale was somehow Bena herself quietly remains behind the laughter.
It's just... hardly anyone's ever been the first to talk to me before.
Its Flame Dark for Decades, Still Someone Guards the Lighthouse No One Comes To

Somewhere in northern Iberia, a coastal city ruined by the Seaborne Catastrophe. **Blue Poison** and **Glaucus** descend underground to investigate a building fallen to ruin — the operator who wields poison, and the engineer from Iberia.

Inside lay the corpse of a bounty hunter, bound by seaweed and drowned — a trace of how this place had been submerged by seawater twice. In the dark, the two speak cautiously of the Ægir, of poisons, and of that catastrophe too dreadful to name.
Deeper in, a white light flickers. Caught by Blue Poison's arrow was a lost Iberian caster, **Aria**. She's the one who tells them that this building is, in fact, a lighthouse.

Aria is an apprentice lighthouse keeper. She says she still guards the lighthouse — one that has never been able to light its flame again since the catastrophe — together with her teacher. Even though the ships crossing the sea vanished long ago.
The light we make can only illuminate a tiny corner, that's all. The ships that once traveled the sea vanished long ago — and yet we still keep the lighthouse.

Glaucus suggests taking the broken energy core back to Rhodes Island to study it, and Aria makes up her mind to join them. Then, belatedly reacting to Blue Poison's neurotoxin, she keels over — only to come right back around. This lighthouse might just become a new Rhodes Island outpost guarding the coast.
Bored of the Noble City, She Went for a Walk — and Picked Up a Runaway Infected
This is Leithanien. **Carnelian** is a Sargonian attendant who has come here in Count Hyacinth's stead for a cooperation summit. Being of a different race, she drew plenty of unkind stares.

Bored of the glittering downtown, she walks toward the outskirts and helps an Infected girl being chased by the guards. The girl's story went like this: in a mining town, her parents never came home from a shaft collapse, and the guards sealed off the village and hauled its people away.

When a Leithanien caster shows up after her, Carnelian bears down on him fiercely — pressing him about the Arts that enslave the Infected, like a dark remnant left behind by the Witch King. The caster hands the girl over and withdraws.
Carnelian entrusts the girl to a Rhodes Island operator hidden nearby. The girl prepares to set off — to find the children scattered every which way, and one day to avenge them all.
As long as you hold on to the conviction you have now, don't you think we might meet again someday?
You may refine yourself to fit a foreign land, but there's no need to force yourself into its mold. Better to remain an outsider — to simply be true to yourself, she thinks.
To Send Her Sister a Single Letter, the Young Lady Secretly Climbed All the Way Up the Snowy Mountain

The last stop is Rhodes Island once more. **Cliffheart** is the youngest of the SilverAsh family and a mountaineer operator. She wants to deliver a single letter to her sister Enya, who left for the Holy Mountain and became a Saint.

She asks **Courier**, a retainer of the family, but with Kjerag's situation complicated right now, the Manju Order strictly bars any contact with the Saint. Told that the letter would be hard to deliver, Cliffheart pretends to give up.
Then she secretly packs her mountaineering gear and heads for the mountain alone. Courier, catching on, belatedly catches up and tries to stop her — why go so far as to do something this dangerous?
Cliffheart answers: she can no longer pretend not to notice how the relationship between her brother Enciodes and her sister keeps worsening.
If even family won't try to understand one another — if the day ever comes when they turn against each other — then what are we supposed to do?!

In the end, Courier throws up his hands and agrees to deliver the letter in her stead — though the monastery is as far as he can go, and he can't promise the Saint will actually receive it. Some time later, Courier returns wearing a somehow heavy expression: this time, there's something SilverAsh has asked him to pass along to Cliffheart.
What It Means to Find a Place to Belong

The wastes, Minos, a lounge, a lighthouse, a noble city, a snowy mountain. The stages were all different, but the six were really doing the same thing — stopping mid-flight to protect someone, and grasping an unfamiliar hand.

Aria headed for Rhodes Island cradling the lighthouse's core, and the darkened lighthouse may yet become an outpost guarding the coast once more. And Heavyrain's journey, too, will one day drift toward that light.
Six people from different nations, with different tales. And yet, in the end, they were all searching for the same thing: a place where they're allowed to be, and someone to stay by their side.

act15d5.tagline: A story of people who took to the road each for their own reasons, carrying different tales, who in the end all drift toward the same single ship. On a divided land the roads never break off, and at their end someone is always reaching out a hand.

chars.name: Hoshiguma
chars.desc: An inspector of the Lungmen Guard Department. A one-horned Oni who fights with a shield as her partner.

chars.name: Swire
chars.desc: Head of the Guard Department's special inspection team, and Hoshiguma's next-door junior.

chars.name: Shirayuki
chars.desc: A quiet swordswoman who knows Ch'en well.

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: A former police officer who left the Lungmen Guard Department to join Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Chiave
chars.desc: An optimistic young man from the lowest rung of Siracusa's Sicilia League. He founded his own 'Chiave Family'.

chars.name: Aosta
chars.desc: The strategist of the Chiave Family. Cautious and prone to nagging.

chars.name: Broca
chars.desc: The assault leader of the Chiave Family. Taciturn and strong.

chars.name: Jaye
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator with a great talent for cooking.

chars.name: Iris
chars.desc: A young lady raised in 'the Castle'. She has come to Victoria to keep an old promise.

chars.name: Hildegard
chars.desc: A nun of Laterano's Landon Monastery. She traveled all the way to Lungmen to save her monastery.

chars.name: Bison
chars.desc: A young man working at Mountaindash Logistics. He is Euril's son.

chars.name: Snowsant
chars.desc: A timid engineer from Columbia.

chars.name: Texas
chars.desc: The driver of Penguin Logistics, a Lupo.

chars.name: Croissant
chars.desc: A hammer-wielding member of Penguin Logistics.

chars.name: Sora
chars.desc: A former idol of Penguin Logistics.

chars.name: Leonhardt
chars.desc: A Catastrophe Messenger from Rim Billiton.

chars.name: Ayerscarpe
chars.desc: Leonhardt's partner. A taciturn man who wields a sword.

chars.name: Greyy
chars.desc: A young Rhodes Island Caster who maintains the ship's power facilities.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The leader of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Rosmontis
chars.desc: A young Rhodes Island operator.

chars.name: Myrrh
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island Medic well-versed in herbs and their blending.

chars.name: Utage
chars.desc: A well-connected and personable Rhodes Island operator.

chars.name: Beanstalk
chars.desc: A Rhodes Island operator who commands small animals.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease that hardens the body and eventually leads to death. The Infected face discrimination everywhere, and if discovered are often dragged off to quarantine zones.

terms.name: Catastrophe Messenger
terms.desc: A profession that observes the signs of Catastrophes and delivers evacuation warnings to cities. It is dangerous, and when an evacuation turns out to be for nothing, they can even end up resented.

terms.name: Lungmen Guard Department
terms.desc: The armed body responsible for keeping the peace in Lungmen, a city of Yan.

terms.name: Sicilia League
terms.desc: A Siracusan organization made up of many small families.

terms.name: Landon Monastery
terms.desc: A venerable monastery in Laterano, now facing financial hardship.

blocks.x: Terra is a land torn apart by Catastrophes, borders, and an incurable disease known as Oripathy. Between one city and the next stretches an endless wilderness.
There is no grand event in this story. It simply holds seven short fragments of travel, of people who took to the road each for their own reasons.
One sets out to get some fresh air on a day off, one flees in a stolen car, one goes to keep a promise made twenty years ago. And yet, strangely, those roads keep pointing in the same direction.
The inspector who banged on her neighbor's door first thing on her day off

**Hoshiguma** is an inspector of the Lungmen Guard Department, a one-horned Oni who fights with a shield as her partner. On a rare day off, she is up at dawn picking out shoes, then banging on the door next door.

The one who opens it is her neighbor and junior, **Swire**. With all the mess Reunion left behind piling up, she hasn't even slept. And now that a certain 'dragon'—Ch'en—is gone, all the Guard Department's work has fallen to Swire.

The breakfast Hoshiguma brought is two servings of fried noodles. She says her hand 'just reached for it out of habit,' but with mushrooms, a vegetable stir-fry sauce, and an extra-large portion, one of them was tuned exactly to Ch'en's taste—even though Ch'en left for Rhodes Island quite a while ago.

Leaving Swire to sleep, Hoshiguma takes out her beloved ride—the motorcycle 'Partner'—and heads for a circuit on the city's outskirts. She mutters to herself that she's worried about Ch'en's foul temper over at Rhodes—

**Shirayuki** appears and says flatly that Ch'en is no longer stubborn and has grown more open-hearted, so there's nothing to worry about. Then she hands over a comic book—the debut work of a rookie manga artist, she says.

The protagonist on the cover is a tall figure holding a shield. It looks oddly familiar—and it's her. Guessing who must have drawn it, Hoshiguma snorts a laugh: 'What nerve.' What should she call that girl the next time they meet? 'Operator Ch'en' suits her rather well.

As Hoshiguma rides the circuit, some latest-model bike overtakes her. Admitting a complete defeat, she finds her opponent is Chief Wei's wife, Fumizuki. That flashy bike parked beneath the Chief's building, the one that never suited his taste—she only now realizes it was actually his wife's.
A stolen car, an empty fuel tank, and one piece of good news and one bad

In the middle of the wilderness, a broken-down car. **Chiave**, his strategist **Aosta**, and his assault leader **Broca**—three young men who call themselves the 'Chiave Family.' They've just rebelled against a boss who tried to sell them out and fled from Bite City.
Since the car is stolen, they have no camping gear, no spare fuel—nothing properly equipped. Even so, Chiave is thrilled, saying he even likes the phrase 'the road to death.'
But Chiave has Oripathy. So does Broca. Even as they blast music and crack jokes, they keep coughing up blood. Aosta acts unfazed, but he's actually the most frightened of them all.

When Chiave says that staying together all day will get him infected too, Aosta answers that he doesn't care if he catches it—because it's better than just standing by, watching the two of them suffer—

They accidentally rescue a merchant caught by robbers and flee together, the fuel runs dry, and in the end Chiave loses consciousness. That's when a Rhodes Island operator with the codename Straightedge appears. She takes the three of them to Rhodes Island's office in Locomotiva and hands Chiave over to a Medic.

All of this is an old tale told late at night in the Rhodes Island canteen, with **Jaye**'s cooking as a side dish. The story of those days—when Aosta even gave up the car to cover the medicine—goes on into the small hours, along with the drinks.
The one who came to return a doll to a child of twenty years ago

**Iris** is a young lady raised in 'the Castle.' The people there invite good children to the Castle, safeguard the precious treasures the children entrust to them, and return them at the promised time—to prove that fairy tales are eternal.

Iris's first mission is to find the village of Tonnerre in Victoria and deliver the promised item to a woman named Mabel Green. A name and a few lines describing her appearance are all the clues she has, and since it happened nearly twenty years ago, the village has already been swallowed up by factories and vanished.

Wandering house to house asking after her, Iris meets Dora, a little girl with Oripathy who hides away at home. Unable to go outside, she plays only with her doll. Iris tells her a story of a castle where dolls come to life—but—

Dora shakes her head. The doll was a birthday gift from her mom and dad, and she never wants to part with it—her treasure is hers to protect, she says. In the end, unable to grant any wish, Iris can only leave an apology behind.
If the fairy found out she couldn't grant my wish, how sad would she be? So I mustn't tell her.

The one who helped Dora was a Rhodes Island field operator who happened to be passing through the village. She points Dora's parents toward a place where the Infected can receive proper treatment. And she tells Iris that she thinks she knows who the Mabel she's looking for might be—
Though it may already be too late, she adds—so it would be best to go meet her in person. Iris's journey truly begins from here.
The sister who came to save her monastery and fell head over heels for the land

**Hildegard** is a nun of Laterano's Landon Monastery. With the venerable monastery on the verge of 'bankruptcy' due to financial trouble, she has come all the way to the unfamiliar Yan city of Lungmen in search of a way out.

Her goal is a contract with Mountaindash Logistics, a major Lungmen shipping company, but its head, Euril, has already prepared everything so perfectly that there's little room for Hildegard to step in. The contract goes smoothly—leaving her, if anything, a little deflated.

Instead, Hildegard roams around Lungmen, captivated by all the things she's never seen before: the timid engineer **Snowsant** in the room across from hers; Penguin Logistics' **Texas**, **Croissant**, and **Sora** drifting through downtown just to catch a single thief; an idol concert broadcast; and even Emperor, the bartender with the flamboyant way of talking.

The big picture Euril is painting is to build bridges between divided nations, with messengers leading the way. On this land that neither Catastrophes nor arrogance could fully divide, he asks, why shouldn't there be at least one road?

Hildegard postpones her scheduled return home, having grown eager to see a little more of this land. Before she leaves, Emperor passes her a note through Euril—the contact address of a certain company, marked 'Rhodes Island.'
Why is it that those who warn of Catastrophe are always resented?

A buggy heading toward a Catastrophe-warning zone. Aboard are the Catastrophe Messenger **Leonhardt**, his partner **Ayerscarpe**, and **Greyy**, the young Rhodes Island Caster on her first field assignment.

Following a distress signal from Rim Billiton, the three talk about what it means to be a Catastrophe Messenger. It's a thankless yet vital job—warning of Catastrophes so cities can evacuate—but when an evacuation turns out to be for nothing, people often turn cold, calling them 'ill-omened messengers.'
Greyy, wanting to repay the Rhodes Island that took her in, is anxious to shoulder heavier responsibilities. To her, Ayerscarpe says calmly:
What matters is not what you did or how great an achievement you made, but what you strove for.

Whether the distress signal is a rioters' trap or a genuine emergency, they won't know until they arrive. Still, the Greyy of now is ready to face whatever the outcome may be—because this, too, is a part of life that can only be seen once you leave the ship.
The 'apostle of justice' who caught the culprit, tied him up in front of the police station, and vanished
**Ch'en**, who left Lungmen to join Rhodes Island, is now in Columbia. She chases down an Infected man who fled after being falsely accused of murder, cornering him.

Ch'en knows he is not the murderer. But she won't let him go, saying the crime of injuring a police officer and fleeing cannot be denied. In exchange, she promises she will clear his name no matter what.

Between the moments of the story, Ch'en thinks of **Amiya** and **Rosmontis**. Who has the right to judge another? How should one treat a person who has done wrong in an unjust land?—questions without answers that she holds onto.

From the Infected man, Ch'en hears news of old Tom, who used to hate her. He fell out of favor with someone he never should have crossed, and died at the end of a private punishment. The fact that no one was held accountable for his death pains Ch'en all the more.
No one should have to bear a punishment heavier than they deserve. And if there's someone who takes that for granted, then that someone is the one who's wrong.

A few days later, the true culprit is left tied up in front of the police station, along with the evidence. The tabloids buzz, calling the unidentified figure an 'apostle of justice,' but Ch'en herself is unimpressed—if the police hadn't gone after the wrong person in the first place, no such figure would have needed to step in at all.
His name cleared, the Infected man follows Ch'en empty-handed, saying he has nothing worth commemorating—as a new temporary member of Rhodes Island.
After telling their fill of Iberian ghost stories, in the real Iberia

**Myrrh**, **Utage**, and **Beanstalk** are out on an herb-gathering mission. Checking the map, they realize they've come all the way to near the Iberian border before they knew it. Debating whether to keep heading north, the three somehow end up holding an Iberian ghost-story contest.

A city where not a single blade of grass grows; a ghost town where the pure-gold statues are actually petrified people; a festival thrown by corpses—one eerie rumor after another pours out. Only the frightened Beanstalk stiffens up. What kind of place is Iberia, really? Myrrh wonders.

And the scene shifts to a village in the real Iberia. Antonio, a boy who stole money for his friend José's tuition, and José, who lies to cover for him. Before the two children appears an Iberian bishop.

The bishop does not scold the children. Theft and lies are both wrong, yet he recognizes that they came from hearts that cared for each other. He counsels them to ask their own hearts what leads people to do wrong, and that to point out a wrong, one must first show what is right.
The power to make Iberia—to make us—better comes from within the heart.
Where did all the roads lead?

The seven fragments of travel share one thing in common. Their beginnings are all different—a circuit on a day off, a fugitive's wilderness, a Victoria visited to keep a promise, a nun's search for a way forward, a Catastrophe zone, a back alley in Columbia, the Iberian border—yet those roads keep converging in a single direction.

One is treated by Rhodes Island and becomes a member; one takes Rhodes's hand; one tucks Rhodes's address into a pocket. And some, like Ch'en, have already boarded that ship. Even upon this divided land, the roads always led somewhere after all.

There is no grand event, no spectacular ending. Simply people who took to the road carrying their own stories, quietly brushing past one another and reaching out a hand at the road's end. As long as the roads never break off in this divided land, the stories will go on too.

act13d0.tagline: People who set out to forget the place they left and live a new life, only to end up each speaking of somewhere they long to return to. About the smells, the alleys, and the songs that even lost memory can never erase.

chars.name: Surtr
chars.desc: An operator afflicted by an inexplicable memory disorder, wandering from place to place through her jumbled memories, one location at a time.

chars.name: Meteorite
chars.desc: A Sarkaz operator assigned to a survey mission.

chars.name: Arene
chars.desc: A Sankta Infected who left Laterano behind. She detests procedures of every kind.

chars.name: Adnachiel
chars.desc: An apprentice craftsman at the Third Repair Workshop. A Sankta whose bent halo keeps him from holding a gun.

chars.name: Ambriel
chars.desc: A sniper who left Laterano to live freely.

chars.name: Purestream
chars.desc: A native of Yan who loves lively crowds and is preparing a festival from her hometown.

chars.name: Elysium
chars.desc: An ever-cheerful operator who left Iberia behind.

chars.name: Thorns
chars.desc: A taciturn operator from Iberia.

chars.name: Absinthe
chars.desc: A caster who inherited her father's worn casting tools.

chars.name: Mudrock
chars.desc: A Sarkaz who leads her Infected kin.

chars.name: April
chars.desc: A sniper from the mining nation of Rim Billiton. Her codename comes from the title of a song.

chars.name: Ansel
chars.desc: A medic operator from Rim Billiton.

chars.name: Phantom
chars.desc: A ghostly operator haunted by the specters of his past.

chars.name: Mint
chars.desc: A rookie who just joined the caster team. A graduate of a Victorian university.

chars.name: Shamare
chars.desc: An operator from Siracusa who travels with her doll, Morté.

chars.name: Suzuran
chars.desc: An operator from Higashi, a gifted storyteller whose father was a shrine priest.

chars.name: Mousse
chars.desc: A Victorian operator who weaves voice acting into her tales.

chars.name: Popukar
chars.desc: A young operator who lives together with Orchid.

terms.name: Belloni Village
terms.desc: A mobile mining town on the frontier of Kazdel. It was sealed off after a mining collapse.

terms.name: Laterano
terms.desc: The nation of the Sankta. They revere firearms and do not accept the Infected.

terms.name: Iberia
terms.desc: The homeland of Thorns and Elysium. Once hostile toward the Ægir.

terms.name: Rim Billiton
terms.desc: A nation built on mining. The hometown of April and Ansel.

terms.name: Kazdel
terms.desc: The homeland of the Sarkaz. A wasteland built atop the ruins of an old war.

blocks.x: Rhodes Island is a mobile vessel that drifts across Terra carrying Oripathy patients and those with nowhere left to go. Every operator here is, without exception, someone who left somewhere behind.
Lately, one word keeps echoing through that ship in particular. Home. Someone is preparing a festival, someone is tracing old memories, someone is mailing a letter.
Why do people who have left keep thinking of the place they'll return to? This is a story in seven strands, told by people who live carrying that question.
The one who went looking for the home in her memory, and came back having only solved a mine collapse

Belloni Village, on the frontier of Kazdel. Rhodes Island sends an initial survey team to this old town, sealed off after its mine collapsed — the Sarkaz operator **Meteorite**, and **Surtr**, who insisted on tagging along.

Surtr carries an inexplicable memory disorder. So many memories are jumbled in her head that she can't tell which are real, so she goes from place to place, seeking out the locations within them one by one. This region felt oddly familiar to her.
But once she arrives, it's similar yet utterly different. She remembers the smell of dry plants, yet the streets in her memory were full of the smell of mud.

Growing impatient, Surtr plunges alone into a dangerous mine shaft. And she cleanly pinpoints nothing less than the cause of the collapse — a nest of Originium slugs beneath the shaft, and a vast underground cavern below that.
The problem was solved, but the memory she was looking for wasn't here. "I must have gotten it wrong after all." Mixing up the place in her memory isn't the first time it's happened to Surtr.

Meteorite tells her this: even if you didn't find it today, it's not as if you have nowhere to return to. If you need to, you can lean on me. On the way back, Surtr asks the village chief for just one thing — that ice cream the children were fighting over earlier, just one.
The Sankta who went to get a single signature, and ended up settling in
**Arene** heads to the Third Repair Workshop to get a signature on a weapon reliability certificate. A Sankta Infected who left Laterano, she finds this kind of procedure nothing but a nuisance.
But the craftsmen were all away. The only one left was the apprentice **Adnachiel** — a rather unusual Sankta, born with a bent halo that keeps him from even holding a gun.

Arene, who meant to just grab a signature and be done with it, ends up getting an Arts unit inspection too. Along the way, the two chat about Laterano — trivial things, like the habit Sankta have of not being able to wear hats. Arene even secretly forges the signature by copying it with Arts.

**Ambriel**, a sniper who lives freely, joins in too. Ambriel, who has no attachment to Laterano; the craftsman who won't go back because he hates guns... their reasons for leaving are all different, but not one of them intends to return.

The reason the gruff Arene ended up spending her whole day there? "Because I found some interesting people." That's reason enough. The forged signature is found out in the end, so she agrees to come back that afternoon to get it done properly.
Why, when asked if she misses home, she just answers that it's a dull place
The operators' lounge. **Purestream**, a native of Yan, is preparing a festival from her hometown. She's a girl who loves crowds and liveliness more than anything in the world.
There, the Iberians **Elysium** and **Thorns** gather, along with **Absinthe**, a caster who inherited her father's worn casting tools.
Asked whether she misses home, Elysium answers with a smile. Iberia is just a stifling, dull place, she says, so there's nothing much to tell.
But Thorns quietly counters her. Iberia was never so kind to the Ægir, he says. Many of them had to wander the outskirts of the city, or hold on while evading the tribunal's pursuit.
The truth Elysium had tried so hard to brush off with cheer slips out that way. Even so, Elysium says it to the end.
I have a feeling that maybe, someday, I might be able to go back there.
The ones who saw their kin home, and decided not to return themselves
And then, there's a bundle of very old letters. Letters **Mudrock** used to send to 'Bob,' an old friend who left for Columbia.
Back in the Reunion days, Mudrock was leading Leithanien's Infected toward Kazdel, her homeland. But in the thick fog, unknown casters came chasing after them.

Those casters used the Infected as tools for their Arts — by sending the Originium in the Infected's bodies into overload. Not knowing who, how many, or by what means they were being watched, her comrades fell one by one.

Chainsmoke, who had been sending regards to Bob, was the first to die, and there was a comrade who chose to end his own life rather than become a weapon. Merely escaping that forest cost half of their number.
Mudrock kept repeating the same words. Don't look back.
Let's move forward. Don't look back.

When they finally reached a village near Kazdel, Mudrock's group sent their kin on ahead. They chose to stay on the bridge and settle things with their pursuers — as if seeing them home was all that was left for them to do.

Onto that bridge, where they had resolved to die, someone walks. A young Sarkaz who, with a mere touch, stopped Mudrock's giant stone statue and even swept away the fog. "Who are you?" The letter breaks off there.
In her last letter to Bob, Mudrock writes calmly. She can never gather with those comrades again, she says. But they fell with honor.
She said she didn't care about home — yet ended up asking for a photo from a rooftop
**April** happens to see the ship's courier receiving a letter from **Ansel** of the medical department. Both of them are from the mining nation of Rim Billiton.

Ansel became a doctor against his uncle's objections. Though he claims going back is too much of a hassle, the truth is he still misses his hometown's great chimneys, the sound of machinery, and its bustling streets.
April had said she hated Rim Billiton. That hazy, soot-black place where the water would cut out mid-shower. But as they talk, the good memories keep coming back to her.

The neighbor who shared side dishes, the meatballs you couldn't buy anywhere else, a song she'd lie on the rooftop and listen to through her headphones. The title of that song was her very own codename — 《April》.

In the end, April asks the courier heading to Rim Billiton for a favor. To climb to the roof of a tall building and take a photo of the scenery visible from there. "Huh, turns out I cared quite a lot after all."
The ghost born in a home that doesn't exist
**Phantom** has the same dream every night. The stage of the Crimson Troupe, and the dead actors.

blocks.cap: On a stage in the dark, a low song drifting through the air.

blocks.x: In the dream, the actors of the old troupe return as specters. The brothers who once sang with him. They say Phantom killed the devil, and all of them too, and then left this place.

blocks.cap: "Who am I?" The ghost of the night, standing at the center of the stage.

blocks.x: To Phantom, who is losing his voice, they whisper. You must not sing, they say; you do not belong on this stage.

blocks.cap: The dead actors return to the stage once more.

blocks.x: Meanwhile, HR is troubled because Phantom's file is blank. The birthplace he'd written down, 'Celaiblason,' was a place name that, no matter how they searched, did not exist.

Phantom hadn't lied. It was simply what the person who took him in had told him. To escape the specters of his past, he came all the way to this ship in search of someone who could tell him his true past.
And the moment the rookie **Mint** repeated that unfamiliar place name, an operator approached and said, "A very long time ago, I used to live there." Adding that it would be a very long story.
Why do all the old tales end that way?
The night of an anniversary. As **Shamare** stitches up a burst seam on her doll Morté beside the sofa, operators gather one by one to share stories.

**Suzuran** tells the Yan myth «Shiya Benyue» and the Higashi legend of Nitō Jō; **Mousse** tells a Victorian tale of a dragon and siblings; Shamare shares a Siracusan tale of a mother wolf. **Popukar** haltingly carries on the tale of the wax doll and the bride.
But every one of the tales ends much the same. Greed and war, a monster that devours good people, and ruins. Popukar, who'd hoped for an ending where nobody dies, grows glum.
When Morté asks why the stories all end that way, Shamare answers quietly. Think of the shape of this world, she says — of the places we once lived.
You understand now, don't you, why the endings turn out that way?
On a ship of people who have lost the place they'd return to

Someone never did find the village in her memory; someone carries the name of a home that doesn't even exist. Someone's homeland to return to is gone; someone simply longs for a single view once seen from a rooftop.
Even so, each of them confirms the same thing in their own way. That the fact the place they left made them who they are now is not so easily erased, even when memory fades.
Why do people who have left keep thinking of the place they'll return to? Perhaps all of us are simply searching for a place to return to. Just like what Meteorite said back in Belloni Village.

act10d5.tagline: The story of five girls who clawed their way out of a school where Reunion had trapped them, only for survival itself to become a lifelong sin. And one more person who had to walk the same winter on the outside. — We became each other's habits.

chars.name: Gummy
chars.desc: The spirited cook of the Rhodes Island canteen, who goes around carrying a frying pan and a shield.

chars.name: Zima
chars.desc: The leader of the Ursus Student Self-government Group, who loves a good fight and rarely listens to the Doctor.

chars.name: Istina
chars.desc: The most level-headed strategist of the Self-government Group, and a lover of detective novels.

chars.name: Leto
chars.desc: The cheerful youngest fighter of the Self-government Group, who loves playing guitar.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The young girl who leads Rhodes Island.

terms.name: Ursus Student Self-government Group
terms.desc: A small group of Ursus students who survived Chernobog and came to Rhodes Island. Only five of them remain now.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: A city of Ursus that fell to Reunion's assault.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization made up of the Infected. It occupied Chernobog and locked the students inside a school.

terms.name: Peterheim High School
terms.desc: The school where Reunion gathered and confined students from several different schools.

terms.name: Catastrophe
terms.desc: A massive natural disaster that sweeps across the land, scattering Originium in its wake.

blocks.x: On Rhodes Island lives a cluster of young operators who all speak with an Ursus accent. They call themselves the **Ursus Student Self-government Group**.
Zima, Istina, Leto, Gummy, and Natalya. On the surface, they're just ordinary kids who eat, train, and bicker with one another.
But each of these kids carries a 'habit' of their own. And every one of those habits is a scar left from surviving a certain winter in a certain city.
When the Little Bear Gets Hungry, She Bites Whatever's Closest
**Gummy** is the second-earliest riser in the dorm. She's the spirited cook of the canteen, always toting a frying pan and a sturdy shield.
Today, big sis Istina left nothing but a note and headed out early. Gummy, who overslept, dashes off to the kitchen without even eating breakfast.
The problem is that Gummy has a certain habit. When she gets too hungry, she loses her self-control and chomps down on whatever is nearest — no matter what it happens to be.
That day, what Gummy bit happened to be the waist of the instructor, big sis Dur-nar. She apologizes, fills her stomach, and goes back to work.

During afternoon training, another habit rears its head. When she swings at something twice, the third swing goes in with all her might, entirely on reflex. That habit — cleaving a cutting board clean in two — sends her sparring partner Dur-nar flying.
Gummy did nothing wrong. She was just upset. She wants to do better, but Gummy can't change who 'Gummy' is.
But that day happened to mark exactly one year since the five of them came to Rhodes Island. Zima and Istina come to find Gummy, and the three of them lie side by side on the narrow bed.
Gummy has a great many habits. But when Zima and Istina are by her side, those habits stop being habits at all — because those two are Gummy's habit.
But one person was missing from this gathering. Gummy thinks to herself: next time, she hopes big sis **Rosa** will be here too.
The Words 'I'm Fine' Were Her Oldest Lie
**Istina** is the careful, detective-novel-loving strategist of the Self-government Group. Her real name is Anna Morozova.

One day, Istina sits down before a machine she borrowed from Closure. Its purpose: 'self-diagnosis for post-traumatic stress disorder.' She confides in herself the things she could never tell anyone else.
Istina speaks calmly. Everyone seems fine now, she says, but Gummy, Zima, Leto — and she herself — still haven't gotten past what happened at that school.
Just then, **May** — who fancies herself a detective — and her old friend Natalya drop by for some black tea. The room briefly erupts into chaos over shoving vegetable crackers into someone's mouth.
But when May absentmindedly picks up an old doll, Istina snatches it back sharply. It belonged to Vika — a friend who is no longer with them.
The three talk about detective novels. What about a sin that can be uncovered but never judged, Istina asks. May answers: what has already happened can never be forgotten by the one who lived it.
Long, long ago, Istina could only watch as Vika slipped from her fingertips and fell. She has never once forgotten that memory — the memory of failing to catch her.
After the guests leave, Istina speaks to Vika in the empty room. She knows, too, that someday she'll have to face it.
But not now. The time has not yet come.
A Single Candlestick Brought Down Everyone's Winter

**Zima** is the fight-loving leader of the Self-government Group who rarely listens to the Doctor. Her real name is Sonya. At her public school, she was known as 'General Winter,' the guardian who protected the weak.
Every night, Zima has the same nightmare. It begins at an ordinary breakfast table and always ends up back at that school.
It was when Reunion had occupied Chernobog and locked students from many schools into a single one. When the first fire burned down the food stores, the students began stealing each other's food.
Trapped inside, Anna (Istina) asked Sonya to become their new leader, and so the 'Ursus Student Self-government Group' was born.
To ease Anna's worries, Sonya launches a night raid on the last food store, which the noble students had seized. But she happened to knock over a candlestick.
It was the second fire. Even the last of the food was reduced to ashes, and the school fell into even greater chaos.
Then, without a sound, Reunion left the school. They could finally get out — but the very next day—

blocks.cap: What filled the space Reunion left behind wasn't freedom, but a Catastrophe that had swallowed the whole city.

blocks.x: The Catastrophe struck the school. And the kids who made it outside realized that the world beyond the school was far more brutal.

In the end, Sonya was rescued by a Rhodes Island operation team. But the fire, and ruining what Anna had wished for — it all felt like her fault. And so every night she dreams that everyone blames her.
The Noble Who Killed No One, Yet Called Herself the Filthiest of All

Natalya is a star of Rhodes Island's logistics department. She looks after the refugees with great care and has everyone's trust — and she was once the student council president of the Fourth High School.
But one day, it's Zima who comes to find Natalya first. The two aren't friends, nor do they even like each other — yet that's precisely why there were things they could say to one another.
For the first time, Natalya lays it all bare. At that school, it was she who rallied the noble students together, and she who agreed to the 'plundering of the commoners.'
In that place where everyone killed and was killed, I didn't kill a single person. But the filthiest one of all was probably me.

What despaired her most, Natalya says, was the shattering of her illusion that she was a good person. She confesses she thought of slitting her wrist with a paper knife, and held back by counting the urges, one after another.
Zima says she doesn't know how to comfort anyone, and just tells Natalya exactly what she thinks. Without you, Anna could never have endured those ten days alone. You did what you were able to do.
Wander around here and there, eat when you're hungry, go sleep when you're tired. Don't just sit still and stew over pointless thoughts.
Natalya laughs through her tears. Neither of them solved a single one of the other's problems — but they both survived all the same.
And Natalya decides to become a fighter — under the codename **Rosa**, which means 'dew' in Ursus.
A Not-at-All-Funny War Story, Spilled Out in a Honey-Drunk Haze
**Leto** is the youngest-ish fighter of the Self-government Group, and she loves playing guitar. Her original name is Rosalind. She was 'General Summer,' the counterpart to 'General Winter.'
One day, while training with Beehunter, Leto drinks a whole lot of honey mead, and the two of them end up honey-drunk.
As the buzz sets in, Leto starts spilling old stories — like the time at that school when she brought canned food to go join Sonya's side.
On a night when both food and people were scarce, a terrified Rosalind goes to meet the leader of Reunion. She didn't know what a mere student like her could do, but she felt she had to at least try.

The white-haired child, wearing a look somewhere between a smile and none at all, said this and that before letting her go. 'The suffering is only beginning' — or something like that; she doesn't quite remember.
When she came to her senses, Beehunter had passed out cold. Leto tells the rest of the story to her guitar, 'Stage,' instead.
The next morning, Leto doesn't remember a word of what she said. All she knows is that she slept soundly for the first time in ages.
Because Spring Always Comes — A Certain Policeman's Daughter
Here is one person who walked a different winter than the five girls inside the school.
She's a girl named Zoya, with a policeman for a father. Her father always promised that he would protect this home and this city, and that when spring came, they'd all go on an outing together.
But then Reunion descends upon the city. Zoya, who had been trapped in a school, slips out during a moment when a Reunion Caster deliberately looked the other way.
Outside was hell. From Valery, her father's colleague, Zoya learns that her mother never made it back from the district that was attacked first.
Her father had volunteered to go to Peterheim High School, where other students were trapped. Zoya takes a communication device to contact the army and heads there to help him.
Repeating to herself that she mustn't cry. That she, too, would become someone like her father.
And what Zoya found when she reached Peterheim was— a pitch-black, charred floor, and the school uniforms that covered every inch of it.
It was a sight she had never seen even in her dreams.
In that moment, only the promise her father had made that winter echoed in Zoya's ears.
Because in this city too, spring always comes.
Epilogue — The One Who Recognized That Uniform
Meanwhile, Amiya agonized for a long time over Natalya's application to become a fighter. From the Self-government Group's kids, she sensed not sorrow, nor anger, but an immense 'powerlessness.'
Still, Amiya decides to respect their choice. Protecting them by force could heal nothing.
Someday, I hope Rhodes Island can become their new home.
Some time later, an operator picks up a new record card from the logistics department. Her codename is **Absinthe**.
On her way to top up her card because she was hungry, Absinthe spots a group of operators chatting boisterously about a card game some distance away.
Gummy, Rosa, Leto, Istina, and Zima. Absinthe recognizes those uniforms at a single glance. They were the students of that school.
The five girls still each have their own habits. Biting whatever's closest when hungry, lying that they're fine, having nightmares every night — those habits.
But as long as they have each other by their side, those habits are no longer habits. And now, one more person who lived through the same winter stands beside them.

act7d5.tagline: Finding a pet-sitter, reopening an old diary, sighing in the kitchen — people who drifted here for their own separate reasons each quietly decide, 'Maybe I'll stay a little longer.' An ordinary day made all the more tender for being so ordinary.

chars.name: Vanilla
chars.desc: An intern huntress dispatched from Blacksteel. She dotes on her pets.

chars.name: Steward
chars.desc: A member of Reserve Op Team A4. An operator who looks after others with great care.

chars.name: Melantha
chars.desc: A taciturn Guard who rarely lets her true feelings show.

chars.name: Blaze
chars.desc: A blunt-spoken operator of Team A4. She's gentle with children.

chars.name: Ptilopsis
chars.desc: A Medic from Rhine Lab. She writes in her diary every day so she won't forget.

chars.name: Silence
chars.desc: The Rhine Lab researcher who brought Ptilopsis to Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Matterhorn
chars.desc: A shield operator from Kjerag who also mans the ship's kitchen as its cook.

chars.name: Ethan
chars.desc: A cheeky operator who uses his special skills to swipe food from the kitchen.

chars.name: Vulcan
chars.desc: A blacksmith from Minos.

chars.name: Orchid
chars.desc: A civilian-turned-operator who ended up a combatant almost by accident.

chars.name: Popukar
chars.desc: A young operator with tremendous strength.

chars.name: Midnight
chars.desc: Hailing from Higashi, an operator with a keen read on people's hearts.

chars.name: Dobermann
chars.desc: The instructor who leads the Reserve Op Teams, notorious for her harshness.

terms.name: Blacksteel
terms.desc: Officially known as Blacksteel Worldwide. A mercenary organization allied with Rhodes Island.

terms.name: Reserve Op Teams
terms.desc: Rhodes Island squads made up of rookie and civilian operators. Formations like A4 and A6 exist.

terms.name: Rhine Lab
terms.desc: A science and technology enterprise that pursues both the treatment of the Infected and research. Sharing common goals with Rhodes Island, it works in partnership with them.

terms.name: Originium Slug
terms.desc: A small creature that carries Originium crystals on its back. They're said to be common in the Sargon desert.

terms.name: Bolívar
terms.desc: A nation locked in a civil war among three intertwined factions. Dobermann's homeland.

blocks.x: Rhodes Island is a pharmaceutical company on wheels that treats the Infected. Aboard this vessel live operators who march to the battlefield, children who came to be treated, and people washing dishes in the kitchen — all side by side.
There's no grand event in this story. It's simply a collection of fragments from a few ordinary days.

People leaving their pets in someone's care, opening old diaries, sighing in the kitchen. Those who drifted onto this ship for their own separate reasons quietly ask themselves, 'Why am I here?' — that's the kind of day this is.
I asked someone to watch Roundy, Spiky, and Blackie — turns out they were all explosive Originium Slugs
**Vanilla** is an intern huntress dispatched from Blacksteel. With a mission alongside her seniors coming up in a few days, she found herself needing someone to look after her beloved pets.

Roundy, Spiky, and Blackie are Originium Slugs, and Sturdy is a mini Sand Metal Crab. When the Doctor's eyes lit up at the word 'Originium,' Vanilla panicked and cried out that they must never, ever eat the little ones. (Press Blackie's horn, apparently, and it explodes.)
Luckily, help turns up. **Melantha** is a taciturn Guard who rarely shows her true feelings, and **Steward** is the sort who looks after her attentively. The two of them agree to take the pets in.
Then **Blaze** shows up with Dora, a child who came for treatment, and bluntly declares that Rhodes Island isn't a place that heals people for free.

They don't take money, she says, but they do take payment — because these children will grow up to do greater things, so it's an 'advance investment.' Steward sees it a little differently: not everyone has to share the same intent; as long as they're all headed the same direction, they can walk together.

Vanilla, who had been showing off even the exploding shell-horn back in her room, makes a request as they part: to call her along next time they go to visit the children. She says she's come to want to fold paper flowers together with them.
She writes in her diary every day because every day she forgets
**Ptilopsis** is a Medic who came from Rhine Lab, following Silence. She takes out an old diary and reads back through it, page by page.
Oripathy has taken much from her. Sometimes when she wakes, she says, she forgets where she is or what she had been doing. So she writes in her diary every day, so as not to forget.

On an outing after a long while, Ptilopsis realizes she has been living while forgetting far too many of the things she took for granted — and that she had always drawn a line around the stories of her comrades' lives, dismissing them as 'nothing to do with me.'
What matters is that simply not forgetting is not enough.

So she goes to see **Silence** — wanting to talk not about their usual topics, but about things she had never thought to bring up before. She's come to want to know the people dear to her a little more.
Rhodes Island's fussiest cook, caught mid-sigh at the stove
**Matterhorn** is a shield operator from Kjerag who also mans the ship's kitchen as its cook. When it comes to hygiene, he's exacting.
Catching **Ethan** sneaking food, Matterhorn gets angrier not at the theft itself but at 'eating with bare hands without even washing them.' Ethan keeps teasing him by calling him 'old man.'
All that day, Matterhorn sighed often. His master, SilverAsh, had dropped by briefly to request a taste of home for his younger sister, Enya, before leaving again.

The one who most loved drinking tea with fragrant balm stirred in had originally been his master himself — yet ever since returning from Victoria, he had never once taken it that way. Perhaps he had forgotten. Matterhorn forces the thought aside.

To this Matterhorn, Ethan quietly presses a clumsy sandwich into his hands and bolts, saying it should at least count as a heartfelt token of thanks. Matterhorn took it gruffly — and according to Vulcan, the look on his face was actually rather pleased.
A wanderer who roamed the world in search of her name — and it had been carved on her axe all along
There was a wanderer who had been captured and brought in from Leithanien. She barely spoke the language and swung her weapon carelessly, so everyone just called her 'Pero.'
**Vulcan** is a blacksmith from Minos. Examining the old axe the wanderer cherished, she manages to read the ancient Minoan script carved into its handle.
It was her name: Ceobe. A name no one in the world had ever been able to read, and Vulcan was the first to call it aloud. She even gave her the nickname 'Keo.'

Ceobe's wishes were humble: to eat her fill, to sleep under a fluffy blanket, and to have a big room to hold all her treasures. When told that achieving this would mean using her head and fighting bad people, Ceobe's eyes sparkled as she answered, 'So you're telling me to become a hero?'
Vulcan writes her own name in the guarantor field. When the personnel officer asks why she'd take in a woman like that, the blacksmith gives a short answer.
There's no such thing as a bad person among those who love weapons.
She picked combat duty because she hated desk work — and now brings trouble on herself every single day
**Orchid** is a civilian-born operator who used to live in the city. The truth is, she hated sitting in an office, so on a whim she went and applied for combat duty.

Orchid always frets over young **Popukar**. The child had been working at a logging camp before Kal'tsit and Ansel brought her in, and she still clings to the words that her parents 'went far away.' At that, Orchid wordlessly pulls the child into a tight embrace.
**Midnight**, who hails from Higashi, is an operator with a sharp read on people's hearts. To Orchid, whom he meets at the training grounds, he tells his own story.

Once, when he was riding high, there was a junior who envied him. The junior went so far as to infect him with Oripathy, and in the end the two turned their blades on each other. Midnight had to bring that ending about with his own hands.

Even so, Midnight is calm about it. Having glimpsed the cruel truth of the world, he says, doesn't mean you must let that truth stain you. Don't blame yourself for having grown up sheltered — decide for yourself how you'll live from here. In the end, Orchid resolves to stay a combatant, even though it doesn't really suit her.
Honestly… I'm always bringing trouble on myself.
The most terrifying instructor was, in truth, the kindest

It was test day for the Reserve Op Teams. **Dobermann** is an instructor notorious for putting rookies through the wringer. When Fang and Beagle volunteer to help clean her room, a somewhat special story begins.

From the wardrobe, a set of old dog tags falls out. Allen, Juan, José… inscribed with the names of comrades who died back in Dobermann's Bolívar days. They weren't close — they'd barely exchanged more than a few words — yet to her, they were precious people.

Once a colonel of the autonomous government, then passing through the resistance before drifting here, she says this about soldiers: the soldier who gives up on thinking is the finest soldier. But that is a cruel thing, and there is only one answer — to hand down the 'precise orders' that a soldier can trust and follow.
Dobermann, who had never seen such a thing anywhere in Bolívar, says that at Rhodes Island she saw it for the first time.
Because this is a utopia that idealists risk their lives to protect, for the sake of small wishes.

Grinning mischievously that anyone who gives that exact answer on the test scores a zero, Dobermann, alone in the room the youngsters have left, murmurs the three names again. Allen, Juan, José. Even having found a new future, she says, her roots are still in Bolívar — and someday she'll have to go back.
Come in through different doors, looking the same direction

The huntress who left her pets in someone's care, the Medic who started writing her diary anew, the wanderer who gained a name, the civilian who stayed almost by accident, and the instructor who never forgot her roots. The reasons they drifted to Rhodes Island were each their own.

For money, for treatment, out of a hatred for the office, or simply to find a name. And yet one day, without quite meaning to, they all come around to thinking, 'Maybe I'll stay a little longer.' Not because anyone told them to — but of their own accord.

There was no grand event. Just an ordinary day of feeding pets, writing in diaries, laying out a meal, and folding paper flowers. And those small fragments, gathered together, are what nudge this ship on wheels a little further forward today, too.

act6d5.tagline: The tale of a three-day, three-night struggle against a legendary calamity come to destroy Lungmen—only to discover it was all the filming of a New Year's movie. Why must the villain be so utterly incapable of honesty?

chars.name: Ch'en
chars.desc: A senior superintendent of the Lungmen Guard Department, and one of the youngest superintendents in its history.

chars.name: Snowsant
chars.desc: A rookie intern who is also head of Lungmen's Defense Engineering Department. Her promotions are lightning-fast, but her confidence is rock-bottom.

chars.name: Amiya
chars.desc: The chief of Rhodes Island. Young in years, yet trusted by everyone.

chars.name: Lava
chars.desc: An elite operator of Rhodes Island. An Arts master and a disaster specialist.

chars.name: Nian
chars.desc: A legendary calamity said to descend upon Lungmen at the close of every year.

terms.name: Lungmen Guard Department
terms.desc: The organization overseeing Lungmen's public order and defense. It answers to the supreme ruler, Secretary Wei Yenwu.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization made up of the Infected. To the citizens of Lungmen, they are objects of terror.

terms.name: Puppet Soldiers
terms.desc: Enemies of unknown nature that take the forms of Reunion members or the dead. No Originium reaction can be detected from them at all.

terms.name: City Defense Cannon
terms.desc: A super-massive cannon guarding Lungmen's outskirts. It runs on an Originium-driven core.

blocks.x: On the night the year turns over, Lungmen is always on edge—all because of a legend that a monster called 'Nian' descends upon the city at every year's end.
But this Nian hasn't shown so much as its nose for decades. The rookies all write it off as an urban legend.
This year, though, the Guard Department is said to have caught some kind of lead. And so a Rhodes Island vessel arrives at Lungmen.
All of Lungmen Pulls an All-Nighter Over a Monster That Hasn't Appeared in Decades
On New Year's Eve, the lights of the Defense Command Center never go dark. **Wei Yenwu**, Lungmen's supreme ruler of unknown origin, has ordered the entire city to prepare for the worst.

Senior superintendent **Ch'en** of the Guard Department brings in a rookie who'd been wandering around lost. **Snowsant**, the head of the Defense Engineering Department, just back from Columbia—her promotions are lightning-fast, yet her confidence is rock-bottom.

blocks.cap: Snowsant—the rookie engineer with no sense of direction and no shortage of fear.

blocks.x: Snowsant had thought Nian was just an old story her grandmother used to tell. But Ch'en tells her that's nothing more than self-comfort, a way of turning away from the truth.
At that moment, a Rhodes Island vessel docks at the harbor. **Amiya** has come, escorting the Doctor. But Amiya has been fretting for a while now, unable to find **Lava**.
A Guard Department Superintendent Comes to Detain the Hooligan Big Sis Mid-New-Year's-Party
In Lungmen Park, the oni big sis is throwing a New Year's drinking bash with her underlings. She caused so much trouble last year that Ch'en has come to rein her in ahead of time this year.
The two are old acquaintances, so in the end they settle it with a drinking contest. If the oni big sis wins, the Guard Department will tell her about the lead it found.
But Ch'en wins—slurring her words, thoroughly drunk.
And she lets it slip: they've found a massive pit with no known cause, melted buildings, and scorch marks over an enormous area. Yet there was no trace of Originium Arts.

In one corner of the park, a retired old detective plays Go with a former colleague, while an honor student who passed the Guard Department exam enjoys the new year with a friend. It was a peaceful night.
Into That Peaceful Street, the Dead Came Walking
All at once, groups bearing Reunion's insignia appear throughout the city.

But something's off. They don't speak, they don't bleed, and when cut down, even their corpses vanish. There's no Originium reaction at all, either. The Guard Department decides to call these things **Puppet Soldiers**.

blocks.cap: Heat sources register but no physical bodies—the command center is in chaos.

blocks.x: Worse still, some of the Puppet Soldiers look exactly like Guard Department members, and others wear the faces of Infected who have already died. The officers are shaken.

Here, Snowsant flips the whole approach on its head: run every Originium-powered machine at maximum output, then look only for the spots where there's no reaction—that way, they can pin down the hidden heat sources.
It's a gamble that could paralyze all of Lungmen, but Secretary Wei trusts Snowsant and pushes it through.
There Was Exactly One Person on Rhodes Island Who Knew the Monster's True Nature
The vanished **Lava** appears before Nian. She's Rhodes Island's Arts master and disaster specialist, and she's said to have been raised her whole life to hunt calamities like Nian.

blocks.cap: Lava—an operator whose Arts overcome even the constraints of space.

blocks.x: Nian reveals that she is one of twelve siblings—calamities who have killed one another over an unfathomable span of years.
Nian's power is 'smithing'—melting, casting, and polishing. The Puppet Soldiers, too, were forged using people's memories and fears as raw material.
Melt, cast, and polish. This city is just perfect to use as a forge.
Lava combs through a mountain of ancient legends and finds Nian's weakness: sound and light—that is, firecrackers.
For the Firecracker to Bring Nian Down, Lava Chose the City Defense Cannon

It's the third day since the Guard Department lost District 21. Lava asks Snowsant for the underground layout. Snowsant, having memorized the blueprints whole, sketches them out on the spot without missing a beat.
Lava's plan is to blow the **City Defense Cannon**'s core and use it as a giant firecracker—luring Nian right up to it by making herself the bait.
Just before the blast, Lava thrusts even her own body into the source of the explosion—ready to die alongside it.
But Nian ends up saving Lava. She hates the sound of firecrackers so much that she lunged in before she even realized it.
Just When They Thought They Had Her, Nian's Parting Words Went Like This
Nian pretends to surrender meekly, then tells them the real reason she attacked this city.
The reason I attacked this city is… because Lungmen's Sichuan pepper is, honestly, just far too bland.
And then Nian's body vanishes—she'd used the blast's energy to create an illusion of herself and slip away.
Not content with that, Nian hoists all of Lungmen into the air and raises it up as a colossal beast—even giving it the name 'Xuanji Colossus.'

blocks.cap: The whole city lifts into the air and becomes a monster.

blocks.x: But That Colossal Beast Was a Scene That Wasn't in the Script
"Wait, wait! What are you chanting all of a sudden?! This is not okay!"
Lava stops Nian. …Yes, all of this was, in fact, the filming of a **New Year's short film**. Its title: 'Ancient Forge.'
Lava is the director, and Nian is the lead. That whole invasion drama we watched with sweat in our palms was a script written by Nian.

But Nian keeps rewriting the script however she pleases—throwing in a colossal beast, a zombie horde, casting operators like Blaze and Emperor as she pleased, and even tacking on a teaser for a sequel.

blocks.cap: 'Ancient Forge 2, Coming Soon.' A trailer the lead actress secretly slipped in.

blocks.x: Lava blows her top, saying there's no B-movie quite this B-grade.
Does a Villain Really Have to Be This Incapable of Honesty?
As it turns out, Nian is a movie-obsessed calamity who has settled down to live on Rhodes Island—so much so that she goes around recommending Rhodes Island even to her less human-hostile siblings.

**Closure** puts in her two cents: the script crams in way too much information, and the B-movie sensibility is over the top. But Nian says only she knows how much of it is real and how much is made up.
The reason Nian went out of her way to make Lava the protagonist is simple: she likes Lava a great deal. Her effort, her way of thinking, and her growth all bring Nian joy.
Back on set, Lava reads over the script Nian had rewritten at her own whim.

blocks.cap: Lava, quietly reading down the script that's been turned into a mess.

blocks.x: "Lungmen's Sichuan pepper is far too bland"… Really. That never-honest-even-if-it-kills-her personality of hers hasn't changed one bit.

That legendary monster said not to have appeared in decades? She was actually somewhere aboard the ship, eating hotpot and rewriting a New Year's movie script. Honestly—what an utter mess of a villain.

act4d0.tagline: Stories of people who set out to save someone, only to be handed a dying wish of their own: to go on living. Were the deaths of those who fell nameless truly without any meaning at all?

chars.name: Hellagur
chars.desc: A veteran warrior of Ursus. He now works at Rhodes Island's clinic.

chars.name: Patriot
chars.desc: Leader of Reunion's Northern Tundra guerrillas.

chars.name: Scout
chars.desc: A Sarkaz operator of Rhodes Island.

chars.name: W
chars.desc: The new leader of the Sarkaz mercenary band under Reunion.

chars.name: Flamebringer
chars.desc: A Sarkaz mercenary who alone survived.

chars.name: Dobermann
chars.desc: An instructor at Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Executor
chars.desc: An executor of wills at Laterano's Notarial Hall.

chars.name: Vermeil
chars.desc: A one-armed Vulpo girl living in the forests of Siracusa.

chars.name: Liskarm
chars.desc: A marksman of Blacksteel.

chars.name: Jessica
chars.desc: A rookie dispatched from Blacksteel to Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Franka
chars.desc: A swordswoman of Blacksteel.

chars.name: Ifrit
chars.desc: A young operator who wields fire Arts.

chars.name: Frostleaf
chars.desc: An operator who uses frost Arts.

chars.name: Projekt Red
chars.desc: An operator who reflexively slashes at anyone who comes too close.

chars.name: Magallan
chars.desc: A field observer for Rhine Lab on the northern ice fields.

chars.name: Santalla
chars.desc: An operator from Sami.

chars.name: Kal'tsit
chars.desc: The one who leads Rhodes Island.

chars.name: Closure
chars.desc: Rhodes Island's chief engineer.

chars.name: Heavyrain
chars.desc: A new operator from Sargon.

terms.name: Chernobog
terms.desc: An industrial city of Ursus. Reunion triggered a Catastrophe and brought it down.

terms.name: Reunion
terms.desc: An armed organization made up of Infected from Ursus.

terms.name: Oripathy
terms.desc: An incurable disease in which Originium infection slowly turns the body to stone. The Infected face severe discrimination.

terms.name: Sarkaz
terms.desc: A race that has long suffered persecution. Many of them are formidable warriors.

terms.name: Notarial Hall of Laterano
terms.desc: A Laterano institution charged with executing wills and contracts.

blocks.x: The Ursus industrial city of Chernobog has fallen. Reunion, the armed organization of the Infected, unleashed a Catastrophe that shattered the fortress and the city alike.
Scattered across those ashes are the stories of people who seem to have nothing to do with one another. Some question the meaning of war; others are handed a reason to keep on living.
And every one of these stories pauses, for a moment, before the same question: were the deaths of those who fell nameless truly without any meaning?
Reunited after twenty years, comrades now on opposite sides

A Guard operator — whose name and insignia we never quite make out — wakes among the ruins of Chernobog. His unit was apparently wiped out after infiltrating to rescue the Doctor, and all that lies beside him is a note written on special Rhodes Island paper: stay hidden and wait for night.

Just then he overhears two men talking nearby. One is **Hellagur**, an Ursus veteran now serving at Rhodes Island's clinic. The other is **Patriot**, leader of Reunion's Northern Tundra guerrillas — and, twenty years ago, the very Sarkaz soldier 'Buldrokkas'tan' whom Hellagur once revered.

Oripathy has ruined Patriot's vocal cords, so he can no longer speak at length. Even so, word by careful word, he vows to fight for the rights of the Infected. Had Ursus only regarded the Infected as fellow Ursus, he says, there would have been no war to begin with.
Hellagur is weary. To him, what Reunion is waging is, in the end, a civil war in which Ursus slaughters Ursus. All he wants now is to protect the people close to him.
You want to light the bulbs again; I only wish the shopping center would be rebuilt. That is the difference between us.

Patriot knows he has little time left; Oripathy will take him in the end. So he tells Hellagur, who hates war, that he will do the fighting in his stead. The two part with a promise never to be enemies to each other again.
A warrior who threw away his life for a man whose name he never even asked

Before they part, Patriot hands over a keepsake. A certain Sarkaz warrior of Rhodes stood alone against a mercenary band and died, he says. Cutting down elite after elite, the warrior deliberately lured the enemy toward the ruins — as if to hide something, or rather someone.
That Guard lying in the ruins was that very 'someone.' The one who saved him was **Scout**, a Sarkaz operator of Rhodes Island.

Scout's final words come through. He worries about the Doctor — fearing that war and slaughter may have turned the Doctor into a war machine, and wishing the Doctor would never command a battle again and live a new life instead.

And he speaks of his own team. Twelve of them. Teammates who broke through enemy lines and repelled the reinforcements, but who all fell in battle. No one will remember their names, he knows, yet he begs that someone bear witness to them.
No one will remember my team or the names of its members — but their deaths were dignified. They were never, ever afraid of dying.

His last request was this: nameless brother, live on for your own sake. Had he wanted you dead, he would not have said so much. The Guard sobs and vows to see it through, and Patriot tells him he is free to choose — to head south tonight, or to stay in Chernobog and help its people.
The Sarkaz who went out of his way to refuse an offer to join

Meanwhile, a troubling report reaches **W**, the new leader of Reunion's mercenary band: a pursuit squad that had been chasing a unit that betrayed them and fled has vanished entirely. The culprit is a single Sarkaz warrior.

He is **Flamebringer**, the mercenary 'Sword-Caster' who alone survived an ambush in some war. W recognizes him and, smiling, tries to lure him to her side, but Flamebringer refuses, saying he has no interest in senseless destruction. He is only searching for the meaning of his own survival — a place to hone his blade.

blocks.cap: At W's words, 'You'll probably die,' Flamebringer can hardly hide his excitement.

blocks.x: W dangles the decisive bait: there was a conspiracy behind the annihilation of Flamebringer's old mercenary unit, and its mastermind might be at the very place he is headed. Flamebringer breaks through the encirclement and makes for that place — Rhodes Island.

Instructor **Dobermann** takes him in, with a warning that she will show no mercy if he breaks the rules. And in the medical department, someone who wished to see him was waiting. Seeing that face, Flamebringer gives a faint smile.
Only by breaking free of suffocating shackles can one take another step forward.
A guest who dismantled every trap on his way in — and says he's come to save her
The stage shifts far away, to the forests of Siracusa, where **Vermeil** lives — a one-armed Vulpo girl consumed by vengeance who has laid traps all around her.

Into that forest comes **Executor**, an executor of the Notarial Hall of Laterano — a man who catches incoming arrows barehanded and dismantles every trap. What he has come to execute is a will. The 'old hunter' who once fitted Vermeil with an iron arm was, in fact, Dürrenmatt, the man who left that will.
He died on the operating table from an incurable illness. Leaving his entire fortune to cover Vermeil's treatment, he asked for just one thing: 'Let Vermeil go on living.'
'Do you wish to go on living?' Yes or no — you need only answer.

Vermeil desperately refuses, but Executor will not back down. In the end, he secures her promise to go to Rhodes Island once she has said farewell to her people. To the very last, the hunter wished for that child to escape a life like his own and truly live.
Three hours of shooting drills — but what's really lacking is confidence

Aboard Rhodes, docked at Lungmen, Blacksteel's **Liskarm** is teaching marksmanship to her junior **Jessica**. Jessica is the type who, in trying to do everything perfectly, only ends up shrinking back.

**Franka** butts in, pretending she's just there to goof off. Glancing at Jessica's evaluation records, she has a word to say: Jessica may not be at the top, but her overall marks are by no means behind — this is absolutely not the data of a poor student. What Jessica truly lacks is not skill, but confidence.
The girl who keeps burning her homework

In Rhodes's 'classroom,' **Ifrit** is wrestling with the homework the Doctor assigned. This fire-wielding girl, when her emotions flare, scorches everything around her without meaning to — her desk, and the homework she worked so hard to finish.

In her dreams, a 'monster' always mocks Ifrit — a monster that hurts others and cannot even control itself. After snapping at a nurse who came to examine her, Ifrit nearly explodes in front of **Frostleaf**, who wields frost, and **Projekt Red**, who slashes at anyone who comes near.
Then the memory of an old friend quells the flames with water Arts. Coming to her senses, Ifrit resolves to apologize to Frostleaf and the nurse, admitting that this time it really was her fault.
I'll burn away everything that blocks my path — but the things I mustn't burn, those I will never lay a hand on!
The stubbornness to guard an outpost for someone who may never come

On the frozen ice fields of the north, Rhine Lab's observer **Magallan** guards a lone outpost all by herself — an optimistic, talkative explorer. When a woman appears before her through the blizzard, Magallan mistakes her for a ghost and is startled out of her wits.
The woman's name is Simón — actually **Santalla**, a Sami operator sent by Rhodes, now cooperating with Rhine Lab, to come fetch Magallan. But of all times, the outpost's heating breaks down.
Magallan insists she will guard the outpost even at the cost of her life — even though not a single visitor has ever come. She believes that someday, someone is certain to pass through this place.
Someday, our footprints will cover this whole land.
Moved by that stubbornness, Santalla goes out into the blizzard in her place — carrying the power the dead of Sami left to her. As she sings, the northern blizzard begins to subside.
Deep in a pitch-black duct, changing a single light bulb

Back at Rhodes, there are **Kal'tsit** and chief engineer **Closure**. After bickering over how to welcome the Doctor, who has lost their memory, the two head into a dark duct to repair a severed cable.

Along the way they also meet **Heavyrain**, a new operator from Sargon. As she changes a single bulb in the pitch darkness, Closure asks Kal'tsit what's truly on her mind — how Kal'tsit felt, having no choice but to support Amiya in her decision to head for Chernobog.
At least we made it through the night, so all that's left to do is survive until the sun rises.
The bulb lights up. After all, to walk a night road, one needs light. Kal'tsit murmurs quietly that it is a truly warm light.
What the nameless left behind

After Chernobog fell, the stories of these strangers each run their own course. Yet, curiously, every one of them carries someone's dying wish — a plea to fight in their stead, to please go on living, to keep faith in the one who will someday come.

Were the deaths of those who fell nameless truly without any meaning? At least as long as there remain people who remember those names in their stead and live on bearing their wishes, surely they were not.
